Title: Associate Contracts Manager (UK)
Description: Are you looking for a new challenge in a fast-paced, innovative company? Come make history with Amazon's legal department.Continue to article...We are looking for an Associate Contracts Manager to support Amazon Music's growing businesses. You will work with our digital music team in the UK and in the EU on all aspects of their businesses, and in particular on our music streaming services (e.g. Amazon Music Unlimited and Prime Music).The successful candidate will possess the ability to work efficiently and independently; manage high volumes of tasks and projects with little guidance; react with appropriate urgency to situations and events that require a quick response or turnaround; take effective action without having to know the total picture; and think creatively and be proactive in solving problems that affect the Music team or other related businesses.Your responsibilities will include working with the business to review marketing assets and other customer-facing messaging; negotiating and drafting a variety of third party contractual relationships, including marketing agreements, master services agreements, content license agreements and NDAs; developing internal processes to track transactions from beginning to end; and coordinating with business clients and legal teams.This position will be based in London.Basic Qualifications2+ years of experience as a contracts manager or similar office experienceExperience working with third party contractual relationshipsPreferred QualificationsBachelor's degreePrevious in-house legal experience is a plusExcellent written and oral communication skillsExcellent organizational skills; ability to manage multiple projects at once, follow through and meet deadlinesAbility to work in German, French, Italian or Spanish is a plus but not requiredAmazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on objective criteria including your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. We welcome applications from all sections of society irrespective of colour, race, religion or belief, nationality, ethnic or national origin, gender, gender reassignment, gender identification, sexual orientation, sex, marital status, disability, age or trade union membership. Please let us know if you have any special requirements in relation to this recruitment process.

Preferred Qualifications: Previous in-house legal experience is a plus
Excellent written and oral communication skills
Excellent organizational skills; ability to manage multiple projects at once, follow through and meet deadlines

Title: Manager, Revenue Operations, EMEA (UK)
Description: We are looking for a Manager, Revenue Operations to support the Order-to-Cash team with Revenue Accounting, Compliance Contract Review, and Accounts Receivable in EMEA.Continue to article...The ideal candidate will be a motivated self-starter who thrives working in a dynamic environment. Above all, your work will impact the way the world experiences music. The ideal candidate will have at least 8+ years in finance and/or accounting with 4+ years focused on the advertising industry. The position is based in London, UK.What you will doEnsure accurate and timely reporting of the Company's financials in accordance with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), Sarbanes-Oxley control requirements, and local regulatory guidelinesAdvise the Compliance outsourced team members in Manila on the global advertising contract review policies in accordance with the Company's policies and IFRS.Support the global Accounts Receivable accounting close including the allowance for doubtful accounts calculations, ensuring accurate results are delivered on time for reporting in accordance with IFRS.Review trading agreements and related accruals during the revenue month-end close in accordance with IFRSBuild effective relationships with the local sales team members to identify and resolve collections and revenue risks for EMEA.Work cross functionally with the Financial Engineering team to help drive key system initiatives and system improvementsAdvise the Order-to-Cash team on EMEA-specific billing requirements and business practices for the advertising industry.Identify areas for continuous improvement in the Order-to-Cash programs by reviewing company policies and advising management on possible risks.Responsible for leading the implementation of best practice, creating and communicating policies, and managing the daily operations, frameworks, and processes to cross functional teams in the EMEA regions to scale our global revenue operations and achieve key operational metrics.Other responsibilities include but are not limited to participate in special projects, system implementations and enhancements, or ad hoc analyses as assigned.Who you are:Minimum of 8+ years finance and/or accounting experience in the Technology, Podcasting, Entertainment or Media industriesMinimum of 4+ years in advertising revenueCPA license requiredBachelor's degree in AccountingSOX and local compliance knowledge for EMEA countriesExperience with major ERP system required. Acceptable systems include Netsuite, Oracle or SAP.Proficient in excel, with prior experience building reports for revenue insights.Prior experience with reporting revenue and/or results to senior management requiredPrior experience with month end close requiredAble to work under time pressure, prioritize a high workload, and meet strict deadlines.Positive attitude and flexible to changing priorities, especially in the period of month/quarter/year-end closing.You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what's playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It's in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world's most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 271 million users.

Title: Product Manager, Personalized Playlists (US)
Description: The Personalization team makes deciding what to play next easier and more enjoyable for every listener. From Daily Mix to Discover Weekly, we’re behind some of Spotify’s most-loved features. We built them by understanding the world of music and podcasts better than anyone else. Join us and you’ll keep millions of users listening by making great recommendations to each and every one of them.Continue to article...We are looking for a passionate Product Manager to join our Mixer team. Spotify users enjoy listening to personalized playlists including Daily Mix, Discover Weekly and Radio — in this position you will be responsible for building platform capabilities that help users find the best content for them while helping Artist to increase their fan base.As Product Manager, you will work with a cross-functional team from Design, User Research, Data Science, and Engineering to define the roadmap for the platform that matches Artist and Fans in a personalized and relevant way.You will partner with stakeholders across our Mobile, Partner Platform, and Personalization teams as well as Marketing and Business Development.What You’ll DoBuild a roadmap for your team by understanding Listener, Artist and Spotify business needs, utilizing data insights and working closely with stakeholdersDefine metrics and KPI’s for the features that your team buildsPrioritize new features based on user needs, Spotify short term and long term business goals and technical platform scalabilityMake sure that your team is strong and healthy – including capabilities, happiness, resilience and growthWho You AreYou want to change the music industry by helping artists to reach out to their fans and build connections with them. You want to make this happen at scale for millions of artists and fans.You have 3 years of experience in building and shipping Platform Products together with teams of designers, engineers and data scientists, resulting in significant business impactYou have built scalable ML technologies in the past. You understand the concepts in ML model training and testing. You are comfortable with explaining a business problem to an ML engineer and can simplify complex ML concepts for your non-engineer stakeholders.You are comfortable with making data based decisions and have experience in A/B tests, user research and data analysisYou are passionate about bringing clarity into the product. Ambiguity doesn’t scare youYou know how to inspire and motivate people to get things doneYou are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 286 million users.

What You\u2019ll Do: Build a roadmap for your team by understanding Listener, Artist and Spotify business needs, utilizing data insights and working closely with stakeholders
Define metrics and KPI’s for the features that your team builds
Prioritize new features based on user needs, Spotify short term and long term business goals and technical platform scalability
Make sure that your team is strong and healthy – including capabilities, happiness, resilience and growth
Who You Are: You want to change the music industry by helping artists to reach out to their fans and build connections with them. You want to make this happen at scale for millions of artists and fans.
You have 3 years of experience in building and shipping Platform Products together with teams of designers, engineers and data scientists, resulting in significant business impact

You have built scalable ML technologies in the past. You understand the concepts in ML model training and testing. You are comfortable with explaining a business problem to an ML engineer and can simplify complex ML concepts for your non-engineer stakeholders.

You are passionate about bringing clarity into the product. Ambiguity doesn’t scare you
You know how to inspire and motivate people to get things done

Title: Associate Counsel, Digital Legal Affairs (US)
Description: A little bit about our team:Continue to article...The Digital Legal Affairs team is a part of WMG's corporate legal department. The team is charged with negotiating and drafting WMG's key global, multi-territory, and US digital deals in tandem with WMG's Digital Strategy and Business Development team. The Digital Legal Affairs team also contributes to digital licensing policy and strategy and coordinates digital deal implementation.This position reports directly to the Senior Vice President, Head of Digital Legal Affairs, and ultimately to the General Counsel. In addition to working with the rest of the Digital Legal Affairs team and the Digital Strategy and Business Development team on a day-to-day basis, the role involves regular communication with WMG's labels, Warner Chappell Music, others in the corporate legal department and internal business units and constituencies (e.g., Digital Marketing, Finance, Reporting, Operations, Royalty Accounting, Litigation, Tax, etc.) on important topics raised by digital deals and developments.Job description:As Associate Counsel in Digital Legal Affairs, you will draft and negotiate complex and often first-of-kind digital music licensing deals (e.g., ad-supported streaming, subscription streaming, webcasting, social media platforms, apps using music, retail establishment music services, VR & AR, and other emerging technologies), support various aspects of the deal-making process, advise and execute on digital strategy, and brief WMG's senior management on digital deals and developments. Overview: At Sony Music Entertainment, we are passionate about helping our artists succeed in an industry that sees constant change and opportunity for innovation. The Global Digital Business team is on the front line, ensuring that Sony Music makes the most of our relationships with major digital distribution partners, and also working with exciting digital music startups around the world. We strive to be ahead of the curve and shape how customers connect with the music they love.

As the Director of Global Business Development, you will join an international team of data-savvy negotiators who focus on executing digital distribution partnerships with a variety of global and multi-territory accounts.

On day one, you should be able to articulate digital music deal structures and strategies, evaluate deal performance and options, and start taking a leading role in negotiations with Sony Music’s commercial partners.
You will be based in New York City and report to the Vice President of Global Business Development.

This is a rare opportunity to have direct influence on music industry evolution, present recommendations to senior management, and negotiate deals that benefit Sony Music’s entire artist roster, while closely collaborating with diverse teams across the company and the globe.

Have a track record negotiating innovative, high-value digital content licensing deals that align interests among sophisticated business organizations
Strong analytical skills, using data, insights and market realities to inform your recommendations, and to regularly re-assess the tactics and resources required to achieve objectives
Ability to manage several projects simultaneously while working collaboratively with individuals across multiple functional areas of the business
Demonstrated ability to pragmatically handle complex negotiation situations in a fast-paced international environment, and be a strong closer
Proven experience in internal and external stakeholder management with the ability to build successful relationships founded on collaboration

Very good communication skills, with the ability to thoroughly and concisely advise senior management, as well as educate colleagues, build relationships and enhance the overall organization’s culture, diversity and performance
Qualifications: 4-year undergraduate degree

At least one of the following:MBA/JD from top-tier program, and 3-6 years with a digital music/media organization in a business development, legal, or commercial role4-6 years’ experience in a top tier management consultancy, investment banking, or corporate / business development position, and 2-4 years with a digital music/media organization in a business development role
MBA/JD from top-tier program, and 3-6 years with a digital music/media organization in a business development, legal, or commercial role

4-6 years’ experience in a top tier management consultancy, investment banking, or corporate / business development position, and 2-4 years with a digital music/media organization in a business development role
A strong team orientation required

Title: Manager, International Music, APAC Region (UK/US)
Description: The International Music Team is looking for a Manager to oversee and advise our music partners in Southeast Asia, Japan, Australia, and New Zealand.Continue to article...This position will be based in New York City or London.What You'll DoAct as a strategic advisor to music teams in the APAC region; including Editors and Artist and Label Partnerships teams in Southeast Asia, Japan, Australia and New ZealandRepresent APAC markets in central music team forumsOperationalize global music strategies and policies across APAC markets Support APAC with multi-market and international initiatives, driving global export of local music, artists and playlistsMaintain regular 1:1s with music team leaders in each market Regularly travel to the region and embed in local meetings and events Lead monthly regional APAC music team meetings Support APAC with new market launches and territorial expansions Provide central onboarding and training for new music team hires in APAC Advise on the development, testing and go-to-market strategy for new products and features that will impact music teams in APAC Nurture cross-territory learning and collaboration Drive the expansion of international priority campaigns and programs into APACWho You AreYou have extensive music industry experience. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world's most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 271 million users.

Excellent communication skills across multiple stakeholders, including senior leadership,  to get things done efficiently and correctly.

Title: Dance Marketing Manager, ADA Music (UK)
Description: With its broad and diverse roster of new stars and legendary artists, Warner Music Group is home to a collection of the best-known record labels in the music industry including Asylum, Atlantic, East West, Elektra, FFRR, Fueled by Ramen, Nonesuch, Parlophone, Rhino, Roadrunner, Sire, Warner Records, Warner Classics and Warner Chappell Music, one of the world’s leading music publishers with a catalogue of more than one million copyrights worldwide.Continue to article...For more than four decades, WMG has been an industry-leading force in providing a world-class array of services designed to help artists and labels grow their careers and their businesses. Artist & Label Services is the umbrella for WEA (Warner-Elektra-Atlantic) 3 the pioneering WEA distribution and marketing network 3 and Alternative Distribution Alliance (ADA) 3 the ground-breaking global distribution company for independent artists and labels.Job Title: Dance Marketing Manager, ADA Music UKA little bit about our team:ADA is the world's leading distributor of independent music. From AJ Tracey to Defected, Nafe Smallz to 88 Rising and BMG to Macklemore, we work with labels and artists of all genres, spanning the hottest breaking talent to deeply established leaders in their field. Our award winning UK team are close knit and driven, actively collaborating with ADA's wider global network from our London HQ.Why this could be your next big break:As Dance Marketing Manager at ADA you'll own the Dance lane, collaborating closely with our evolving roster of directly signed Dance artists and label partners such as Defected, Good Company, Perfect Havoc, Dimension, Shy FX and more to deliver cutting edge globally minded campaigns. With the position's high focus on Dance and Electronic music, we're seeking an individual with a great passion and knowledge of the space and its many genres, with strong community connections. You'll be an active player in our dance A&R conversations and represent ADA at the key events on the Dance community's calendar, whilst continually developing ADA's Dance offering and strategy. You'll be dynamic, creative, organized and accountable, with a deep understanding of marketing and consumer behaviour and an excellent grasp of streaming and the DSP landscape.Here you'll get to:Own the Dance lane as the primary relationship holder in the UK for ADA's evolving roster of Dance label partners and artist signings.You'll collaborate closely with internal and external teams including artists, management, labels, digital accounts, promotions, social, sync and brands to craft groundbreaking campaigns.You'll represent ADA at key events on the Dance community's calendar and play an active role in our Dance A&R.You'll continue to develop ADA's Dance offering to ensure we're providing partners with best in class services across every element of their campaigns.You'll champion your releases with the commercial teams, sharing campaign objectives, delivering powerful updates and participating in the formation of partner marketing strategy.Provide leadership, advice and creative planning support to partners in respect of their releases, catalogue and revenue.Managing all facets of the release process with a high focus on campaign presentation, commercial objectives and the smooth release of all formats, taking in digital, physical and D2C; collaborating closely with Sales & Digital, Supply Chain, Ops and Finance to achieve this.Actively contribute to ADA's label strategy, bringing your experience and perspective on modern music marketing to both your campaigns and our regular label meetings as a key voice in the Marketing & Label Management team.About you:Proven track record delivering successful artist marketing campaigns, with a particular emphasis on Dance genres.Passionate and knowledgeable about Dance and Electronic music and culture.Dynamic and outcome oriented, with a keen eye for detail and ability to deliver on ideas and objectives.Experience working with commercial teams with a particular focus on digital retail and the intersection between audience, marketing and streaming.Strong interest in social media and its power to influence commercial behaviour.A team player with a desire to make a difference and contribute to successful outcomes.Excellent communication skills, being the primary advocate for your partners and projects.Accountable and organized with strong sense of time management.Demonstrable experience managing multiple priority projects simultaneously.It would be music to our ears if you also had:3 years marketing experience in a label or management company environment.A strong network of connections in the industry and wider music community.Lateral experience in music, marketing or promotional roles that sets you apart from the competition.Why join us?Here at Warner Music Group we're all about our people. We are one global company made up exclusively of knowledgeable, passionate, and creative people.It is the mission of every member of the WMG team around the world to create a nurturing environment for artists, songwriters, and the people behind the music – at every stage of their career. We strive to set WMG apart by embracing a philosophy of innovation 3 it's part of our company’s DNA.Consider a career at WMG and be a part of one of the most influential forces in culture today.Love this job and want to apply?Click the CApplyD link at the top of the page, or apply directly with your LinkedIn. Applying with LinkedIn will import all of the information you put in your profile, but will still allow you to upload a resume and cover letter.Don't be discouraged if you don't hear from us right away. We're taking our time to review all resumes, and to find the best people for WMG.Thanks for your interest in working for WMG. We love it here, and think you will, too.We are an equal opportunity employer that is committed to inclusion and diversity and will not discriminate against any applicant or employee on the basis of age, sex, sexual orientation, race, color, creed, religion, ethnicity, national origin, alienage or citizenship, disability, marital status, familial status, military or veteran status, or any other legal recognized protected basis under federal, state or local laws, regulations or ordinances.We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation because of a disability for any part of the application or employment process, please send an e-mail to Recruitment@wmg.com and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

Description: Please visit https://senmusi.cc/2wVmxIL for complete detailsContinue to article...Company website: https://sentricmusicgroup.com/Job SummarySentric Music Group is looking for a driven and personable Senior Client Manager with solid music industry knowledge to deliver a first class relationship and reporting service across clients of Sentric Music Group, coordinating all operational stakeholders involved in the delivery of service objectives.Based in the heart of Soho, London with regular travelling to our Liverpool HQ – or vice versa – the Senior Client Manager Manager will play an integral part in the recently formed Client Management division function of Sentric Music Group. International travel may be required.Company & Recruitment BackgroundSentric Music Group is a rapidly expanding music publishing company with 70 employees based across our offices in Liverpool, London, Hamburg, Palma, New York and LA. The company handles copyright and royalty administration for a large client base consisting of songwriters, record labels and music catalogue owners.Sentric Music is in an exciting phase of significant growth into new markets; our industry-challenging platform is transforming the traditional models for royalty collection and our client base is consistently growing. The Senior Client Manager is instrumental in achieving our goal of offering fair and transparent publishing solutions to the music industry, utilising our solid in-house publishing expertise with cutting edge technology.Job DescriptionClient Management – Strategy ImplementationResponsible for the operational delivery of Client Management service strategy across the Sentric Music Group, as set out in liaison with CEO and Business Development/A&R team.Ensure a seamless, first-class experience for all Sentric Music's clients – from initial dialogue through to signed and ongoing partnerships – using agile work practices to deliver service timely and to set standards with consistent communication.Contribute to the development of processes to ensure that all company stakeholders and operational teams have an ongoing awareness of Sentric Music Group's client management strategy, as well as their own part in delivering the service.Client Relationship ManagementContribute to the development of Sentric Music Group's processes surrounding client interaction, financial reporting and relationship-building. Drive a high level of operational focus, co-operation and inter-communication between the various departments in order to service clients through involving – but not limited to – Business Development, Business Affairs, Client Management, Rights and Royalty teams.Act as Client Manager for a select group of clients and oversee client management activities to ensure delivery of service standards:ensuring that all clients receive proactive communication through regular calls and meetings.delivering comprehensive reporting and maintaining an ongoing dialogue on how to maximise clients' publishing earnings.Provide day-to-day management, coaching and support for other members of the Client Management team. Aligning individual and team objectives to those of the wider business and using own experience to develop interaction with clients.Monitor Client Management team performance on an ongoing basis. Evaluate and adapt practices to achieve continuous improvement.Contribute actively to Client Management team meetings, help formulate clear actions, goals and targets for the team with a focus on delivery for managed clients.Ensure regular meetings are held with other operational stakeholders and teams, setting out clear actions and addressing any barriers for delivering client services to the high standards set.Work with HR & Operations Manager to identify training and development needs, provide in-house training or support to relevant teams to ensure a consistent pro-active Client Management function.Reporting / FinancialWork with wider teams to compile, record and analyse statistical information as required to report Client Management team performance and service delivery to each individual client’s internal stakeholders.Work closely with Royalties & Finance team to ensure that clients receive comprehensive financial reporting in line with set timeframes.DevelopmentSeek ways in which to improve personal, team and business performance surrounding client management activities across the whole business.Qualifications & ExperiencePreferable BA degree or equivalent qualification. Music Industry or Business studies are meriting.Strong working knowledge of music publishing, neighbouring rights, copyright and licensing is essential.Minimum 3 years experience of client relationship management/account management.An advanced understanding of technology/software is meriting. An adept user of Microsoft Office.Personal QualitiesHighly motivated and effective self-starter.Sociable and relationship-building – with an ability to liaise with clients and stakeholders at all levels in an organisation.Ability to handle multiple high-priority tasks in a fast-paced and diverse commercial environment.Excellent attention to detail, highly organised and methodical approach to work at all times.Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, with a clear and effective communication style.Excellent organisational skills and demonstrated business judgment.Comfortable working within a team and independently, although being a team player is of utmost importance.Enjoys frequent travelling as part of work dutiesThrives in a creative company with a music industry profile. Appreciates an ambitious and target-oriented, yet social and friendly work environment.Employment packagePerkbox employee benefits membership, offering discounted rates at gyms, restaurants, high-street shops and online retailers.Christmas holiday shut-down period added on top of holiday entitlement.Ability to work from home on selected weekdays, subject to prior agreement with management.

Title: Senior Live Producer, Special Events (US)
Description: Imagine what you could do here. At Apple, new ideas have a way of becoming extraordinary products, services, and customer experiences very quickly. You bring passion and dedication to your job and there’s no telling what you could accomplish.Continue to article...You will form part of a team that identifies, builds and produces a wide spectrum of events from live performance broadcasts, festivals, marketing activations and hospitality events across international markets. We are looking for a Senior Producer to join the Event Production Team based in Los Angeles, producing world class events and experiences for Apple Music. The ability to be flexible and adapt to changing situations is critical.Key QualificationsMinimum 15 years of experience in producing large scale live broadcasts, festivals and special eventsUnparalleled track record leading world class consumer experiencesCandidate has a wealth of production experience having lead teams in production companies, creative agencies or in-house at entertainment brandExtraordinary attention to detail, while still working to tight deadlinesCollaborative approach, experience in managing and leading teams.Strong experience with Live Music Production technology and Multi-Camera Production and Live-Streaming experienceExcellent budget management experience leading all aspects of multi million pound projectsConsistent track record of leading creatives, venue and production vendorsDemonstrated experience of working with Artist Management and LabelsAudience Management, Hospitality, and Ticketing experienceStrong written and verbal communication skills to external and internal audiences, and customersWide range of experience producing varying types of outstanding events in international markets.High level of dedication, collaboration and passion towards your areas of expertiseDescriptionLead teams who produce events end-to-end; coordinate all details, including but not limited to: Physical production of creative execution Financial budgeting and oversight Operational, logistical and technical event management Talent and guest hospitality and experience Project scheduling and timelines Vendor management Communications to internal and external partners Ensure delivery of the highest quality and act on client feedback. Work with Business Affairs department to complete all contracts, agreements and confirm all clearances are met. Collaborate closely with event designer(s) on ground plans, floor plans and renderings. Provide efficient communication between all parties, ensuring information regarding all projects are accurate. Engage, partner and collaborate with internal teams to achieve the best results for the business. This role reports into the Global Head of Production based in Los Angeles.

Title: Head of Pop (US)
Description: The people here at Apple don't just create products 4 they create the kind of wonder that's revolutionized entire industries. It's the diversity of those people and their ideas that inspires the innovation that runs through everything we do, from amazing technology to industry-leading environmental efforts. Join Apple, and help us leave the world better than we found it. We’re growing and urge you to apply.Continue to article...We believe the role would place you in a highly-energized environment with some of the most talented creative individuals in the industry where you would be inspired to share your years of experience along with providing extraordinary support for the team. Apple revolutionized personal technology with the introduction of the Macintosh in 1984.Today, Apple leads the world in innovation with iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch and Apple TV. Apple's software platforms provide seamless experiences across all Apple devices and empower people with breakthrough services including the App Store, Apple Music, iTunes, iBooks, Apple Pay and iCloud.Apple's employees are dedicated to making the best products on earth, and to leaving the world better than we found it. Apple's fast-growing global Apple Music business seeks a creatively-minded programmer to join its global Editorial team.The successful applicant will work with company leadership to ensure that overall editorial objectives and initiatives are fulfilled.The candidate must have a deep passion for music, is driven and ambitious, highly organized and detail-oriented. This person helps provide the editorial and curatorial voice and vision of iTunes and Apple Music to ensure that our content meets the needs of our audience and our business.Key QualificationsYou have a thorough understanding of the digital content spaceYou have the ability to help build and execute editorial plans, while also tracking projects across multiple divisions with great attention to detail, commitment to delivery and also deadlinesYou have a proven track record of managing diverse, creative teamsYou have exceptional interpersonal skills with the ability to work cross-functionally to bring ideas and plans togetherYou have experience in implementing overall an editorial mission to support business objectives across content initiativesYou have a deep passion and proven understanding of mainstream music. The right candidate eats, breathes and sleeps music and is passionate about creating memorable music experiences for a targeted audienceYou have professional experience in working with artists, managers and label professionals.The position requires a self-motivated individual; extraordinarily driven, highly adaptable with outstanding communication and organizational skills, who is also able to work under pressureYou have the ability to make qualitative and data-driven assessments of all inputs, assumptions and results of personalization assignmentsYou have a proven track record in working on complex, large-scale, and time-critical projectsYou have a strong understanding of, and empathy with, the Apple brand and productsYou have a proven ability to manage workflow around multiple projects and tight deadlines.You are passionate about digital technology and possess a Cget it doneD attitudeYou have an appreciation and knowledge of the history and evolution of Pop, and are on the forefront of new and developing artists and sounds.DescriptionYou will work to develop and implement the overall editorial mission and implement new editorial products to engage listeners, expand audience and support business objectivesYou will provide knowledge and expertise as necessary for the programming of Apple Music You will help the implementation of new editorial products to engage listeners, expand audience and support business objectivesYou will establish a positive presence in the music community and represent the organization in a knowledgeable, professional manner

You have the ability to help build and execute editorial plans, while also tracking projects across multiple divisions with great attention to detail, commitment to delivery and also deadlines
You have a proven track record of managing diverse, creative teams
You have exceptional interpersonal skills with the ability to work cross-functionally to bring ideas and plans together

Title: Product Marketing Manager, Creators (US)
Description: What’s next in music is first on SoundCloud. As the world’s largest open audio platform, SoundCloud is powered by a connected community of creators, listeners and curators who share, discover and influence what’s new, now and next in music and audio.Continue to article...We are always working to build better experiences for the diverse and global audio creators that engage with our platform. This includes artists, musicians, DJs and podcasters.In this role, you will work in tight partnership with our Product team and Marketing  Operations teams to ensure that our Creators understand the why, what, and when as we roll out new products and features. You will be responsible for developing go-to-market plans, and building out strategies around the best way to educate our creators about the best way to get value out of our products depending on their customer segment and needs.Responsibilities include:Defining product value propositions and positioning in the context of product strategy and market landscape – developing the understanding of ‘why’ we are building products and featuresCompetitor analysis, market research, and other research initiatives to inform market positioningOwning the go-to-market responsibilities for all initiatives in your verticalBuilding strong and sustainable cross functional relationships across the Business, Product, Marketing, Content and Finance teams as well as potentially managing external partnersInspiring and developing our overall customer strategy, compiling regular reports and presentations, driving go to market alignment, and knowing our customer segments inside and outBeing able to independently run, ad-hoc projects to understand business implications of proposed product and marketing decisionsWho you arePassionate about the future of musicDegree in Business, Marketing, or related field5 years in Marketing, Business Strategy, or Consulting, ideally with Music experienceStrong communication/presentation skills with a track record of presenting to executives/senior management and peersExcellent business judgment; ability to handle complex situations with clarity and confidence and thorough analysisExcellent ability to build relationships and networks with multiple functions, regions, and at all levels of the organization (stakeholder management)Adaptability and ability to get things done despite an often-changing market and competitive situationStrong sense of urgency and attention to detailDiversity at SoundCloudSoundCloud is for everyone. Diversity and open expression are fundamental to our organization; they help us build a social platform and global community where anyone can create, discover, and share sounds. We acknowledge the challenges in our industry, and strive to develop an inclusive culture where everyone can contribute.

Responsibilities include:: Defining product value propositions and positioning in the context of product strategy and market landscape , developing the understanding of ‘why’ we are building products and features
Competitor analysis, market research, and other research initiatives to inform market positioning
Owning the go-to-market responsibilities for all initiatives in your vertical
Building strong and sustainable cross functional relationships across the Business, Product, Marketing, Content and Finance teams as well as potentially managing external partners
Inspiring and developing our overall customer strategy, compiling regular reports and presentations, driving go to market alignment, and knowing our customer segments inside and out
Being able to independently run, ad-hoc projects to understand business implications of proposed product and marketing decisions
Who you are: Passionate about the future of music
Degree in Business, Marketing, or related field
5 years in Marketing, Business Strategy, or Consulting, ideally with Music experience
Strong communication/presentation skills with a track record of presenting to executives/senior management and peers
Excellent business judgment; ability to handle complex situations with clarity and confidence and thorough analysis
Excellent ability to build relationships and networks with multiple functions, regions, and at all levels of the organization (stakeholder management)
Adaptability and ability to get things done despite an often-changing market and competitive situation
Strong sense of urgency and attention to detail

Sony Music Entertainment is a wholly owned subsidiary of Sony Corporation of America.OverviewThe Vice President, Partner Development manages Sony Music's macro business relationship with a selection of SME's largest digital video partners globally. The successful candidate will be the global internal expert on the video accounts they manage and will be responsible for developing and practically applying a cohesive digital strategy, with a primary focus on short-form (e.g., music) video and user-generated-content. Additionally, this role is responsible for all aspects related to the strategic global management of the allocated video accounts, as well as for developing projects and initiatives that optimize revenue / opportunities for Sony Music at those accounts.Responsibilities will include, but are not limited to, optimizing Sony Music's revenue at each account, initiation and management of projects to generate account level opportunities, educating all levels of the business about key aspects of the partner businesses, and being a key global contact for both the partner and internal stakeholders with respect to SME's evolving business with these accounts.This role is based at Sony Music's offices in New York and reports to the SVP, Partner Development.ResponsibilitiesQuickly develop extensive expertise and assume global strategic account-management responsibility for all aspects related to the video/UGC partners allocatedRecommend and deploy a long term video strategy in order to optimize revenue on both ad-supported and paid subscription offerings around the myriad of asset types on video platforms, taking into account content commitments and policies across the global digital market placeBuild strong relationships and lines of communication with key contacts at allocated partners and with internal stakeholders at all levels: including repertoire owners (e.g. label marketing, label sales), local and regional offices, analytics, operations and finance teams, and senior managementProject manage various activities related to the partner. Strong technical knowledge in all development stages.

Title: Global Partnerships Business & Strategy Manager (US)
Description: Launched in 2011, Twitch is a global community that comes together each day to create multiplayer entertainment: unique, live, unpredictable experiences created by the interactions of millions.Continue to article...We bring the joy of co-op to everything, from casual gaming to world-class esports to anime marathons, music, and art streams. Twitch also hosts TwitchCon, where we bring everyone together to celebrate, learn, and grow their personal interests and passions. We're always live at Twitch. Description: We are Warner Music Group, home to a broad roster of new stars and legendary artists through a collection of the best-known record labels in the music industry including Asylum, Atlantic, Big Beat, Canvasback, East West, Elektra, Erato, FFRR, Fueled by Ramen, Nonesuch, Parlophone, Reprise, Rhino, Roadrunner, Sire, Spinnin', Warner Records, Warner Classics, and Warner Music Nashville, as well as Warner Chappell Music, one of the world’s leading music publishers, with a catalog of more than one million copyrights worldwide.Continue to article...For more than four decades, WMG has been an industry-leading force in providing a world-class array of services designed to help artists and labels grow their careers and their businesses. Artist & Label Services is the umbrella for WEA (Warner-Elektra-Atlantic) 3 the pioneering WEA distribution and marketing network 3 and Alternative Distribution Alliance (ADA) 3 the groundbreaking global distribution company for independent artists and labels.We currently have a fantastic opportunity to join our innovative and highly sought-after Research  Analysis team at Warner Music Group.  A little bit about our team:The Research  Analysis department works to influence WMG's strategy and optimize day-to-day operations through data and insights. This position sits within RA's NYC-based team and will work closely with fellow analysts and account management to understand the trends and dynamics driving major streaming services. Our goal for this role is to serve as the conduit to our colleagues in Business Development, helping to produce findings and insights that inform strategy around WMG's key partners,Why this could be your next big break:Data is the lifeblood of today's record business, and in this role you'll get a chance to apply your creativity and problem-solving skills to help our deal-leads create and implement new deals and strategy by transforming massive amounts of data into tangible insights and recommendations.  Because the RA team works closely with Business Development, you will have the opportunity to see the inner workings and decision-making of an international record label as it engages global digital partners – and the chance to drive the analysis that influences partner-related decisions. We're a collaborative team that loves learning new things, sharing ideas, thinking about the future of the music industry 3 and helping our company get there.Here you'll get to:Build partner-by-partner and industry deep dive and trends analysis that informs key deal / business decisionsAssess conditions that impact existing business models to provide insight and context on market outlook for innovation partnershipsBuild relationships with colleagues & partners to gain strong understanding of business operations and conduct appropriate analysis to drive growthHelp to create market outlook for existing digital partner base and create longer term view that takes into account projections for new and existing lines of digital businessAssist in identifying growth opportunities by applying rigorous analytics to the business performance and market/partner outlook Collaborate with cross functional colleagues across WEA and R&A to exchange insights.Rockstar candidates will be able to:Transition between group projects and independent researchProduce and communicate succinct perspectives around market trends and category growthPrepare recommendations as they relate to deal initiatives and strategic acquisitionsDemonstrate basic proficiency in data mining programs including SQL, Tableau and QuboleIt would be music to our ears if you also had:A bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance or Accounting preferred4-6 years' experience working at a record label or content firmExperience transforming large data sets into actionable insights and strategiesStrong attention to detailConfidence in communicating findings both on paper and in public settings (including in PowerPoint and Excel)A self-start with the ability to multi-task and set priorities & milestonesPassion for music and the music business

Come innovate with the Amazon Music team: Amazon Music is seeking an exceptional Global Social Marketing Manager to manage innovative global social media programs for new products and features within Amazon Music.

This team member will drive best-in-class customer experience and engagement. We are looking for an experienced and creative social marketer with a genuine passion for storytelling, news, music and culture.

The successful candidate will have a proven track record delivering results for social programs, driving audience growth, and publishing a high-volume of content, plus a drive for testing, learning, and making insight- and data-driven decisions.
The Global Social Marketing Manager will manage the content planning, curation, story ideation, and direct fan communication for new products, features and content on Amazon Music social handles.
The team member will work across internal teams including Marketing, Creative, Programming, Editorial, PR, Label/Artist Relations, Paid Social, Product and international teams.
You will work with key social media and digital platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, Twitter and YouTube, as well as seek out emerging platforms that can help us grow our customer base and brand.

Success in this role requires a passion for social media, love for music culture, audio content, digital and app experiences, excellent customer instincts, strong brand understanding, extreme attention to detail, excellent project management skills, an aptitude for measurement and performance monitoring, a keen eye for compelling and innovative content, and a passion for continuous improvement.
You will need to possess an entrepreneurial ability to try new and different things with minimal direction.
Overall, you will be challenged to push boundaries, propose and execute new ideas, and establish best practices for new product, feature and content posts across Amazon Music channels.
Core Responsibilities: Manage new product and content campaigns on Amazon Music social media channels
Build out GTM calendars for assigned handles
Partner with other Social Media Managers to integrate campaign-specific messaging across all channels
Develop programs to grow audiences and foster global social conversation
Guide the creation of micro-content such as social graphics, visual development, animated GIFs, and short-form video related to campaigns
Execute paid promotion in partnership with Paid Social team
Track performance of social media campaigns, content, and overall health of the assigned social handles with regular reporting to leadership and stakeholders

Work closely and collaboratively across Marketing, Creative, Programming, Editorial, PR, Label/Artist Relations, Product, and international teams to champion social-first ideas and execute global integrated activations, events, and strategies
Aid in the development of the social strategy as it relates to messaging, engagement, and content performance
Surface insights for business and marketing strategies
Partner with social platforms to identify new opportunities and develop innovative activations
Present to senior leadership regularly on social plans and results
Continuously ideate and innovate within the social space

Experience using data and metrics to measure impact and determine improvements
Experience using Microsoft Excel to manipulate and analyze data
BA or BS degree, preferably in Communications, Business, Marketing, and/or related field
3 years of experience working in social media marketing, community management, digital/brand marketing
Proven competency and superior knowledge of social platforms, tools, and performance tracking
Passion for storytelling, listening to what the community has to say, and providing engaging experiences in the social space
Excellent writing and copy-editing skills with the ability to translate traditional marketing content into brand-relevant conversations

Passionate and knowledgeable about social media channels such as Facebook, Twitter, YouTube, Instagram with a strong desire to continue learning in order to stay on top of trends and emerging platforms
Strong work ethic and high level of confidentiality
Ability to manage multiple projects and creative ideas on tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment

Job Title: Marketing Manager, ADA Music: A little bit about our team:
A little bit about our team:: ADA is the world's leading distributor of independent music. From AJ Tracey to Defected, Nafe Smallz to 88 Rising and BMG to Macklemore, we work with labels and artists of all genres, spanning the hottest breaking talent to deeply established leaders in their field. Our award winning UK team are close knit and driven, actively collaborating with ADA's wider global network from our London HQ.
Why this could be your next big break:: As a Marketing Manager at ADA you'll strategise and deliver campaigns for a range of directly signed artists and our label partners. This position has a high focus on urban music, and we're seeking an individual with strong community connections and a deep knowledge of the space.
Here you'll get to:: You'll be the primary relationship holder in the UK for a roster of artists and label partners, creating and delivering campaigns for their releases.
You'll collaborate closely with internal and external teams including digital accounts, promotions, social, sync and brands to craft groundbreaking campaigns for your artists.
You'll champion your releases with the commercial teams, sharing campaign objectives, delivering powerful updates and participating in the formation of partner marketing strategy.
Provide leadership, advice and creative planning support to partners in respect of their releases, catalogue and revenue.

Managing all facets of the release process with a high focus on campaign presentation, commercial objectives and the smooth release of all formats, taking in digital, physical and D2C, collaborating closely with Sales & Digital, Supply Chain, Ops and Finance to achieve this.

Actively contribute to ADA's label strategy, bringing your experience and perspective on modern music marketing to both your campaigns and our regular label meetings as a key voice in the Marketing & Label Management team.
About you:: Proven track record delivering successful artist marketing campaigns, with a particular emphasis on urban genres.
Dynamic and outcome oriented, with a keen eye for detail and ability to deliver on ideas and objectives.
Experience working with commercial teams with a particular focus on digital retail and the intersection between marketing and streaming audience building.
Strong interest in social media and its power to influence commercial behaviour.
A team player with a desire to make a difference and contribute to successful outcomes.
Excellent communication skills, being the primary advocate for your partners and projects.
Accountable and organized with strong sense of time management.
Demonstrable experience managing multiple priority projects simultaneously.
It would be music to our ears if you als: 3 years artist marketing experience in a label environment.
A strong network of connections in the industry and wider music community.
Lateral experience in music, marketing or promotional roles that sets you apart from the competition.
Why join us?: Here at Warner Music Group we're all about our people. We are one global company made up exclusively of knowledgeable, passionate, and creative people.

headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It's in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. We’re looking for people with proven track records of shipping complex, customer-facing products under demanding timelines, with bonus points for new product introductions.This is a hands-on position where the EPM will be involved in requirements analysis, functional and technical design discussions, engineering implementation, QA testing, proof-of-concept, working with partners, and more. They must communicate responsibilities and scope clearly, manage conflict, and thrive in uncertainty while striving to find solutions. They must build strong relationships across the entire organization, especially with Senior Directors & VP's, as they're frequently responsible for executive presentations. Autonomy is critical; EPM's only receive consultative direction from leadership and must Cfigure it outD themselves. How we LEAD:: Universal Music Group Nashville (UMGN) is currently seeking a Director, Data Analytics and Research reporting directly to their EVP and COO.
This position will be the point person for all data analysis as well as owning all levels of marketing and sales reporting produced within the Universal Music Group Nashville.
The position requires both a wide-ranging skill set for mining and analyzing data along with the ability to present meaningful findings to all departments across UMGN
How you'll CREATE:: Own the production of all existing reports on a daily, weekly and monthly basis within Universal Music Group Nashville (UMGN).
Communicate data-driven insights and recommendations to label and management, through clear visualizations and presentations.
Perform analyses on large sets of data to extract actionable insights that will drive decisions, with a focus on the label's sales, streaming and marketing efforts.
Participate as a key stakeholder in all department discussions around research findings and forward-thinking analysis within our industry.
Liaison directly with UMG Corporate Data Analytics team to ensure that UMGN is properly represented in all data research discussions being done on a corporate level.
Develop and produce ad-hoc reports for a wide range of needs across UMGN on a timely, as-needed basis.
Bring your VIBE:: A minimum of 7 years of experience using databases and analytic tools to solve business problems.
Music industry experience is a preferred.
Working knowledge of data analyzation tools such as DOMO, SQL, Google Data Studio and Big Query.
Ability to use HTML for data visualization.
Advanced user of Microsoft Office Suite with heavy Excel work required.
Familiarity with Nielsen Soundscan/Music Connect and/or BuzzAngle is a plus.
An ability to extract the essence of a business request across various departments and translate those needs into a data solution.
A direct background and current position with a heavy use of data analytics.
Extensive knowledge of the current music industry including market trends, major accounts, artists, etc. (a healthy emphasis in the
streaming world is preferred).
A passion for data, especially for creating efficient and scalable models
A keen interest in data visualization, high-level presentations and long-range planning/thinking.
A rabid desire to acquire knowledge from anywhere, whether related parallel industries to highly unlikely sources, and apply it to our
situation with constant trial and error.
A relaxed and confident approach to all business relationships internally and externally.
Undergraduate degree with highly relevant background required.
Music Industry or Music Business majors preferred.
Certification in YouTube and Google Analytics are a plus.
Graduate degree or currently working towards an MBA is a plus.

Paid Time Off 3 Paid Holidays, CGift WeekD, Summer Fridays
Universal Music Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer

Title: Label Manager (UK)
Description: Label Manager OverviewContinue to article...The Orchard has an immediate opening for an experienced Label Manager in our London office. Managing key relationships you will be the first point of contact for a bespoke roster of distributed labels and artists across a variety of genres. You will act as a constant and reliable source of advice and expertise for your clients, always striving to add maximum value to their numerous releases and campaigns. You must have a minimum of two years relevant experience at a distributor, label or management company.A passion for music and the industry are a must. Your professional and confident approach will enable you to build and maintain incredibly strong relationships with labels and artist management, as well as key external partners, agencies and suppliers. The Orchard is a fast-paced environment, you'll have plenty of support but we'll also need you to stand on your own two feet, to work independently, conscientiously and with an entrepreneurial spirit.The Orchard is a leading music distribution company operating in over 40 markets worldwide 3 distributing music from independent artists including Skepta, Jorja Smith, Albert Hammond Jr. and Ezra Collective. With cutting edge operations and an unparalleled global team, The Orchard partners with labels of all sizes to make their music and video available across hundreds of digital and physical retailers around the world. At The Orchard, our focus is to provide a comfortable, social and engaging environment to encourage productivity and creativity.What you will do:Be the first point of contact and maintain day-to-day relationships for a varied portfolio of clientsLiaise with internal departments, from operations, product, sales and marketing to finance, legal and management to ensure all clients' business needs are satisfied.Oversee scheduling of releases, ensuring deadlines are met and all represented sales and marketing needs are managed across digital and physical in the UK and abroad.Manage physical product logistics, including co-ordination of manufacturing when required and ensuring all stock requirements and deadlines are met.Proof digital metadata using Orchard technology systems to ensure all releases are delivered to required specifications and in a timely manner.Strategise and manage priority release campaign logistics from inception through to analysis, working with other internal departments, including retail marketing, digital marketing and advertising and any external partners including press, radio and marketing agencies.Supply Sales departments with all materials necessary for selling releases into retail with maximum impact.Consistently inform and educate labels with current and developing best practice across all business areas. Develop knowledge and relationships across the industry.Drive product adoption of The Orchard's tools and services across client base.Represent The Orchard at events and conferences as required. Your Skills and Experience:Minimum 2 years relevant experience in a music distributor, label or management environmentA strong understanding of digital distribution, metadata requirements and digital marketing.Highly experienced in the creation and distribution life cycle of physical releases and associated deadlines from production through release date.Established relationships with a variety of publicists, press and radio companies.Highly advanced organisational skills with the ability to multitask and prioritise to tight deadlines.Excellent communication skills, with the ability to speak effectively across all levels of business and the capacity to identify key relationships and develop them accordingly.A willingness to approach new areas of business, with a positive and considered attitude.Awareness of changes and developments in the industry, with particular emphasis on competitor activity.Strong industry network and relationships within the independent label space.Fluent in English, additional languages desirable but not essential.Able to travel as required.The ideal candidate is:Patient and empathetic with all clients from label owners and artist managers to interns.Friendly, approachable and enthusiastic with an innate desire to understand the needs of clients.A brilliant all round communicator – on phone, email and in person.Someone with supreme attention to detail and follow-through skills.Resourceful and takes the initiative – an expert troubleshooter.Passionate about music and the music industry with drive and enthusiasm to excel.

Title: Global Artist Booking Specialist (AT)
Description: Red Bull's Culture Marketing program is a successful pillar of our overall marketing mix, with music being a global priority and key focus when it comes to further establishing and cementing our brand's relevance and innovative, cutting-edge image via impactful initiatives, events, campaigns and content.Continue to article...To strengthen our Global Music Marketing Team, we're looking for a Global Artist Booking Specialist (m/f/d). In addition, you'll send offers, negotiate with agents and review artist booking contracts. In order to address our events' talent programming requirements, you'll grow Red Bull's network with talent agencies and leverage these relationships.REPORTING & SUPPORTYou'll support and train the local Red Bull teams on the usage of our internal booking reporting tools. Lastly, you'll create reports and deliver presentations in order to uncover useful programming insights.Your areas of knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role:2 years of experience in music programming and artist booking roles, including dealing with agents, promoters, artists and managersGood understanding of the music industry ecosystem, with special focus on the live/touring businessA big passion for music and a solid knowledge of artists, scenes, events and the latest international trendsKnowledge of general legal and tax aspects connected to artist performances and content creation contractsGreat project management skills, ability to manage multiple priorities and to meet deadlinesExcellent computer knowledge (Word, Excel, Power Point, etc.)A strong ability to write, communicate, present and articulate ideas clearlyUnderstanding of marketing, branding and storytelling notions a plusUniversity degree (marketing, business, law, creative industries, media preferred) or strong working experience in a relevant fieldFluent in written and spoken English, German and/or other languages are a bonusTravel up to: 25%

Title: Global Marketing Manager (US)
Description: We are UMG, the Universal Music Group. We are the world's leading music company. In everything we do, we are committed to artistry, innovation and entrepreneurship. We own and operate a broad array of businesses engaged in recorded music, music publishing, merchandising, and audiovisual content in more than 60 countries. Title: Commercial Partnerships Manager, Global Classics & Jazz (UK)
Description: It's the passionate and dedicated team at Universal Music who help make us Britain's leading music company. From A&R to finance, legal to digital, sales to marketing, Universal Music is the place to grow and develop your career within a truly commercial and innovative business that leads in everything it does.Continue to article...Everyone is welcome to apply for our roles, and we are determined to ensure that no applicant or employee receives less favourable treatment because of gender, race, disability, sexual orientation, religion, belief, age, marital status, background, pregnancy or caring responsibilities. We also recognise the importance of diversity of thought within our teams and are fully committed to embracing the talents of people with autism, dyslexia, ADHD and other forms of neurocognitive variation.We will always seek to make appropriate adjustments to recruitment, workplaces, and work processes to be fully inclusive to people with different needs and working styles. If you need us to make any reasonable adjustments for you from application onwards, including alternatives to the online form or to disclose a neurocognitive condition, please email Helen@umusic.comThe A Side: A Day in The LifeAs Commercial Partnerships Manager, you will be working across a number of our global streaming partners for UMG's market-leading Global Classics & Jazz team which includes the legendary Decca, Deutsche Grammophon, Verve and Blue Note labels. A problem solver, people magnet and communicator you'll understand the importance of commercial partnerships and relationships. As part of our global digital team you'll be able to make a real impact upon our relationships with our existing and new digital partners, and present Universal Music Group Global Classics & Jazz as a partner of choice for labels and artists in the world of music.As a global player and confident navigator, you jump at opportunities to network and getting the best out of people and relationships. There are game changers in the music industry, and you know exactly who they are and how to talk to them. Your understanding of the importance of data in shaping the way people think is part of your leading nature. You harness your influencing skills, and you thrive on getting to grips with what your partners really need.The B Side: Skills & ExperienceBe CollaborativeAdopt a partner-centric approach to identify opportunities with digital partners and liaise with repertoire owners and international marketing teams to maximize theseContinually and proactively manage and assess the partners needs and objectives – finding new and exciting ways to ensure the partnership is as fruitful as it can possibly beBuild and maintain excellent working relationships with key digital contacts within UMG labels and digital & marketing teams at local operating companiesBring back the human-to-human connection to working with digital accounts, involving traveling to relevant territories to foster better, stronger and more productive relationshipsBe Commercial Monitor and co-ordinate account developments and new features to understand the unique features of each account and present trends back to the businessBuild meaningful and lasting relationships with commercial digital partners, identify new product initiatives and business development opportunities, educate and influence key stakeholders within UMG, and be able to take these initial partner conversations and build out into digital campaigns to grow revenueWork closely with business analysts to assess results and forecasts, analyze revenue and consumption data from digital partners to establish trends, and share and communicate your recommendations as an expert to the wider businessPersonal SpecificationYou have a natural curiosity to find new innovative ways to share knowledge and apply skills, including challenging our processes and ways of working. You're comfortable implementing iterations and seeking feedback, in a nimble, collaborative and user-centric wayYou are revenue-focused with strong analytical skills and the ability to present data meaningfullyYou are a confident and assured presenter and communicatorYou have a good working knowledge of collaborating with digital streaming music providers 3 experience of this in previous employment is essentialExpert presentational and data analysis skills essential, including a sound working knowledge of Powerpoint/Keynote and Excel and other proprietary data analysis packagesAn expert juggler, you know how to manage multiple projects with conflicting deadlinesYou believe confidence paired with humility is key to fruitful relationships, and you are seen as a mentor to many people around youYou have a strong work ethic and commitment to excellence; we don't compromise on thisYou drive a culture of positivity whilst having the ability to remain calm in an often-pressured environmentYou love music, and have some knowledge of classical and jazz.You have a proven track record of building, maintaining and optimizing successful business relationships within the music industry, or similarBonus Tracks: Your BenefitsGroup Personal Pension Scheme (between 3% and 9%)Private Medical Insurance25 paid days of annual leaveInterest Free Season Ticket LoanHoliday Purchase schemeDental and Travel Insurance optionsCycle to Work SchemeSalary Sacrifice CarsSubsidised Gym MembershipEmployee Discounts (Reward Gateway)

Title: Royalties Assistant (UK)
Description: Joining the Royalties team in the Publishing department in our London office, the primary role of the Royalties Assistant will be to support the team with the end to end processing of income received for our clients.Continue to article...To be successful in the role, you need to have a detail-driven mind-set and be comfortable with repetitive and data heavy tasks. Cultivate relationships with Ingrooves labels, artists and writers to maintain communication and confidence in our sync department
Plan and execute strategic marketing campaigns to maximize potential of sync deals by way of email blasts, samplers, artist showcases, etc.
Support and contribute to Ingrooves Business Development team with A&R initiatives and feedback on new/potential signings and upcoming releases
Create, develop & maintain new and existing relationships in the licensing community including music supervisors, film and TV networks, advertising agencies, video game companies, etc.

Responsible for Licensing Team and delegating responsibilities to ensure catalog is curated, tagged and assets stored in DISCO, databases (Music Maestro) are up to date with quotes/licenses, reporting is consistently generated and shared ahead of deadlines
Oversee and track the conclusion of license deals with Licensing, Finance and Legal Teams
Communicate with labels and artists to obtain advance music assets, instrumentals and help manage all music by maintaining and updating databases (DISCO).
Work closely with Rights & Royalties Department to maintain streamlined processes across multiple departments
Generate monthly reporting to communicate status of Sync business, including department goals/projections, confirmed uses, outstanding payments and general sync highlights
Attend industry events such as conferences, panels, showcase and mixers to stay current and aware of upcoming sync opportunities
Meet with or connect with new signings to introduce them to synch department/what we do.
Maintain all licensing related spreadsheets and Muma (deals, rights, inventory/finance).
Provide synch history/status lists for labels/artists/writers when needed/follow up on pay-out for labels/artists/writers.
Bring your VIBE:: 5 years of experience working in music licensing within the music label/publisher/television/entertainment or related industry.
Proven relationships with creative, licensing and A&R departments, Business Affairs offices and/or persons throughout the licensing/music industry.
Ability to grow a licensing department and experience managing licensing department staff
Perks Playlist:: Competitive Compensation Package including Salary, Benefits and Generous 401k Savings Plan
Paid Time Off 3 Paid Holidays, CGift WeekD, Summer Fridays

Title: Assistant, Digital Accounts (UK)

We're dedicated to inclusiveness and provide all the resources and benefits you need to grow your career and reach your potential. We offer competitive compensation packages, professional development opportunities, and an entrepreneurial culture that makes working here not just fun, but also rewarding.

Title: Partner Manager, Supply Chain (UK)

Job Title:: Associate Manager, Rights Administration, Business and Legal Affairs
A little bit about our team:: The Catalog Research and Rights Administration Group is a subdivision of Rhino Entertainment Company's Business & Legal Affairs Department and is responsible for researching WMG's rights to exploit its existing catalog recordings in response to queries from Rhino, WMG Global Catalog, and WMG's Synch and Master Use Licensing Groups.

Conduct chain of title review and provide concise analysis;
Prepare contract summaries of the recording agreements that govern WMG's existing catalog recordings;
Prepare product memos outlining royalties information applicable to products being released;
Perform general legal research pertaining to WMG's rights in its existing recording agreements; and
Perform such other duties and tasks related to the Business & Legal Affairs Department's role and responsibilities as reasonably may be required.
Rockstar candidates will be able to:: Be able to work well independently and as part of a team;
Possess excellent verbal and written communication skills;
Possess excellent analytical, mathematical and organizational skills;
Be able to meet deadlines consistently and effectively while working under pressure;
Possess unwavering attention to detail;
Be intellectually curious and have a passion for the music business;
Be able to manage multiple assignments at any given time and prioritize them effectively and appropriately;
Have strong computer skills, including strong data entry skills (with an emphasis on accuracy) and strong proficiency using Microsoft Office's Excel, Word and PowerPoint applications.
Bachelor's degree required
Juris Doctor or relevant legal experience required
It would be music to our ears if you als: Preferably, candidates will possess 1 to 3 years of experience working in a position or positions involving the review, analysis and interpretation of recording agreements and other music industry agreements.
Why join us?: Warner Music Group is all about our people. We are one global company made up of the most knowledgeable, passionate, and creative people in our business.
