Title: Junior Artist Manager (UK)
Description: We are looking for a proactive and passionate music industry professional to become part of a small team that represents both internationally established and rising artists / producers.Continue to article...The position will be split between artist management and heading up our in-house record label, predominantly managing artist campaign strategies and objectives. It will be a flexible role that includes, but is not necessarily limited to, the responsibilities listed below.The ideal candidate will have experience in a similar role and should be willing to travel and work remotely.RESPONSIBILITIES Take a hands-on approach to the planning and execution of artist single & album campaigns, including release schedules, touring, marketing, promo and press. Implement and drive forward a relevant streaming and growth strategy for the label and artist releases. Support the team in creatively product managing all aspects of marketing campaigns. Create content and maintain online presence through artist websites and social media channels. Be aware of trends and actively look for marketing opportunities for artists. Liaise with artists, management, online distributors, record labels, PR, booking agents, radio pluggers, accountants, lawyers, merch companies etc. Build relationships with external promo teams and maximise key opportunities to ensure delivery of marketing and media strategies. Collate and distribute key artist information to internal/external teams and ensure everyone has the necessary tools to maximise impact across media. Issue master recording contracts and licenses to prospective artists and liaise with legal teams to ensure finalised contracts. Keep on top of PRS, PPL and neighbouring rights registrations. Act as the day-to-day manager for artists when appropriate, accompanying them to promo and live engagements. General housekeeping, admin and diary management.Coordinate design, delivery and sale of merchandise.REQUIREMENTS Personal Qualities: Ninja communication skills. The ability to prioritise, multi-task and adapt to shifting schedules. The ability to work independently in a fast-paced environment. Self-driven and highly motivated. Trustworthy and dependable under pressure. Willing to work remotely and travel in the UK and potentially abroad. Previous Experience / Knowledge: 1-2 years management or record label experience. An in-depth knowledge of the music industry and passion for music, specifically indie/alternative. Strong understanding of social media, marketing tools and trends.Please send a CV and cover email to musicpajobapplication@gmail.com Deadline:Monday 9th MarchSalaryNegotiable, dependent on experience.

Title: Artist Marketing Lead, Music Marketing (US)
Description: Spotify has transformed the way people access and enjoy music. Today, millions of people in 79 countries and territories have access to more than 40 million tracks, whenever and wherever they want. At Spotify, we're focused on providing the right music experience for every moment.Continue to article...The Music Marketing team plays a critical role in defining and driving Spotify's promotional strategy around new and existing content, focused on the holistic music experience. As part of the Music Marketing team you will help build and shape the marketing processes, cross functional infrastructure, operations and implementing innovation and best practices across new album releases, individual artist and industry moments and Spotify's original and programmed artist content.This position requires experience and passion for the music and entertainment industry with a firm pulse across a wide range of genres and its cultural impact. This position will lead artist co-promotion marketing campaign orchestration; driving strategy, campaign planning, production, and delivery of integrated and interconnected brand and artist stories.This person is a passionate student of music, artists, entertainment, culture, brands, and storytelling, and has experience leading cross functional teams, shaping global brand stories, managing end-to-end processes, budget, and workflows and execution across various channels such as TV, OOH, Mobile, Social, Display, experiential etc.As a leader on this team, you will help build and shape marketing programs that acquire, engage, and retain priority audiences across new album releases and help to position Spotify as the lead streaming partner.What You'll Do:Shape and oversee the Artist Marketing roadmap, media partnerships and processes in close collaboration with the Artist & Label Marketing team, continually developing new strategies that facilitate meaningful artist to fan interaction through creative marketing solutions.Provide a strategic lens and direction on execution of programs and Spotify franchises.Manage day-to-day workflow and resource bandwidth; drive cross-functional alignment across internal and external teams required to achieve the team's long- and short-term objectives and key results.Guide the development of performance analyses and reporting in conjunction with our Analytics and Insights teamImplement creative partner collective to support driving innovationPartner with internal and external partners on the larger album releases, creative and strategic opportunities to grow the Spotify brand, grounded in artist, music, content and cultureCollaborate with a team of experts across brand, creative, social media, comms, media, analytics and business disciplines responsible for creating and managing the reporting/measuring analytics to optimize content distribution and effectivenessWho You Are:8 years experience as a marketer at a relevant brand, platform, agency, or publisher, ideally in roles that were music- and content-focusedYou have a thorough understanding of the music industry, and its intricaciesYou possess strong communication, writing and leadership skills, and have led complex cross functional teamsYou thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic and high energy environment and are comfortable with occasional ambiguity and a team player willing to pitch in whenever neededYou are highly organized and pay careful attention to detailYou are creative and constantly ideating on how to uniquely bring marketing campaigns to lifeYou possess excellent communication and writing skillsYou are an ambitious and diligent worker, and are able to create success in itYou are proficient at using Google Drive, Excel, and KeynoteAbility to work under pressure with imminent deadlines and prioritize in real-timeStrong knowledge of the music industry and cultural trends in the marketplace.You are highly proactive, and always take initiativeYou are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what's playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It's in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world's most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 258 million users.

What You'll Do:: Shape and oversee the Artist Marketing roadmap, media partnerships and processes in close collaboration with the Artist & Label Marketing team, continually developing new strategies that facilitate meaningful artist to fan interaction through creative marketing solutions.
Provide a strategic lens and direction on execution of programs and Spotify franchises.
Manage day-to-day workflow and resource bandwidth; drive cross-functional alignment across internal and external teams required to achieve the team's long- and short-term objectives and key results.
Guide the development of performance analyses and reporting in conjunction with our Analytics and Insights team
Implement creative partner collective to support driving innovation
Partner with internal and external partners on the larger album releases, creative and strategic opportunities to grow the Spotify brand, grounded in artist, music, content and culture

Collaborate with a team of experts across brand, creative, social media, comms, media, analytics and business disciplines responsible for creating and managing the reporting/measuring analytics to optimize content distribution and effectiveness

Title: UK Sales Manager (UK)
Description: BMAT started in 2005 when a group of engineers with a passion for music decided to spin-off from an audio research lab in Barcelona.Continue to article...The company started with an idea 3 to index all the music in the world 3 and a mission 3 to better the music eco-system 3 and a team of nerds eager to learn how to build a successful business.Today the company has grown to more than a hundred people – spread across Barcelona, Buenos Aires, Lima, SÃ£o Paulo, London, Paris, Oslo, Tel-Aviv, Johannesburg, Singapore, Tokyo and Seoul – that team up to deliver daily 1 million identifications and 3 billion matches to our partners from all over the music industry.Our CultureWe love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady).Make her life easier and her future brighter. We apply our scientific, data-infused brains to tell everyone what music has been played 3 so that creators get the recognition they deserve. We hear everything everywhere and tell everyone who wants to know. 8What plays around will come back around' we say. We believe by helping this happen we can make a positive change in the music ecosystem and enjoy talent for another million years.We are a team of pretty decent people who enjoy working with each other. We're open-minded and well-traveled, coming from all parts of the world. We like coffee, tea, chai, and mate all the same. We've got rappers, producers, cello players, DJs, and songwriters. The passion for music, the excitement for technology and Barcelona as a backdrop has proven to make a good combination. We say there is a BMAT touch. We believe it has to do with being passionate, obsessive, nice, generous, honest, self-motivated, entrepreneurial, intrepid, crazy, extraordinary, creative, agile, human, friendly, smart, witty, talented, naive, optimistic, surprising, sensual, and nerdy, but not very hipster (unfortunately).Sales at BMATSales is a core team at the BMAT compound of regional managers, business vice-presidents, and executive directors. Its dynamic is very open, co-operative and no hierarchical. We understand sales as a discovery process through which, based on our expertise, we ask and listen our customers telling us about their challenges and in return we advise them on how we can help them tackle those through innovation.What You Will Be DoingUnderstand the newest needs/requests and problems faced by the Music and Audiovisual industries to propose relevant services.Provide accurate and regular forecasts on the UK market to the business development team.Define product strategy together with Global Sales management.Develop relationships with the industry to generate new business development opportunities for our five verticals – TVAV, Broadcast, Digital, Venues, Charts.Spot and answer tender calls.Take responsibility for the UK Sales P&L.All About YouMinimum 10 years experience in an international Sales facing role within a music and/ or technology company.The ideal applicant will have a strong network and a good understanding of international copyrights.Experience managing a sales division within Music publishing/ Production music/ Film & TV/Synch is valued.Superior analytical and communication skills.Fluency in English.Bonus TrackAvailability for training in our Barcelona HQ.This role may require travels.You Can EnjoyFull-time job.Flexibility on working hours.Flat hierarchy.Full autonomy of your dutiesBecoming part of a diverse and multicultural team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry.Diverse perspectives drive innovation, and innovation is kind of our thing. Our open-minded and flexible culture organically fosters this diversity. We’ve learnt that we thrive on learning from each other, so we don’t just welcome but actively hire a range of open-minded people from all walks of life. Our team is made up of people with different backgrounds, perspectives, orientations, abilities, musical tastes, dress sense and time-zones – but we all have one common goal: Making the music world a better place. With equal opportunities for everyone, BMAT is a pretty nice place to work.

Our Culture: We love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady).
What You Will Be Doing: Understand the newest needs/requests and problems faced by the Music and Audiovisual industries to propose relevant services.
Provide accurate and regular forecasts on the UK market to the business development team.
Define product strategy together with Global Sales management.
Develop relationships with the industry to generate new business development opportunities for our five verticals , TVAV, Broadcast, Digital, Venues, Charts.
Spot and answer tender calls.
Take responsibility for the UK Sales P&L.
All About You: Minimum 10 years experience in an international Sales facing role within a music and/ or technology company.
The ideal applicant will have a strong network and a good understanding of international copyrights.
Experience managing a sales division within Music publishing/ Production music/ Film & TV/Synch is valued.
Bonus Track: Availability for training in our Barcelona HQ.
You Can Enjoy: Becoming part of a diverse and multicultural team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry.

About Merlin: Merlin, the digital licensing hub for the world's independent music sector, commands over 15% of the global recorded music market. Merlin offers digital music services and emerging technology companies the means to efficiently license the world's most commercially successful independent music. This enables Merlin's members to more effectively capitalize on revenue from these services, while ensuring their repertoire is appropriately valued.
Areas of responsibility include:: Manage Member administrative duties including record keeping and light data entry;
Processing membership applications and communications with applicants through our on-boarding process;
Coordinate and assist in creation and distribution of DSP best practice / summary guides;
Manage records of member participation in Merlin opportunities;
Message and coordinate member participation in Merlin presentations and workshops with partner DSPs;
Assist Department Heads in creation and on-going maintenance of all opportunity distributions;
Assist Merlin staff and members in on-going education and certifications relating to partner DSPs;
Provide general support for members' questions and issues;
Liaise with the project coordinator on IT issues and development.
Specific Duties: Provide operational support materials to new members each month including, but not limited to, operational summaries, best practice guides and marketing opportunities;
Gather and maintain member data relative to maximizing opportunities for members with DSP partners in relation to new releases/catalogues;
Provide support to members implementing operational aspects of Merlin commercial deals;
Work alongside Merlin Member Services & Operations teams to maintain Participating Member lists as they relate to the operational aspects of Merlin deals;
Training and certification on rights management and other DSP tools to assist in practical member support;
Coordinate scheduling and admin of member workshops and presentations;
Manage inbound Merlin Member Services & Operations email accounts;
Assist with set-up and maintenance of Merlin-DSP pitching and participation forms;
Assist Merlin team on admin tasks relating to maximizing participation across the membership;
Manage error tickets and other notices of operational requirements from DSPs;
Coordinate project management for member facing IT issues and tools developments;
Core skills:: 2 or more years of experience in a client facing support role;
Excellent math, verbal and written communication skills (English; multilingual is a plus);
Exceptional attention to detail and well organized;
Skilled listener in order to accurately respond to inquiries and requests from within a global reaching organization and team;

Proficiency with computer applications including Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint), Apple Products (KeyNote), Google Applications Suite (Google sheets, docs etc) and various task management applications;
Comfortable tracking and prioritizing multiple issues across different platforms;
Able to work under pressure in a busy office environment;
Comfortable communicating to staff across all levels via email and, where applicable, via phone and in person;
Comfortable assisting staff, where required, in meetings (both internal and external).
Advanced skills:: Previous experience working with a member-based organization (distributor, collecting society, trade association);
Knowledge of the digital music industry;
Knowledge of/interest in independent music, Hip-Hop and/or Latin music genres;
Experience in digital service provider operations;
Understanding of music rights and their stakeholders;
Proficiency with rights management / content recognition tools;
Understanding of social media marketing methods and tools;

Numerate;

Attention to detail;
CGo 3 with 3 the 3 flowD attitude; i.e. ability to be able to change the plan in order to accommodate urgent matters which may occur during the day;

Ability to work well with others.

Title: Finance Assistant, International Recordings (UK)
Description: Your Mission at BMG.Continue to article...BMG are on the lookout for a part qualified accountant to join us as a Finance Assistant in our Paddington Office. You will be responsible for supporting the International Finance team within its worldwide hub.Our recordings hub manages the financial and management reporting for the global recording business for revenues and costs and provides key business support.Your Responsibilities.SAP support including Journal posting and preparationAssist with preparation of Balance Sheet RecsMonth end file roll forwardSupport mapping table review for new projects and missing codesSupport with IC matrices; ADA revenue & cost analysis, Quarterly Intercompany matrix, Monthly Cost recharges, Quarterly PPI (all to be turned around in tight bottleneck time periods and require manual analysis/processing)Review of G/Ls looking for posting errors (including mid-month reviews)Support with BW report preparation and reviewAd-hoc data queries and wider support to the team as requiredYour Profile.Minimum 2 years experience working in a similar Finance positionPrevious experience in a similar role within the music industry preferred but not essentialPart Qualified AccountantProficient in excel (intermediate level)SAP experience preferableCommitment to continual professional self-developmentTeam playerMethodicalHighly organisedPassion for music, of course!Now, what’s in it for you?25 days annual leave plus 3 days between Christmas & New YearSubsidised gym membershipPrivate Health InsuranceCompetitive pension schemeAnnual gig allowanceArtist showcasesCycle to work schemeSeason Ticket LoanAccess to our Employee Assistance ProgrammeExcellent development opportunitiesA fun and sociable office environmentWhat you should know about us.BMG is a growing international music company, founded in 2008 with a revolutionary vision for the digital age. We offer unparalleled creative support, provide the best access to innovative platforms and work hard to maximise the value of each and every copyright; empowering all of our artists and songwriters to achieve their greatest potential. A little bit more of how it should be (we like to think). BMG is a company with service, transparency and fairness at its heart and a commitment to being the best music company for songwriters, artists and everyone who works with us. BMG's 14 offices across 12 core music markets now represent over 2.5m songs and recordings, including the catalogues of Chrysalis, Bug, Virgin, Mute, Sanctuary, Primary Wave and Talpa Music, as well as literally thousands of artists and songwriters attracted by its fresh approach.BMG is owned by international media company Bertelsmann, which also includes the broadcaster RTL Group, the trade book publisher Penguin Random House, the magazine publisher Gruner  Jahr, the service provider Arvato, the Bertelsmann Printing Group, the Bertelsmann Education Group, and Bertelsmann Investments, an international network of funds.

Title: Global Content Programming Manager, Music (AT)
Description: In this role, you’ll create strategic content programming plans to maximise the growth of and engagement with our key target audience for music.Continue to article...For that you’ll collaborate with countries, align with our internal music audience team, and have an eye on analytics.Areas that play to your strengthsAll the responsibilities we’ll trust you with:SET THE STRATEGYYour responsibilities will include defining a content programming strategy to reach a global music audience and establishing strong alignments and collaborations between channels & content creation teams to ensure release strategy success. Title: Senior Software Engineer, Backend (US)

Title: Licensing Manager (US)
Description: Warp is looking for an experienced licensing person to join our Sync team in Los Angeles to deliver licensing opportunities in North America and creatively pitch our artists across Warp Records, Warp Publishing and other represented catalogues.Continue to article...This is an exciting new position in which you will be joining the Sync team and working alongside the Creative Director, Film & TV, to deliver exceptional licensing opportunities for our artists in Advertising, Trailers, Video Games, as well as contributing to the department's work in Film & TV and Original Score.You will be responsible for music searches and maintaining and developing relationships with music supervisors, editors, directors and producers. You will also be responsible for handling licensing requests, reporting on licensing activity, marketing (e.g. mailouts, website and social networks, playlists, live events) and generally contributing to the day to day running of the Sync department in LA and North America.Desired skills and experience:At least 3 years of professional experience in sync licensing, music supervision or related industries;Detail oriented with strong project management skills;Excellent communication skills;A passion for music to picture;Knowledge of Warp Records and Warp Publishing catalogues;General knowledge and passion for current and upcoming Film & TV projects;Strong relationships within the Sync community in LA and North AmericaSalary: dependent on experience.The full-time position is based in Los Angeles.To apply, send your resume and a covering letter explaining why you think you are suitable to applywarpsync@warprecords.com stating 8Licensing Manager, Warp, Los Angeles' in the subject line.The closing date for applications is Friday 7th February 2020.We look forward to hearing from you.

Desired skills and experience:: At least 3 years of professional experience in sync licensing, music supervision or related industries;
Detail oriented with strong project management skills;
A passion for music to picture;
Knowledge of Warp Records and Warp Publishing catalogues;
General knowledge and passion for current and upcoming Film & TV projects;
Strong relationships within the Sync community in LA and North America

Title: Label Manager (US)
Description: About USContinue to article...Founded in 2018, Beatsource was created through a partnership between DJcity and Beatport. This collaboration between the world's largest record pool and the worldwide home of electronic music for DJs has created a new home for open-format and mobile DJs to purchase and discover new music in an environment specifically designed with the open-format and mobile DJ in mind. The Beatsource store offers six core genres – Hip-Hop, R&B, Latin, Pop, Dance, and Reggae/Dancehall – carrying the biggest hits, all in premium digital formats and with world-class DJs providing the store’s curation.The Label Manager acts as a liaison between Beatsource and our label partners. They maintain ongoing relationships with the major labels, label aggregators, and independent labels. Working closely with our Curation and Content Operations teams, they manage content acquisition (signing new labels and tracking down content that is missing from the catalog) and ensure that the DSP has all relevant new releases on street date.This is a relationship position that requires excellent verbal and written communication and negotiation skills. You need to be able to analyze situations easily to be able to connect the dots in a fast-paced environment.You will:Serve as Label Manager to assigned and newly introduced labels. Connect label partners to appropriate teams within Beatsource to address queries, concerns, and issues.Maximize both Beatsource's and supplier's businesses by securing original and exclusive content for Beatsource's social media and editorial, monitoring activity to ensure all business terms are being followed (i.e. exclusive content is only available through Beatsource); monitoring release frequency and relevance.Put together and run integrated marketing campaigns with partners.Research labels that are relevant to Beatsource's business and pursue to secure new supplier relationships. Negotiate terms of business. Work with Beatsource legal resources to generate and approve contracts. We hire people who love what they do and we make sure they have plenty of room for growth. You will work with a team that values collaboration and mentorship.Equal Employment OpportunityBeatsource strongly supports equal employment opportunity for all applicants regardless of race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, age, marital status, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, sexual orientation, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by state or federal law.Hiring PracticesThe preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this classification. Beatsource may pursue all avenues available, including promotion from within, employee referrals, outside advertising, employment agencies, Internet recruiting, job fairs, college recruiting and search firms.

Title: Music Lead, Spotify Studios (US)
Description: Spotify Studios is looking for a senior development executive to oversee our exclusive content strategy for the Music category.Continue to article...In this role, you'll oversee Spotify's Music & Music Talk content portfolio and a dedicated creative team. You will source, develop and produce premium audio and video series that engage existing and growth audiences, with an eye toward bolstering Spotify's presence and voice in the category.The right candidate has extensive experience across development, production and talent relations, and crucially will bring a clear point of view to translating that experience into impactful audio and video formats. This is a unique opportunity to leverage Spotify's standing in the music industry with our investment in new audio and video platforms.This role is based in Los Angeles and reports to the Head of Creative Development, Spotify Studios.What you'll doOversee Music podcast and video development for Spotify StudiosManage budget toward impact on clear KPIs as the executive ultimately accountable for the success of the categoryCollaborate with genre leads (Reality, Sports, Comedy, etc.) at Spotify to surface IP and develop formatsEvaluate future extensions of the portfolio from film & TV, live and international formatsWork cross-functionally to maintain Spotify's position in the Music space, to include brand, social channels and content offeringSupport Head of Creative Development in managing the development slate; partner with other Creative Leads to keep cross-functional documents updated; provide regular Status Reports for all divisions, including production and LeadsBuild and maintain Spotify's external relationships across all facets of the Music communityLead the team accountable for execution of that strategy, consisting of both direct reports and dedicated resources from stakeholder teamsPartner with our in-house Music editorial team to support programming around specific artistsShop fully realized IP to television networks while leveraging existing and new relationships with talent and premium networksWho you areDeep experience in Music format development working at a studio, network or production company; a passion for storytelling in the audio space; and knowledge of the podcast spaceEstablished relationships with agencies, management companies, writers and music executivesHighly passionate individual, who's resourceful and actively seek out unique IP opportunities, develop and execute material at the highest level within budgetStrong communication skills and ability to analyze problems and identify logical solutionsExperience working with music industry talent, managing budgets and production during developmentYou are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what's playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It's in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by these creators. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world's most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 258 million users.

Title: A&R/Label Manager (UK)
Description: About Us Continue to article...Love & Other is an independent House music label, and part of the Toolroom Records group, based out of Maidstone, Kent.Over the past few years, the label has built a reputation for releasing quality, radio friendly House music with a commercial edge 3 releasing material from the likes of Endor, Kisch, Kokiri, Tazer and many more. Love & Other has achieved countless Radio 1 plays, streaming stats in the tens of millions, and live showcase events at Egg Club London, XOYO and Studio 338.In this position, the successful candidate will take creative and strategic oversight of the label, looking to build on its success in 2020 and beyond.Key ResponsibilitiesManage and oversee a busy and consistent release schedule for the labelRegularly bring in successful new tracks and constantly look for new artists and talent.Build strong relationships with artists, artist managers and partners.Develop existing talent with long-term relationships with the label.Scouting and representing Love & Other at club nights, festivals and conferences.Work alongside artists to optimise each and every release, ensuring Love & Other's quality threshold is met through detailed A&R feedback.Commission and deliver remixes when required.Work closely alongside distribution teams to ensure each track is delivered and released on time, with a compelling promotional strategy.Ensure all administration and contractual details are on-point.Your ProfileAt least 3 years experience in the recorded music industry.A comprehensive knowledge of the current electronic music landscape, particularly within commercial House music.A track record of A&R successA good communicator, team orientated with a positive attitude.Strong attention to detailKnowledgeable about the process of label management and what's involved in releasing music.The Benefits Competitive salaryPension scheme20 days holiday plus Bank HolidaysOffices within walking distance of the train station(s)/free onsite parkingApplicationPlease apply with a CV and covering letter to George Ankrett, A&R Director,George@ToolroomRecords.comDeadline10th February 2020

Key Responsibilities: Manage and oversee a busy and consistent release schedule for the label
Regularly bring in successful new tracks and constantly look for new artists and talent.
Build strong relationships with artists, artist managers and partners.
Develop existing talent with long-term relationships with the label.
Scouting and representing Love & Other at club nights, festivals and conferences.
Work alongside artists to optimise each and every release, ensuring Love & Other's quality threshold is met through detailed A&R feedback.
Commission and deliver remixes when required.
Work closely alongside distribution teams to ensure each track is delivered and released on time, with a compelling promotional strategy.
Ensure all administration and contractual details are on-point.
Your Profile: At least 3 years experience in the recorded music industry.
A comprehensive knowledge of the current electronic music landscape, particularly within commercial House music.
A track record of A&R success
A good communicator, team orientated with a positive attitude.
Strong attention to detail
Knowledgeable about the process of label management and what's involved in releasing music.
The Benefits: Offices within walking distance of the train station(s)/free onsite parking
Application: Please apply with a CV and covering letter to George Ankrett, A&R Director,

Title: Marketing Manager (US)
Description: Marketing Manager, Independent Label Continue to article...Work with Head of Marketing to lead artist releases at an indie record label.Being a small but growing label, the applicant should be well versed in adapting themselves within multiple tasks which require varying skillsets. A good understanding of putting together marketing campaigns, but technical knowledge for digital aspects too.Responsibilities (including but not limited to): The core role will be to deliver thumb-stopping marketing campaigns for developing artists across a wide range of genres.Coordinating daily with external teams, press, live, radio, managementForming original and creative ideas in order to get artist and music noticed and shared in alternative waysManaging social media accounts daily for artists, including Facebook/Instagram/Twitter/YouTube/Spotify for artistsCommission content, artwork, photoshoots and music videosMonitor and analyse streaming/social data and share most impactful insights with wider team/partnersCreate and/or commission content for social media postingCommunicate with artist and management teams effectively for timely approvalManage and cultivate strategic partnerships with key digital platforms and subscription services, with the aim to grow each account’s business, maximise revenue and exposure for all artists and talentMaintain relationships with DSPs and key digital/social partners and work with them on promotional activities, exclusive content and other partnership opportunities.Spot digital trends, understand new technologies and align these with business objectives to create new and innovative marketing opportunities and solutions.Work closely with the UK Marketing & Digital counterpartsProvide regular updates to internal stakeholders on releases and campaignsSkills and Qualifications: Exceptional interpersonal and communication skillsImpactful presentational skills to convey information and results clearlyAbility to work effectively under pressure and balance prioritiesExcellent time-management, organisational skills and strong attention to detailAbility to take broad campaign concepts and translate into partner specific initiatives that result in sales/streams and impressionAt least 2 years prior experience at a major or established independent record labelWorking knowledge of the music industry, and a passionate commitment to music and artists

Responsibilities (including but not limi: The core role will be to deliver thumb-stopping marketing campaigns for developing artists across a wide range of genres.
Coordinating daily with external teams, press, live, radio, management
Forming original and creative ideas in order to get artist and music noticed and shared in alternative ways
Managing social media accounts daily for artists, including Facebook/Instagram/Twitter/YouTube/Spotify for artists
Commission content, artwork, photoshoots and music videos
Monitor and analyse streaming/social data and share most impactful insights with wider team/partners
Create and/or commission content for social media posting
Communicate with artist and management teams effectively for timely approval

Manage and cultivate strategic partnerships with key digital platforms and subscription services, with the aim to grow each account’s business, maximise revenue and exposure for all artists and talent
Maintain relationships with DSPs and key digital/social partners and work with them on promotional activities, exclusive content and other partnership opportunities.
Spot digital trends, understand new technologies and align these with business objectives to create new and innovative marketing opportunities and solutions.
Work closely with the UK Marketing & Digital counterparts
Provide regular updates to internal stakeholders on releases and campaigns
Skills and Qualifications:: Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills
Impactful presentational skills to convey information and results clearly
Ability to work effectively under pressure and balance priorities
Excellent time-management, organisational skills and strong attention to detail
Ability to take broad campaign concepts and translate into partner specific initiatives that result in sales/streams and impression
At least 2 years prior experience at a major or established independent record label
Working knowledge of the music industry, and a passionate commitment to music and artists

Title: Senior Artist Manager (US)
Description: An Artist Management and Independent Record Company is seeking an experienced, driven and well organised day to day Senior Artist Manager to manage a priority LA artist signed to a major record company.Continue to article...This is an incredible opportunity to work in a dynamic, forward-thinking and innovative organisation.DUTIES:Manage all aspects of Artist’s day to day activities including:Working and interacting daily with Artist and third partiesOverseeing artists team (social media team, label team, accountant etc.) and chasing on all tasks and objectivesWorking across global release campaignsAssisting in all areas of creative (from A&R to artwork and videos, content etc)Diary management and itinerary creationBooking travel and meetingsAttending relevant meetings both with the artist and on behalf of the artistOverseeing day to day activities and logisticsCo-ordination of promotion with label teamOverseeing touring plans working with live agent and promoterWorking closely with the artists live team, including tour manager and music director to co- ordinate various performances. Have a strong grasp of live budgets and be able to negotiate rates as and when relevant.Creating and overseeing budgets.Reviewing deal memos and legal contractsLiaising with Artist accountant, including approving invoices and budgets when appropriate and lawyerSourcing new opportunities with industry developments and innovationQUALIFICATIONS, EXPERIENCE AND KNOWLEDGE Sound knowledge of the entertainment industryKnowledge of the creative and cultural community is important, with ability to input ideas on videos, styling, photography etc.Will ideally have understanding of and relevant experience in artist management or working directly with artistsGood understanding of social media and digital marketingREQUIRED SKILLS Excellent IT and digital skillsExcellent time management and ability to manage multiple tasks seamlesslyEnergy, enthusiasm and a ‘can do’ attitudeExcellent interpersonal skills, with proven skills in building effective working relationships internally and externallyAble to apply common sense and pragmatismStrong organization and administration skillsExcellent written and verbal communication skillsDetail orientated with excellent level of accuracyA flexible approach and ability to adapt to changing prioritiesProactive and forward thinking and ability to use own initiativeDiscrete and diplomatic, with ability to deal with highly sensitive and confidential information and issues in a professional mannerROLE REQUIREMENTS Full time. Office hours are 10am – 6pm with a requirement to work some evenings and weekends, as well as some business travel.Only those eligible to work in the US or have a valid US work permit / visa will be considered for this role.

DUTIES:: Working and interacting daily with Artist and third parties
Overseeing artists team (social media team, label team, accountant etc.) and chasing on all tasks and objectives
Assisting in all areas of creative (from A&R to artwork and videos, content etc)
Attending relevant meetings both with the artist and on behalf of the artist
Overseeing day to day activities and logistics
Co-ordination of promotion with label team
Overseeing touring plans working with live agent and promoter

Working closely with the artists live team, including tour manager and music director to co- ordinate various performances. Have a strong grasp of live budgets and be able to negotiate rates as and when relevant.
Reviewing deal memos and legal contracts
Liaising with Artist accountant, including approving invoices and budgets when appropriate and lawyer
Sourcing new opportunities with industry developments and innovation
QUALIFICATIONS, EXPERIENCE AND KNOWLEDGE: Sound knowledge of the entertainment industry
Knowledge of the creative and cultural community is important, with ability to input ideas on videos, styling, photography etc.
Will ideally have understanding of and relevant experience in artist management or working directly with artists
Good understanding of social media and digital marketing
REQUIRED SKILLS: Excellent IT and digital skills
Excellent time management and ability to manage multiple tasks seamlessly
Energy, enthusiasm and a ‘can do’ attitude
Excellent interpersonal skills, with proven skills in building effective working relationships internally and externally
Able to apply common sense and pragmatism
Strong organization and administration skills
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Detail orientated with excellent level of accuracy
A flexible approach and ability to adapt to changing priorities
Proactive and forward thinking and ability to use own initiative
Discrete and diplomatic, with ability to deal with highly sensitive and confidential information and issues in a professional manner
ROLE REQUIREMENTS: Full time. Office hours are 10am – 6pm with a requirement to work some evenings and weekends, as well as some business travel.
Only those eligible to work in the US or have a valid US work permit / visa will be considered for this role.

Title: Senior Product Manager (US)
Description: Ninja Tune – Senior Product ManagerContinue to article...JOB DESCRIPTION: Work with all label departments & client partners, including agents & managers, to effectively plan & execute marketing campaigns with special focus on our key Counter Records artists.Take a key leadership role in coordinating and energizing various departments to proudly deliver clear, innovative & organized marketing campaigns.Develop robust creative marketing strategies – from ideation to implementation – ensuring campaign goals are exceeded across primary KPIs & sales.Oversee the bigger picture ensuring all areas of a campaign are working together.Coordinate and facilitate set up of digital & physical releases, as well as ensuring logistical processes run smoothly- including but not limited to physical manufacturing, distribution and stock levels, as well as delivery & pitching to DSP partners.Hire marketing retainers and other third parties for press and radio where necessary, drive and manage those relationships and campaigns to ensure effective work.Work with the overall team to ensure campaign assets are procured in a timely fashion, ranging from artwork, remixes, visual content, and social messaging plans.Create and maintain campaign timelines, briefing teams and sharing release information efficiently worldwide.Create detailed reports of all marketing work on individual campaigns, to ensure that all results are communicated to internal staff, global distribution partners, digital sales team, external marketing agencies and artist management.Create and maintain campaign budgets.Collaborate with the digital strategy team to develop integrated digital campaigns and embrace new technologies, social media trends and their benefits.Work with the sales teams to coordinate and confirm a strong physical retail and DSP plan to drive consumption including streams, pre-orders, pre-saves and sales.Coordinate the manufacture of supporting marketing assets including retail posters, stickers, merch, etc.Plan & implement advertising campaigns with the digital team as well as Head of Marketing, including ongoing analysis of effectiveness and insights.Analyze data including social media and sales data to best plan and manage campaigns.Work alongside the entire team to suggest & implement creative marketing to support your campaigns, drive brainstorming sessions and promote collaboration to inspire fresh campaign ideas.Look for new, innovative ways to market and promote our artists, releases, and label especially Counter Records.Manage promotional and launch events where required.Accompany artists, as well as organize, their promo and events where required.All other work as necessary to reach campaign goals.QUALIFICATIONS:Minimum of 5 years of product management experience in the music industry.Minimum of 5 years of experience focused specifically on the US music market with a deep understanding of landscape and key players.Bachelor's degree, or higher level of education, preferred.Passionate about music, specifically an awareness & appreciation of Ninja Tune.An excellent understanding of marketing and a proven track record managing music industry specific campaigns.Excellent organizational skills.Ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.Meticulous attention to detail.Proven capability to work autonomously, demonstrating the ability to make decisions.Efficient, with excellent time management skills and the ability to prioritize.Drive and ability to push projects through to completion.Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.Ability to work with people effectively and politely at all levels.Excellent relationship-building and relationship-management skills.Ability to handle difficult situations with tact and diplomacy.Excellent ability to problem-solve and keep calm under pressure.Current awareness & understanding of social media platforms & trends.IT savvy with understanding of GSuite (Google Drive, Google Sheets,Google Docs).Hard working and enthusiastic.ABOUT US:Ninja Tune is an independent record label based in London, England, created in 1990 by the duo known as Coldcut: pioneers of the emerging hip- hop/electro scene in the eighties.It has satellite offices in Los Angeles, Berlin and Paris. Ninja Tune and Counter Records have since released music from a myriad of artists such as Bonobo, ODESZA, Maribou State, Big Wild, Kasbo, What So Not and many more.

JOB DESCRIPTION:: Work with all label departments & client partners, including agents & managers, to effectively plan & execute marketing campaigns with special focus on our key Counter Records artists.
Take a key leadership role in coordinating and energizing various departments to proudly deliver clear, innovative & organized marketing campaigns.
Develop robust creative marketing strategies , from ideation to implementation , ensuring campaign goals are exceeded across primary KPIs & sales.
Oversee the bigger picture ensuring all areas of a campaign are working together.

Coordinate and facilitate set up of digital & physical releases, as well as ensuring logistical processes run smoothly- including but not limited to physical manufacturing, distribution and stock levels, as well as delivery & pitching to DSP partners.
Hire marketing retainers and other third parties for press and radio where necessary, drive and manage those relationships and campaigns to ensure effective work.
Work with the overall team to ensure campaign assets are procured in a timely fashion, ranging from artwork, remixes, visual content, and social messaging plans.
Create and maintain campaign timelines, briefing teams and sharing release information efficiently worldwide.

Create detailed reports of all marketing work on individual campaigns, to ensure that all results are communicated to internal staff, global distribution partners, digital sales team, external marketing agencies and artist management.
Create and maintain campaign budgets.
Collaborate with the digital strategy team to develop integrated digital campaigns and embrace new technologies, social media trends and their benefits.
Work with the sales teams to coordinate and confirm a strong physical retail and DSP plan to drive consumption including streams, pre-orders, pre-saves and sales.
Coordinate the manufacture of supporting marketing assets including retail posters, stickers, merch, etc.
Plan & implement advertising campaigns with the digital team as well as Head of Marketing, including ongoing analysis of effectiveness and insights.
Analyze data including social media and sales data to best plan and manage campaigns.
Work alongside the entire team to suggest & implement creative marketing to support your campaigns, drive brainstorming sessions and promote collaboration to inspire fresh campaign ideas.
Look for new, innovative ways to market and promote our artists, releases, and label especially Counter Records.
Manage promotional and launch events where required.
Accompany artists, as well as organize, their promo and events where required.
All other work as necessary to reach campaign goals.
QUALIFICATIONS:: Minimum of 5 years of product management experience in the music industry.
Minimum of 5 years of experience focused specifically on the US music market with a deep understanding of landscape and key players.
Bachelor's degree, or higher level of education, preferred.
Passionate about music, specifically an awareness & appreciation of Ninja Tune.
An excellent understanding of marketing and a proven track record managing music industry specific campaigns.
Excellent organizational skills.
Ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.
Meticulous attention to detail.
Proven capability to work autonomously, demonstrating the ability to make decisions.
Efficient, with excellent time management skills and the ability to prioritize.
Drive and ability to push projects through to completion.
Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.
Ability to work with people effectively and politely at all levels.
Excellent relationship-building and relationship-management skills.
Ability to handle difficult situations with tact and diplomacy.
Excellent ability to problem-solve and keep calm under pressure.
Current awareness & understanding of social media platforms & trends.
IT savvy with understanding of GSuite (Google Drive, Google Sheets,
Google Docs).
Hard working and enthusiastic.ABOUT US:

Title: Head Of Creative And Sales (UK)
Description: At Cezame, we believe that production music is a creative field in its own right.Continue to article...The composers working with us readily embrace this idea as a starting point in the creative process which is ultimately aimed at professionals in the audio-visual sphere.Whether you are a director, editor, producer or sound-scapist, our goal is to share with you our belief and the authentic musical integrity of our composers through their sensitivity, originality and diversity.We are currently looking for a Head of Creative and Sales who will share our vision with TV companies and Advertising agencies, in fact anyone looking for high quality music for their projects in the UK.ABOUT THE JOB:We are looking for someone to really push and sell the Cezame catalogue in the UK.This is a full-time position.You will be responsible for your own time management, but you will be expected to be available between 9am and 6pm.We will give you training in Paris to learn about the company ethos, absorb the catalogue, use the search engine and create playlists.We are looking for someone to travel extensively throughout the UK, promoting the catalogue at conferences and meeting production companies and ad agencies. Travel expenses will be paid for.We would like you to come to Paris about once a month, to meet with your colleagues and to give us a report on the UK market, new strategies and sales.You will receive a basic salary plus a bonus for completed sales.EXPERIENCE REQUIRED:Self-starter with at least 3 yrs working in the UK Production Music Market.You love music and can talk about the construction of music and songs.You love meeting people face to face.You love to travel (within the UK and occasionally to Paris, France).You have a proven track record of sales.You have experience in the creative elements of pitching and placing music in media.You have experience in negotiating, pricing and concluding deals from one off placements to blanket licences.You love building relationships and have an existing black book of contacts within media such as music supervisors, ad agencies, brands, marketing and creative executives, TV and Radio producers, editors, producers and talent such as artists and composers.You are well organised and disciplined enough to work autonomously.You can create pitch reports and updates on the local market.You can absorb the music of a new catalogue very quickly.You are familiar with search engines and can create accurate playlists.You are not complacent and will advise your manager of new strategies.You are not a clock watcher and will be flexible as the job demands.You understand copyright and publishing.If you speak French, that would help, but it's not a deal breaker.WHAT TO DO NEXT?Send in your CV with a short covering letter explaining why we should pick you.Let us know of your approximate salary expectation.If you have the potential, we will then contact you to arrange for an interview.The start date will be dependent on your current situation.WHERE TO APPLY? Please send your CV and covering letter to Jay Mistry jay@jmconsultancy.biz

ABOUT THE JOB:: We are looking for someone to really push and sell the Cezame catalogue in the UK.
You will be responsible for your own time management, but you will be expected to be available between 9am and 6pm.
We will give you training in Paris to learn about the company ethos, absorb the catalogue, use the search engine and create playlists.
We are looking for someone to travel extensively throughout the UK, promoting the catalogue at conferences and meeting production companies and ad agencies. Travel expenses will be paid for.
We would like you to come to Paris about once a month, to meet with your colleagues and to give us a report on the UK market, new strategies and sales.
You will receive a basic salary plus a bonus for completed sales.
EXPERIENCE REQUIRED:: Self-starter with at least 3 yrs working in the UK Production Music Market.
You love music and can talk about the construction of music and songs.
You love meeting people face to face.
You love to travel (within the UK and occasionally to Paris, France).
You have a proven track record of sales.
You have experience in the creative elements of pitching and placing music in media.
You have experience in negotiating, pricing and concluding deals from one off placements to blanket licences.

You love building relationships and have an existing black book of contacts within media such as music supervisors, ad agencies, brands, marketing and creative executives, TV and Radio producers, editors, producers and talent such as artists and composers.
You are well organised and disciplined enough to work autonomously.
You can create pitch reports and updates on the local market.
You can absorb the music of a new catalogue very quickly.
You are familiar with search engines and can create accurate playlists.
You are not complacent and will advise your manager of new strategies.
You are not a clock watcher and will be flexible as the job demands.
If you speak French, that would help, but it's not a deal breaker.
WHAT TO DO NEXT?: Send in your CV with a short covering letter explaining why we should pick you.
Let us know of your approximate salary expectation.
If you have the potential, we will then contact you to arrange for an interview.
The start date will be dependent on your current situation.
WHERE TO APPLY?: Please send your CV and covering letter to Jay Mistry jay@jmconsultancy.biz

Title: Metadata Coordinator (UK)
Description: We're looking for a Metadata Coordinator to join the team based at our offices in Wandsworth, London.Continue to article...You will be the specialist metadata resource within the group, bringing an obsessive attention to detail and accuracy and at least 2 years experience working in a fast paced, deadline driven digital music supply chain environment.RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE:Working with our labels to source, enter, maintain and validate metadata for all types of releases and products as needed into the Groups master metadata systemsCreating releases, products, recordings as needed by our labels.Work with Digital Production Managers to make sure data available is up to required spec, complete and approved in a timely manner to achieve desired release datesWork alongside the wider team to review data processes and help to write and communicate data best practice process guides and support rolling these out to the businessSKILLS AND EXPERIENCE: At least 2 years in digital music supply chain role, label, aggregator / distributor or DSPGood working knowledge of meta data and its use in respect of digital music supply chain though to DSP's, MLC's and widerKnowledge of Content ID supply and best practice.Understanding of digital audio files and digital video requirements in a production environment.Excellent excel skills and total comfort working with data in general.Understanding of XML to assist any troubleshooting via XML Spy or similarSQL skills desirable.To apply please send a CV and covering letter, detailing your salary expectation and your current notice period to jobs@beggars.com

RESPONSIBILITIES INCLUDE:: Working with our labels to source, enter, maintain and validate metadata for all types of releases and products as needed into the Groups master metadata systems
Creating releases, products, recordings as needed by our labels.
Work with Digital Production Managers to make sure data available is up to required spec, complete and approved in a timely manner to achieve desired release dates
Work alongside the wider team to review data processes and help to write and communicate data best practice process guides and support rolling these out to the business
SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE:: At least 2 years in digital music supply chain role, label, aggregator / distributor or DSP
Good working knowledge of meta data and its use in respect of digital music supply chain though to DSP's, MLC's and wider
Knowledge of Content ID supply and best practice.
Understanding of digital audio files and digital video requirements in a production environment.
Excellent excel skills and total comfort working with data in general.
Understanding of XML to assist any troubleshooting via XML Spy or similar

Title: Assistant Accountant (UK)
Description: ATC Live is a live booking agency based in Camden, London, created to provide artists with a new style of representation, bringing an unparalleled level of creative and strategic thinking to our artists.Continue to article...Due to expansion, we are looking for a finance assistant to join our team.OVERVIEWThis is an exciting opportunity for an experienced client accountant to join a busy live booking agency. The key focus of the role will be to work alongside our Management Accountant to maintain a high level of accounting services to a growing roster of artists.MAIN JOB TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIESMaintaining a weekly statement run for the ATC Live rosterHandling artist / management queries on statementsProcessing deposit releases to artists and refunds to promotersAssisting internal staff with queries surrounding payments and credit controlAssuring all artists are paid accurately and timelyLiaising with management accountant and finance assistant to ensure all internal procedures and processes are metBuilding good working relationship with artist management teamsKey Attributes1 3 2 years' experience in a similar role (preferably with a booking agency)Keen interest in musicAbility to work under strict deadlinesAbility to manage a busy workload and prioritise key artistsProfessional and Friendly manner with good work ethicQualified / Part Qualified desirable but not essentialHigh level of attention to detailKnowledge of Quickbooks accounting (not essential)Understanding of VAT procedures and how they apply to both UK and International artistsBASISMonday 3 Friday 9am-5pmSALARYDOE

OVERVIEW: This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced client accountant to join a busy live booking agency. The key focus of the role will be to work alongside our Management Accountant to maintain a high level of accounting services to a growing roster of artists.
MAIN JOB TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Maintaining a weekly statement run for the ATC Live roster
Handling artist / management queries on statements
Processing deposit releases to artists and refunds to promoters
Assisting internal staff with queries surrounding payments and credit control
Assuring all artists are paid accurately and timely
Liaising with management accountant and finance assistant to ensure all internal procedures and processes are met
Building good working relationship with artist management teams
Key Attributes: 1 3 2 years' experience in a similar role (preferably with a booking agency)
Ability to work under strict deadlines
Ability to manage a busy workload and prioritise key artists
Professional and Friendly manner with good work ethic
Qualified / Part Qualified desirable but not essential
High level of attention to detail
Understanding of VAT procedures and how they apply to both UK and International artists

Title: Creative Intern (UK)
Description: London-based independent music company Good Soldier is seeking a Creative Intern. The successful candidate should have experience within graphic design and animation and have a portfolio of relevant projects.Continue to article...OverviewThe candidate will assist the Creative Manager in her daily tasks and artist-related projects. This will involve amending tour posters, creating infographics, managing assets, co-ordinating photo and video shoots, creating simple promotional animations for artists around releases and assisting in various administrative duties.The Creative Intern position at Good Soldier requires the candidate to be a driven individual, who can handle taking on various projects at the same time and the pressure that ensues from this.They will be able to take initiative, when the Creative Manager is in meetings or unavailable to answer questions, by being able to work independently. They should also be an enthusiastic team member.Main responsibilities includeManagement of promotional and creative assets ( photos, videos etc) for externals (requests from press and industry personnel)Design of infographics and marketing material for internal and external circulationDesign and amendment of tour postersAssisting in vinyl and CD designAssisting in merchandise designCo-ordinating photo and video shootsResponsible for communication with consultants, such as stylists, photographers etcWork with Label Assistant on budgets and invoicingCreating simple animations for artists around releasesKnowledge of the full Adobe SuiteNon-essential but desired, video editing skills (Final Cut Pro and/or Premiere Pro) and illustrative skills.

Overview: The candidate will assist the Creative Manager in her daily tasks and artist-related projects. Title: DACH Regional Sales Manager (DE)
Description: DACH Regional Sales Manager Continue to article...About BMAT BMAT started in 2005 when a group of engineers with a passion for music decided to spin-off from an audio research lab in Barcelona. The company started with an idea – to index all the music in the world – and a mission – to better the music eco-system – and a team of nerds eager to learn how to build a successful business. Today the company has grown to more than a hundred people – spread across Barcelona, Buenos Aires, Lima, São Paulo, London, Paris, Oslo, Tel-Aviv, Johannesburg, Singapore, Tokyo and Seoul – that team up to deliver daily 1 million identifications and 3 billion matches to our partners from all over the music industry.Our Culture We love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady). Make her life easier and her future brighter. We apply our scientific, data-infused brains to tell everyone what music has been played – so that creators get the recognition they deserve. We hear everything everywhere and tell everyone who wants to know. ‘What plays around will come back around’ we say. We believe by helping this happen we can make a positive change in the music ecosystem and enjoy talent for another million years.We are a team of pretty decent people who enjoy working with each other. We’re open-minded and well-traveled, coming from all parts of the world. We like coffee, tea, chai, and mate all the same. We’ve got rappers, producers, cello players, DJs, and songwriters. The passion for music, the excitement for technology and Barcelona as a backdrop has proven to make a good combination. We say there is a BMAT touch. We believe it has to do with being passionate, obsessive, nice, generous, honest, self-motivated, entrepreneurial, intrepid, crazy, extraordinary, creative, agile, human, friendly, smart, witty, talented, naive, optimistic, surprising, sensual, and nerdy, but not very hipster (unfortunately).Sales at BMATSales is a core team at the BMAT compound of regional managers, business vice-presidents, and executive directors. Its dynamic is very open, co-operative and no hierarchical. We understand sales as a discovery process through which, based on our expertise, we ask and listen our customers telling us about their challenges and in return we advise them on how we can help them tackle those through innovation.What You Will Be DoingUnderstand the newest needs/requests and problems faced by the Music and Audiovisual industries to propose relevant services.Provide accurate and regular forecasts on the DACH markets to the business development team.Define product strategy together with Global Sales management.Develop relationships with the industry to generate new business development opportunities for our five verticals – TVAV, Broadcast, Digital, Venues, Charts.Spot and answer tender calls.Take the responsibility for the DACH Sales P&L.All About You10 years of experience in sales with a record of quota over-achievement, preferably in a SaaS environment.The ideal applicant will have direct experience and network in the music and audiovisual industries and a good understanding of international copyrights.Experience managing a sales division within Music publishing/ Production music/ Film & TV/Synch is valued.Superior analytical and communication skills.Fluency in English.Bonus TrackAvailability for 3 months training in our Barcelona HQ.This role will require frequent travels within the region.You Can EnjoyFull-time job.Flexibility on working hours.Flat hierarchy.Full autonomy of your dutiesBecoming part of a diverse and multicultural team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry.Diverse perspectives bring about challenges and drive innovation, and innovation is kind of our thing. Our open-minded and flexible culture organically fosters this diversity. We’ve learnt that we thrive on learning from each other, so we don’t just welcome but actively hire a range of open-minded people from all walks of life. Our team is made up of people with different backgrounds, perspectives, orientations, abilities, musical tastes, dress sense and time-zones – but we all have one common goal: Making the music world a better place. With equal opportunities for everyone, BMAT is a pretty nice place to work.

About BMAT: BMAT started in 2005 when a group of engineers with a passion for music decided to spin-off from an audio research lab in Barcelona. The company started with an idea – to index all the music in the world – and a mission – to better the music eco-system – and a team of nerds eager to learn how to build a successful business. Today the company has grown to more than a hundred people , spread across Barcelona, Buenos Aires, Lima, São Paulo, London, Paris, Oslo, Tel-Aviv, Johannesburg, Singapore, Tokyo and Seoul , that team up to deliver daily 1 million identifications and 3 billion matches to our partners from all over the music industry.
Our Culture: We love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady). Make her life easier and her future brighter. We apply our scientific, data-infused brains to tell everyone what music has been played – so that creators get the recognition they deserve. We hear everything everywhere and tell everyone who wants to know. ‘What plays around will come back around’ we say. We believe by helping this happen we can make a positive change in the music ecosystem and enjoy talent for another million years.
Sales at BMAT: Sales is a core team at the BMAT compound of regional managers, business vice-presidents, and executive directors. Its dynamic is very open, co-operative and no hierarchical. We understand sales as a discovery process through which, based on our expertise, we ask and listen our customers telling us about their challenges and in return we advise them on how we can help them tackle those through innovation.
What You Will Be Doing: Understand the newest needs/requests and problems faced by the Music and Audiovisual industries to propose relevant services.
Provide accurate and regular forecasts on the DACH markets to the business development team.
Define product strategy together with Global Sales management.
Develop relationships with the industry to generate new business development opportunities for our five verticals , TVAV, Broadcast, Digital, Venues, Charts.
Spot and answer tender calls.
Take the responsibility for the DACH Sales P&L.
All About You: 10 years of experience in sales with a record of quota over-achievement, preferably in a SaaS environment.
The ideal applicant will have direct experience and network in the music and audiovisual industries and a good understanding of international copyrights.
Experience managing a sales division within Music publishing/ Production music/ Film & TV/Synch is valued.
Bonus Track: Availability for 3 months training in our Barcelona HQ.
You Can Enjoy: Becoming part of a diverse and multicultural team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry.

Title: Global Artist Manager (AUT)
Description: Red Bull's Culture Marketing program is a successful pillar of our global marketing mix.Continue to article...With the strong focus on Music and Dance but also Art and Design as well as Film we´re covering many fields and are immersed in selected scenes and cultural environments.To strengthen our Global Music Marketing Team, we´re looking for a Global Artist Manager (m/f/d).In this role you'll collaborate cross-functionally to support Red Bull's music entities in the ideation, fulfilment and execution of artist led music projects with Red Bull Media Network music content, Red Bull Records, and Red Bull Music Publishing. Through artist and industry relationships that tie into our programs and projects, you'll ensure that Red Bull builds brand affinity within the music space while paying into Red Bull's global strategies.Areas that play to your strengths:All the responsibilities we’ll trust you with:PLANNING & COLLABORATIONAs Global Artist Manager, you’ll enrich Global Culture Marketing business plan through proposal of innovative and feasible artist lead event & content projects. You’ll support countries in the execution of artist relations initiatives through the development and implementation of guidelines and tools.MUSIC & CONTENTIn this role, you’ll collaborate with the Red Bull Studios team to identify unique music creation projects. To identify relevant artist stories, help create opportunities to amplify their reach via Red Bull Media Network Music Content team is part of your responsibilities. Furthermore, you’ll support the facilitation of artist deliverables for assigned projects to ensure optimal impact for the brand.RELATIONSHIPSYou’ll identify and maintain relationships with artists and music industry influencers to drive product trial, amplify our existing culture programs, and create unique marketing initiatives. In addition, you’ll work cross-functionally to develop, implement and track artist lead brand integration. To identify scenes and artists that significantly influence brand image with our target consumers is part of your role. Lastly, you’ll manage day-to-day priority artist relationships including managing the delivery of a diverse range of requests across the regional Red Bull business.EXPERIENCEYour areas of knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role:5 years of experience in a creative-based role (editorial, record label, agency, management, etc.) working directly with music artists on deadline-driven projects3 years of experience working within a complex brand or advertising agency structureClear understanding of global entertainment, music and culture industry, including legal and licensing, as well as a firm understanding of youth Culture market & scenes globallyAn established network of diverse and well-respected artist and music industry contactsA proven “taste” record identifying, supporting and delivering results for innovative music artists and projectsManagement of collaborative projects from ideation through planning and execution including complex budget managementA strong ability to write, present, and articulate ideas clearlyA history of coaching, leading, and influencing development in order to foster a collaborative and creative environmentBachelor's degree (eg in Marketing, Communications, Music) or relevant working experienceFluent in English, any additional language a plus

PLANNING \u0026 COLLABORATION: As Global Artist Manager, you’ll enrich Global Culture Marketing business plan through proposal of innovative and feasible artist lead event & content projects. You’ll support countries in the execution of artist relations initiatives through the development and implementation of guidelines and tools.
MUSIC \u0026 CONTENT: In this role, you’ll collaborate with the Red Bull Studios team to identify unique music creation projects. To identify relevant artist stories, help create opportunities to amplify their reach via Red Bull Media Network Music Content team is part of your responsibilities. Furthermore, you’ll support the facilitation of artist deliverables for assigned projects to ensure optimal impact for the brand.
RELATIONSHIPS: You’ll identify and maintain relationships with artists and music industry influencers to drive product trial, amplify our existing culture programs, and create unique marketing initiatives. In addition, you’ll work cross-functionally to develop, implement and track artist lead brand integration. Lastly, you’ll manage day-to-day priority artist relationships including managing the delivery of a diverse range of requests across the regional Red Bull business.

3 years of experience working within a complex brand or advertising agency structure
