Title: Head of Finance (UK)
Description: The Head of Finance leads the day to day planning, implementing, managing and controlling of all finance related activities at Village Underground and EartH (a group of 6 companies with a combined turnover of £5M).Continue to article...You will provide accurate and timely financial analysis and insight into the operational activities of the business to influence and contribute to decision making and business planning throughout the organisation whilst ensuring a robust control environment and balance sheet integrity.You will be a fully qualified accountant and have an excellent understanding of financial management and accounting and a commitment to continuous improvement. You will have experience of successful delivery by leading and developing a Finance team. You will ideally have experience within the arts and/or music sector, and working in organisations in time of change.ROLES & RESPONSIBILITIES Strategy  Contribute to the development of organisational strategic business plans and future delivery models. Lead on the preparation of annual budgets and forecasting. Responsible for maintaining the highest standards of financial integrity. Manage all aspects of financial management and ensure financial procedures, â¨policies and systems are up to date and fit-for-purpose. Produce efficient and timely management accounts, reports and management â¨analysis of all financial aspects of the organisation and its trading subsidiary. Produce the statutory accounts for one of the group's companies. Manage risk, governance and regulatory matters including reporting to Companies â¨House and other external bodies. Ensure that the organisation complies with all relevant legislation. Lead on business process improvement projects to simplify and enhance the quality â¨of service and develop strategic solutions to address customer/market needs.Work in partnership with all teams to empower and enable them to be financially literate, thus effective and efficient.Organisation  Oversee the management of the day to day financial operations for the organisation. Lead on monthly month end closure processes, balance sheet accounting and â¨general ledger management.Budgets  Develop annual company budget, including budgets for each team. Prepare longer term financial forecasts. Prepare budgets and financial reports for one off projects. Monitor annual expenditure and cash flow. Work closely with all budget holders to monitor income generation targets. Meet regularly with teams to review budgets. Responsible for short term financial planning. Identify cost savings and new income generating opportunities.Cash Flow  Ensure effective management of organisational cash. Prepare cash flow updates for teams and EartH board members.Reporting  Produce quality, relevant and timely management accounts and financial reports for senior management and the EartH board. Ensure the team is kept informed on the financial position and communicate/advice on the financial implications of future projects. Provide support as required to strengthen the business cases of funding bids.Financial Systems  Review all systems and implement new processes to ensure optimal efficiency and effectiveness. Ensure operation of appropriate financial controls, including revenue control, credit and cost controlsAnnual Accounts  Prepare annual accounts and all relevant documentation for external accountants. Be responsible for implementation of changes in financial reporting requirements.Tax, VAT and HMRC Ensure management and processing of all the organisation's tax requirements, particularly in relation to VAT, PAYE and Corporation Tax.Finance Staff Lead, manage and develop the Finance team to ensure that a comprehensive financial support service is maintained.Ensure the professional development of the Finance team.General  Manage relations with external stakeholders such as banks, insurers, auditors and HMRC. Be proactive in keeping up to date with external developments in the Finance sector, advising on any changes as necessary. Actively be involved in the financial aspects of key projects undertaken by the organisation. Be an active member of the management team and contribute to meetings and organisational priorities.PERSON SPECIFICATIONS Qualifications ACCA/CIMA qualifiedSkills & Experience  Advanced IT skills 3 excellent user of MS Office and Google Apps. Experience of using Xero accounting software for both accounting and payroll In-depth experience of financial modelling Proven experience in financial processing and systems, design and implementation in â¨previous roles. Highly numerate with experience of managing budgets and interpreting financial â¨information. Strong analytical skills with the ability to convert data into analysis/information which â¨enable users to understand business performance and make informed decisions. Previous experience within the arts and/or music sector is desirable. Demonstrable significant experience of working at senior level contributing and â¨leading on an organisation's strategic plans. Excellent written skills, administrative, organisational and project management skills 3 â¨an ability to effectively self-manage workload and meet set deadlines.Leadership & behaviours  Understanding of the demands and challenges faced during organisational change. Experience of leading and managing a team of finance professionals with the ability â¨to lead, motivate and inspire others. Self-motivated and results orientated. Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills at all levels, adaptable to a range â¨of audiences and situations. High integrity and openness combined with commitment to good governance. Highly motivated, with an enquiring mind and passion for excellence and innovation in â¨pursuit of business growth and success. A commitment to championing diversity and representation, with a knowledge of a â¨diverse range of genres and cultures and a shown ability to work with people from a wide variety of backgrounds.About us Village Underground is a multidisciplinary venue presenting live concerts and electronic music events alongside theatre, performing arts and visual arts. Each year, VU has an attendance of over 150,000 people, stages more than 500 performances and works with more than 1500 artists.EartH (Evolutionary Arts Hackney) is a multi3arts space for the 21st Century. Programming is broad, global, open and rooted in the highest quality artistic pursuit. We seek to bring together both the leading, rising and other important talents of varied artistic fields under one roof to provide a vital new home for the arts in London.EartH looks to become a leading light in the city for audiences of all ages and walks of life to meet, listen, watch, dance and enjoy all that artistic endeavour and creativity has to offer.Both venues play host to commercial events, the revenue of which is put back into the artistic and education programmes.villageunderground.co.uk earthackney.co.ukAccessibility Our office is located above Village Underground and, due to the nature of the building, is only accessible by a staircase and does not have a lift.To applyPlease send your CV, a short cover letter and your salary expectation to Amelie Snyers ( amelie@villageunderground.co.uk )â¨. Subject line: Head of FinanceDeadline: 5PM on Friday 31 January

Description: Supernature is looking for an experienced, creative music marketer to fill a new full-time position as we expand our recordings division.Continue to article...This is a unique opportunity to play a key role in the growth of an ambitious young music business.ABOUT USSupernature is an independent artist services company based in London, with a growing roster of emerging and established talent including AJ Tracey, Shygirl, Conducta, Sega Bodega, Iglooghost and Coucou Chloe.Starting life as an artist management company in 2018, Supernature has expanded to offer end-to-end record label services, with in-house A&R, marketing, distribution and creative capabilities. Title: Global Marketing Strategy Lead (US)
Description: One voice understood in every language. That's what the Markets team 3 in locations across the globe 3 delivers every day. Help us grow our brand in your corner of the world.Continue to article...Spotify is the world's largest audio platform, home to over 50 million tracks of music and 500 thousand podcasts from around the globe. We available in 79 markets around the world with over 248 million monthly active users and 113 million subscribers. Our mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by enabling millions of creators to live off their work and billions of listeners to enjoy it.Spotify is looking for a Global Marketing Strategy Lead to join our Consumer Marketing team. As Strategy Lead, you will be responsible for developing a marketing strategy to guide Spotify's marketing investments around the world, leading integrated marketing programs to grow awareness and love for Spotify's masterbrand, and commercializing new growth initiatives that helps Spotify expand its reach to new markets and audience segments. Title: Junior Programme Manager (UK)
Description: Abbey Road Studios is looking for a highly organised Junior Programme Manager to join the team at Abbey Road Red and help manage their music tech incubation programme and other innovation activities.Continue to article...Follow this link for more details.The A Side: A Day in The LifeAbbey Road Red is Abbey Road's music tech incubation programme. The first of its kind in Europe and at the forefront of the music technology landscape since launch in 2015, our programme has incubated 15 music tech start-ups spanning the value chain from creation to logistics, marketing and consumption.Our aim is to help brilliant founders introduce the next set of universally adopted technologies into the music business, in the same way our predecessors did.Abbey Road Red also represents our other innovation activities here at Abbey Road. We host yearly hackathons, launch digital music products, explore cutting edge developments in spatial audio and immersive technologies, and produce a programme of knowledge sharing content and events, from Red Talks on cutting edge trends to blogs, video and other social media posts.In the role of Junior Programme Manager you will work closely with Abbey Road Red's Innovation Manager, helping to run the incubation programme and all its associated activities including scouting, start-up research, diligence, programme management, Red Talk research and production, blog post authoring and more; as well as feed into our other innovation activities from product ideation to helping run Hackathons.The B Side: Skills & ExperienceBe managerial:Programme management: you will be responsible for running the incubation programme with oversight from the Innovation Manager; this will include but not be limited to helping plan incubation frameworks, setting up and overseeing mentorship sessions and workshops with executives from Universal Music and Abbey Road Studios as well as our wider mentor network.Be inquisitive:Start-up research: you will be responsible for helping to scout and research startups; maintaining and updating a large start-up database, producing trends charts and analysis; and handling inbound applications from our online platform and our network.Be administrative:General administration and research: you will be responsible for helping the Innovation Manager prepare presentations and materials for board meetings, internal briefings, conference keynotes and other events; you will be expected to conduct ad hoc research into specific areas of interest as requiredBe collaborative:Marketing: you will work with the Abbey Road marketing team to generate and publish content and marketing assets related to Red's activity and areas of interest online and on social mediaDigital project management: you will work alongside the Innovation Manager, Head of Digital and Head of Audio Products to manage relationships with developers commissioned to prototype new in-house ideas, test iterative builds and monitor delivery against project plans.Be a confident communicator:Copywriting: you will be expected to generate copy for website updates and blog posts, event outlines, meeting summaries, internal briefings, and other documents.Be organised:Event management: you will be responsible for organising and producing events with oversight from the Innovation Manager; these events will include Red Talks and our yearly Demo Day and start up activations.The ideal candidate will:Be exceptionally organised and efficient and thrive on completing tasks and projects on or ahead of timeExcel in a dynamic work environment with changing day-to-day challenges and tasksBe comfortable producing reports and summaries as well as creative and research-led blog postsBe confident, professional and personable, comfortable meeting new people and building relationships on a regular basis.Continually show initiative and have an analytical mindsetShow a genuine passion for exploring cutting edge emerging technologies.Have a strong understanding of the music technology start-up ecosystem and music business, with a desire to learn moreHave experience using a range of music software and hardware and be familiar with commonly used audio-visual tools and technologies.Be comfortable using Microsoft Outlook, PowerPoint Word and Excel (ideally with good experience in advanced Excel tasks); as well as learning and maintaining any other project management toolsDesirable but non-essential:A passion for music and the meeting points between culture and technology.A knowledge and appreciation of the history of recording technologies.An understanding of content marketing and how to maximise reach and impact on social media.A basic level of experience editing HTML and using WordPress.

Title: VP of Music (US)
Description: Founded and operated in the heart of music city, Soundstripe is one of the fastest growing music technology companies to emerge in the last 5 years.Continue to article...Providing quality music to tens of thousands of video creators – from YouTubers to freelance videographers, nonprofits and Fortune 1000 companies – we believe individual experience and cultural fit are as valuable to our team as resume credentials. We seek the strongest talent in the industry to join us on our mission to Keep Creatives Creating and believe every Soundstripe employee's alignment with our Core Values is the key to our success.Soundstripe is The Tennessean’s #1 Top Workplace in 2019 for small businesses, and one of the best startup cultures in Nashville. Our Core Values influence every decision our organization makes. We believe these values are what has made Soundstripe special and what has and will continue to drive our growth and success.Provide all customers with genuine and whimsical care.Confront harsh realities with optimism.Keep it light.Strive to always grow and learn.Develop and practice honest communication.Make it better.Date the model. Marry the mission.Be humble and retain a giving and serving heart and mind.Quality over quantity.Done is better than perfect.Position OverviewSoundstripe's VP of Music will develop, manage, and direct the vision and daily operations of Soundstripe Productions, LLC. Our VP of Music will influence every facet of Soundstripe's music business, making high-level business and creative decisions concerning Soundstripe's original content strategy, vision, administration, and operations, as well as decisions regarding the careers of artists on Soundstripe's roster. The role of Soundstripe's VP of Music is to strategize, conceptualize and execute our music business's policy and plans. They will oversee the daily on-goings of Soundstripe Productions business development, staffing, personnel management and collaboration, and all aspects of Soundstripe Productions label operations: production, distribution, marketing, corporate communications, label strategy, and artist discovery and promotion. Soundstripe's VP of Music will improve the financial performance of Soundstripe Productions, LLC, reporting results and informing Soundstripe's Board of the original content strategies that will drive Soundstripe Inc.'s growth and success.ResponsibilitiesAs VP of Music at Soundstripe, responsibilities will include but not be limited to:Directing the vision and daily operations of Soundstripe Productions.Strategizing, conceptualizing, and executing Soundstripe's music policies and plans.Leading with inspired direction, creativity and innovation, collaborating and driving performance through engaging personnel management of Business Affairs, A&R, and Marketing and Sales Directors and teams.Steering the business development of Soundstripe Productions.Overseeing Soundstripe Productions' business affairs: administration, publishing, and distribution.Overseeing Soundstripe Productions' A&R: talent and content acquisition strategy as well as music production operations and execution.Overseeing Soundstripe Productions' sales and marketing: brand direction, artist marketing initiatives and business development opportunitiesReporting on Soundstripe Productions' financial performance; strategically driving profitability and contributing to the continued success and growth of Soundstripe Inc.QualificationsSoundstripe's VP of Music will embrace and believe in our Core Values.In addition to embodying these values, our VP of Music will demonstrate the following competencies and skills required for success in this role.Skills & RequirementsExtensive music industry experience, preferably as a senior or executive role within a record label.A deep, proven knowledge of all departments within a record label: sales, marketing, A&R, creative, publishing, distribution, production and promotion.Proven ability to manage vision and purpose, drive results, and lead and develop teams.CompetenciesBusiness AcumenAs Soundstripe's VP of Music, you know intimately how the music industry works and perhaps more importantly, where it doesn't work. You will pair your extensive industry experience with your ability to identify and react to creative trends to impart insight and strategy over the daily operations of Soundstripe Productions. You will oversee all of the label's daily activities, leading with curiosity and competitive insights to ultimately drive the success of its leaders and teams. You will understand deeply what sets Soundstripe's model apart, and how we stack up against our competition. You’ll always point us toward the direction of our future success.Managing Vision and PurposeAs VP of Soundstripe Productions, you will communicate a compelling and inspired vision and sense of core purpose to our music leaders and teams. You will comfortably talk beyond CtodayD, about the future and its possibilities. You’ll demonstrate optimism, creating milestones and benchmarks for the creative side of Soundstripe Inc. to rally behind. You’ll cast the creative and business vision for Soundstripe Productions, making the vision shareable by everyone. You will inspire and motivate the entirety of Soundstripe Productions.Building Effective TeamsAs the Executive leader over Soundstripe's music organization, you will demonstrate an ability to effectively blend people into teams. You will identify the talent and experience required to lead Soundstripe's music teams, and hire to the strengths that will generate results and bring our creative talent together. You will build strong morale and spirit amongst your leaders and teams, sharing wins and successes, fostering open dialogue, and empowering people to finish and be responsible for the work their responsible for. You will be skilled at defining success in terms of the whole team, and in doing so creating a feeling of belonging amongst the music, administrative, and all Soundstripe Productions teams.Dealing With ParadoxAs a creative business leader, you will be forced to act in ways that seem contradictory. Driving results for a creative organization will require you to be flexible and adaptable when facing tough calls. Communicating the Cblack and whiteD of business operations within a creative, CgreyD organization will require you to be compassionately tough. You will be counted on to drive the business goals of Soundstripe Productions, while inspiring and uplifting the creative output of our artistic staff. You'll set strong but flexible standards for the music side of Soundstripe's business. You'll be counted on to act in a varying and appropriate manner, based upon the situation, and will be seen as a strong yet sensitive and approachable leader despite the conflicting demands of our artistic business.Strategic AgilityAs VP of Music, you'll be counted on to Csee ahead clearlyD. You'll be relied upon to know our market and the emerging trends that will impact our label's strategic decisions. You'll base the plans of Soundstripe Productions' marketing, A&R, and operations on what will work now and in the future. You'll stay ahead of the industry curve, pushing Soundstripe Productions to be growing and scaling in a way that is always new and innovative. You'll push the boundaries of the expected, by tailoring and pivoting our label's strategies nimbly to serve both Soundstripe Inc.'s mission and bottom line.

Title: Deezer 3 Social Editor & Community Manager (UK)
Description: We are music and tech fans hailing from all over the globe, working to make Deezer the most personal music streaming service. From data scientists to tech experts, artists & labels specialists to marketers, and even in-house music editors, our team is spreading the love for music to over 180 countries. We’re all ears.Job DescriptionThe social global team in London is looking for a new member to join its ranks; we're a team of storytellers and producers in love with music, culture and technology. You're an individual with ideas, stories and a strong sense of what's happening now in culture we can elevate in our online community.Be bold, be courageous, be 8on' with what is happening in the world and experiment with formats, stories and topics. We love someone with a 8hacker' mentality.Take our brand-new Community advocacy program to the next level by growing our user communities.Manage the engagement in our Community on a daily basis; we're looking for someone who can know their memes and emojis.Create and execute on a Community activation plan (short and long-term)Drive and evolve a unique and engaging Community strategy that makes Deezer stand out in the online world.Grow the Deezer Community by keeping our users engaged and entertained and encourage them to join the conversation, all in line with what our Company stands for.Identify and manage Community super-users.Work closely with our in-house Social Media and Customer Service team to ensure alignment between platforms.Track, analyse and report on the performance of our online community, identify and drive improvement opportunities.Create and execute online and offline events and experiences to drive our community of super fans..QualificationsWhat we are looking for:A deep understanding of social platforms, their users' behaviours and expectations, how those can be leveraged and turned into attention-grabbing social stories/content.A storyteller. A creator. You need to know the difference between 8were' and 8we're' ðGreat storytelling skills on any social platforms or medium.A really nice person who is not afraid to speak up for what is right in the world. Title: Editor, US Latin (US)
Description: Spotify is looking for an experienced Editor with an expertise in Latin urban music to help drive curation, editorial and related strategies for the US Latin audience.Continue to article...In this role, you will ensure a top-notch programmed experience for the Latin music fan US, as well as an engaging editorial approach. Above all, your work will impact the way the world experiences music. Both spoken and written (editorial level).You have the ability to work with 3 and respond to 3 analytics.You care about attention to detail and organization, have a team spirit, strong work ethic, and a friendly, warm attitude.You have the ability to assume responsibility for large strategic initiatives, and understand how to set up, organize and run complex projects and report on/present the results.You have a story that demonstrates your passion for music that you can tell us about!You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what's playing in your headphones. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world's most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 258 million users.

Program Latin urban music for the US market, optimizing current programming and playlist hypotheses and developing new ones.

Travel to our main offices in the US (New York, Los Angeles, Miami) and work directly with the team on the ground.
Work alongside Spanish and LatAm teams to help curate global Latin playlists for Spotify's 240m worldwide users.
Represent US Latin Editorial strategy in cross-functional bets within the Company.

You have a deep knowledge of music, entertainment and culture when it concerns US Latin and its music marketplace in general, and you are also an expert when it comes to knowledge of US Latin artists, festivals, history, newcomers, influencers 3 basically everything to do with the diverse lifestyle and culture there.
Perfect Spanish and English are a must for this position. Both spoken and written (editorial level).
You have the ability to work with 3 and respond to 3 analytics.
You care about attention to detail and organization, have a team spirit, strong work ethic, and a friendly, warm attitude.

You have a story that demonstrates your passion for music that you can tell us about!

The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It's in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.

Title: Royalty Specialist, Inside Sales (US)
Description: This role will be responsible for providing songwriters, artists, composers, and producers with financing to cover everything from recording sessions and touring, to debt consolidation.Continue to article...The Royalty Specialist will primarily make outbound calls to create leads and build relationships with creatives and referral partners.JOB DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Initiate transactions and build relationships with creatives.Partner with Sound Royalties team to develop solutions tailored to the needs of creatives.Review and prepare pricing models, summary sheets, and other required documents for creatives.Act in an advisory role to creatives regarding finance and royalty matters.Work with creatives to ensure all deliverables are received, reviewed and addressed in a timely â¨manner.Maintain a real-time project/deal pipeline and systematically follow-up on new leads.Track all client-related activities in SalesForce.com CRM.KNOWLEDGE & SKILLS Excellent written and verbal communication skillsProficient in Microsoft Office suite (Outlook, Excel, Word)Strong organizational and planning skillsAbility to remain calm under pressureREQUIRED EXPERIENCE Prior experience working in sales with a focus on outbound calling and relationship-based sales is a plus.Prior experience with music rights and clearances, music publishing, publishing administration, music supervision and/or music copyright production, or other relevant experience a plusâ¨WHO WE ARE Sound Royalties is a privately-owned specialty finance firm that helps music industry professionals fund personal and professional projects without ever taking ownership of their copyrights, allowing for pass-through income, and empowering creatives to choose from a variety of flexible pricing options.The company’s core business is offering royalty advances of anywhere from $5,000 to $10 million. It does this by advancing artist, producer and songwriter royalties paid through music labels, distributors, publishers and PROs such as BMI, ASCAP, SESAC, SoundExchange and many more. To date, Sound Royalties has worked with a wide range of leading music industry professionals, including GRAMMY Award winners, platinum recording artists and notable music industry executives in every genre.

JOB DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Initiate transactions and build relationships with creatives.
Partner with Sound Royalties team to develop solutions tailored to the needs of creatives.
Review and prepare pricing models, summary sheets, and other required documents for creatives.
Act in an advisory role to creatives regarding finance and royalty matters.
Work with creatives to ensure all deliverables are received, reviewed and addressed in a timely â¨manner.
Maintain a real-time project/deal pipeline and systematically follow-up on new leads.
Track all client-related activities in SalesForce.com CRM.
KNOWLEDGE \u0026 SKILLS: Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Proficient in Microsoft Office suite (Outlook, Excel, Word)
Ability to remain calm under pressure
REQUIRED EXPERIENCE: Prior experience working in sales with a focus on outbound calling and relationship-based sales is a plus.
Prior experience with music rights and clearances, music publishing, publishing administration, music supervision and/or music copyright production, or other relevant experience a plus
\u00E2\u0080\u00A8WHO WE ARE: Sound Royalties is a privately-owned specialty finance firm that helps music industry professionals fund personal and professional projects without ever taking ownership of their copyrights, allowing for pass-through income, and empowering creatives to choose from a variety of flexible pricing options.

Title: Senior Global Creative, Creative Solutions (US)
Description: Spotify's mission is to 8unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by those creators'.Continue to article...We are looking for a passionate experienced creative to join our Global Creative Solutions team. Creative Solutions is a global group of thoughtful innovators who solve client problems in uniquely Spotify ways. We are strategists, creatives, story tellers, designers, writers, producers, musicians, music fans, data detectives, and innovators. eg Photoshop.You are a pro in audio tools; e.g. ProTools.You exhibit curiosity, proactively solve problems, and get things doneYou are able to manage your own time, stakeholders, and accountabilities in a collaborative, fast-paced, entrepreneurial environmentAct as creative expert in internal, client- and industry-facing meetings and eventsYou're excited by audio, and particularly digital audio's opportunity for creativity.You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what's playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be brilliant. So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It's in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.Spotify transformed music listening forever when we launched in 2008. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world's most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 258 million users.

Title: Global Head of Commercial Development, Music Publishing (US)
Description: The people here at Apple don't just create products 4 they create the kind of wonder that's revolutionized entire industries.Continue to article...It's the diversity of those people and their ideas that inspires the innovation that runs through everything we do, from amazing technology to industry-leading environmental efforts. Join Apple, and help us leave the world better than we found it.Apple is seeking a senior expert in rights in musical compositions to build and lead a team to manage the commercial relationships relating to rights in musical compositions. This is a key role to support the rapid expansion and growth of Apple Music, iTunes, and all use of musical compositions across the Apple ecosystem. This position is in the new Global Music Publishing group and is based in Los Angeles.Are you a self-starter who is dedicated and demonstrates creative and critical thinking abilities? Do you possess a passion for music, and the craft of songwriting while also having a superior commercial mind? We’re growing and urge you to apply. We believe the role would place you in a highly-energized environment with some of the most talented creative individuals in the industry where you would be inspired to share your years of experience along with providing extraordinary support for the team.Key QualificationsA minimum of 10 years experience working in senior commercial and/or licensing roles within the music industry, ideally within music publishers, collecting societies and/or digital music servicesWe believe an extraordinary understanding of and passion for the global music publishing licensing landscape will be critical to your successYou have an intimate understanding of digital music and the content distribution industryYou possess excellent business development, negotiation, and partnership management skillsYou have a consistent record of working collaboratively within international, cross-functional teams and a high-growth environmentYou are willing to travelExcellent written and verbal communication skills and the ability to build and deliver impactful presentations at all levels of the organizationExcellent organizational skills and attention to detailAn ability to see the big picture and handle a high level of ambiguityConfidence in calling out issues and presenting to senior management on project status and schedulesHoned analytical skills, with an ability to parse through extensive data to extract the key elementsThe ability to build and deliver persuasive arguments in favour of complex concepts.You work well within a fast-paced, time-sensitive environment with proven experience working on multi-faceted, international, large-scale, time-critical projects.DescriptionIn this role, you will build and manage a team that will be responsible for business negotiations with music publishers and collecting societies around the world, building and maintaining relationships with their commercial and business affairs teams to enable the use of musical compositions within Apple as needed. You will work in partnership with the Legal team and the Operations branch of the Music Publishing team on negotiating complex licenses with publishing rights holders to support expansion to new markets, product features, pricing, and commercial programs designed to accelerate the growth of Apple Music. You will also collaborate closely with other elements of the Music Publishing team, as well as other facets of the Apple business, such as the Business Development team to facilitate and build business models for the use of musical compositions throughout the Apple business. A great mind for numbers will stand you in good stead for this role.

Title: Non Executive Chair (UK)

Title: Global SEO Lead (US)

Amazon Music offerings are available in countries around the world, and our applications support our mission of delivering music to customers in new and exciting ways that enhance their day-to-day lives.Come innovate with the Amazon Music team!We are seeking a talented Global Lead to own Search Engine Optimization (SEO) for Amazon Music, advancing SEO management, growth, testing and optimization globally.In this role, you will identify large-scale opportunities for search engine optimization and translate those opportunities into business requirements, projects and site features. You will be capable of both developing SEO strategies and rolling up your sleeves to implement them.You have an excellent understanding of current best practices, as well as where the industry is headed. You are also a persuasive communicator, evangelizing the value of SEO amongst internal stakeholders.A successful candidate will have strong quantitative skills and enjoy diving deep and discovering the interplay of the many various signals in search engine traffic. Title: Director, Live Entertainment (CA)
Description: GSL Group includes a wide range of businesses including Real Estate Development, Holdings and Construction (Commercial, Industrial, Residential and Sports & Entertainment Facilities), E-Commerce, Sports and Entertainment, Travel & Hospitality, Food & Beverage, and Agriculture.Continue to article...We are looking for a Director, Live Entertainment who is responsible for booking & managing all events in our venues: Save On Foods Memorial Centre (Victoria), Prospera Place (Kelowna) and all our outdoor music festivals (Vancouver, Kelowna, Prince George).  The primary focus of this role will be to proactively secure all levels of music/talent for the events and pursue any other opportunities that drive greater community engagement and supportive revenues.In addition, maintaining our current relationships and level of business with our existing promoter clients and core venue tenants is integral to this role.RESPONSIBILITIESThis multi-faceted role will have a number of accountabilities, including but not exclusive to those listed below:Identify potential new events, negotiate agreements; work with the Venue Operations Lead to manage bookings and communicate all schedule information with promoters and agentsWork closely with internal Event Staff, Select Your Tickets team and Promoters on execution of eventsDevelop strong working relationships with key promoters and industry stakeholdersManage date challenges, work closely with WHL and other key tenants to book as many shows as possible to generate greater community engagement and revenue opportunitiesNetwork throughout the industry to negotiate venue rental/booking opportunitiesWork closely with internal and external marketing teams to maximize sales and revenue for each event bookingDevelop key media contacts and manage all event announcementsDevelop ticket information and show manifests for all eventsCoordinate on-sale and ticket settlements with Select Your TicketsEvaluate all risks related to new business and ensure financial and operational precautions are in place; ensure all costing for the event is negotiated as part of venue agreementDevelop all P&L documents for event proposalsDevelop annual operating budget for Live Entertainment; managing cost and ensuring adherence to budgetProvide all relevant information to Finance to facilitate event settlementRepresent venue management on event days with all key stakeholders; Event Manager, Select Your Tickets/Ticketing, Hospitality, Promoter, Tour ManagementManage all Live Entertainment website information to confirm accuracy and timelinessProvide all internal and external communications related to all event detailsSome travel within BC (mostly between Kelowna, Victoria and Vancouver)REQUIREMENTS:Post-Secondary degree in business, sales & marketing or equivalent experience5 years' experience in live event production and/or managementExisting relationships within the music and live entertainment industry, ability to develop strong working partnerships with executivesProven experience negotiating new business opportunities and managing long term relationships with key stakeholdersProven experience is developing and delivering digital marketing programs for live eventsAbility to identify and manage the feasibility of new business and revenue opportunitiesKeen attention to detail when it relates to all contract mattersProactive leader with ability to thrive in event-based industry under the pressure of timelines and deliverablesStrong ability to manage a number of projects at one time and make key decisions as neededStrong written and verbal communication skillsSponsorship, ticketing, VIP experience.Role is primarily dayshift, however, must be available evenings and weekends with ability to travel as required, mostly within BC (this will be based on event schedule)THE IDEAL CANDIDATE:Is creative, innovative and has a keen understanding of the music and entertainment industryHas the ability to be innovative and insightful 3 constantly looking for new ways of doing thingsSomeone who is highly organized and detail-oriented; ability to handle multiple projects with tight timelinesIs a team player who works well with others and is highly collaborative and focuses on building relationshipsIs receptive to new ideas and approaches; adapts well to change; remains positive and calm under pressureHas very strong business acumen, and excellent analytical skills, with ability to assess the potential of ticket salesHas excellent interpersonal and communication skills (written and verbal)Compensation: will be commensurate with experience.GSL is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are dedicated to providing a work environment that is free from harassment and discrimination. All employment decisions are made based on qualifications, job requirements and business needs. GSL is passionate about being known for an inclusive culture where talented people of all backgrounds want to work.

RESPONSIBILITIES: Identify potential new events, negotiate agreements; work with the Venue Operations Lead to manage bookings and communicate all schedule information with promoters and agents
Work closely with internal Event Staff, Select Your Tickets team and Promoters on execution of events
Develop strong working relationships with key promoters and industry stakeholders
Manage date challenges, work closely with WHL and other key tenants to book as many shows as possible to generate greater community engagement and revenue opportunities
Network throughout the industry to negotiate venue rental/booking opportunities
Work closely with internal and external marketing teams to maximize sales and revenue for each event booking
Develop key media contacts and manage all event announcements
Develop ticket information and show manifests for all events
Coordinate on-sale and ticket settlements with Select Your Tickets
Evaluate all risks related to new business and ensure financial and operational precautions are in place; ensure all costing for the event is negotiated as part of venue agreement
Develop all P&L documents for event proposals
Develop annual operating budget for Live Entertainment; managing cost and ensuring adherence to budget
Provide all relevant information to Finance to facilitate event settlement
Represent venue management on event days with all key stakeholders; Event Manager, Select Your Tickets/Ticketing, Hospitality, Promoter, Tour Management
Manage all Live Entertainment website information to confirm accuracy and timeliness
Provide all internal and external communications related to all event details
Some travel within BC (mostly between Kelowna, Victoria and Vancouver)
REQUIREMENTS:: Post-Secondary degree in business, sales & marketing or equivalent experience
5 years' experience in live event production and/or management
Existing relationships within the music and live entertainment industry, ability to develop strong working partnerships with executives
Proven experience negotiating new business opportunities and managing long term relationships with key stakeholders
Proven experience is developing and delivering digital marketing programs for live events
Ability to identify and manage the feasibility of new business and revenue opportunities
Keen attention to detail when it relates to all contract matters
Proactive leader with ability to thrive in event-based industry under the pressure of timelines and deliverables
Strong ability to manage a number of projects at one time and make key decisions as needed
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Sponsorship, ticketing, VIP experience.
Role is primarily dayshift, however, must be available evenings and weekends with ability to travel as required, mostly within BC (this will be based on event schedule)
THE IDEAL CANDIDATE:: Is creative, innovative and has a keen understanding of the music and entertainment industry
Has the ability to be innovative and insightful 3 constantly looking for new ways of doing things
Someone who is highly organized and detail-oriented; ability to handle multiple projects with tight timelines
Is a team player who works well with others and is highly collaborative and focuses on building relationships
Is receptive to new ideas and approaches; adapts well to change; remains positive and calm under pressure
Has very strong business acumen, and excellent analytical skills, with ability to assess the potential of ticket sales
Has excellent interpersonal and communication skills (written and verbal)

Title: Wixen Music 3 Music Publishing Administrator (UK)
Description: Wixen Music UK Ltd. has a position available for a music publishing administrator at entry level.Continue to article...This position is for a fixed contract of a year, which could become permanent depending on performance and the company's needs.The applicant must have excellent organisational and time management skills, close attention to detail, and be able to work well with others as part of a small team.The individual must present well in person and on the phone. The successful candidate will have the following attributes: hard working, reliable, responsible, trustworthy and able to work to tight deadlines. Must have in-depth knowledge and experience with Excel and Word.Prior experience in music publishing and neighbouring rights would be advantageous, but we are willing to train and educate the successful candidate, provided they possess the skills listed above. Proficiency in a secondary language would be beneficial, but not essential.Please send a covering letter and CV to info@wixenmusic.co.uk, FAO Tania Woodcock.The deadline for all applications is Thursday 30th of December.

Title: Label Assistant (UK)
Description: Circus Records and its associated group of companies are looking for a new addition to their growing team.Continue to article...You will have both responsibility and involvement within general planning, project setup, social media planning and execution, communication with artist/manager and overall project deliveries.As an assistant within the label team you will be entrusted to support, plan and execute a number of processes on behalf of the record label and wider stakeholders.We are looking for a candidate who has intern level experience in a similar role within the music industry. You should be able to display strong communication skills and a high level of attention to detail is a must.Ideally those that apply will have also worked across social media management and brand support, plus have a grasp of modern record label functions and digital mediums. Understanding of the record industry and/or direct label experience would be viewed as beneficial.As a direct link between the label and our artist/managers you must display confident communication, management and people skills.As a group we are always looking for hard working, self-motivated individuals who can add value to our growing family of businesses.Ideally the perfect candidate will be someone looking to develop within our company and grasp the opportunities that we can offer.If you're interested in joining this group of forward thinking international entertainment companies, please email your CV and covering letter demonstrating your experience and successful track record to enquiries@circus-records.co.uk with the subject 8Label Assistant'.The closing date for applications is Wednesday 18th December.

Title: VP, Creative, Latin (US)
Description: Our client, a global leader in music rights management, are seeking a new Vice President Creative for their Latin market, based in LA, to create and maintain relationships with songwriters, artists and publishers worldwide and within the music community at large.Continue to article...The role will include:Creating and sustaining Industry relationships in the music business to build the clients presence as leader in the Latin music community.Optimizing relationships to deliver greater market share and favorable catalogue assets.Leading a team in establishing and maintaining close working relationships with established writers and publishers, their agents, managers and attorneys.Proactively following current and emerging music trends to facilitate a constant flow of new music to build their market share across all genres and platforms within Latin.Demonstrating an ability to identify budding talent and then a willingness to support, foster and grow talent while connecting and maintaining business relationships.Addressing issues unique within the Latin music community while at the same time aligning to the value, principles and strategies for the company.Working with the Creative leadership team to define innovative songwriter/ composer developmental strategies to attract and develop new writers and composers to ensure diversity and growth in their repertoire.Leading and participating in Creative recognition events ranging from small congratulatory dinners to the large award events.Collaborating with the Corporate Communications team to develop branding and promotional strategies that position the company and its affiliates as a strong creative and business-minded force within the Latin music community.Formulating and monitoring annual budgets for Latin and provide guidance on cost controls to align with organizational strategies.The ideal candidate will possess a University degree in a related field; Master's degree or equivalent preferred.Qualified candidates should have an extensive experience in an executive position interfacing with creators, users and/or rights owners within the music industry.Must be bi-lingual and able to speak and write in Spanish.An awareness and thorough understanding of the music publishing arena is essential, experience working with a large network of industry professionals and a passion for Latin music and proven extensive connections within this genre essential.Please email your CV with a covering letter to helen@themusicmarket.co.uk

The role will include:: Creating and sustaining Industry relationships in the music business to build the clients presence as leader in the Latin music community.
Optimizing relationships to deliver greater market share and favorable catalogue assets.
Leading a team in establishing and maintaining close working relationships with established writers and publishers, their agents, managers and attorneys.
Proactively following current and emerging music trends to facilitate a constant flow of new music to build their market share across all genres and platforms within Latin.
Demonstrating an ability to identify budding talent and then a willingness to support, foster and grow talent while connecting and maintaining business relationships.
Addressing issues unique within the Latin music community while at the same time aligning to the value, principles and strategies for the company.

Working with the Creative leadership team to define innovative songwriter/ composer developmental strategies to attract and develop new writers and composers to ensure diversity and growth in their repertoire.
Leading and participating in Creative recognition events ranging from small congratulatory dinners to the large award events.

Collaborating with the Corporate Communications team to develop branding and promotional strategies that position the company and its affiliates as a strong creative and business-minded force within the Latin music community.
Formulating and monitoring annual budgets for Latin and provide guidance on cost controls to align with organizational strategies.

Title: Senior Digital Account Manager (SE)
Description: The Orchard is an innovative music distribution company, operating in over 40 markets globally.Continue to article...We're proud to be leading the way in both distribution and artist services across the world, providing creative, comprehensive and independent sales and marketing solutions for content owners across a diverse and exciting range of artists.The Orchard has an immediate opening for a Senior Digital Account Manager. You will work directly with our local and international teams across our extensive catalogue of labels and artist projects. You would be representing The Orchard with labels, managers and artists giving you the opportunity to guide and develop their strategy for playlisting and streaming in the markets.We are a growing Swedish team who are looking for an experienced and passionate senior digital digital account manager to join us, you will be responsible for managing The Orchard's local and global repertoire in the Nordics as well as drive a Nordic digital accounts team to reach the goals set for Orchard projects and our clients. This position would be based in Stockholm, Sweden.RESPONSIBILITIES:You would, together with local managers, be responsible for driving maximum promotions and exposure for The Orchard in the Nordics across all digital formats.You will have the opportunity to enhance the performance of the team through continued training and education and tracking progress via monitoring all key accounts in the region.You would look after pitches across our releases to in-depth marketing plans for projects.You would support and lead local account managers in their communication with the digital key accounts in the region as well as coordinating information and communication across Nordic countries.You would be required to analyse data to continually monitor ROI and impact of initiatives. Everything we do is driven by our love for music and podcasting. Today, we are the world's most popular audio streaming subscription service with a community of more than 230 million users.

What You'll Do: Support multiple project publicity campaigns, executing in the US and helping to provide assistance for global markets with toolkits/messaging/advice/guiding principles
Draft, contribute to media announcements surrounding new podcasts and trend stories
Work alongside multiple agencies
Build and maintain strong relationships with key media in order to drive communication objectives and generate accurate and positive coverage
Perpetually remain up to speed on the latest news in the entertainment, technology and podcasting space
Help create messaging and assemble supporting research for Spotify's key spokespeople and talent for announcements, panels and interviews
Work closely with internal data resources to deeply understand consumer behavior and identify opportunities to capitalize on that information
Work in close coordination with our global Communications team from our offices in Los Angeles or New York
Who You Are: 4 years relevant communications experience in entertainment or business public relations, digital marketing, and podcast industry 4 must have agency, network or studio experience
Self-sufficient and demonstrable ability to develop and execute strategic PR campaigns
Creative and motivated self-starter with a hands-on attitude and tireless work ethic
A deep love for podcasts, entertainment, music and technology
Excellent writing, organizational and presentation skills
Proficiency in multiple languages is a significant plus
Strong media relationships and the ability to cultivate additional contacts in the space
Ability to multitask and work calmly in an extremely fast-moving industry and environment
Collaborative and creative individual who enjoys partnering closely across teams and departments to create and distribute the most impactful communications possible
Strong writer, strategic thinker, and resourceful problem-solver
Ability to balance short- and long-term priorities
Of course, a proud representative of the Spotify brand!

Sony Music Entertainment is a wholly owned subsidiary of Sony Corporation of America.OverviewThis role supports the label’s roster with an emphasis on artist and content/brand development, social media marketing, online promotion and advertising. The content ranges from custom videos to Snapchat lenses to memes and more.QualificationsMust have at least 2 years digital marketing experience in a music environment.The job entails both normal business hours as well as attending concerts and other music events at night. Our team is ultra-inclusive and we're looking for open-minded and passionate music fans who collaborate well and bring a positive energy to any task that comes their way.

Title: Artist Management Assistant (UK)
Description: ABOUT USContinue to article...Supernature is an independent artist services company, partnering with exceptional creators across music and visual arts.With a firm focus on artistic freedom, and a range of in-house capabilities including marketing, distribution, content creation, product development and business management, we are uniquely positioned to help independent artists build successful, sustainable careers.Read more about Supernature hereTHE ROLEWe are looking for an organised, proactive Artist Management Assistant to work full-time from our London office in Elephant & Castle. Working in a small team, you would be expected to provide organisational support and structure to the artist management arm of the business. You would be responsible for key aspects of day-to-day management across the whole roster, including but not limited to:Managing schedules and maintaining shared diariesAdvancing live shows, booking travel and accommodation, and creating detailed itinerariesManaging social media channels and coordinating contentBuilding and maintaining artist press packs, riders and other essential assetsKeeping PRS, PPL and neighbouring rights registrations up to dateCo-ordinating the design, delivery and sale of merchandiseAccompanying artists to live engagements as management representativeActing as liaison and first port-of-call for artists and their various team membersABOUT YOUWe're looking for someone who shares both Supernature's independent spirit and artist-first ethos. The ideal candidate will have 2-3 years’ experience in a similar organisational role, a high level of competence in all of the areas detailed above, and a passion for current music of all kinds. Although there are designated office hours, you will often be required to work outside of those hours, including evenings and weekends. To work here, you must be:Self-driven and highly motivatedCalm and dependable under pressureIncredibly well-organised and methodicalA clear and personable communicatorAccountable for your responsibilitiesA positive, creative problem solverWilling and able to learn new skills quicklyWilling to travel in the UK and abroadThis is a unique opportunity to work and grow with an innovative company with a track record for helping brilliant artists achieve independent success. We are looking to interview for this role immediately, and the successful applicant would be expected to start before Christmas.

Advancing live shows, booking travel and accommodation, and creating detailed itineraries

Acting as liaison and first port-of-call for artists and their various team members

Willing to travel in the UK and abroad

Title: Global Podcast Manager, Content Communications (US)
Description: This role will work with Spotify's Content Communications team to map out, execute and analyze global campaigns supporting numerous podcast projects in every imaginable genre around the world. Beginning with market by market analysis and project announcements, carrying through show launches and then sustaining programming audience growth efforts 4 this individual will work with Spotify podcast creators, talent and their representatives on a daily basis.Continue to article...For this role, you must be a strong writer and communicator with a high level of comfort with frequent travel. Experience managing multiple overlapping projects and established relationships with top business, entertainment and trade press is also essential.Do you enjoy foreign language films, international TV and global music? Do you have experience assessing an audio show or series as it is being developed? Have you plotted out and prioritized the rhythm of a publicity campaign while sourcing content from multiple departments and stakeholders to deliver verifiable results? Do you enjoy drafting announcements, debating just the right adjective for a positioning statement, as well as obsessively reading every study and trend piece on the podcast space that the internet has to offer? Are you comfortable directly pitching press, researching & outlining Q&A strategies, designing the communications plan for an awards campaign and then relaxing by pouring over show data to identify the next audience that we should pursue? If so, then please keep reading.Success in this role requires a passion for supporting creators, executives and our growing slate. You should be familiar with global Spotify business priorities and how they ladder up to company objectives, as well as how to create understanding and connectivity with the rest of the company. You also need to be familiar with different global market needs in order to help localize best practices. Above all, your work will impact the way that the world understands and learns about Spotify's ongoing efforts to grow the global audio ecosystem. If you have a love for podcasts, creators and global culture, this might be the job for you. What you'll doWork with the Content Communications team in markets around the world to plan and optimize podcast publicity campaignsHelp design strategy and work to support global markets with toolkits/messaging/best practicesDraft, contribute to media announcements surrounding new podcasts and trend storiesWork alongside multiple agenciesBuild and maintain strong relationships with key media in order to drive communication objectives and generate accurate and positive coveragePerpetually remain up to speed on the latest market by market news in the entertainment, technology and podcasting spaceBrief and create messaging and supporting research for Spotify's key spokespeople and talent for announcements, panels and interviewsWork closely with internal data resources to deeply understand consumer behavior and identify opportunities to capitalize on that informationWork in close coordination with our global Communications team from our offices in Los AngelesWho you are 5-7 years relevant communications experience in entertainment or business public relations, digital marketing, and podcast industry 4 must have agency, network or studio experienceA deep love for podcasts, entertainment, music and technologyExcellent writing, organizational and presentation skillsProficiency in multiple languages is not a requirement but is a significant plusStrong media relationships and the ability to cultivate additional contacts in the spaceAbility to multitask and work calmly in an extremely fast-moving industry and environmentCollaborative and creative individual who enjoys partnering closely across teams and departments to create and distribute the most impactful communications possibleAbility to travel internationally on a regular basisOf course, a proud representative of the Spotify brand!

Title: Director of New Media, Sync (UK)
Description: With its broad and diverse roster of new stars and legendary artists, Warner Music Group is home to a collection of the best-known record labels in the music industry including Asylum, Atlantic, East West, Elektra, FFRR, Fueled by Ramen, Nonesuch, Parlophone, Rhino, Roadrunner, Sire, Warner Records, Warner Classics and Warner Chappell Music, one of the world’s leading music publishers with a catalogue of more than one million copyrights worldwide.Continue to article...For more than four decades, WMG has been an industry-leading force in providing a world-class array of services designed to help artists and labels grow their careers and their businesses. Artist & Label Services is the umbrella for WEA (Warner-Elektra-Atlantic) 3 the pioneering WEA distribution and marketing network 3 and Alternative Distribution Alliance (ADA) 3 the ground-breaking global distribution company for independent artists and labels.A little bit about our team:We're a close-knit team of 10 music-lovers, highly encouraging of each other and keen to impress our artists. The essence of our team, within Warner Records, is partnering with a wide range of media companies to influence and secure placements on film content, for our huge roster of music. We work across the entire Warner Records catalogue, from superstars through to breaking-artists. We also own a series of offshoot initiatives to help us grow our business. Such as W songs – a development publisher for brand-new talent. As film content includes anything from the latest blockbuster to random YouTube videos, we have our work cut-out, to understand who, what, when, where and why the next exciting content will come from.Why this could be your next big break:Never has this area of our business been so important. Many of the artists we work with are keen to have their music synced to picture and nearly all care about this outlet for their creative work. We work hard to provide as many opportunities as possible, however as this area becomes more congested we need to become smarter in our approach. This role is at the forefront of our creative work, implementing exciting new ways to celebrate our music. Unlike traditional sync, this role explores creatively how we place our music against picture, whether that be via technology solutions, strategic signings or on new growing channels.Here you'll get to:Working with the VP of Sync, lead a new sync strategy within the New Media and tech space.Handle the commercial offering of our in-house publishing arm, within Film/TV and Games.Launch new initiatives into the marketplace and make key decisions for their success.Work closely with artists and managers to realise their vision in our space.Explore and build opportunities with external partners for our artists and WMG UK.About you:You are culturally curious and open-minded with a start-up attitude.You love music.You are a standout colleague.You spot trends, follow leads and close down opportunities.You’re a phenomenal communicator, and an even better ambassador.You’re a forward-thinking negotiator.You have 9 years in the entertainment/tech industries, including experience working at or with music companies.It would be music to our ears if you also had:Experience within sync or a licensing environment.Experience with Keynote and deck creation.

Title: Director, Commercial Marketing (US)
Description: We are UMG, the Universal Music Group. We are the world's leading music company.Continue to article...In everything we do, we are committed to artistry, innovation and entrepreneurship. We own and operate a broad array of businesses engaged in recorded music, music publishing, merchandising, and audiovisual content in more than 60 countries.We identify and develop recording artists and songwriters, and we produce, distribute and promote the most critically acclaimed and commercially successful music to delight and entertain fans around the world.How we LEAD: Universal Music Group is looking for a Director, Commercial Marketing to join our industry-leading Commercial Marketing team in the Caroline division within Capitol Music Group.This position will be the primary account lead on various Caroline commercial accounts across streaming (including Spotify). This role will build commercial marketing plans, seek and execute various commercial opportunities and creative release strategies while nurturing important relationships with our account, artist and label partners. This role will work in tandem with various Caroline/Capitol/UMG departments under the direction of the Head of Commercial Marketing, Caroline. The successful candidate will have exceptional relationship management skills, be proactive, results orientated, a strategic thinker with an analytical mind and serve as an advocate for Caroline's artists and label partners.How you'll CREATE:Partner Account Lead:act as the primary point of contact for Caroline repertoire across a number of commercial accounts (including Spotify) while ensuring ongoing knowledge base of all best practices, personnel and opportunities across key commercial accountsStreaming and Commercial Marketing Strategy: Work with partners to create dynamic and engaging release plans and campaigns that drive successful commercial resultsData Analysis: Provide strategic analysis on consumption, listener behavior and account performanceLabel Relations:Work directly with Caroline's labels to identify opportunities for their release schedule, manage account and label call agendas and ensure goals are being met via key performance indicators, reporting and Caroline team communicationsLabel Meetings:Be available for all label meetings to communicate release performance, provide strategic recommendations, build plans and strategizeDigital Marketing Strategy: Work with the Caroline digital and marketing departments to ensure that our partners are building creative, innovative campaigns that engage fans utilizing pre-existing platforms and new technologies.Artist Relations:liaise directly with managers and artists during specific events to ensure smooth executionProduct Forecast/P&L/Budgeting:Project sales potential and monitor/advise on best product cost structures resulting in reasonable marketplace valuation at retail while providing maximum profitability margins with built-in per-unit marketing funds.Product Production:Liaise with production teams on projecting sales, pricing and forecastsCommercial Promotions: Through a series of reporting, keep a watchful eye on stock levels, title performance and suggest opportunities to further exploit or eventize revenue-generating moments for our artist's repertoire across our commercial partners.Relationship Management: Maintain strong relationships and communication with account and label partners. Title: Director Of Marketing, Communications & Public Affairs (UK)
Description: The Ivors Academy of Music Creators is a non-profit membership association that has supported, protected and celebrated music creators in the UK for the last 75 years.Continue to article...It is an exciting time in our history, having rebranded from the BASCA to The Ivors Academy of Music Creators in March this year, we have an ambitious strategy of growth which is seeing us expand into new areas with new partners. This is a pivotal role for a creative, ambitious and skilful communicator and marketer with experience of policy development, who understands the changing music landscape and wants to be at the heart of the debate as a champion of music creators.ABOUT THE ROLEReporting to the CEO you will be responsible for the effective management of messaging across the organisation to drive recruitment of new members, increase positive engagement with existing members and partners, maximise awareness and impact of our campaigning activities with opinion formers.Duties include:Development and implementation of The Ivors Academy Brand strategy,Development and implementation of marketing strategies including events, advertising, digital marketing, PR and partnerships to drive increased membership,Development and implementation of communications strategies for existing members including website, email, social media, print, video, podcasts and direct mail,Day to day management of the new Ivors Academy Policy Unit including policy development, press and public affairs strategy,Maintaining a network of industry partners to maximise awareness and impact,Management of our Equality, Diversity and Inclusion agenda,Development and management of The Ivors Academy Youth Network and Ivors Academy Friends offering which seek to increase the awareness and support for Academy initiatives and recruitment,Day to day management of Academy Genre Committees, marketing, communications, policy and equality team,Management of third-party providers and consultants as required.Essential Skills and Person Specification:Significant experience including qualifications in marketing, communications and PR,Experience working in a membership organisation context,Evidence of setting and implementing successful strategies,Has creatively led a team that deliver engaging and impactful communications,Demonstrates passion and skills in campaigning, lobbying and PR,Thrives on the challenge of managing multiple projects at any one time,Demonstrable experience and knowledge of CRM and segmentation strategies,Experience of managing teams and resources,Excellent written and verbal communication skills,Confident in a public-facing role connecting across the industry,A self-starter who can create opportunities,Knowledge and contacts in the music industry a plusTo apply, please send a CV and an accompanying cover letter to Graham Davies, CEO at: contact@ivorsacademy.comApplication deadline: Monday 25 November 2019Interviews: week commencing 2 December 2019 Start date: As soon as possibleAbout The Ivors AcademyThe Ivors Academy exists to support, protect and celebrate music creators in the UK. We are the independent professional association representing songwriters and composers in all genres, whether they create song, symphony or sync. We are entirely self-funded, relying on the continued support of our members and partners to carry on our work. Music is the most profound, enduring and popular of art forms. It crosses all borders, conveys all emotions, and touches all people. None of the music that the world loves exists until brilliant songwriters and composers create it. We represent, support, protect and celebrate the creators of music. Our Ivor Novello Awards are the ultimate accolade for songwriting and composing in the industry. The Ivors celebrate the talents of songwriters and screen composers, whilst The Ivors Composer Awards celebrate music creators in the classical, jazz and sonic art fields. The Ivors Academy Gold Badge recognises those who have supported and inspired our community. Each of these celebrations has at its heart the power and brilliance of music creators. We campaign together nationally and internationally to ensure that the rights of our members are protected, especially as our industry continues to rapidly change. And we cultivate talent, creating a community across the country in which our members share their valuable knowledge, creators cross-pollinate and collaborate, and all voices are heard. We guide new talent entering the industry and continue to encourage our members as they progress to greater successes. We are the champions of music creators. We are The Ivors Academy. ivorsacademy.com

About The Ivors Academy: The Ivors Academy exists to support, protect and celebrate music creators in the UK. We are the independent professional association representing songwriters and composers in all genres, whether they create song, symphony or sync. We are entirely self-funded, relying on the continued support of our members and partners to carry on our work. Music is the most profound, enduring and popular of art forms. It crosses all borders, conveys all emotions, and touches all people. None of the music that the world loves exists until brilliant songwriters and composers create it. Title: UK Licensing Coordinator (UK)
Description: Concord is looking to employ a full time UK Licensing Coordinator reporting to Director of Licensing Operations (UK) in our London office working across our publishing and recorded music catalogues.Continue to article... Company and Department Overview Concord is the independent, worldwide leader in the development, management and acquisition of sound recordings, music publishing and theatrical performance rights. Concord has offices in Los Angeles, New York, Nashville, London, Berlin and Miami.Concord Music Publishing represents more than 390,000 copyrighted works spanning all musical genres and including many of the world's most celebrated compositions by its most legendary creators. Concord Music Publishing is also home to a diverse group of contemporary songwriters and composers creating important and commercially-successful new songs and musical works. Boosey & Hawkes, the world's leading classical music publisher, and The Rodgers & Hammerstein Organization are also business units of Concord Music Publishing.Concord Recorded MusicConcord Recorded Music is comprised of five active labels across many musical genres: Concord Records, Fantasy Records, Fearless Records, Loma Vista Recordings and Rounder Records. Concord is also home to the #1 kids' music brand, KIDZ BOP.The company's historical labels are managed by its Craft Recordings team, and include such storied imprints as Fania, Independiente, Milestone, Musart, Nitro, Pablo, Prestige, Riverside, Savoy, Specialty, Stax, Telarc, VarÃ¨se Sarabande, Vee-Jay and Wind-up. Concord’s master recording portfolio contains more than 12,000 active albums and includes no less than 273 GRAMMY® winners (representing approximately 6.2% of all the GRAMMYs® ever awarded) and over 400 gold, platinum, multi-platinum and diamond RIAA certifications across 215 titles.The UK Licensing team is a vital part of Concord, working alongside the publishing and recorded music sync teams and our global network of sub-publishers to ensure we grasp all synchronization opportunities for writers, artists and various catalogues.The Role Primarily handling licensing requests for TV, film and online uses, the position requires someone with a high degree of organisational skill and a keen eye for details, with an understanding of the intricacies of licensing both published works and master recordings.The candidate must be comfortable dealing with clients, song-writers, artists and their managers on a daily basis, and will be adept at both quoting and issuing paperwork for all manner of syncs. The position will also require someone who is able to efficiently log, track and produce reports on the licensing activity from the UK team and sub-publishers.We seek a highly motivated team player, who is passionate about music and able to perform their duties quickly and efficiently. The post will be full time in a lively, friendly and fast-paced environment based in our central London office.Key Duties and Responsibilities of the UK Licensing CoordinatorDeal with licence requests queries from clientsProduce paperwork for licencesMonitor return of signed licences and file as necessaryMonitor payment of invoices and chase as necessaryLog, track and report licensing
