Title: Senior Manager, Society Relations (UK)
Description: Kobalt Neighbouring Rights is seeking a hard-working and motivated people-person to lead our Society Relations team that manage the registration process of our performer client roster at societies around the world.Continue to article...The role requires a personable and motivating people manager with a track record of managing and developing a team. You may have experience working for a music publisher or Neighbouring Rights society where you have extensive and regular contact with collection societies around the world. But most important is that you are enthusiastic, a great team member who efficiently follow up on issues until they are fully resolved.The role offers a fantastic opportunity to work for one of the leading companies in a fast-growing industry. Kobalt Neighbouring Rights started in 2011 and we offer a global service for artists and independent labels to collect their Neighbouring Rights income. Today, our team represents artists like A$AP Rocky, The Chainsmokers, Dua Lipa, Kygo, Lil Wayne, Sia, Young Thug and more.Founded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.WHAT DOES A SENIOR MANAGER, SOCIETY RELATIONS AT KOBALT DO?Provides management and leadership to the London-based Society Relations TeamProvides team and department trainingAssists team with daily planning and problem solvingActs as first escalation point for society issuesInforms senior management of significant changes and issues at societies and initiates 2nd level escalation in line with agreed processWorks with tech department to define and prioritise new and improved solutions to support the day to day activities of the teamInvestigates requirements for registering clients in additional territories and establishes relationships to facilitate the setup of efficient processes with the collection societiesStreamlines processes in existing business territories, both internally and in working with the collection societiesMaintains a thorough understanding of society issues, qualification criteria and distribution policies as well as current industry newsTravels as required to meet business needsWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? Previous Neighbouring Rights experience preferred but not essential, but ideally a music background at CMO with strong international networkProven people management experience with experience of motivating and mentoring team membersExcellent communicator with interpersonal skillsAble to take initiative and drive things forward through to completionHas an eye for detail, but also capable of bigger picture thinking and the ability to identify issues resulting from process changesAble to propose options for escalated problem solvingA keen interest in tech, with experience of working with databases (SQL skills are an advantage) and strong proficiency in all Microsoft Office programs as well as Google Suite

Title: Digital Sales & Marketing Director (UK)

Our family of songwriters and world class artists include Worship Central, Matt Redman, Martin Smith, Rivers & Robots, Danny O'Callaghan and Lucy Grimble.From our new home in the heart of Brighton, we are re-inventing ourselves for our next season, it's an exciting time to be part of this team. Your passion for songs and technology, social media and analytics will help us drive engagement.In this new role, bringing marketing and sales teams together, you will be responsible for facilitating an innovative, disruptive and forward thinking digital marketing and sales strategy, to advance the Integrity Music story and it's family of primarily music based products.Your strong leadership and interpersonal skills will inspire your marketing and sales team, enabling them to achieve more and reach their potential as well as raising the impact and engagement of the songs and resources we create.This role has a strong emphasis on marketing our products in the digital space, developing new opportunities for sales and strategic partnerships for the songs and music services we offer. Your ability in the online space will help maximise physical sales but transition us into a fully digital music label and song publisher.Position Requirements:Formal Education:Whilst a degree in digital marketing is helpful, we know that great people in this space sometimes have a different journey, so send us your CV anyway.Experience:Experience in a similar role within a global brand, in the area of marketing, sales, strategy and content creation.You will need to be passionate about Christian worship music and have a knowledge of churches and movements in the UK and Europe.Specialized Knowledge/Certification:Exceptional knowledge of social media, SEO as well as physical product marketing into stores. Experience of working in a creative music marketing environment is a bonus.Working Conditions:Comfortable working conditions, handling light weight, intermittent sitting, standing and walking.Working environment 3 open plan and co-working spaces with some noiseDetail-oriented work requiring mental acuity and accuracy.Normal business hours (9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.) are typical but must have a willingness and flexibility to attend events and work in the digital space during evenings and weekends.Some travel will be required.Job Description:Oversee the development, management, coordination, and execution of all marketing plans, budgets and strategies, and implement tactical plans that meet and/or exceed budgeted company expectations.You will instigate and set the digital road map with the team and lead a visionary digital sales strategy. Your work will inspire the stakeholder artists and songwriters who are part of Integrity Music.Develop and maintain strong working relationships with external strategic partners, distributors and digital platforms. Ensure interaction and co-ordination to execute releases at the best possible pricing and collaboration.You will be able to report to the senior management team on sales and marketing 3 updating data and sending through financial analysis and updates on a weekly basis.Bring storytelling excellence to all consumer and gatekeeper focused digital communication and media. Inspiring the people who hear our story to value our songs and brand through brilliant content and consumer engagement. This will define a compelling end to end consumer journey, that is refined to keep it relevant and serve those who consume our music.You will allocate resources and hold the team accountable to clear KPIs in the areas of sales and marketing. Through data analysis, you will measure team effectiveness. By knowing what works, you will grow it. By understanding what doesn't, you will stop it.Build and engage in the development of a global Integrity Music culture of collaboration. You will, in partnership with our US office, help to build a world-class, diverse global team across all disciplines (Digital, Media and Consumer services), developing the team that every other music brand wants to hire.You will quickly understand music services and be able to see how we can serve others where they are at, rather than forcing them to fit in with our plans 3 this personal and servant style strategy will set you apart from template driven sales.Research and analyze economic trends, buying habits and the demographic characteristics of consumers, their preferences and purchasing habits for Integrity Music products and those of competitors.You will be a respectful thought-leader within Integrity for all things digital sales and marketing, your expertise will be valued and trusted. We hope you will create a culture of proactive and transparent-communication to share expertise and work across the entire business.Live your role, inspiring those around you to deliver outstanding work, that engages consumers with our songs and content.Your energy and drive will see you move faster than the industry you are in. Unearthing new opportunities and partnerships that deliver new sales channels, working with these partners you will deliver outstanding work that surprises consumers whenever Integrity Music turns up.This role requires someone who will do the work, leading by example, be out and about and causing disruption in our market segment.Collaborate with global and domestic A&R, Publishing, Commercial Partnerships, and Operations teams to identify and secure music services partnerships with artists and labels aligned with Integrity's mission and vision.Perform other job-related duties as assigned.

Your energy and drive will see you move faster than the industry you are in. Unearthing new opportunities and partnerships that deliver new sales channels, working with these partners you will deliver outstanding work that surprises consumers whenever Integrity Music turns up.

Title: Sync Manager (UK)
Description: Company DescriptionContinue to article...Founded in 2005, believe is the worldwide leader in digital audio and video distribution for independent artists. By combining technological and artistic skills, the group has built expertise and has quickly positioned itself as a new player in the music industry landscape.With more than 1,000 employees in 45 countries, the group distributes more than a third of the world’s digital music in volume and has achieved an average annual growth of 30% over the last ten years.believe’s mission can be summed up as: “To best serve and develop all artists with care, transparency, fairness, expertise and innovation, in all local markets around the world”.Want to collaborate in a dynamic, entrepreneurial company that puts people at the center of its strategy? believe is for you!Believe Sync is the creative licensing department at Believe. We provide music for advertising, TV, film and video games from a vast and diverse selection of independent music. Working closely with our partners in the creative industries, we offer free creative services including catalogue searches, bespoke recordings and wider brand partnerships with the music and artists we represent.Job DescriptionBelieve Sync is looking for a self-motivated and entrepreneurial individual based in London to source, negotiate and license sync placements for Believe's catalogue and frontline roster.Reporting to the Global Head of Sync, you will handle day-to-day sync activity in the UK 3 including both creative and licensing work – whilst continuously seeking new business opportunities and developing Believe Sync’s overall presence in the market.Based in London with an existing network of contacts, you will be responsible for sourcing, developing and maintaining relationships with clients in the advertising, TV, film and video games industries. You will also travel regionally across the UK with the aim of expanding Believe's client base.Main responsibilities include:Sourcing, developing and maintaining business relationships with clients across the UK in the advertising, TV, film and video games industries.Procuring, leading and contributing to creative pitches.Handling quotes, approvals and licensing for deals in the UK and other EU territories from time to time.Designing and organising appropriate marketing and promotional activity for Believe's frontline roster, including mailouts, gigs and showcases.Organising and maintaining Believe's online music catalogue. Creating themed playlists, featuring artists and tracks, and tagging music.Day-to-day administrative work including license tracking and financial reporting.Attending gigs with clients and maintaining a good relationship with Believe's A&R, digital marketing and in-house label departments across the world.QualificationsA passion for and knowledge of a wide variety of music is essential, as is an existing network of clients and an ability to work autonomously.You should have:At least 3-5 years experience of working in sync; either at a label or publishing company, or through a music supervision role elsewhere.Excellent communication and networking skills; you must be confident meeting new people and seeking new business opportunities, as well as dealing with artists and managers on a day to day basis.An outstanding knowledge of, and passion for, independent music.A creative mind which is willing and able to answer music briefs openly and thoroughly.Excellent time management skills and attention to detail. You must be able to meet short- and long-term targets whilst maintaining a consistently high standard of work.You should be:A passionate self-starter able to work with and without direct supervision.An active and well-known member within the UK sync community.An enthusiastic arts lover who lives and breathes music; you are consistently intrigued and excited by the use of music in moving media, and are willing to try new things to advance this particular area of the industry.Additional InformationBelieve is committed to ensuring equal opportunities in employment, regardless of origin, sex, morals, sexual orientation, gender, age, family status pregnancy, race, political opinions, trade union activities, religious beliefs, physical appearance, family name, place of residence, state of health, or disability.

Job Description: Believe Sync is looking for a self-motivated and entrepreneurial individual based in London to source, negotiate and license sync placements for Believe's catalogue and frontline roster.
Main responsibilities include:: Sourcing, developing and maintaining business relationships with clients across the UK in the advertising, TV, film and video games industries.
Procuring, leading and contributing to creative pitches.
Handling quotes, approvals and licensing for deals in the UK and other EU territories from time to time.
Designing and organising appropriate marketing and promotional activity for Believe's frontline roster, including mailouts, gigs and showcases.
Organising and maintaining Believe's online music catalogue. Creating themed playlists, featuring artists and tracks, and tagging music.
Day-to-day administrative work including license tracking and financial reporting.
Attending gigs with clients and maintaining a good relationship with Believe's A&R, digital marketing and in-house label departments across the world.
Qualifications: A passion for and knowledge of a wide variety of music is essential, as is an existing network of clients and an ability to work autonomously.
You should have:: At least 3-5 years experience of working in sync; either at a label or publishing company, or through a music supervision role elsewhere.
Excellent communication and networking skills; you must be confident meeting new people and seeking new business opportunities, as well as dealing with artists and managers on a day to day basis.
An outstanding knowledge of, and passion for, independent music.
A creative mind which is willing and able to answer music briefs openly and thoroughly.
Excellent time management skills and attention to detail. You must be able to meet short- and long-term targets whilst maintaining a consistently high standard of work.
You should be:: A passionate self-starter able to work with and without direct supervision.
An active and well-known member within the UK sync community.

An enthusiastic arts lover who lives and breathes music; you are consistently intrigued and excited by the use of music in moving media, and are willing to try new things to advance this particular area of the industry.
Additional Information: Believe is committed to ensuring equal opportunities in employment, regardless of origin, sex, morals, sexual orientation, gender, age, family status pregnancy, race, political opinions, trade union activities, religious beliefs, physical appearance, family name, place of residence, state of health, or disability.

Title: Senior Marketing Manager (Global Marketing, EMEA) (UK)

About BIMM Institute: BIMM, The British and Irish Modern Music Institute, has colleges in eight cities where music matters most – London, Berlin, Dublin, Brighton, Manchester, Bristol, Hamburg and Birmingham – and is proud to be the largest and leading provider of contemporary music education in Europe.
Role Specification: Working closely with the Group Head of Careers and Employability and careers teams in Colleges, the Talent Manager will screen and support students throughout their recruitment experience with industry employers.

Experience with (phone and in-person) interviews, candidate screening and evaluation.

Title: Publishing Coordinator (UK)
Description: Warp Publishing, a leading independent music publishing company, is looking for a Publishing Coordinator to join its team in the North London office.Continue to article...The companyWarp Publishing provides a personalised music publishing service to a diverse and unique roster of artists, score composers and hit songwriters including Anna Meredith, Danny Brown, Oneohtrix Point Never, Yves Tumor, Khruangbin, Death Grips, Kwes, Miguel Atwood-Ferguson, Morgan Geist, Boards of Canada and many more.Warp has a rich reputation for innovation in music and for the business infrastructure that supports its artists, with offices in London and Los Angeles. 2019 is Warp's 30th Anniversary.The roleYou will provide the highest level of publishing administration services for our artists, looking after copyright and other publishing tasks as part of the Copyright & Royalties team. You will work closely with collection societies, digital service providers, artists and their management and report to the Copyright & Royalties Manager.YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR:Registering all new works and agreements with collection societies and sub-publishers;Reviewing catalogue information using multiple sources of information (collection societies, agreements, discography);Submitting live setlists and cue sheets to collection societies;Working closely with the Copyright & Royalties Manager in all income tracking exercises;Resolving any queries from Artists/Management, Royalties, Finance, DSPs, collection societies & sub-publishers worldwide;You will be encouraged to take on additional responsibilities as you gain autonomy in your work.NECESSARYGood knowledge of music copyright from previous experience and/or degree of study;Minimum of one year's experience working in an administrative role for a publisher or collection society;Passionate about independent music, the Warp Publishing roster, and the music publishing business;Enthusiastic about providing a great service to our artists.DESIRABLEExperience with Music Maestro or similar rights and royalty management software;Being comfortable with Excel and handling large amounts of data.Salary: dependent on experienceIf you wish to apply for this role, please send your CV along with a covering letter explaining why you would be suitable.Closing date: Thursday 21st November 2019Email applications to jobs@warppublishing.net with 8Publishing Coordinator' as the subject header.

The company: Warp Publishing provides a personalised music publishing service to a diverse and unique roster of artists, score composers and hit songwriters including Anna Meredith, Danny Brown, Oneohtrix Point Never, Yves Tumor, Khruangbin, Death Grips, Kwes, Miguel Atwood-Ferguson, Morgan Geist, Boards of Canada and many more.
The role: You will provide the highest level of publishing administration services for our artists, looking after copyright and other publishing tasks as part of the Copyright & Royalties team. You will work closely with collection societies, digital service providers, artists and their management and report to the Copyright & Royalties Manager.
YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR:: Registering all new works and agreements with collection societies and sub-publishers;
Reviewing catalogue information using multiple sources of information (collection societies, agreements, discography);
Submitting live setlists and cue sheets to collection societies;
Working closely with the Copyright & Royalties Manager in all income tracking exercises;
Resolving any queries from Artists/Management, Royalties, Finance, DSPs, collection societies & sub-publishers worldwide;
NECESSARY: Good knowledge of music copyright from previous experience and/or degree of study;
Minimum of one year's experience working in an administrative role for a publisher or collection society;
Passionate about independent music, the Warp Publishing roster, and the music publishing business;
Enthusiastic about providing a great service to our artists.
DESIRABLE: Experience with Music Maestro or similar rights and royalty management software;
Being comfortable with Excel and handling large amounts of data.

Title: Publicist (UK)
Description: Huxley is seeking a Publicist to join its team based in London, UK.Continue to article...The successful candidate will be thorough and dedicated with a strong network of global editorial contacts across cultural and entertainment outlets.They should be driven and focused in their approach, thinking both creatively and pragmatically about how they represent their clients.They will have three years or more experience in a consumer-facing Publicity role and are available for international travel.Please send applications to info@huxley.world

Title: Coordinator, Content Operations (UK)
Description: We're looking for a Coordinator, Content Operations to join this expanding team within AWAL's Digital Operations department.Continue to article...The successful candidate will be responsible for maintaining the quality of our release deliveries, ensuring our metadata is best in class. Experience with digital service metadata requirements and content management systems is required. An obsessive eye for detail and encyclopedic knowledge across a variety of genres would be a definite plus too!BACKGROUNDKobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the center of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL.Whether an artist is looking for the best digital distribution or a truly global campaign, AWAL has become the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers. This has created a high-quality path for artists to remain outside of the traditional label system while maintaining ownership of their masters.Leading artists who have already chosen AWAL include Lauv, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Tom Misch, Omar Apollo and Little Simz, as well as labels like Glassnote, B-Unique, 30th Century and Chess Club.We deliver audio and video to all of the key platforms using our bespoke supply chain system, powered by Kobalt's market leading technology, making thousands of deliveries per week.We're currently 600 people based across 13 offices and we're still growing.WHAT DOES COORDINATOR, CONTENT OPERATIONS AT AWAL DO?QC audio release submissions in accordance with partner metadata specifications.Provide structured feedback for client-facing teams when releases fail checks.Carry out metadata updates as required across the AWAL catalogue.Contribute to internal Content Operations documentation, including up-to-date DSP best practices and metadata style guide.Work with client-facing teams to develop tools and resources to increase first time success rate for uploaded content.WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? Experience in a Content Operations, Digital Supply Chain or similar role at a label or distributor.Good knowledge of music metadata in a digital distribution context, particularly the iTunes and Spotify style guides.Experience working with content management systems (iTunes Connect and Spotify preferred).Meticulous attention to detail.Passion for a range of musical genres with bonus points for Classical or Bollywood knowledge.

BACKGROUND: Kobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the center of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL.
WHAT DOES COORDINATOR, CONTENT OPERATION: QC audio release submissions in accordance with partner metadata specifications.
Provide structured feedback for client-facing teams when releases fail checks.
Carry out metadata updates as required across the AWAL catalogue.
Contribute to internal Content Operations documentation, including up-to-date DSP best practices and metadata style guide.
Work with client-facing teams to develop tools and resources to increase first time success rate for uploaded content.
WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKIN: Experience in a Content Operations, Digital Supply Chain or similar role at a label or distributor.
Good knowledge of music metadata in a digital distribution context, particularly the iTunes and Spotify style guides.
Experience working with content management systems (iTunes Connect and Spotify preferred).
Meticulous attention to detail.
Passion for a range of musical genres with bonus points for Classical or Bollywood knowledge.

Title: Executive-in-Residence or Assistant Professor, The Business of Creative Enterprises Program (US)
Description: Emerson College seeks an experienced faculty member for a full-time teaching position in our Business of Creative Enterprises (BCE) program.Continue to article...For students intending to become leaders in the creative industries, the Business of Creative Enterprises (BCE) major is the premiere program of its kind, founded on the principle that creativity and ideas are integral strategic components of successful businesses in the creative economy. The appointment begins August 20, 2020.Emerson College is committed to an active, intentional, and ongoing engagement with diversity4in people, in the curriculum, in the co-curriculum, and in the college's intellectual, social, cultural, and geographical communities. Emerson endorses a framework of inclusive excellence, which recognizes that institutional excellence comes from fully engaging with diversity in all aspects of institutional activities. Therefore, we strongly encourage applications from candidates who can demonstrate through their teaching, research, and service that they can contribute to our excellence in this area.The BCE major attracts students with a passion for arts and communication who seek careers leading and managing businesses in the creative economy. Distinct from traditional business majors or arts management programs, the BCE major prepares students to become executives, managers, thought leaders and innovators in the creative enterprises in the following, sectors and beyond: Performing Arts, Music, Film, TV, Tech, Gaming, Media, Theater, Publishing. The BCE curriculum can be viewed here.Depending on the professional expertise or research/academic field of the candidate, this appointment may be made respectively as an Executive-in-Residence (renewable full-time contract) or Assistant Professor (tenure-track). Successful candidates will have expertise in management and business with experience teaching courses such as Business Fundamentals, Finance, Analytics, and/or Strategic Management. Program duties will also include advising students as they matriculate through the major and contributing to the overall curricular and co-curricular development of this relatively new and innovative program.Applicants for an Executive-in-Residence appointment should have previous management experience of at least five (5) years in the creative economy. Assistant professor applicants should be able to show a record of scholarly work addressing some aspect of the creative economy commensurate with this rank. In both cases, candidates are required to show evidence of successful classroom teaching at the university level. Candidates should also be able to demonstrate professional and/or academic experience in teaching or working with diverse populations and in multicultural settings.All successful applicants must have a minimum of a master's degree in a related field.Emerson College is the nation's only four-year institution dedicated exclusively to majors in communication and the arts in a liberal arts context. Its main campus is located in the center of the dynamic multicultural city of Boston, in close proximity to major publishing houses, arts institutions and research centers. The college also has campuses in Los Angeles and the Netherlands. Emerson College enrolls over 4,535 graduate and undergraduate students from more than 759 countries and all 50 states.Please submit a cover letter indicating your professional experience, teaching qualifications and interest in Emerson College, specifically the BCE Program, along with a resume/CV and contact information for three references who will not be contacted without the applicant's written approval. Each material should be saved as a separate PDF or Word doc. and uploaded into the upload section where it requests your CV. Although the upload section just shows “upload CV” – you should upload all materials in this section as the separate documents.Review of applications will begin immediately, and continue until the position is filled.Questions about the position should be directed to the chair of the faculty search committee, BCE Program Director Wes Jackson at wes_jackson@emerson.edu.

Title: Music Programmer (US)
Description: The people here at Apple don't just create products 4 they create the kind of wonder that's revolutionized entire industries.Continue to article...It's the diversity of those people and their ideas that inspires the innovation that runs through everything we do, from amazing technology to industry-leading environmental efforts. Join Apple, and help us leave the world better than we found it.We’re growing and urge you to apply. We Believe the role would place you in a highly-energized environment with some of the most talented creative individuals in the industry where you would be inspired to share your years of experience along with providing extraordinary support for the team.Apple revolutionized personal technology with the introduction of the Macintosh in 1984. Today, Apple leads the world in innovation with iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch and Apple TV. Apple's software platforms provide seamless experiences across all Apple devices and empower people with breakthrough services including the App Store, Apple Music, iTunes, iBooks, Apple Pay and iCloud. Apple's employees are dedicated to making the best products on earth, and to leaving the world better than we found it.Apple's fast-growing global Apple Music business seeks a creatively-minded Jazz music programmer to join its global Editorial team. The successful applicant will work with company leadership to ensure that overall editorial objectives and initiatives are fulfilled. The candidate must have a deep passion for music, is driven and ambitious, highly organized and detail-oriented. This person helps provide the editorial and curatorial voice and vision of iTunes and Apple Music to ensure that our content meets the needs of our audience and our business.Key QualificationsYou have a thorough understanding of the digital content spaceYou have the ability to help build and execute editorial plans, while also tracking projects across multiple divisions with great attention to detail, commitment to delivery and also deadlinesYou have experience in implementing overall an editorial mission to support business objectives across content initiativesYou have a deep passion and proven understanding all eras and styles of Jazz music as well as affiliated genres 4 from classic bop and swing to free improvisation, contemporary pop instrumental, and moreYou have professional experience in working with artists, managers and label professionals. Title: LATAM Regional Sales Manager (LATAM)
Description: About BMAT Continue to article...BMAT started in 2005 when a group of engineers with a passion for music decided to spin-off from an audio research lab in Barcelona. The company started with an idea – to index all the music in the world – and a mission – to better the music eco-system – and a team of nerds eager to learn how to build a successful business. Today the company has grown to more than a hundred people – spread across Barcelona, Buenos Aires, Lima, São Paulo, London, Paris, Oslo, Tel-Aviv, Johannesburg, Singapore, Tokyo and Seoul – that team up to deliver daily 1 million identifications and 3 billion matches to our partners from all over the music industry.Our Culture We love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady). Make her life easier and her future brighter. We apply our scientific, data-infused brains to tell everyone what music has been played – so that creators get the recognition they deserve. We hear everything everywhere and tell everyone who wants to know. ‘What plays around will come back around’ we say. We believe by helping this happen we can make a positive change in the music ecosystem and enjoy talent for another million years.We are a team of pretty decent people who enjoy working with each other. We’re open-minded and well-traveled, coming from all parts of the world. We like coffee, tea, chai, and mate all the same. We’ve got rappers, producers, cello players, DJs, and songwriters. The passion for music, the excitement for technology and Barcelona as a backdrop has proven to make a good combination. We say there is a BMAT touch. We believe it has to do with being passionate, obsessive, nice, generous, honest, self-motivated, entrepreneurial, intrepid, crazy, extraordinary, creative, agile, human, friendly, smart, witty, talented, naive, optimistic, surprising, sensual, and nerdy, but not very hipster (unfortunately).Sales at BMATSales is a core team at the BMAT compound of regional managers, business vice-presidents, and executive directors. Its dynamic is very open, co-operative and no hierarchical. We understand sales as a discovery process through which, based on our expertise, we ask and listen our customers telling us about their challenges and in return we advise them on how we can help them tackle those through innovation.What You Will Be DoingUnderstand the newest needs/requests and problems faced by the industry to propose relevant services.Identifying opportunities by researching the industry and market trends.Defining product strategy together with Global Sales management.Sales service to all industry actors, setup test pilots and grow the business.Spot and answer tender calls.Take the responsibility for LATAM Sales P&L.All About You7 years of experience in international / LATAM sales.10 years of experience in sales with a record of quota over-achievement, preferably in a SaaS environment.The ideal applicant will have deep direct experience in the music industry and a good understanding of international copyrights.Experience managing a sales división within Music publishing/ Production music/ Film & TV/Synch is preferred.Proven track record in Entertainment and Tech industries.Superior analytical and communication skills.Fluency in English and Spanish.Bonus TrackAvailability for a minimum of 3 months training in our Barcelona HQ.This role will require frequent travels within the region.You Can EnjoyFull-time job.Flexibility on working hours.Flat hierarchy.Full autonomy of your dutiesBecoming part of a diverse and multicultural team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry.

Our Culture: We love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady). Make her life easier and her future brighter. We apply our scientific, data-infused brains to tell everyone what music has been played – so that creators get the recognition they deserve. We hear everything everywhere and tell everyone who wants to know. ‘What plays around will come back around’ we say. We believe by helping this happen we can make a positive change in the music ecosystem and enjoy talent for another million years.
Sales at BMAT: Sales is a core team at the BMAT compound of regional managers, business vice-presidents, and executive directors. Its dynamic is very open, co-operative and no hierarchical. We understand sales as a discovery process through which, based on our expertise, we ask and listen our customers telling us about their challenges and in return we advise them on how we can help them tackle those through innovation.
What You Will Be Doing: Understand the newest needs/requests and problems faced by the industry to propose relevant services.
Identifying opportunities by researching the industry and market trends.
Defining product strategy together with Global Sales management.
Sales service to all industry actors, setup test pilots and grow the business.
Spot and answer tender calls.
Take the responsibility for LATAM Sales P&L.
All About You: 7 years of experience in international / LATAM sales.
10 years of experience in sales with a record of quota over-achievement, preferably in a SaaS environment.
The ideal applicant will have deep direct experience in the music industry and a good understanding of international copyrights.
Experience managing a sales división within Music publishing/ Production music/ Film & TV/Synch is preferred.
Proven track record in Entertainment and Tech industries.
Bonus Track: Availability for a minimum of 3 months training in our Barcelona HQ.
This role will require frequent travels within the region.
You Can Enjoy: Becoming part of a diverse and multicultural team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry.

Title: PA / Office Manager (UK)
Description: Leland Music are looking for a PA / Office Manager to join our London Team.Continue to article...This will be an interesting and varied role, supporting a small but close-knit team of music supervisors, in which you can utilise your coordination and people skills to ensure that our office runs seamlessly.  This role will cover: office management; fielding calls / welcoming our guests to the office; detailed administration work; PA duties to the managers; and assistance to the day-to-day work flow of the office.  Although this is not a creative role, we are looking for someone who is as passionate about music and the media arts as we are!Key qualities:Super-organised, helpful and proactiveThorough and methodical, with a keen eye for detailCalm, yet tenaciousEnthusiastic, confident and reliableEssential skills:Confident telephone and Cfront of houseD mannerStrong IT skills with solid experience of working with ExcelNatural flair for organizing and a love of processesExcellent communication skillsDesirable skills:Basic understanding of music copyright2 years’ administration / PA experienceSalary:£22k – £25k, dependent on experienceBenefits:25 days holiday, plus bank holidaysRegular team lunches and social eventsFriendly and peaceful North London officePlease email your CV and covering letter to: liz@lelandmusic.co.uk. Title: Digital Content & Communications Manager (UK)
Description: The BPI is looking to recruit a passionate, creative and strategically driven Digital Content & Communications Manager to work across its digital, social media and press & PR channels.Continue to article...Reporting to the Communications Director, the role will focus on generating content for and managing the BPI's social media and digital output, while also contributing to the BPI's internal and external communications in support of the organisation's communications and public affairs strategy and wider goals.The Digital Content & Communications Manager will in particular be responsible for generating content and managing activity and campaigns across the BPI's social media accounts and digital platforms. The role will support the Communications and other departments as they work to actively promote the BPI and its brands and projects across its various platforms along with the rights, interests and reputation of its members and of the wider recorded music sector.The position is to commence as soon as possible, and offers a competitive salary package (details on application), including full BPI benefits. Based at the BPI's County Hall head office on Westminster Bridge Road, the role would most likely suit a candidate looking to move on from their first professional role.The BPI is committed to diversity in the workplace and equality of opportunity, and applications are invited from candidates of all backgrounds with the relevant skills, aptitude and experience.CVs and supporting letters of application are invited by 15th November 2019, and should be addressed to gennaro.castaldo@bpi.co.uk or Gennaro Castaldo at BPI, Riverside Building, County Hall, Westminster Bridge Road, London, SE1 7JA. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:Planning, creation and implementation of content and regular content calendars.Generating digital/other engaging content in support of BPI activity/campaigns, incl. graphic design/infographics, visual content and editing, written articles/press releases and more.Managing, updating and maintaining the BPI social media channels, website and homepages, and other sites and platforms as required (such as for The BRIT Trust and National Album Day).Creating and distributing regular social and digital analytics and other reports.Ensuring that the BPI has a strong online voice to reflect its industry profile and public affairs objectives, and is engaged in the wider conversation around the business of music.Creating presentations, reports, flyers, advertisements and other digital and written content for BPI departments 3 ensuring this is produced in the correct tone and on-brand for the organisation.Supporting the Communications Director in drafting/approving producing, distributing and 8selling in' comments, announcements and press releases to media, as directed.Generating new and innovative ideas to promote the BPI and support its initiatives including the Music Export Growth Scheme, Insight Sessions, Wisdom Wednesdays and more.Attending events to represent the BPI and to capture content through photography or videography.Supporting BPI-related partners/suppliers in the delivery of digital campaigns such as for the BRIT Awards, BRIT Certified Awards, Mercury Prize and National Album Day, as directed.Providing general administrative support to the Communications department, such as raising purchase orders for invoices and collating/generating media monitoring reports.SKILL REQUIREMENTS:2 years minimum experience working in social media/digital communications and PR 3 most likely suited to an individual moving on from their first professional role.A passion for digital marketing and music and always on top of the latest trends.Strong writing skills to create engaging copy, press releases and long form written content.Experience working across a variety of CMS and social media platforms.Proficient in Adobe Creative Suite (or other graphic design and video editing software).Strong attention to detail.A good eye for creative and attention-grabbing content, 8telling a story' in an engaging way.Understanding/sensitivity to and experience of the public affairs/policy landscape.YOU MUST BE:Passionate about social and digital media and in creating engaging content.A music enthusiast with a handle on the latest trends and an appreciation of popular culture.Personable, principled and with a strong sense of integrity.Organised, confident and results-driven working to high standards and with a focus to deliver on priorities 3 whether working as a self-motivated individual or collaboratively as part of a team.Committed to developing your skills and talents and with the potential to grow into the role and in due course into senior leadership positions.A keen communicator and relationship builder 3 internally, among members and externally.Imaginative in developing creative solutions, but with the energy and tenacity to implement any new ideas and thinking as part of overall strategy.Versatile in your approach 3 able to navigate different ways around an opportunity or issue 3 finding more than one way to achieve a goal or to 8tell a story' as a communicator.Sensitive at all times to your position as a communications professional representing your employer and the industry in which it operates.About the BPI (British Phonographic Industry) Promoting British MusicThe BPI is the UK record labels' association that promotes British music and champions the UK's recorded music industry 3 the world's fourth largest and the biggest exporter of recorded music in the world after the US. The BPI helps to safeguard the rights of its members and of all the artists, performers and record label members of collecting body PPL 3 who collectively create around 99 per cent of all legitimate sales and streams of music in the UK. The BPI's membership consists of well over 400 independent labels and the UK's three 8major' companies, which together account for up to 85 per cent of legitimate domestic music consumption.UK artists account for one in eight artist albums sold worldwide. The BPI helps to promote British music overseas through numerous trade missions as well as through the Music Exports Growth Scheme, which since 2014 has awarded £3.8 million in DIT government funding in support of 242 music projects benefitting mainly independently-signed artists. The BPI provides valuable insights, training and networking with its free masterclasses and presentations and through its Insight Sessions, Wisdom Wednesday events, and its authoritative yearbook and reports.The BPI certifies The BRIT Certified Awards 3 the iconic Platinum, Gold and Silver Awards Programme; co-owns the Official Charts; owns and organises The BRIT Awards with Mastercard 3 which, through the BPI's charitable arm, The BRIT Trust, has helped to raise over £25m for music education and wellbeing charities, including the BRIT School, ELAM, Nordoff-Robbins music therapy, Music Support, Key4Life and Mind. The BPI is also home to the Hyundai Mercury Prize.www.bpi.co.uk

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:: Planning, creation and implementation of content and regular content calendars.
Generating digital/other engaging content in support of BPI activity/campaigns, incl. graphic design/infographics, visual content and editing, written articles/press releases and more.
Managing, updating and maintaining the BPI social media channels, website and homepages, and other sites and platforms as required (such as for The BRIT Trust and National Album Day).
Creating and distributing regular social and digital analytics and other reports.
Ensuring that the BPI has a strong online voice to reflect its industry profile and public affairs objectives, and is engaged in the wider conversation around the business of music.
Creating presentations, reports, flyers, advertisements and other digital and written content for BPI departments 3 ensuring this is produced in the correct tone and on-brand for the organisation.
Supporting the Communications Director in drafting/approving producing, distributing and 8selling in' comments, announcements and press releases to media, as directed.
Generating new and innovative ideas to promote the BPI and support its initiatives including the Music Export Growth Scheme, Insight Sessions, Wisdom Wednesdays and more.
Attending events to represent the BPI and to capture content through photography or videography.
Supporting BPI-related partners/suppliers in the delivery of digital campaigns such as for the BRIT Awards, BRIT Certified Awards, Mercury Prize and National Album Day, as directed.
Providing general administrative support to the Communications department, such as raising purchase orders for invoices and collating/generating media monitoring reports.
SKILL REQUIREMENTS:: 2 years minimum experience working in social media/digital communications and PR 3 most likely suited to an individual moving on from their first professional role.
A passion for digital marketing and music and always on top of the latest trends.
Strong writing skills to create engaging copy, press releases and long form written content.
Experience working across a variety of CMS and social media platforms.
Proficient in Adobe Creative Suite (or other graphic design and video editing software).
A good eye for creative and attention-grabbing content, 8telling a story' in an engaging way.
Understanding/sensitivity to and experience of the public affairs/policy landscape.
YOU MUST BE:: Passionate about social and digital media and in creating engaging content.
A music enthusiast with a handle on the latest trends and an appreciation of popular culture.
Personable, principled and with a strong sense of integrity.
Organised, confident and results-driven working to high standards and with a focus to deliver on priorities 3 whether working as a self-motivated individual or collaboratively as part of a team.
Committed to developing your skills and talents and with the potential to grow into the role and in due course into senior leadership positions.
A keen communicator and relationship builder 3 internally, among members and externally.
Imaginative in developing creative solutions, but with the energy and tenacity to implement any new ideas and thinking as part of overall strategy.
Versatile in your approach 3 able to navigate different ways around an opportunity or issue 3 finding more than one way to achieve a goal or to 8tell a story' as a communicator.
Sensitive at all times to your position as a communications professional representing your employer and the industry in which it operates.
About the BPI (British Phonographic Indu: The BPI is the UK record labels' association that promotes British music and champions the UK's recorded music industry 3 the world's fourth largest and the biggest exporter of recorded music in the world after the US. The BPI helps to safeguard the rights of its members and of all the artists, performers and record label members of collecting body PPL 3 who collectively create around 99 per cent of all legitimate sales and streams of music in the UK. The BPI's membership consists of well over 400 independent labels and the UK's three 8major' companies, which together account for up to 85 per cent of legitimate domestic music consumption.

Title: Publishing Relations Manager (US)
Description: Publishing Relations ManagerContinue to article...We seek an outstanding publishing relations manager to join our global music publishing team. You will develop, build and maintain Spotify's relationships with the publishing industry across the world, creating opportunities for partnership and creative services. Above all, your work will impact the way the world experiences music.What you'll doIdentify, analyse, and execute opportunities to drive growth for Spotify and the publishing industry.Develop creative services and tools for Spotify to provide to publishing companies.Provide business strategy, framework, guidance and insight to different areas of Spotify's publishing program.Optimise existing product initiatives and support new areas of exploration in publishing.Maintain, develop and grow Spotify's partnerships with publishers and industry bodies.Communicate across internal stakeholders on behalf of publishers, societies and other industry bodies to maximize the value and performance of these partnerships.Educate and advocate on behalf of Spotify to publishers, societies and the publishing community.Troubleshoot and manage key relationships to maintain active dialogue with partners.Work cross-functionally with licensing, legal, Creator R&D, artist & label services, original content, editorial, marketing, and finance.Who you areAt least six (6) years of experience working with publishers, PROs/Collecting Societies and/or songwriters. You have knowledge of digital music and streaming; key stakeholders, business models, market dynamics and opportunities.You have a deep understanding of music publishing, from creative development to digital music rights and copyright admin.You are experienced at working across different cultures and timezones.You have the ability to succeed in an entrepreneurial, ambiguous, R&D start-up environment.You are a confident and articulate presenter, both to small and large audiences.You have a history of self motivated performance with the ability and tenacity to execute.You have strong personal organization and project management abilities.You are a team player who recognizes that we are scaling fast, and can listen to and voice viewpoints internally.You have a high level of comfort interfacing with all levels of management (externally and internally) and to communicate cross-functionally in a structured and clear manner to influence outcomes.You have an excellent network of contacts in the publishing & songwriting worlds.You are fluent in both written and spoken English. Fluency in another language is a plus.

What you'll do: Identify, analyse, and execute opportunities to drive growth for Spotify and the publishing industry.
Develop creative services and tools for Spotify to provide to publishing companies.
Provide business strategy, framework, guidance and insight to different areas of Spotify's publishing program.
Optimise existing product initiatives and support new areas of exploration in publishing.
Maintain, develop and grow Spotify's partnerships with publishers and industry bodies.
Communicate across internal stakeholders on behalf of publishers, societies and other industry bodies to maximize the value and performance of these partnerships.
Educate and advocate on behalf of Spotify to publishers, societies and the publishing community.
Troubleshoot and manage key relationships to maintain active dialogue with partners.
Work cross-functionally with licensing, legal, Creator R&D, artist & label services, original content, editorial, marketing, and finance.
Who you are: At least six (6) years of experience working with publishers, PROs/Collecting Societies and/or songwriters. You have knowledge of digital music and streaming; key stakeholders, business models, market dynamics and opportunities.
You have a deep understanding of music publishing, from creative development to digital music rights and copyright admin.
You are experienced at working across different cultures and timezones.
You have the ability to succeed in an entrepreneurial, ambiguous, R&D start-up environment.
You are a confident and articulate presenter, both to small and large audiences.
You have a history of self motivated performance with the ability and tenacity to execute.
You have strong personal organization and project management abilities.
You are a team player who recognizes that we are scaling fast, and can listen to and voice viewpoints internally.
You have a high level of comfort interfacing with all levels of management (externally and internally) and to communicate cross-functionally in a structured and clear manner to influence outcomes.
You have an excellent network of contacts in the publishing & songwriting worlds.
You are fluent in both written and spoken English. Fluency in another language is a plus.

Title: Digital Analyst (US)
Description: 300 Entertainment is one of the most prominent independent record labels currently.Continue to article...The digital team at 300 Entertainment is a full-service marketing team, dedicated to building artist careers through the intersection of music and technology.We are responsible for all digital strategy and marketing for all artists on the roster, acting as artists partners across music, touring, and all other aspects of the artist career. Through creative marketing campaigns, social and streaming communities, with an eye towards evolving the way that fans listen to, participate in, and consume music  related products. We create, market and optimize content, develop and implement CRM/fan acquisition strategies, and work with new, engaging digital platforms and startups. Additionally, we work directly with all social and streaming partners to strengthen our marketing ideas, strategy plans, and aim to be strategic partners.What's the job? As data becomes not only an ever-abundant resource, but an ever-evolving mechanism for painting a picture on how consumers react to music, the Digital Analyst role at 300E looks to take the data that's surrounding our music and artists and build the book that narrates for each of our artists where their careers have built to, and directions on how to keep building on their journey.This person will look to break down the core question for a record label 3 how do we break an artist across the world?What this person must love and aspire to do is to be able to take problems and questions surrounding our artists, and using data work to understand the consumer behavior around how fans connect with music, whether that be information from radio, sales, streaming, touring, merch, and most importantly social media interaction and reactivity.Why would you want this job? What this job allows for is an opportunity to take an analytical mindset that revels in making data make sense conceptually to both themselves and everyone around them, and do it in the extremely exciting and fast-paced music industry. The job puts you in a position to be one of the closest, if not the closest, person to the story of an artist's progression, and as such this job puts you in a position to directly be the person that tells this story.Where we need you to hone your skills:  Collecting and translating data into both actionable insights and easily read-able and digestible reporting for all departments of 300E Providing an empathetic and economic review approach to data, allowing your analysis to lead and dictate strategy deployment that could range from overall advertising budget spending decisions to specific marketing campaigns centered around specific geographic territories. Discerning how to best use and apply a multitude of analysis tools, both as stand-along tools and also as tools that work together to paint â¨o In turn, this also requires prioritizing various facets of the tools at hand to make sure you're reviewing the data you need, and not the data that you don't Analyze the effects of various influences that surround music to understand how it affects the consumption of our artists â¨o When analyzing consumption, this doesn't only relate to sales and streaming consumption, but the understanding that a person's interaction with an artist via any platform (either actively or passively) is consumption of an artist, and using that to strategize on how to keep a consumer coming back once they've consumed is key.â¨Ideal candidates bring the following to the table:  Impeccable organizational skills Impeccable communication skills (both written and verbal) Ability to understand prioritization of tasks and assignments Maneuver-ability to accomplish goals and tasks within an extremely fast-paced working â¨environment An appreciation and knack for empathetic problem solving Looking to be a self-starter and have autonomy with respect to working tasks A love for the internet, technology, social media, and musicSome of the general prerequisites we'd like in a candidate:  1-2 years experience Familiarity with Nielsen SoundScan/MusicConnect, Mediabase, and Spotify analytics â¨tools Background in analytics and writing

Title: Assistant, Label Integrations (UK)
Description: We're looking for a Label Integrations Assistant to join the AWAL team to support the team in the day to day of onboarding priority artist and label catalogues.Continue to article...This is an office-based role that will require a hardworking, detail-oriented and organised individual.The role is ideal if you have previous experience in distribution, production, publishing or at a label in an operational role. But more importantly, you are a detail-oriented and flexible team player comfortable with rapidly evolving priorities, with very strong written and verbal communication skills. Also, If you have an interest in knowing more about digital metadata, physical logistics, neighbouring rights, synch and how Kobalt's proprietary system works.BackgroundKobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the center of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL.Whether an artist is looking for the best digital distribution or a truly global campaign, AWAL has become the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers. This has created a high-quality path for artists to remain outside of the traditional label system while maintaining ownership of their masters.Leading artists who have already chosen AWAL include Lauv, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Tom Misch, Omar Apollo and Little Simz, as well as labels like Glassnote, B-Unique, 30th Century and Chess Club.We deliver audio and video to all of the key platforms using our bespoke supply chain system, powered by Kobalt's market leading technology, making thousands of deliveries per week.We're currently 600 people based across 13 offices and we're still growing.WHAT DOES A LABEL INTEGRATIONS ASSISTANT AT KOBALT DO? Assisting the Integrations Senior Manager with project work which may involve internal and client facing communication and support when required.Review, quality-check and cleanse digital, physical and neighbouring rights catalogue metadata.Review catalogue audio, artwork and video assets for DSP compatibility to the highest industry standards.Research physical catalogue stock locations, levels and parts availability and own logistics processes during stock transfers.Establish & build upon good relationships across multiple business areas when required.Identify and highlight potential integration obstacles to Integrations Senior Manager.Organise meetings, take meeting notes and distribute action points.WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Previous experience in an operational department at a label, publisher, distributor or other music organisation.Strong knowledge of music metadata & systems desired 3 knowledge of iTunes style-guide is highly desirable.Proactive self-starter able to manage own workload and support busy department.Experience working with digital audio, video and artwork assets.Physical production and stock management is desirable.Highly proficient in spoken and written English.Strong working knowledge of Excel, Google Sheets, repertoire management systems and DSP's.Good interpersonal skills and ability to communicate effectively across the business and with external clients when required.Obsessive attention to detail and an ability to follow instructions.Numerate and IT literate

Title: Global Head of Podcast Business Affairs (US)
Description: The Spotify Content Licensing team is seeking a highly experienced professional to be based in its Los Angeles (preferred) or New York offices.Continue to article...This role will be the business affairs leader in charge of securing a global slate of original and exclusively licensed podcasts.The ideal candidate will have 20 years experience working with the entertainment industry and a rich background in digital content. This is a leadership position that will play a central role in helping Spotify achieve its vision of becoming the world's top audio network.What you'll doYou will manage a multi-time zone, global team tasked with securing agreements with talent agencies and production companies to create a slate of hundreds of owned and exclusively licensed podcasts.You will serve as a primary negotiator for flagship deals and support your team on second tier deals.You will work directly with Spotify's Chief Content Officer and the Content leadership team.Your work will significantly impact the podcast content strategy and Spotify's efforts to grow audience and monetization.Working with internal legal, accounting and other teams, you will ensure that all programs are in alignment with Spotify's fiduciary and operational best practices.You will gather data, analytics and insights from other internal groups to be applied to the development of new initiatives.Who you areYou are a superior manager with a track record of attracting and growing talent, managing a global team and managing a deal pipeline of several hundred concurrent projects.You have at least twenty (20) years work experience in entertainment, with at least fifteen (15) years working on the licensing of video, audio and/or podcast content. We prefer candidates who have some experience working in a technology company.You have an exceptional network of contacts in the entertainment industry, including relationships with talent agents, producers and senior industry executives.You have a track record of recommending and popularizing new business models.You will be a champion of Spotify's values (Innovative, Collaborative, Sincere, Passionate, Playful) and demonstrate a Growth Mindset.You are a self-starter with a commercial mindset.You are a skilled negotiator willing and able to close deals, having closed multiple agreements with commitments of $20 million or more.You must be familiar with the complexities related to audio, video and interactive technology, production cycles and IP rights management.You have strong analytical skills and the ability to build and explain financial models.You possess the ability to work in a fast-paced, all-hands-on-deck, start-up environment that requires efficient communications with cross-functional teams.You deeply understand various types of intellectual property deal types (licensing, windowing, co-production, etc.).You have an in-depth understanding of the roles played across the media company value chain (production companies, technology companies, talent agencies, record labels), as well as the current and emerging cross-media platforms (social, broadcast, radio, etc.)You have a BA Degree. An MBA or other higher education is preferred.You are willing to travel (at some times extensively) and work across distant time zones.We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. We strongly believe that diversity of experience, perspectives, and background will lead to a better environment for our employees and a better product for our users and our creators. This is something we value deeply and we encourage everyone to come be a part of changing the way the world listens to music.

What you'll do: You will manage a multi-time zone, global team tasked with securing agreements with talent agencies and production companies to create a slate of hundreds of owned and exclusively licensed podcasts.
You will serve as a primary negotiator for flagship deals and support your team on second tier deals.
You will work directly with Spotify's Chief Content Officer and the Content leadership team.
Your work will significantly impact the podcast content strategy and Spotify's efforts to grow audience and monetization.
Working with internal legal, accounting and other teams, you will ensure that all programs are in alignment with Spotify's fiduciary and operational best practices.
You will gather data, analytics and insights from other internal groups to be applied to the development of new initiatives.
Who you are: You are a superior manager with a track record of attracting and growing talent, managing a global team and managing a deal pipeline of several hundred concurrent projects.

You have at least twenty (20) years work experience in entertainment, with at least fifteen (15) years working on the licensing of video, audio and/or podcast content. We prefer candidates who have some experience working in a technology company.
You have an exceptional network of contacts in the entertainment industry, including relationships with talent agents, producers and senior industry executives.
You have a track record of recommending and popularizing new business models.
You will be a champion of Spotify's values (Innovative, Collaborative, Sincere, Passionate, Playful) and demonstrate a Growth Mindset.
You are a self-starter with a commercial mindset.
You are a skilled negotiator willing and able to close deals, having closed multiple agreements with commitments of $20 million or more.
You must be familiar with the complexities related to audio, video and interactive technology, production cycles and IP rights management.
You have strong analytical skills and the ability to build and explain financial models.
You possess the ability to work in a fast-paced, all-hands-on-deck, start-up environment that requires efficient communications with cross-functional teams.
You deeply understand various types of intellectual property deal types (licensing, windowing, co-production, etc.).

You have an in-depth understanding of the roles played across the media company value chain (production companies, technology companies, talent agencies, record labels), as well as the current and emerging cross-media platforms (social, broadcast, radio, etc.)
You have a BA Degree. An MBA or other higher education is preferred.
You are willing to travel (at some times extensively) and work across distant time zones.

Title: Music Supervisor (UK)
Description: Major Tom are one of the UK's leading Music Supervision companies.Continue to article...Established in 2009, we provide a variety of creative and professional music services to the commercial, Film, TV and trailer industries. We work from an uncompromisingly creative perspective with an obsession for originality and high production values. We have a rare opportunity for an experienced music supervisor to join our London based team.The successful candidate will be responsible for working within a team of highly experienced music supervisors, producers and composers in delivering first class music searches, licensing and original composition services to our UK and international advertising clients.A minimum of 5 year's experience working within music supervision for commercials or within a licensing division of a record company or publisher is essential as well as proven track record of successful placements and / or composition productions.You will have a thorough knowledge of all aspects of the music industry and a deep understanding of music licensing alongside an impeccable knowledge and passion in all genres of music. Title: Digital Marketing Agent (ES)
Description: Sonosuite’s mission is to empower the independent music sector. We provide our proprietary technology, depth and breadth of industry expertise alongside a carefully curated selection of leading edge partners to artists, labels and distributors worldwide.Continue to article...Founded in 2006 as an independent digital distributor, we have developed a market leading digital asset management and delivery platform augmented by sales, reporting and analytics tools alongside comprehensive rights and royalties administration.We feel that what’s good for the independent sector is good for Sonosuite and that approach informs everything we do. We believe in sharing insights, knowledge and data for the greater good of the music business.We’ve crystalised our philosophy into the ‘The 4 Cs’: Capture – Working with global, local and ‘glo-cal’ players in the value chain Connect – A trusted conduit between creators and digital music services Collect – The data, insights and most importantly royalties that make for a healthy business Care – We are coaches, mentors and guides. We’re looking for talented individuals to help us ensure we capture, connect and collect for everyone that we care for. Our team is our most important assets. We believe real success starts and ends with hiring the very best people, empowering them to get the job done, and making sure our team has a whole lot of fun along the way!Who You Are  You are not only a music-lover, you have a keen interest in the mechanics of the industry and are excited about innovations in how we create, market and consume music. You have a finger on the pulse of trends, key players and challenges. You’ll be an enthusiastic consumer of trade press and will need the ability to parse this information into creative campaigns and initiatives you’ve designed to meet objectives. Process oriented and methodical but results driven and empathetic. We need people who understand and are enthusiastic about the direct correlation between their efforts and the impact that has for artists, labels and the wider music business community. You are an excellent writer and can demonstrate previous experience of designing and implementing a successful inbound marketing plan, processes and infrastructure. You’re a creative thinker and writer in both English and Spanish with an understanding of marketing localization. You’re adaptable, self-motivated with a positive disposition, team spirit and strong work ethic. You view your job description as a foundation to build on, rather than a set of restrictions. You are conscientious with an eye for detail but capable of thinking bigger-picture. You love technology. You are data-driven with the ability to translate business objectives to metrics and measurement methods, interpret data, derive insights, and create an actionable plan. As a competent professional, you are able to grasp B2B marketing trends and generate creative ideas. You like Seeking out industry best practices, and proactively incorporate relevant, creative ideas. You are an expert with Google Adwords and have previous experience with social media ads (Facebook / Instagram / Twitter / LinkedIin)What You Will Do  Write and manage a rich content/editorial calendar that attracts a qualified audience (including blog posts, whitepapers, ebooks, reports, webinars, infographics, etc.) Optimise and define the SEO strategy in content creation. Execute integrated marketing campaigns, including content creation, scheduling, and campaign management. Manage website priorities including ongoing SEO, creative/design/copy, campaign landing pages, blog presence, and market-level property search experiences. Propose, translate and develop attractive content for newsletters levering on existing material – testing new email subjects and topics to continuously optimise performance (open rates, CTR). This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Title: Chief Revenue Officer (US)
Description: Downtown Music Holdings is a global company that owns, manages, and develops businesses with a vision for a more equitable and innovative music ecosystem.Continue to article...With operations across North America, Europe, Asia, Australia, and Latin America, we are the world's leading provider of end-to-end services to artists, songwriters, labels, music publishers and other rights holders. Through our portfolio of companies — Downtown Music Publishing, Songtrust® and the AVL Digital Group — we manage millions of music copyrights, with a catalog that spans more than 100 years of popular music and the single largest independent sound recording catalog in the industry.Our integrated platforms help democratize global music rights management and simplify the distribution, monetization, and promotion of creative works.As the primary corporate officer responsible for all revenue-generating processes, this role provides an unprecedented opportunity to transform the music industry and shift the balance of power for independent rights holders globally. You will oversee a licensing team working across all relevant subsidiaries (e.g. Downtown Music Publishing, Songtrust, CD Baby,® AdRev®, DashGoS M , Soundrop®). The role covers multiple rights types (including both song copyrights and sound recordings) and will be responsible for devising the group-wide strategy of our licensing, catalog marketing and income tracking across all major pay sources. The CRO is also responsible for the company’s global digital licensing strategy, focusing on the exploitation of our comprehensive catalog of owned and administered song copyrights and sound recordings through various streaming music partnerships and new music initiatives (fitness apps, social media, etc.).You will: Optimize integration and alignment between revenue-related functions, including licensing negotiations, catalog marketing, income tracking and analysis, across all company business units. Manage the digital licensing team and collaborate with division leaders on all licensing and fulfillment strategy, including enforcement and audits. Own the company’s global digital licensing strategy through various streaming music partnerships and new music initiatives. Strengthen relationships with the Group's key digital partners, including DSPs and licensing organizations. Educate key partners as to the scale, professionalism and values of the enterprise. Serve as the key business owner and point of contact when specific licensing conflicts â¨or copyright issues cannot be addressed at the business unit level. Drive visibility on DSP related issues at the Group's C-level. Lead negotiation of new deals and manage renewals of existing deals in coordination with division heads and digital partnership managers at each of the relevant subsidiaries. Develop a digital strategy for the Group taking into account the unique culture and on-going day-to-day operational realities of each unit. Description: Imagine what you could do here. At Apple, new ideas have a way of becoming extraordinary products, services, and customer experiences very quickly. You bring passion and dedication to your job and there’s no telling what you could accomplish.Continue to article...Apple is in search of an experienced Digital Marketing Analyst to join the Apple Services Digital Marketing team in Culver City, Los Angeles.
