West One Music Group operates globally across all time zones, so flexibility in service of the entire global operation will be necessary at this level.The person we are looking for in this role will have excellent relationship skills, is fully qualified as an accountant and who can operate with the appropriate boundaries for a senior finance person and who also knows what it is to participate and collaborate with a wide array of stakeholders in a successful music production and publishing company.The Finance function of West One Music Group reaches into each and every part of our integrated company activities 3 so reporting, management accounting, royalty accounting and analysis and commercially driven financial feedback is of utmost importance.In addition to the above finance functions, you'll also need to be able to commit to:Taking responsibility for all company secretarial administration, preparing final accounts and corporation tax computations, liaising with the companies' auditors, accountants and financial advisors.Dealing with all confidential HR and payroll administration in partnership and continual open communication with the Global Head of HR. Title: Social Media Manager, Dance (AUT)
Description: In this role, you'll take the lead on curating our social media properties for dance. Your key responsibility will be to ensure that our dance priorities are communicated across our social properties creatively and optimised for every platform. The goal is to engage our audience and grow it even further in dance.Continue to article...RESPONSIBILITIESAreas that play to your strengths (all the responsibilities we’ll trust you with):DEVELOP A GLOBAL STRATEGYYou'll define an audience reach strategy to align with company goals. Your goal is to grow audiences and engage users within the dance verticals. You'll define a global strategy across Red Bull Dance properties and work with our markets in defining publishing flow and unified tonality. Furthermore, you'll set-up cross platform release strategies and build a clean content workflow. You'll align and communicate all social posting around key event, artists and projects.MANAGE SOCIALYou'll oversee the global social media presence for our dance focused verticals. You'll be responsible for the Red Bull Dance channels which include: Red Bull Dance (YouTube and IG), Red Bull BC One (YouTube, IG, Twitter and FB), Red Bull Dance Your Style (FB) and Red Bull Dance Connect (YouTube). You'll guide the editorial tone, voice, and communication and ensure that we speak to endemic audiences credibly, as well as to main-stream audiences to bolster community growth.SOCIAL CONTENTYou'll ensure that our channels have ample content to promote around our key projects, artists, and events. You'll also be tasked to develop and deliver user-focused Facebook and Instagram Live & non-live stories around them.You'll monitor the performance of all content within the dance channels to ensure that we are optimizing reach potential. Communicating analytics data to all key stakeholders behind the event or project will be another task of yours.EXPERIENCEYour areas of knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role:3 years of experience publishing content to big brand Social Media channelsDeep knowledge of social optimisation across key channels (YouTube, Twitter, FB & IG)Strong understanding and interest in DancePrior experience with video production & editing tools (such as Lightroom, Premier, AfterEffects, Final Cut, etc.)Strong understanding of digital products, internet trends, video consumption patterns, and social media formats &platform algorithmsResilient, passionate, structured and well-organized personFluent in English

You'll define a global strategy across Red Bull Dance properties and work with our markets in defining publishing flow and unified tonality. Furthermore, you'll set-up cross platform release strategies and build a clean content workflow. You'll align and communicate all social posting around key event, artists and projects.
MANAGE SOCIAL: You'll oversee the global social media presence for our dance focused verticals. You'll be responsible for the Red Bull Dance channels which include: Red Bull Dance (YouTube and IG), Red Bull BC One (YouTube, IG, Twitter and FB), Red Bull Dance Your Style (FB) and Red Bull Dance Connect (YouTube). You'll guide the editorial tone, voice, and communication and ensure that we speak to endemic audiences credibly, as well as to main-stream audiences to bolster community growth.

Deep knowledge of social optimisation across key channels (YouTube, Twitter, FB & IG)

platform algorithms

Title: Global Music Communication Manager, Content & Artists (AUT)
Description: Our mission at Red Bull is to give wings to artists and music scenes around the world.Continue to article...The scope and scale of the music program is vast and as Music Communication Manager you'll play a strong role in communicating what we do in the realm of music to the world.RESPONSIBILITIESAreas that play to your strengths (All the responsibilities we’ll trust you with):MUSIC AUDIENCEYour role will require you to understand the wide variety of music audiences and their media consumption behaviours. You will identify ways to maximize the impact of our artist and artist content projects working with your HQ colleagues and the Red Bull country network. You'll advise upon and implement activation strategies for our projects, provide regular updates on audience development, support and provide countries with guidance on their projects and develop education initiatives to increase specialised communications knowledge and competency across the Red Bull network.EARNED MEDIAIn this role, you'll lead the earned media promotion of music and artist-based content projects including digital, moving image and audio products and some event related projects. You'll be responsible for developing and managing the earned communication strategies for these projects, as well as project managing the development, launch and activation of the projects in collaboration with the content, programming, social, marketing and artist teams.PROJECT MANAGEMENTYou will manage the activations of artist and music content projects in collaboration with your counterparts, defining messaging and media angles around these projects, leading the earned media outreach with country communication teams, identifying opportunities for social influencer outreach and collaborating with the social media team to meet objectives on assigned projects.COLLABORATIONBy working closely with the insights team, you'll analyse and evaluate projects and provide meaningful learnings to the business. You'll establish strong collaboration with markets, to understand local priorities and needs. Furthermore, you'll evaluate local market communication plans, identify local optimization potentials and global release opportunities. Translating results and learnings from release plans into long term strategies and educational resources will be another focus of yours.EXPERIENCEYour areas of knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role:3 years' of experience in a music-based communication or PR roleExperience working with music artists and entitiesClear understanding of global entertainment, music and culture industry 3 including best practices around audio and video campaignsIdeally relationships in the global music and entertainment media landscapeSuperior project management skillsStrong understanding of audience profiling, able to build relationships & to lead a network of consultants, agencies and counterpartsWell organized team player with a strong editorial and media understandingUniversity degreeFluent in EnglishTravel up to: 10%

You'll advise upon and implement activation strategies for our projects, provide regular updates on audience development, support and provide countries with guidance on their projects and develop education initiatives to increase specialised communications knowledge and competency across the Red Bull network.

COLLABORATION: By working closely with the insights team, you'll analyse and evaluate projects and provide meaningful learnings to the business. Furthermore, you'll evaluate local market communication plans, identify local optimization potentials and global release opportunities. Translating results and learnings from release plans into long term strategies and educational resources will be another focus of yours.

Experience working with music artists and entities

Title: Global Content Programming Manager, Music (AUT)
Description: In this role, you will create strategic content programming plans to maximize the growth of and engagement with our key target audience for music. For that you will collaborate with countries, align with our internal music audience team, and have an eye on analytics.Continue to article...Areas that play to your strengthsSET THE STRATEGYYour responsibilities will include defining a content programming strategy to reach a global music audience and establishing strong alignments and collaborations between channels & content creation teams to ensure release strategy success. Think team breakfasts, weekly socials, and company days out.

Title: Business minded Data Analyst / BI Engineer (US)
Description: Location: Los Angeles, California or Denver, ColoradoContinue to article...Founded in 2004 as the principal source of music for DJs, Beatport today is the worldwide home of electronic music for DJs, producers, and their fans. The Beatport Store offers music in premium digital formats and provides unique music discovery tools created for and by DJs. Each week, Beatport’s music collection is refreshed with hundreds of exclusive tracks by the world’s top electronic music artists. Beatport has offices in Denver, Los Angeles and Berlin.About the Job:As a BI Engineer at Beatport, you will be a member of the Business Intelligence team, part of the Business Operations department. You will be responsible for providing actionable insights and analytical support to our business, product, marketing and finance teams. You will handle data requests from Beatport stakeholders as well as ensure the quality of the underlying data. You will also coordinate with Beatport stakeholders to identify and develop reporting requirements.Beatport operates a download store and a subscription service with its own data and business analysis needs and has ambitious plan to develop its platform. Our Data Analyst must be business minded and passionate about diving into and making sense of large, diverse datasets.We are looking for someone who:Has a minimum of four years experience in a data analytics or similar fieldHas a Bachelor / Master degree in analytical discipline e.g. Maths, Physics, Computer Science or equivalentIs eager to learn about various ways to analyze our business notably market analysis, user behavior, customer cohort and churn rate, product KPIs, A/B testingIs proficient with SQL, enjoys scripting and working with data – ability to produce complex queries optimized for performanceIs detail oriented, care about the way data are presented: creating graphs, reports and presentations of analytical findings as well as creating clear and maintainable automated dashboardsCan develop insights and summarize data to all levels of managementProficient in MS Excel / Google spreadsheets, web analytics tools like Google Analytics, data visualization tools (similar to Looker, Tableau)Can find ways to gather data from various websites, APIs, spreadsheets, etc.Can support Digital Marketing: customer journey analysis, marketing channel deep dive (performance)Is a good communicator and enjoys working with teamsIt would be great if you:Have worked in a similar role for an entertainment / media / music companyHave a strong interest in the dance music and the music industry as a wholeCan work in at least one scripting language (Python, R, etc)Have some Web development experienceHave some Project / Product management experienceEnjoy electronic dance musicTo apply:Please send a resume, and a short note introducing yourself and why this position appeals to you.

Title: Copyright Assistant, Italian speaking (UK)
Description: Joining our growing Publishing Copyright department in Kobalt’s London office, the primary role of the Copyright Assistant will be to support the team with managing our catalogue in Italy by ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at the local collection society.Continue to article...The successful candidate will need to have attention to detail and research skills as well as excellent communication skills when communicating internally and with external sources and can deal with pressure but remain optimistic and persistent to complete tasks accurately and efficiently.Founded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We're currently 600 people based across 10 offices and we're still growing.WHAT DOES A COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT AT KOBALT DO?Ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at the collection society SIAEDealing with general enquiries regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreementsHandling song disputes and duplicate claimsProcessing new works and agreements as the company acquires rights to new cataloguesProcessing royalty statements received from collection societiesAnalysing the royalties received to identify possible underpayment or delayed payment, and working with the societies to resolve these issuesYou may be asked to take on extra responsibilities 3 please be aware that the above list is not exhaustiveWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? High level of written and spoken Italian & English is essentialExcellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to audienceTrack record of excellent organization and prioritisation skillsStrong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-setHigh level of numeracyBasic Excel skills are preferred but not essentialPrevious admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable.Most important is that you are ambitious and flexible and can adapt to a growing and changing department!

WHAT DOES A COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT AT KOBAL: Ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at the collection society SIAE
Dealing with general enquiries regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreements
Handling song disputes and duplicate claims
Processing new works and agreements as the company acquires rights to new catalogues
Processing royalty statements received from collection societies
Analysing the royalties received to identify possible underpayment or delayed payment, and working with the societies to resolve these issues
You may be asked to take on extra responsibilities 3 please be aware that the above list is not exhaustive
WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKIN: High level of written and spoken Italian & English is essential
Excellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to audience
Track record of excellent organization and prioritisation skills
Strong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-set
High level of numeracy
Basic Excel skills are preferred but not essential
Previous admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable.

Title: Production Manager (UK)
Description: About AceContinue to article...Ace Records is one of the leading reissue record companies and produces great sounding, well-packaged releases. We work with music repertoire experts, designers with a keen eye for the right period look and highly-skilled audio engineers creating CD, vinyl and digital releases each month.Your role at AceYour primary role will be to manage the production of all physical products released on the Ace group of labels.RequirementsA strong awareness of deadlines, good organisational, administrative and analytical skills and a keen eye for detail are all essential.An ability and desire to drive deadlines on a regular monthly release schedule3-5 years of previous Production experience in the music industry, preferably at a record label.Good communication and interpersonal skills.Basic image editing skills and a knowledge and understanding of the repertoire we release would also be advantageous.What you'll be doing:Lead the weekly scheduling meeting attended by the A&R, digital, licensing, sales and marketing departments. Pay heed to all requirements raised in the meeting, such as licensing deadlines and promotional plans, and organise production parts accordingly. Issue regular deadlines, chasers and updates to all.Work closely with the project compilers, designers, manufacturers and licensees to deliver an agreed number of newly-originated physical packages each month.Processes will include track matching against previous releases, chasing in and entering ISRCs and licensing credits, researching and entering label copy and monthly PPL coding. To commission and edit sleeve notes, and gather all other relevant information and parts for the release, including sourcing and scanning photographs (and obtaining the necessary permissions for usage) and memorabilia.Once the cover photo or image has been selected, choose the designer and give them the cover brief. Organising masters for releases 3 liaising with compilers, labels and studios to ensure sources are the correct versions and the best quality.Once parts are assembled: proof-read all materials and get this checked by a second person. Then put all elements that will be on the product together: all text, logos, memorabilia, photos etc and send to the designer, ensuring they use the chosen cover design and making any necessary tweaks. Secure necessary licensing approval on artwork.Order and manage the approval of test-pressings and – working with our mastering studio – ensure audio is approved and uploaded to the factory on time. It will be your ultimate responsibility that all parts for manufacture are assembled and release dates are met.Organise replacement parts for back catalogue items where they have been lost or damaged. Re-originate packages where necessary.Undertake photo/ artwork rights research when repertoire is licensed to third parties.Please apply with a cover letter & CV to press@acerecords.com

Your role at Ace: Your primary role will be to manage the production of all physical products released on the Ace group of labels.
Requirements: A strong awareness of deadlines, good organisational, administrative and analytical skills and a keen eye for detail are all essential.
An ability and desire to drive deadlines on a regular monthly release schedule
3-5 years of previous Production experience in the music industry, preferably at a record label.
Good communication and interpersonal skills.
Basic image editing skills and a knowledge and understanding of the repertoire we release would also be advantageous.
What you'll be doing:: Lead the weekly scheduling meeting attended by the A&R, digital, licensing, sales and marketing departments. Pay heed to all requirements raised in the meeting, such as licensing deadlines and promotional plans, and organise production parts accordingly. Issue regular deadlines, chasers and updates to all.

Title: Copyright Assistant, German speaking (UK)
Description: Joining the Publishing Copyright department in our London office, the primary role of the Copyright Assistant will be to assist in correctly registering works and agreements in Germany, Austria and Switzerland at the local collection societies and carrying out all related tasks to maintain and manage our catalogue to highest standards, including working with royalties.Continue to article...The successful candidate will need to have excellent communication and administrative skills when dealing with general enquiries from internal and external sources and can deal with pressure but remain optimistic and persistent to complete tasks accurately and efficiently.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We're currently 600 people based across 13 offices and we're still growing.WHAT DOES A COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT AT KOBALT DO?Ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at collection societiesDealing with general enquiries regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreementsHandling song disputes and duplicate claimsProcessing new works and agreements as the company acquires rights to new cataloguesProcessing royalty statements received from collection societiesAnalysing the royalties received to identify possible underpayment or delayed payment, and working with the societies to resolve these issuesYou may be asked to take on extra responsibilities 3 please be aware that the above list is not exhaustiveWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Excellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to audienceNative level of written and spoken German as well as EnglishTrack-record of excellent organization and prioritisation skillsStrong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-setAnalytical skills as well as experience working with ExcelPrevious admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable. Most important is that you are ambitious and flexible and can adapt to a growing and changing department!

Title: Business Development Lead, New Music License Development (UK)
Description: We are looking for a Business Development Lead to join our New Music License Development team on a 12 Month Fixed Term Contract.Continue to article...The role will be working from our Kings Cross office predominately with occasional working from Streatham and home-working where business needs allow.About UsPRS for Music Limited is the UK’s leading collection society, bringing together two collection societies: the Mechanical-Copyright Protection Society and the Performing Right Society. We undertake collective rights management for musical works on behalf of over 140,000 members. We pay royalties to our members when their work is performed, broadcast, streamed, downloaded, reproduced, played in public or used in film and TV. We support them by influencing policy, supporting and hosting awards and events, and investing in new technology to ensure we're fit for the digital music age.About the RoleThe Business Development Lead will be responsible for the management of two team members in delivering new music licensing opportunities, ensuring they are both fit for purpose in the market and operationally efficient.The successful candidate will have proven negotiation skills and be comfortable building relationships with target licensees and their industry representatives. The candidate will lead the licence development process from beginning to end including market analysis and sizing, licence scoping and design, engagement and negotiation with licensee industries and seeking buy-in from internal stakeholders and committees on proposed approach.You will achieve this by:Defining and delivering new mechanical licensing scheme structures and pricing mechanisms. This will ensure member revenue is delivered and maximisedCarrying out extensive market research to justify scheme implementations or amendments and pricing changes supported by reasoned business casesBuilding complex financial models to forecast revenue from new and existing licensing opportunitiesWorking in conjunction with wider licensing teams to prepare for and carry out relevant industry negotiationsConstantly monitoring the music industry and our licensees' industries to understand key developments that could impact businessPreparing and building case materials in support of any Copyright Tribunal reference relating to these scheme developments (if required)Commission and managing external advisors and research projects when necessaryWorking with the Senior Business Development Manager to ensure high levels of engagement amongst the team and demonstrable commitment to the company valuesSalary circa £48,000 per annum depending on experienceAbout YouThe successful candidate will have knowledge of relevant industries, music publishing and music rights, copyright law, MCPS & PRS for Music. You will have senior level negotiating skills with a proven track record of concluding agreements and previous product and/or license development experience. You will be highly analytical with the ability to understand, interpret and present complex data clearly and concisely to a differing audience and you will have an excellent understanding of all Microsoft Office software with a strong liking and ability for Excel and PowerPoint.You will be a strategic thinker who can quickly understand the bigger picture. You will You will have proven business development experience in a fast-moving environment, and you will have excellent written and verbal communication skills with strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build lasting relationships. You will have strong organisation and prioritisation skills with the ability to manage workload across multiple workstreams at one time. You will also be target orientated, highly motivated and thrive in fast-paced environments.The successful candidate must be degree level educated or have relevant equivalent experience and will have experience of successfully managing and developing a team of 1-4 direct reportsWhy PRS?PRS for Music is a forward-thinking music copyright company with a focus on being the number one rights management company in the world.We live by our values they are deeply ingrained in our company culture. We want to help you grow throughout your career, so we have plenty of development opportunities for you.We run a number of well-being activities throughout the year and encourage flexible working and a relaxed dress code.We also have a number of perks including roof terraces on both sites, regular live music events, subsidised cafes, corporate discounts including gym membership and home working.Next StepsDoes this sound like you? If so, we would love to hear from you 3 either apply with your CV or send your CV and covering letter to recruitment@prsformusic.com

Title: Senior Global Creative, Creative Solutions (US)
Description: Spotify's mission is to 8unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the opportunity to enjoy and be inspired by those creators'.Continue to article...We are looking for a passionate experienced creative to join our Global Creative Solutions team. Creative Solutions is a global group of thoughtful innovators who solve client problems in uniquely Spotify ways. We are strategists, creatives, story tellers, designers, writers, producers, musicians, music fans, data detectives, and innovators. Our mission is to engage, inspire, and guide brands with data fueled creativity.The ideal candidate is a dynamic but thoughtful innovator who solves brand challenges with Spotify creativity. This individual will be responsible for developing proactive creative ideas and concepts that are designed to drive advertising revenue, while working closely with creative strategists to manage key deliverables year round.The role is based in New York but travel to major markets is a possibility when creating culturally relevant creative that is inspirational and actionable for Spotify sales teams to sell in local markets.Collaboration is a key value at Spotify, and this individual must partner closely with various Spotify teams in each market to develop creative solutions that sell. This role, based in New York, reports to the strategy lead for global accounts in NY, USA.What you'll doBe a champion of Spotify for Creatives, using it to guide the creative community & foster new creative thinking.Develop innovative audio first ideas that forge a new path in what audio creativity isPut our money where your mouth is, by working closely with in house producers to bring your ideas to life on and off the Spotify platformWork closely with product engineers to consider new creative opportunities in the development of new ad products, particularly in digital audioDevelop creative guidelines, best, and CnextD practices for creative on Spotify, with a special focus on audio products and innovationBe an inspiring writer that can jump in the studio and record VO to bring your copy to lifeInspire creatives with tips, tricks, and strategies for advertising on Spotify.Work closely with the global strategy team to develop ideas for workshops with top Spotify partners and advertisers.Work closely with global strategists to develop materials for interactive workshopsExpress a consistent, credible editorial voice for Spotify throughout all output; interpret brand partners' objectives and carve out meaningful places to serve these objectivesWho you areYou have 9 3 10 years' experience developing and pitching world-class creative concepts within editorial, agency, or branded content fieldsYou have a global approach to creative development, with consideration for content that works across markets, cultures, and audiencesYou are a pro copywriter, comfortable drafting from scratch as well as editing or punching up work from other contributorsYou have a strong portfolio of work that has used audio and/or musicYou are a pro in design tools; eg Photoshop.You are a pro in audio tools; e.g. ProTools.You exhibit curiosity, proactively solve problems, and get things doneYou are able to manage your own time, stakeholders, and accountabilities in a collaborative, fast-paced, entrepreneurial environmentAct as creative expert in internal, client- and industry-facing meetings and eventsYou're excited by audio, and particularly digital audio's opportunity for creativity.

What you'll do: Be a champion of Spotify for Creatives, using it to guide the creative community & foster new creative thinking.
Develop innovative audio first ideas that forge a new path in what audio creativity is

Title: Business Affairs Manager (NL/US)
Description: We're looking for a Business Affairs Manager to strengthen our management and label team.Continue to article...You will be responsible for all legalities of our operation, including the creation of contracts (including master, publishing, license, sync), negotiations with third parties, legal optimization, trademarks, insurances and risk mitigation for both Heroic and bitbird.These are two separate companies who share key personnel and resources. You’ll work closely with the artist managers and operational staff at Heroic, the label staff at bitbird and will report to our CEO (whom is the same person for Heroic & bitbird), COO (Heroic) and Label Manager (bitbird).Heroic is an independent music management agency with headquarters in The Netherlands and team members distributed worldwide. We provide artist management, record label and publishing services for our clients. Our mission is to empower creators to positively influence culture.bitbird is an independent record label spearheaded by San Holo with headquarters in The Netherlands and team members worldwide. We provide record label, publishing and apparel services to our artists and writers. We aspire to be the fairest record company in the business and our motto is Ccreate, foreverD.Responsibilities:Legal strategy: Working closely with our CEO to optimize the legal architecture of our businesses, collaboration with clients and affiliated companies. Create and maintain contracts, agreements and templates.Licensing strategy: Working closely with CEO to devise the optimal licensing strategy for our businesses and management clients with partners such as distributors, DSPs (Spotify, Apple), publishers and sync agencies.Legal operations: Creation, review, negotiation, execution and auditing of all contracts. These include master license agreements, publishing (title) agreements, collaboration agreements between artists, license deals to distributors, sync partners, advertising agencies, management agreements and more. Closely collaborating with legal representatives of our key partners as well as our external legal counsel. Training frontline staff to handle routine agreements.Monitoring of agreements: Documentation of all contracts, their terms and durations in our legal software. Monitoring of durations and servicing of renewal or termination notices when due.Guarding of rights: Monitoring the rightful use of our rights, serving cease and desist notices, issuing take-downs and applying further legal action if so required.RequirementsBachelor of Law, or higher: We are looking for someone who understands law, however do not require you to be a practicing lawyer necessarily. Industry experience and familiarity with music contracts is key.Familiar with music intellectual property law: Understanding of copyrights, master rights, neighbouring rights, public performance, mechanical and performance rights, publishing, synchronization rights.Familiar with company law: Understanding of international company structures, partnership agreements, licensing agreements, workings of value added tax and obligations of private and limited companies.Experience in the music industry: This job can be taught, however experience in the industry is a large perk – particularly when it entails having practiced law in the industry.Negotiation and mediation skills: You need to be able to negotiate on our behalf to attain the best possible deals for our company. Meanwhile you should have the skill to mediate and de-escalate heated discussions to ensure positive results for all parties involved.Willing to try, not afraid to fail: You need a willingness to learn and master unfamiliar skills. We operate in a unique, non-traditional way and require you to adapt to our operations. We are after learning and embrace that failure is a part of that process.Seeking constant self-improvement: We strive to be an organization where the best ideas succeed. We communicate and operate transparently. We strive for continuous evolution and do not shy from giving honest feedback for the greater good of the organization. You must be open to receiving and giving feedback for the greater good of the company.Work hard, play hard mentality: Our primary responsibility is taking care of our artists. The music industry is chaotic and quickly changing, often requiring spurts of work at inconvenient moments. You must be willing to get the job done.Ability to work 40 hours/week: Our office hours are 10:00 – 18:00 CET, however as many of our team members are international, you may have to work at odd hours regularly.Ability to come into our office in The Hague is a bonus.We are open to applications from lawyers, holders of master and bachelor degrees in Laws, Literature, Business or people who otherwise have gained experience with music law. We believe this is a job that can be taught, assuming the candidate has a sufficient foundation and willingness to learn and work.Do you have what it takes?Submit your resume and a motivational letter explaining your history with law, the music industry and why you think you’re a good fit for this role and our team. You'll hear from us if we're interested in your application.

Responsibilities:: Legal strategy: Working closely with our CEO to optimize the legal architecture of our businesses, collaboration with clients and affiliated companies. Create and maintain contracts, agreements and templates.

Licensing strategy: Working closely with CEO to devise the optimal licensing strategy for our businesses and management clients with partners such as distributors, DSPs (Spotify, Apple), publishers and sync agencies.

Legal operations: Creation, review, negotiation, execution and auditing of all contracts. These include master license agreements, publishing (title) agreements, collaboration agreements between artists, license deals to distributors, sync partners, advertising agencies, management agreements and more. Closely collaborating with legal representatives of our key partners as well as our external legal counsel. Training frontline staff to handle routine agreements.
Monitoring of agreements: Documentation of all contracts, their terms and durations in our legal software. Monitoring of durations and servicing of renewal or termination notices when due.
Guarding of rights: Monitoring the rightful use of our rights, serving cease and desist notices, issuing take-downs and applying further legal action if so required.
Requirements: Bachelor of Law, or higher: We are looking for someone who understands law, however do not require you to be a practicing lawyer necessarily. Industry experience and familiarity with music contracts is key.

Familiar with music intellectual property law: Understanding of copyrights, master rights, neighbouring rights, public performance, mechanical and performance rights, publishing, synchronization rights.

Familiar with company law: Understanding of international company structures, partnership agreements, licensing agreements, workings of value added tax and obligations of private and limited companies.
Experience in the music industry: This job can be taught, however experience in the industry is a large perk , particularly when it entails having practiced law in the industry.

Negotiation and mediation skills: You need to be able to negotiate on our behalf to attain the best possible deals for our company. Meanwhile you should have the skill to mediate and de-escalate heated discussions to ensure positive results for all parties involved.

Willing to try, not afraid to fail: You need a willingness to learn and master unfamiliar skills. We operate in a unique, non-traditional way and require you to adapt to our operations. We are after learning and embrace that failure is a part of that process.

Seeking constant self-improvement: We strive to be an organization where the best ideas succeed. We communicate and operate transparently. We strive for continuous evolution and do not shy from giving honest feedback for the greater good of the organization. You must be open to receiving and giving feedback for the greater good of the company.

Work hard, play hard mentality: Our primary responsibility is taking care of our artists. The music industry is chaotic and quickly changing, often requiring spurts of work at inconvenient moments. You must be willing to get the job done.
Ability to work 40 hours/week: Our office hours are 10:00 , 18:00 CET, however as many of our team members are international, you may have to work at odd hours regularly.
Ability to come into our office in The Hague is a bonus.
Do you have what it takes?: Submit your resume and a motivational letter explaining your history with law, the music industry and why you think you’re a good fit for this role and our team. You'll hear from us if we're interested in your application.

Title: Label Manager (UK)
Description: Seven Lions' Ophelia Records is looking for a label manager to help take its rapid growth to the next stage.Continue to article...In just over a year, Ophelia has become one of the hottest new labels in bass music, with collaborations and releases from acts such as SLANDER, Excision, Jason Ross, Wooli and Crystal Skies. Its releases regularly appear in Spotify's MINT playlist and on Sirius BPM. The label has also started to host festival stages at flagship events such as Electric Zoo.Ophelia is run in partnership with Involved Group. With offices in London and Los Angeles, Involved Group is home to globally renowned record labels Anjunabeats and Anjunadeep, as well as an artist management division – Involved Management – whose roster includes talents such as Seven Lions, Dusky, Lane 8 and Spencer Brown.You will plug into one of the most dynamic and respected label teams in electronic music, taking responsibility for the growth and coordination of Ophelia. You will be proactive on coming up with creative ways to develop the label profile and help build a label family in the truest sense. Key responsibilities:Coordinate the scheduling, marketing, distribution and promotion of all releases on Seven Lions' Ophelia Brief and coordinate all artwork and content for releases, ensuring release deadlines are hitCoordinate with third party press, promotion and marketing teams (when applicable) ensuring we get the best from themManage release budgets and forecasts for all releases, communicating to relevant stakeholders and directors Coordinate with in-house distribution and streaming teams to ensure they have all necessary assets, promotional material and pitching information Maintain online and social presence for labels, and develop over arching promotion and growth plansOversee and execute physical production where necessaryProactively raise profile of the label at every opportunityReview all label functions and processes ongoing, to ensure we are set up to succeed and getting the best for our artists, and out of all our partners A&R, artist development and artist communications, building a family vibe with the core roster wherever possible Coordinate any label tours and events Manage all associated release administration including generating ISRC codes, label copy and metadata, delivering said materials to all relevant bodies and rights management.About You Experience working and hustling within electronic music Passionate about melodic bass music An eye for detail and a passion for organisation and systems An awareness of current trends within the record label space, and a passion for marketing and promotion within an ever moving landscapeA confident and dynamic communicator, who acts proactively rather than reactively A team player who gets their hands dirty and leads by exampleInvolved GROUP is committed to equal opportunity in the workplace. We hire on merit and will not discriminate against age, race, gender identity, sexual orientation or religion.Please send your CV and cover letter to allan@anjunabeats.com

Key responsibilities:: Coordinate the scheduling, marketing, distribution and promotion of all releases on Seven Lions' Ophelia
Brief and coordinate all artwork and content for releases, ensuring release deadlines are hit
Coordinate with third party press, promotion and marketing teams (when applicable) ensuring we get the best from them
Manage release budgets and forecasts for all releases, communicating to relevant stakeholders and directors
Coordinate with in-house distribution and streaming teams to ensure they have all necessary assets, promotional material and pitching information
Maintain online and social presence for labels, and develop over arching promotion and growth plans

Review all label functions and processes ongoing, to ensure we are set up to succeed and getting the best for our artists, and out of all our partners A&R, artist development and artist communications, building a family vibe with the core roster wherever possible Coordinate any label tours and events
Manage all associated release administration including generating ISRC codes, label copy and metadata, delivering said materials to all relevant bodies and rights management.

Passionate about melodic bass music
An eye for detail and a passion for organisation and systems
An awareness of current trends within the record label space, and a passion for marketing and promotion within an ever moving landscape
A confident and dynamic communicator, who acts proactively rather than reactively
A team player who gets their hands dirty and leads by example

Title: Assistant, Label Collections, Neighbouring Rights (UK)
Description: Kobalt Neighbouring Rights is seeking an Assistant to join our hard-working and motivated Label Neighbouring Rights team in our London office.Continue to article...The role is accountable for internal system updates and repertoire data management but also includes some client-facing responsibilities. The ideal candidate would therefore be highly organised, able to follow agreed processes, detail orientated and driven by achieving results for our customers and department.The is a fantastic opportunity to be part of the largest Neighbouring Rights administrator in the world. Kobalt Neighbouring Rights started in 2011 offering a global service to performers and labels alike. Our label roster is amongst the best in the world and includes AWAL, Big Machine, Black Crow, Concord Bicycle, Macklemore, MPL Inc (Paul McCartney), Partisan Records, Secretly Canadian and Sub Pop.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We're currently 600 people based across 13 offices and we're still growing. That's where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story!WHAT DOES AN ASSISTANT, LABEL COLLECTIONS DO?Analysing airplay reports to understand potential deal valuesSetting up potential deals in the system so that proposals can be approved and drawn upMeeting the Client Onboarding SLA by setting up new agreements in our internal systemWorking with the Label Society Relations team to resolve double claims with other rightsholdersFirst point of contact for a named client rosterChecking Client statements and monitoring payment trendsWorking with the Distribution & Label Society Relations teams to ensure clients are paid on timeManaging internal repertoire systems and large Excel databases WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skillsExcellent Excel capability and analytical skillsHigh level of written and spoken EnglishExtensive organisation skills and the ability to prioritise tasksRemains calm and works well under pressure 3 familiar with working to tight deadlinesPrevious Neighbouring Rights experience preferableYou may be working for a music label, publisher, or neighbouring rights society where you have hadto liaise with clientele.Most importantly you'd be willing to get stuck in, have the initiative to establish your own processes, have a meticulous eye for detail and are keen and enthusiastic to learn about the complex world of Label Neighbouring Rights.

Title: Marketing Executive (UK)
Description: Marketing Executive (Anjunabeats)Continue to article...London, Bermondsey UK Salary dependent on experienceWith almost 20 years of touring and hundreds of releases under its belt, Anjunabeats remains one of the world’s best-loved independent dance labels. We're looking for an early career marketing all-rounder to help us write our next chapter! Working from our brand new London office, just a short walk from London Bridge Station, you'll be joining a rapidly growing family of 35 young and young-at-heart colleagues who are fanatical about electronic music. We love exploring the clubs of London together and have an eclectic, genre-free policy on our office stereo. You'll be joining a company with (as of next year) 20 years of history behind it. What began as a university project for Above & Beyond has become a globe-spanning business encompassing all aspects of the modern music industry. You'll be at the heart of it. Your role, and we hope it does become your role, would see you reporting directly to our Label Marketing Manager. You'll be in the trenches from day dot working on exciting new music plus the odd destination festival or national tour! What will you be doing? Glad you asked.. You'll be writing copy. Loads of it. Better copy than this. You'll be the voice of an established, highly-respected label and will be able to craft witty, snappy copy with ease and at pace! Posting to and monitoring our core brand channels for Anjunabeats, including Instagram, YouTube, Facebook and Twitter Briefing content builds in to our Art Department, complying with platform best practice Monitoring for and answering fan questions and complaints via our CRM toolsBuilding out social advertising campaigns with the Label Marketing ManagerCollaborating with label Product Managers to build out engaging and visually arresting release campaigns for singles and albumsTravel to festivals and label events, coordinating on-site content teams and collaborating with artists on interviews and other backstage hijinks You'll be keeping one eye on the competition and making recommendations to the marketing team and label artists, ensuring our socials look and sound the best they possibly can! You'll be a self-starter who enjoys a fast-paced office environment where no idea is a bad idea and everyone is working towards a shared goal. As an ambassador for our brand, you'll deal regularly with online community leaders from around the world and build relationships with stakeholders and influencers in dance, tech, wellness and gaming verticals. It's a clichÃ©, but we want people who live and breathe electronic dance music and who are excited to make their mark in the music industry. Involved GROUP is committed to equal opportunity in the workplace. We hire on merit and will not discriminate against age, race, gender identity, sexual orientation or religion.Please send your CV, cover letter, a list of your favourite three albums of the past year, salary expectations and links to any published writing you have done previously to jobs@anjunabeats.com, subject CMarketing ExecutiveD.

Title: Manager, Royalty Analysis (US)
Description: Sony/ATV Music Publishing, established in 1995 as a joint venture between Sony and Michael Jackson, is the world's leading music publisher. Together with EMI Music Publishing, Sony/ATV owns or administers more than 3 million copyrights, ranging from songs by iconic names including The Beatles, Bob Dylan, Michael Jackson, Carole King, Queen and The Rolling Stones to contemporary superstars such as Taylor Swift, Drake, Pharrell Williams, Lady Gaga, Kanye West, Pink, Sam Smith and Ed Sheeran. To learn more: https://www.sonyatv.com/en.Continue to article...Sony/ATV Music Publishing LLC is seeking to fill the position of Manager of Royalty Analysis.  The ideal candidate must have experience with financial data analysis and must be exceptionally skilled at complicated numeric calculations and research. Title: Associate Counsel, Litigation (US)
Description: A little bit about our team:Continue to article...WMG's corporate Legal Department is located at corporate headquarters in NYC (and also in London) and supports WMG's recorded music labels, music publishing entities and all operating companies and departments in various legal matters, including litigation and claims, digital licensing, content protection, anti-piracy, mergers and acquisitions, securities, compliance and employment.Why this could be your next big break:This position is a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in all aspects of the litigations and pre-litigation claims affecting a global entertainment company and its affiliated record labels and music publishing entities. In this role, you will not only handle some of the most cutting-edge legal issues facing the music industry, but you will also work closely with Legal & Business Affairs at the record labels and music publishing entities, Business Development, Finance and many other departments to develop a well-rounded experience in the music industry.Here you'll get to:Handle both affirmative and defensive claims and litigations relating to the cutting-edge legal issues affecting the music industry, including copyright, trademark, the DMCA, fair use, defamation and anti-piracy.Draft cease-and-desist letters, review and revise case filings, analyze artist and songwriter contracts and other agreements, and negotiate and draft settlements.Provide legal advice and guidance to WMG's record labels and publishing companies and other divisions including Business Development, Finance, Insurance, HR, Licensing and Royalties.Investigate digital services, websites and mobile apps that use Warner Music Group sound recordings, musical compositions and artwork without a license.Prepare quarterly reporting documents pertaining to litigations and claims affecting the company.Interact with WMG's internal business units, outside and opposing counsel, artist and songwriter representatives, music industry trade associations, and infringing services.Rock star candidates will have:A JD and active license to practice law in New York.5 years of experience practicing litigation with an emphasis on copyright law.Familiarity with recording agreements, music publishing agreements, administration agreements and licensing agreements.Experience in negotiations.Strong interpersonal skills to successfully interface with outside attorneys, senior management and internal employees.Self-motivation, with the ability to work independently, follow deadlines, effectively manage time and multitask.Excellent writing, communication and organizational skills.It would be music to our ears if you also had:Experience in the music industry or experience working on litigations involving recording agreements and music publishing agreements.Experience working in-house.

Interact with WMG's internal business units, outside and opposing counsel, artist and songwriter representatives, music industry trade associations, and infringing services.

5 years of experience practicing litigation with an emphasis on copyright law.

Experience working in-house.

Title: Junior Publicity Manager Nettwerk, GSA (DE)
Description: Nettwerk Music Group is the umbrella company for Nettwerk Records, Nettwerk Management and Nettwerk One Publishing. It's the biggest independent music company in Canada. Continue to article...Neverland Music represents Nettwerk since 2006 for GSA and since 2008 for Europe (ex. UK). Marketing, PR, Online and radio are predominately handled in-house.We are looking for a (Junior) PR Manager exclusively for Nettwerk.Responsible for Promoting Nettwerk artists in Germany, Austria, Switzerland, and supporting the promotion of those handled by an outside PR company.Position Summary The Publicity Manager's role is to co-ordinate, arrange, manage and execute the promotion of artists being released by Nettwerk in Germany, Austria & Switzerland. In the cases where the promotions are being handled by external third parties, the promotion manager will support them, ensuring that they have the correct assets, and are well versed in what is happening with said artists so it can be effectively communicated to other territories.This role will generally focus on print media online promotion and Influencer promo, but will also sometimes touch on video promotion.The goal of this Publicity Manager is to raise the visibility for Nettwerk releases through whichever facets of media are most appropriate for each individual artist, to increase GSA success, as well as helping these artists in other markets in Europe.Main Duties Promotion: In consultation with Nettwerk EU team, devise and execute promotion plans for various Nettwerk artists releases. The focus should include:Print media- specialist music press, national press, lifestyle and occasional regional publications where appropriateOnline Press- specialist press & music press, from widely read sites such the Intro to blogs all covered, along with general lifestyle, national news, and whatever other areas suit the artist in question.Video- following up with appropriate channels and ensuring that any unique opportunities are taken.Look after the promo schedules for ALL Nettwerk artists that are in the EU, ensuring they are detailed, accurate and communicated to management, tour-managers, out of house third parties and the Nettwerk EU team in a timely manner.Write and service press releases featuring new releases and Nettwerk newsTranslate press releases and artist biographies from English into GermanIn association with the above, arranging any mail outs required, whether â¨physical or digital.Follow up with the various media to try and achieve results for the artists â¨being promoted.Be responsible for ensuring the promotional and marketing results achieved for artists are effectively communicated back to the North American and UK label teams in a timely manner.Collaboration/Teamwork: The Promotions Manager will be part of a small dynamic team in the Hamburg office, working in tandem with the US and UK teams and it is important they have a positive willingness to dedicate himself/herself to making the team work internally in Hamburg, and within the larger global Nettwerk team.The promotion Manager will be involved in weekly calls with US and European Nettwerk offices, to discuss the promotion of the artists that they are promoting, but also those being looked after out of house.Please send CV's with covering letter to martin@nettwerk.com

Title: CEO (UK/US)
Description: The OrganizationContinue to article...Merlin Network is the global digital rights agency for independent record labels and independent music distributors. Structured as a collective management organization, Merlin launched in 2008 and today has offices in Amsterdam, London, New York and Tokyo. Merlin represents over 850 members from 63 countries, who collectively command over 12% of the world's digital music market. The organization acts as a negotiating and collections body on behalf of member labels and distributors. On behalf of its members, Merlin has deals in place with major and emerging music streaming platforms and other services in multiple territories including Spotify, YouTube, Deezer, SoundCloud, Pandora and Facebook, NetEase Cloud Music, Ali Music Group, Tencent Music Entertainment and others. Merlin ensures fair sustainable deals exist between its members and music streaming services and has distributed over $2 billion to members since launch. Between April 2018 and March 2019 alone, Merlin paid to label and distributor members over $700M in revenue, plus over $130M in settlements, non-royalties and proceeds from the sale of Spotify shares.LocationThe position can be based in either New York City or London. Position SummaryThe CEO provides leadership to the Merlin team, its members and the independent music sector globally. The public facing role anticipates and listens to the evolving needs of members, envisions opportunities and how to seize them, and develops and executes strategies to attract and serve members. The CEO also serves as the unifying voice for the Merlin membership, and ensures fair and equitable dealings with current, emerging and future platforms in the global digital ecosystem. Merlin seeks an accomplished executive with deep experience, relationships and respect in the independent music community, experience working internationally across genres and cultures, and a track record of delivering on practical vision for staying ahead of technology.Detailed Responsibilities Serve as the primary outward facing executive, delivering on a strong commitment to label and distributor members, shepherding productive relationships with DSPs and other licensee partners, and continuing to raise the profile of Merlin by reinforcing its mission in the press, at conferences and in social media.Report to the Board of Directors and serve the interests of a diverse base of label and distributor members.Work closely with the board to develop a vision and strategy for the short and long-term future of Merlin.Lead the conversation with DSPs and speak on behalf of Merlin's constituents with partners and adversaries.Ensure regular lines of communication with members, keeping members informed and gathering their input as appropriate.Give voice to the broadest range of members across label and distributor size, stage of growth, genre, territory, culture and market maturity.Directly lead and provide guidance and mentoring to the executives who lead the core functions of the organization including: member acquisition, relations & operations; negotiations, commercial business development & legal; and finance and technology.Shepherd the further evolution of Merlin's team globally and effectively hire, lead and develop staff.Serve as the escalation point for all matters regarding DSP negotiations, P&L, infrastructure, technology and people management.Continue to unify the Merlin members and represent members with a collective voice.Oversee the creation of programs and services that attract a diversity of new members and serve Merlin's ongoing mission.Leverage the hard-won level playing field and seek further advantage for members.Anticipate and define changes in the technology and deal climate affecting independent music in all major and emerging markets and across all digital channels.Set standards for the organization and the industry and enable Merlin to lead the innovation conversation.Explore opportunities for the organization to create infrastructure, process, alternative platforms and deal structures that benefit members in an increasingly data intensive, fast evolving environment.RequirementsA global executive with deep direct experience in the music industry, including an intimate knowledge of deal structures and deals in the music streaming marketplace.Passion for independent labels and their position in culture and society, and empathy for their unique needs.Strong understanding of technology and its impact on recorded music labels and other music rights holders.Experience with music monetization in streaming, social media and other emerging uses.Articulate about the value that independent music creates for technology companies, and ability to command that value in negotiations that benefit member labels and distributors.Track record of fostering diversity in their organizations.Intimate understanding of the current music ecosystem and evolving deal structures involved in music licensing, distribution and streaming.Ability to bring people together around shared visions for an empowered future.Bring both ambassadorial and negotiation skills, exercising impeccable judgement in matching style to the situation.Transparent and collegial with internal colleagues and key constituents.Analytics savvy executive who makes data informed decisions.Available for extensive global travel as needed to represent the organization and its members.Inspirational public speaker and clear communicator, able to work effectively across cultures.Experience scaling a growing organization including driving revenue, managing cost, architecting and closing key deals, attracting and allocating capital and developing people and systems.Strong analytical skills, with an ability to sense needs, predict market changes, and synthesize strategies that overcome threats and take advantage of opportunities.Prior experience setting and executing strategy for an organization with many stakeholders.High level of diplomacy, integrity and work ethic.ContactTo express interest, or recommend a qualified candidate please contact EnterGain, the executive recruiting, talent and strategy firm, at Merlin@EnterGain.com.

Location: The position can be based in either New York City or London.
Position Summary: The CEO provides leadership to the Merlin team, its members and the independent music sector globally. The public facing role anticipates and listens to the evolving needs of members, envisions opportunities and how to seize them, and develops and executes strategies to attract and serve members. The CEO also serves as the unifying voice for the Merlin membership, and ensures fair and equitable dealings with current, emerging and future platforms in the global digital ecosystem. Merlin seeks an accomplished executive with deep experience, relationships and respect in the independent music community, experience working internationally across genres and cultures, and a track record of delivering on practical vision for staying ahead of technology.
Detailed Responsibilities: Serve as the primary outward facing executive, delivering on a strong commitment to label and distributor members, shepherding productive relationships with DSPs and other licensee partners, and continuing to raise the profile of Merlin by reinforcing its mission in the press, at conferences and in social media.
Report to the Board of Directors and serve the interests of a diverse base of label and distributor members.
Work closely with the board to develop a vision and strategy for the short and long-term future of Merlin.
Lead the conversation with DSPs and speak on behalf of Merlin's constituents with partners and adversaries.
Ensure regular lines of communication with members, keeping members informed and gathering their input as appropriate.
Give voice to the broadest range of members across label and distributor size, stage of growth, genre, territory, culture and market maturity.

Directly lead and provide guidance and mentoring to the executives who lead the core functions of the organization including: member acquisition, relations & operations; negotiations, commercial business development & legal; and finance and technology.
Shepherd the further evolution of Merlin's team globally and effectively hire, lead and develop staff.
Serve as the escalation point for all matters regarding DSP negotiations, P&L, infrastructure, technology and people management.
Continue to unify the Merlin members and represent members with a collective voice.
Oversee the creation of programs and services that attract a diversity of new members and serve Merlin's ongoing mission.
Leverage the hard-won level playing field and seek further advantage for members.
Anticipate and define changes in the technology and deal climate affecting independent music in all major and emerging markets and across all digital channels.
Set standards for the organization and the industry and enable Merlin to lead the innovation conversation.

Explore opportunities for the organization to create infrastructure, process, alternative platforms and deal structures that benefit members in an increasingly data intensive, fast evolving environment.
Requirements: A global executive with deep direct experience in the music industry, including an intimate knowledge of deal structures and deals in the music streaming marketplace.
Passion for independent labels and their position in culture and society, and empathy for their unique needs.
Strong understanding of technology and its impact on recorded music labels and other music rights holders.
Experience with music monetization in streaming, social media and other emerging uses.
Articulate about the value that independent music creates for technology companies, and ability to command that value in negotiations that benefit member labels and distributors.
Track record of fostering diversity in their organizations.
Intimate understanding of the current music ecosystem and evolving deal structures involved in music licensing, distribution and streaming.
Ability to bring people together around shared visions for an empowered future.
Bring both ambassadorial and negotiation skills, exercising impeccable judgement in matching style to the situation.
Transparent and collegial with internal colleagues and key constituents.
Analytics savvy executive who makes data informed decisions.
Available for extensive global travel as needed to represent the organization and its members.
Inspirational public speaker and clear communicator, able to work effectively across cultures.
Experience scaling a growing organization including driving revenue, managing cost, architecting and closing key deals, attracting and allocating capital and developing people and systems.
Strong analytical skills, with an ability to sense needs, predict market changes, and synthesize strategies that overcome threats and take advantage of opportunities.
Prior experience setting and executing strategy for an organization with many stakeholders.
High level of diplomacy, integrity and work ethic.
Contact: To express interest, or recommend a qualified candidate please contact EnterGain, the executive recruiting, talent and strategy firm, at Merlin@EnterGain.com.
