Title: Vice President/Senior Counsel (US)
Description: The National Music Publishers’ Association (NMPA), the leading trade organization representing the interests of music publishers in the United States, is looking to fill a Vice President/Senior Counsel position in Washington, D.C. The Vice President /Senior Counsel will report to NMPA’s General Counsel.Continue to article...As Vice President/Senior Counsel, this attorney will help manage NMPA’s ongoing and future copyright-infringement litigations, negotiate licenses and settlements with digital companies, oversee NMPA's anti-piracy program, draft policy initiatives and submissions to government and regulatory bodies, including the Copyright Office and Department of Justice, and be involved in Copyright Royalty Board rate setting proceedings on behalf of music publishers and songwriters, as well as support the overall litigation, legislative and policy initiatives of the NMPA. An understanding of and keen interest in copyright law and policy, especially as applied to music, as well as developments in the digital distribution of content, are essential.The requirements for this position are:Minimum 6 years of experience as a lawyer, including intellectual property experience; law firm or music industry legal background strongly preferred;Expertise in copyright law, especially as it applies to music and new media;Ability to effectively manage complex litigations and supervise outside counsel to maximize results within a budget;Strong negotiation and drafting skills, including a demonstrated ability to negotiate and draft both settlement and licensing agreements in the music and/or digital space;Ability to communicate effectively with governmental bodies, digital music services and music publishers;Ability to think strategically and creatively about the future of the music industry, including solutions to legal, data-based and policy issues facing music publishers and songwriters;Ability to work independently but know when to seek advice;Excellent academic credentials;Member in good standing of, or ability to waive into, the District of Columbia Bar Association.Salary commensurate with experience.Please forward resume with cover letter to:Briana Berkley, NMPA, 975 F Street, Suite 375, Washington, DC 20004bberkley@nmpa.orgAbout the NMPA:The National Music Publishers' Association is the largest U.S. music publishing trade association with hundreds of members. Its mission is to protect, promote, and advance the interests of music's creators. The NMPA is the voice of both small and large music publishers, the leading advocate for publishers and their songwriter partners in the nation's capital and in every area where publishers do business. The goal of NMPA is to protect its members' property rights on the legislative, litigation, and regulatory fronts.

The requirements for this position are:: Minimum 6 years of experience as a lawyer, including intellectual property experience; law firm or music industry legal background strongly preferred;
Expertise in copyright law, especially as it applies to music and new media;
Ability to effectively manage complex litigations and supervise outside counsel to maximize results within a budget;
Strong negotiation and drafting skills, including a demonstrated ability to negotiate and draft both settlement and licensing agreements in the music and/or digital space;
Ability to communicate effectively with governmental bodies, digital music services and music publishers;
Ability to think strategically and creatively about the future of the music industry, including solutions to legal, data-based and policy issues facing music publishers and songwriters;
Ability to work independently but know when to seek advice;
Member in good standing of, or ability to waive into, the District of Columbia Bar Association.
About the NMPA:: The National Music Publishers' Association is the largest U.S. music publishing trade association with hundreds of members. Its mission is to protect, promote, and advance the interests of music's creators. The NMPA is the voice of both small and large music publishers, the leading advocate for publishers and their songwriter partners in the nation's capital and in every area where publishers do business. The goal of NMPA is to protect its members' property rights on the legislative, litigation, and regulatory fronts.

Title: Senior Program Manager (US)
Description: Apple is seeking a Senior Program Manager for the Apple Media Products team.Continue to article...Apple Media Products is an exciting environment and a fast-paced development organization. With customers in 155 countries, we lead a fast-growing marketplace and growing set of services for apps, music, TV & movies, books, podcasts, and more. Specifically, you will drive initiatives related to Apple Music. We're passionate about music and want to talk if you are too. Program Managers are responsible for shipping new features. We’re looking for people with proven track records of shipping complex, customer-facing projects under demanding timelines. Bonus points for new product introductions. This is a hands-on position where the Program Manager will be involved in requirements analysis, functional and technical design discussions, testing, proof-of-concept, working with partners, and more. This is not a task-based job; you will be responsible for successful outcomes and delivery. Strong fundamentals are a basis but you have more. You take responsibility; you feel a personal stake in the product you ship; you communicate responsibilities and scope clearly; you value integrity; you handle conflict; you know how things work; you work for the success of the entire PM team; you thrive in uncertainty and strive to bring order to it; you have deep wisdom and judgement; you keep your eye on the ball; you build relationships; you are aware of politics but do not get mired in them; you are constantly looking to improve yourself and your team; you’re prescient; and you ship code which millions of customers will use.Key Qualifications5 years of program, project, or product management in software development.A clear track record of making a difference in the projects you drive.Naturally accountable and responsible.Self-starting, self-motivated, disciplined, and self-sufficient.Extraordinary communication and presentation skills (written and verbal) to all levels of an organization.Strong facilitation skills (requirements sessions, design meetings, progress and status meetings).Strong technical proficiency in software architecture and development.DescriptionIf you join our team, your responsibilities will be:Drive and ship all aspects of large, cross-functional projects and feature areas.Oversee multiple concurrent projects including scope, requirements, timelines, and resource allocation.Define business, functional, and reporting requirements (often writing them yourself).Take a role in improving how projects are run and executed on the team.Own overall cross-functional project timeline, including development within the Apple Media Products team and other Apple groups (OS teams, device teams, other business teams), and backend reporting systems.Collaborate with IS&T, Legal, Finance, operations and other organizations both inside and outside of Apple to develop solutions for partnerships, promotions and other new business.

Key Qualifications: 5 years of program, project, or product management in software development.
A clear track record of making a difference in the projects you drive.
Naturally accountable and responsible.
Self-starting, self-motivated, disciplined, and self-sufficient.
Extraordinary communication and presentation skills (written and verbal) to all levels of an organization.
Strong facilitation skills (requirements sessions, design meetings, progress and status meetings).
Strong technical proficiency in software architecture and development.
Description: If you join our team, your responsibilities will be:
Drive and ship all aspects of large, cross-functional projects and feature areas.
Oversee multiple concurrent projects including scope, requirements, timelines, and resource allocation.
Define business, functional, and reporting requirements (often writing them yourself).
Take a role in improving how projects are run and executed on the team.

Own overall cross-functional project timeline, including development within the Apple Media Products team and other Apple groups (OS teams, device teams, other business teams), and backend reporting systems.
Collaborate with IS&T, Legal, Finance, operations and other organizations both inside and outside of Apple to develop solutions for partnerships, promotions and other new business.

In this role, you will be a master at working across various parts of the company, and will work closely with engineering, design, marketing, and data analytics teams to build a roadmap of ideas all aimed at improving the Apple Music experience across all platforms.Key Qualifications8 years of experience in software/media product development.Experience with APIs, SDKs, voice platforms, partners, or developer relations a plusStrong developer product instincts; you intuitively understand why product ideas will be effective and gain adoptionYou are fundamentally analytical and technical; you have the ability to collect and analyze data from a variety of sources, identify key insights, spot optimization opportunities, and inform product decisionsDeeply detail focused; you believe that an extraordinary product is the sum of an inordinate number of small, thoughtful decisionsExhibit a bias towards action and experimentationProduce articulate and inspired presentations to communicate your vision and plans.Excellent written and verbal interpersonal skills, with an ability to communicate with a wide range of team members and job functions 3 all the way up to executivesExperience with global teams and international product expansion a plusDo you have experience with APIs, SDKs, voice products a strong plus?Experience with music or media industry preferred.DescriptionLead a cross-functional team of data scientists, designers, engineers, program managers, and marketers to prioritize growth and product enhancements based on impact.Execute rapidly to capture signal, and continuously iterate on the product design and service features to improve customer growth.Work hand-in-hand with engineering, design and data analytics partners to analyze results and synthesize findings. Deep curiosity to identify key opportunities and ability to develop experiment driven proposals.Own and deliver on key measurable metrics each quarter Clearly communicate strategy in a structured way to socialize your roadmap and report resultsStay on top of standard methodologies across the industry to develop a resource of growth tactics

Title: Digital Marketing Manager (UK)

Title: Membership Manager (UK)
Description: The Ivors Academy of Music Creators is a non-profit membership association that exists to support, protect and celebrate music creators in the UK. We are at an exciting time in our organisation's history, having rebranded in March 2019 from the British Academy of Songwriters, Composers and Authors to The Ivors Academy of Music Creators.Continue to article...At the heart of our organisation is our composer and songwriter members and we are recruiting for a Membership Manager 3 a new role that is central to the development and growth of our membership.The Ivors Academy is committed to equality, diversity and inclusion. We encourage applications that help us maintain a diverse workforce.Duties:Reporting directly to the Director of Membership & Awards and line managing the Membership Administrator, the Membership Manager is responsible for the effective management of The Ivors Academy's membership offering, membership administration and member relations.Duties will include the development, maintenance and implementation of an accurate CRM system, all membership administration processes from new member applications to monthly subscription renewals to interrogating membership data and reporting.The post holder will deliver the highest level of client service to our membership, develop annual member engagement strategies and implement the Academy's recruitment strategy across all membership levels. The role is also responsible for the evaluation and delivery of the Academy's membership offering, working closely with the events and awards team.Essential Skills and Person Specification:Significant experience of working in a membership or outward/client-facing role requiring engagement with industry executives of all levelsDemonstrable experience and knowledge of CRM, specifically CiviCRMExperience of direct line management within a small teamAbility to work on multiple projects at any one time and prioritise workload accordinglyExceptional organisational skills and meticulous attention to detailExcellent written and verbal communication skillsAn outgoing, positive, can-do attitudeApplication deadline: 26 August 2019Interviews: W/C 2 September 2019Start date: As soon as possibleSalary: £DOETo apply, please send a CV and an accompanying cover letter to Fran Matthews: contact@ivorsacademy.comTHE IVORS ACADEMYThe Ivors Academy exists to support, protect and celebrate music creators in the UK. We are the independent professional association representing songwriters and composers in all genres, whether they create song, symphony or sync. We can trace our history back over 70 years. And we are entirely self-funded, relying on the continued support of our members and partners to carry on our work.Music is the most profound, enduring and popular of art forms. It crosses all borders, conveys all emotions, and touches all people. None of the music that the world loves exists until brilliant songwriters and composers create it. We represent, support, protect and celebrate the creators of music.Our Ivor Novello Awards are the ultimate accolade for songwriting and composing in the industry. The Ivors celebrate the talents of songwriters and screen composers, whilst The Ivors Composer Awards celebrate music creators in the classical, jazz and sonic art fields. The Ivors Academy Gold Badge recognises those who have supported and inspired our community. Each of these celebrations has at its heart the power and brilliance of music creators.We campaign together nationally and internationally to ensure that the rights of our members are protected, especially as our industry continues to rapidly change. And we cultivate talent, creating a community across the country in which our members share their valuable knowledge, creators cross-pollinate and collaborate, and all voices are heard. We guide new talent entering the industry and continue to encourage our members as they progress to greater successes.We are the champions of music creators. We are The Ivors Academy.www.ivorsacademy.com

Duties:: Reporting directly to the Director of Membership & Awards and line managing the Membership Administrator, the Membership Manager is responsible for the effective management of The Ivors Academy's membership offering, membership administration and member relations.

Demonstrable experience and knowledge of CRM, specifically CiviCRM
Experience of direct line management within a small team
Ability to work on multiple projects at any one time and prioritise workload accordingly
Exceptional organisational skills and meticulous attention to detail
Excellent written and verbal communication skills

And we are entirely self-funded, relying on the continued support of our members and partners to carry on our work.

Title: Product Manager (UK)
Description: Strut Records has an immediate opening for a product manager to help manage the day-to-day operations of the label and its releases.Continue to article...Strut is one of the UK’s leading record labels dedicated to unearthing the lost gems of dance music past, covering music from hard funk, underground disco, original breaks and Nigerian Afrobeat to old skool hip hop and forgotten music library classics. Known for uncompromising high-quality packaging, our releases cut no corners, bringing together killer dancefloor tracks as well as giving a full context to the music, telling the story of the characters behind the tunes, the clubs and the scene of the time. The albums are mastered from original tapes wherever possible and feature extensive sleevenotes, researched and written by respected journalists, rare photographs and memorabilia.TASKS WILL INCLUDE:Manage product logistics, ensuring content is delivered in a timely manner and to required internal specifications.Proof metadata on all incoming releases and provide feedback where necessary.Strategize and manage release campaign logistics from timely inception through to analysis, working with other internal departments as necessary.Work cross-functionally to create compelling pitches and campaigns for retail.Liaise with various departments, from finance and marketing to licensing and management to ensure all business needs are met.Assist in developing the label release schedule and ensure deadlines are met.Assist in finding licensors for obscure tracks and track down rights owners.Develop artwork concepts and create solid design briefs, researching additional information around particular releases and sourcing content.Assist in and source the creation of campaign assetsDay-to-day management of album campaigns, including: setting timelines and ensure deadlines are met; communication with external PR teams, artists, and artist management; feed results back to internal marketing and sales teams,Handle logistics of merchandise and offer support to touring artists.Handle logistics of record fair sales.Administration and organisation of label catalogues and master tape recordings.Handling of D2C communication.QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCEMinimum 3 years of experience in a relevant positionSolid working knowledge of Strut label and the overall market for these areas of musicA strong understanding of physical retail packaging and manufacturing.Excellent oral and written communication skillsAttention to detail is crucialPlease send CV and cover letter to jobs@k7.com Applications close 28 August

TASKS WILL INCLUDE:: Manage product logistics, ensuring content is delivered in a timely manner and to required internal specifications.
Proof metadata on all incoming releases and provide feedback where necessary.
Strategize and manage release campaign logistics from timely inception through to analysis, working with other internal departments as necessary.
Work cross-functionally to create compelling pitches and campaigns for retail.
Liaise with various departments, from finance and marketing to licensing and management to ensure all business needs are met.
Assist in developing the label release schedule and ensure deadlines are met.

Handle logistics of merchandise and offer support to touring artists.
Handle logistics of record fair sales.
Administration and organisation of label catalogues and master tape recordings.
Handling of D2C communication.

Solid working knowledge of Strut label and the overall market for these areas of music
A strong understanding of physical retail packaging and manufacturing.
Excellent oral and written communication skills

Title: Senior Director, Music (CA)
Description: At CBC/Radio-Canada, we create content that informs, entertains and connects Canadians on multiple platforms from coast to coast to coast.Continue to article...CBC/Radio-Canada is Canada's national public broadcaster. It operates a total of thirty services (Radio, Television and On-Line) delivering program content across the country and around the world in English, French and eight aboriginal languages.CBC/Radio-Canada is a well-known and highly respected national employer. Led by our mandate as the country's public broadcaster, we seek to inform, enlighten, and entertain Canadians, from coast to coast.We do this with a diverse team of talented individuals who are committed to quality in all the work we do. It's also something we do proudly in both official languages, as well as eight Indigenous ones, on multiple platforms, with a stated desire to be more local, more digital, and using the latest technology. Our people come from every corner of Canada and beyond. CBC/Radio-Canada makes diversity one of its priorities, which means reflecting Canada and its regions, as well as the country's multicultural and multiracial nature.Our successes and accomplishments are driven by embodying and upholding values, which include creativity, integrity, inclusiveness, and relevance. This is why we've embedded our work processes ways to encourage diversity and inclusion 3 in our workforce, workplace, and our programming. We know this is key to creating content that mirrors and resonates with the rich and evolving demographic makeup of our country.Our training and development programs, combined with a focus on creating a healthy, collaborative work environment, also helps employees thrive and feel a strong sense of commitment to the organization.As a complex, high-profile and sophisticated organization, it presents one of the most unique and challenging human resources opportunities in Canada.Whether on-air or behind the scenes, at CBC/Radio-Canada you join a team that thrives on making the connections and telling the stories that are important to Canadians.THE POSITIONThe Senior Director of CBC Music is comfortable working in a changing environment and has a vision for positioning CBC Music into the future. Being a musician oneself would be considered an asset, as well as a working knowledge of the Canadian music industry.This role demands a creative, high-energy, seasoned executive who has a track record of driving strategy, managing teams and delivering music content across platforms.We are seeking a leader with integrity, committed to developing people and who identifies as an ideas person and an industry leader.In this role you are an effective communicator and a transformational leader who will help shape and implement the CBC Music strategy under the direction of the Executive Director of Radio and Audio. You will drive growth and engagement in CBC Music content, privileging Canadian music and Canadian artists both at home and on the global stage, across platforms.As CBC Music content and products air and are posted on all platforms: radio, digital and television craft knowledge is an important asset in this role.As well you should have a working knowledge of the CBC mission, its programming, and demonstrate commitment to our public service offer and what that encompasses in a digital age.A deep knowledge of technology and music trends is key.The Senior Director of CBC Music works as part of a leadership team of Senior Directors, reporting to the Executive Director of Radio and Audio. In addition, this position works across platforms and departments. In this role you will participate in setting strategy and will collaborate with others to ensure that we are creating not only great music content, but fostering a creative work environment for a highly motivated and skilled team.You are a leader who champions inclusivity in the work environment and within content we provide Canadians.RESPONSIBILITIESWorking closely with the Executive Director CANDIDATE CHARACTERISTICSStrong understanding of the CBC Music brand and programming.Multi-dimensional thinker who exudes composure in high-stress situations.Ability to handle multiple priorities and deal with conflicting demands in a fast-paced environment.A tenacious strategist who loves a challenge and delivers the corporate message aligned with its strategy.Accountable with a strong sense of professionalism and integrity.Committed and enthusiastic, confident and outgoing and not afraid of challenges.Highly organized and goal orientated with flexibility needed to shift gears quickly.Self-motivated with a professional and trusted public demeanour.Strong interpersonal skills, collegial and a team player.Brings a national view and can navigate within a complex organization.Brings a passion for delivering musical experiences to diverse audiences, with a belief in the value of public broadcasting.COMPENSATION A competitive compensation package including base salary, pension and benefits will be provided.HOW TO APPLYPlease send your cover letter and rÃ©sumÃ© to: cbcmusic@searchlightpartnersgroup.com.CBC/Radio-Canada is committed to being a leader in reflecting our country's diversity. That's because we can only create and tell the stories that connect Canadians, by having a workforce that mirrors the ever-changing makeup of our country. That's why we, as an employer, value equal opportunity and nurture an inclusive workplace where our individual differences are not only recognized and valued, but also extend to and pervade all the services we provide as Canada's public broadcaster. For more information, visit the Talent and Diversity section of our website. If you have accommodation needs at this stage of the recruitment process, please inform us as soon as possible by sending an e-mail to recruitment@cbc.ca. You are invited to consult and familiarize yourself with our Code of Conduct, which can be found on our corporate website. All employees must adhere to the Code as a condition of employment. We also invite you to take a look at our policy on conflicts of interest. In the event that you become an employee, it will be important to inform us, as quickly as possible, of any situation that, because of your hiring, constitutes or could appear to constitute a conflict of interest.

THE POSITION: The Senior Director of CBC Music is comfortable working in a changing environment and has a vision for positioning CBC Music into the future. Being a musician oneself would be considered an asset, as well as a working knowledge of the Canadian music industry.
RESPONSIBILITIES: Working closely with the Executive Director of Radio and Audio, help reimagine, conceptualize, and execute CBC's music brand vision.
Serve as integral voice within the Music department and the organization at large by collaborating with internal stakeholders to create a unified and coordinated music strategy.
Key point of contact for key recording labels and management relationships to ensure proper support and alignment is achieved.
Leverage opportunities to ensure the CBC's premier position within the Canadian music industry remains at the forefront of music discovery and development of Canadian musicians.
Serve as the chief architect of comprehensive multi-platform strategic national music initiatives and campaigns to support core and emerging artists, and recorded and visual musical content.

Responsible for managing the creative direction and production of CBC's signature music programs (CBC Music shows Nightstream, Tempo, Afterdark with Odario Williams, Marvin's Room with Amanda Parris, Drive with Richard Terfry, Shift with Tom Allen).
Work with creative to ideate custom-made on- and off-air creative promotional campaigns that are in line with the overall Music strategy and corporate objectives.

Supervise the integration of unique music appearances and performances from across the network and digital tent poles, specials, live events, series, podcasts, and social platforms to bring engaging opportunities to the music landscape.
Manage the Music team responsible for CBC's talent relations and strategy inclusive of label relations, artist bookings, and music content.
Pitch talent for a variety of cross-platform themed programming on the network.
Partner with Finance to manage budget, develop forecasts, and report on results.
Partner with Business and Legal Affairs team to ensure timely and efficient contract negotiations with production companies and artists.
Provide strong leadership and serve as an example of the company's culture and brand by providing inspiration, motivation, and guidance to staff.

Empower team through delegation, coaching, mentoring, and providing positive challenges. A natural leader, you will motivate the team and manage and foster a culture of collaboration and continuous learning.
Act in an ethical and professional manner at all times, adhering to CBC's code of conduct policies.
CANDIDATE QUALIFICATIONS: Ability to clearly articulate brand vision, both externally to the market and internally to the team.
A minimum of 7 years of relevant experience at a senior management level.
A proven track record of influence, innovation, creative thinking and leadership in the Canadian music industry.
Strong knowledge of the music industry and music trends from the past to present, preferably with direct experience in content production.
Must bring well-established relationships with key players in the industry, including talent, agents, and production companies, and possess a good reputation within the music industry.
Proven willingness to take chances and be at the forefront of music strategy, whether by type of media or style.
Demonstrated success in planning and developing long-range projects and initiatives, both in daily content and in long-term strategy.
Keen understanding of consumer trends, especially as they relate to diverse genres, modes of consumption and viewer demographics.
Extensive experience working in a multi-platform environment.
Hands-on experience working in music programming and/or digital environment.
Experience with contract negotiations.
Demonstrated ability to work in a fast-paced and rapidly-changing, collaborative environment.
Strong organizational and decision-making skills with the ability to consolidate and integrate a wide range of inputs.
Proven ability to manage and develop others.
Excellent communicator with strong interpersonal, presentation, and negotiation skills.
A considerable amount of conflict resolution skills utilized both within and outside the department.
Undergraduate degree or experiential equivalent.
Ability to work in collaborative environments.
Candidates may be subject to skills and knowledge testing.
Some travel is required.
CANDIDATE CHARACTERISTICS: Strong understanding of the CBC Music brand and programming.
Multi-dimensional thinker who exudes composure in high-stress situations.
Ability to handle multiple priorities and deal with conflicting demands in a fast-paced environment.
A tenacious strategist who loves a challenge and delivers the corporate message aligned with its strategy.
Accountable with a strong sense of professionalism and integrity.
Committed and enthusiastic, confident and outgoing and not afraid of challenges.
Highly organized and goal orientated with flexibility needed to shift gears quickly.
Self-motivated with a professional and trusted public demeanour.
Strong interpersonal skills, collegial and a team player.
Brings a national view and can navigate within a complex organization.
Brings a passion for delivering musical experiences to diverse audiences, with a belief in the value of public broadcasting.
COMPENSATION: A competitive compensation package including base salary, pension and benefits will be provided.
HOW TO APPLY: Please send your cover letter and rÃ©sumÃ© to: cbcmusic@searchlightpartnersgroup.com.

Title: Armada 3 PR Manager (US)
Description: Armada Music is an Amsterdam-based music company that operates on a global scale. Our aim is to be the best partner for artists in the electronic music realm to work with, not only for the distribution and promotion of their music, but also in developing and shaping a solid career.Continue to article...As a brand, we aim to strengthen our position as a top supplier of the best music available in all electronic genres. We launched the U.S. office in 2016 and the U.K. office followed in 2017. Because of our growth, we are aiming to expand our New York team. We are therefore looking for a PR Manager U.S., who will play a key role in aligning the U.S. Promotion & Marketing activities with the main office in Amsterdam as well as the U.K. office.The main focus of the PR Manager U.S. is to devise a strategic plan for approaching the U.S. market as a music brand and self-sustainable hub for the promotion of dance music. At the same time, he or she will contribute to the creation of promotional plans for singles and albums released by Armada Music. Detailed tasks would include:General Marketing activitiesYou will initiate, execute and oversee various U.S. promotional and marketing projects (from individual releases to projects related to brand awareness);Create plans concerning the allocation of budgets for PR/promotion/marketing;Create 3 or contribute to the creation of 3 promotional PR plans for singles and albums, from individual releases to projects related to brand awareness);Together with relevant colleague, you will devise a strategic plan for approaching the U.S. market as a fully respected brand and self-sustainable hub for the promotion of dance music. You will help make Armada Music a big player in the U.S. music industry;You will report all relevant undertakings and results to artist/management for artists that are handled directly by the U.S. office.Promotional activitiesYou will actively search for opportunities to expand the audience of Armada Music and our artists in the U.S., such as cross promotion, free publicity, paid publicity etc.;You will organize and visit industry gatherings on behalf of Armada Music. You will also organize and plan PR activities, meetings for relevant media and industry outlets, promotional gatherings and pitches, and take care of U.S.-oriented mailouts;You will actively seek out new trends and opportunities in relation to promotion in general, as well as opportunities centered around releases or artists;You will prepare and send out press releases, artist biographies and promo material.Relationships and CommunicationYou will be responsible for initiating, maintaining and developing Armada Music's relationship with (media) partners, including blogs, TV shows, influencers, magazines and newspapers, plus managers, artists and labels in U.S. territory;You will send regular updates to colleagues who are in the lead of certain (release) projects. This includes a weekly report on general progress and activities as well as project-related updates (e.g. concerning releases, artists, general brand promotion);You will follow up on initial publicity activities by phone, e-mail or via face-to-face meetings;You will arrange publicity events, such as media interviews and personal appearances;You will escort artists to interviews or publicity events;You will always be looking to tap into new audiences.Who is the person we're looking for?You are extremely passionate about electronic dance music;You have previously worked in the music industry;You know your way around marketing and promotion, and you're blessed with an inventive and creative mindset;You have extensive knowledge of dance music, and know all about Armada Music and other labels' catalogs and artists in the U.S. and beyond;You are able to cope with pressure and changing deadlines (you can handle a fast-paced schedule of releases while working on multiple projects at the same time);You have excellent (verbal and written) communication, interpersonal and negotiation skills;You love working closely with the various departments of the company to get results quickly.What will we offer you in return?You will be joining the ranks of a young and dynamic team;You will receive a monthly salary befitting your age and experience;We offer health care coverage and other favorable secondary working conditions.Can't contain your excitement? Send your resume and cover letter to jobs@armadamusic.com. Please make sure to put PR Manager U.S. in the e-mail subject. We're looking forward to meeting you!ABOUT ARMADA MUSIC:Armada Music is the biggest independent dance music label in the world, based in Amsterdam, the Netherlands (HQ), London and New York (satellite offices). Founded in 2003, Armada Music represents labels and artists across the electronic music spectrum, including Afrojack, Andrew Rayel, Armin van Buuren, Erick Morillo, Fedde Le Grand, Gareth Emery, Kevin Saunderson, Lost Frequencies, Loud Luxury, Morgan Page and W&W.

General Marketing activities: You will initiate, execute and oversee various U.S. promotional and marketing projects (from individual releases to projects related to brand awareness);
Create plans concerning the allocation of budgets for PR/promotion/marketing;
Create 3 or contribute to the creation of 3 promotional PR plans for singles and albums, from individual releases to projects related to brand awareness);

Together with relevant colleague, you will devise a strategic plan for approaching the U.S. market as a fully respected brand and self-sustainable hub for the promotion of dance music. You will help make Armada Music a big player in the U.S. music industry;
You will report all relevant undertakings and results to artist/management for artists that are handled directly by the U.S. office.
Promotional activities: You will actively search for opportunities to expand the audience of Armada Music and our artists in the U.S., such as cross promotion, free publicity, paid publicity etc.;

You will organize and visit industry gatherings on behalf of Armada Music. You will also organize and plan PR activities, meetings for relevant media and industry outlets, promotional gatherings and pitches, and take care of U.S.-oriented mailouts;
You will actively seek out new trends and opportunities in relation to promotion in general, as well as opportunities centered around releases or artists;
You will prepare and send out press releases, artist biographies and promo material.
Relationships and Communication: You will be responsible for initiating, maintaining and developing Armada Music's relationship with (media) partners, including blogs, TV shows, influencers, magazines and newspapers, plus managers, artists and labels in U.S. territory;

You will send regular updates to colleagues who are in the lead of certain (release) projects. This includes a weekly report on general progress and activities as well as project-related updates (e.g. concerning releases, artists, general brand promotion);
You will follow up on initial publicity activities by phone, e-mail or via face-to-face meetings;
You will arrange publicity events, such as media interviews and personal appearances;
You will escort artists to interviews or publicity events;
You will always be looking to tap into new audiences.
Who is the person we're looking for?: You are extremely passionate about electronic dance music;
You have previously worked in the music industry;
You know your way around marketing and promotion, and you're blessed with an inventive and creative mindset;
You have extensive knowledge of dance music, and know all about Armada Music and other labels' catalogs and artists in the U.S. and beyond;
You are able to cope with pressure and changing deadlines (you can handle a fast-paced schedule of releases while working on multiple projects at the same time);
You have excellent (verbal and written) communication, interpersonal and negotiation skills;
You love working closely with the various departments of the company to get results quickly.

You will receive a monthly salary befitting your age and experience;
We offer health care coverage and other favorable secondary working conditions.

Title: Warner Music / ADA 3 Senior Manager, Legal & Business Affairs (maternity cover, full or part-time) (UK)

Description: With its broad and diverse roster of new stars and legendary artists, Warner Music Group is home to a collection of the best-known record labels in the music industry including Asylum, Atlantic, East West, Elektra, FFRR, Fueled by Ramen, Nonesuch, Parlophone, Rhino, Roadrunner, Sire, Warner Records, Warner Classics and Warner/Chappell Music, one of the world’s leading music publishers with a catalogue of more than one million copyrights worldwide.Continue to article...For more than four decades, WMG has been an industry-leading force in providing a world-class array of services designed to help artists and labels grow their careers and their businesses. Artist & Label Services is the umbrella for WEA (Warner-Elektra-Atlantic) 3 the pioneering WEA distribution and marketing network 3 and Alternative Distribution Alliance (ADA) 3 the ground-breaking global distribution company for independent artists and labels.A little bit about our team:Our UK Legal and Business Affairs team is responsible for negotiating and drafting commercial contracts on behalf of the business, as well as providing commercial and legal support. This is a temporary role for up to 12 months to cover Maternity Leave.We are seeking a qualified lawyer to provide legal and business affairs support to the distribution and label services arm of WMUK. As a Business Affairs Manager for ADA you will assist the Director of Legal and Business Affairs, ADA in providing legal and business affairs support to its marketing, promotion and digital teams.Why this could be your next big break:The role includes providing legal advice on the full spectrum of legal issues faced by the business, drafting and negotiating a variety of commercial agreements and providing in-house legal support and advice to the business.This is an exciting opportunity for a media lawyer to provide BA support to the ADA team and its independent artists and label partners in a time where the profile and opportunities for independently distributed labels and artists has never been higher. As part of Warner Music Group, ADA gives the indie community access to an unparalleled global distribution system and a dedicated team that provides a complete spectrum of marketing, merchandising, promotion, and music licensing services. As part of the Business Affairs team you will be drafting and negotiating the deals that help build and maintain the current ADA roster of indie labels and artists.Here you'll get to:Draft and negotiate distribution and label services agreements (including any amendments and extensions thereto).Instruct finance in respect of running, amending and analysing P&Ls to ascertain commercial potential of new deals.Prepare deal summaries for ADA US / UK.Prepare deal memos to seek approval from the business.Support ADA commercial team to prepare T&C's relating to label competitions.Liaise with ADA's international affiliate teams to ensure we are exercising the full scope of our distribution / manufacture / services rightsAbout you:Qualified solicitor or barrister with a minimum of 3 years post qualification experience in a music/media/rights based law firm or company OR you are not qualified but have a minimum of 5 years relevant experience in a music/media/rights based law firm or company.You have a sound understanding of relevant intellectual property law.Confident negotiator across a broad spectrum of commercial contracts, a team player but also confident working with a relative degree of autonomy in respect of matters within their experience.You have a sound commercial acumen with excellent attention to detail.You have the ability to work well under pressure and are good at prioritising workloadYou are a clear and confident communicator, personable, keen to learn and enjoy building relationships with the business and colleagues.This role will also embody a degree of supervising and delegating work to the ADA Legal Intern thus experience managing/supervising junior member(s) of staff would be an advantage but is not essential. The chosen candidate will need to be willing to help the relevant assistants on matters delegated to/being handled by the ADA Legal Intern including checking their work, answering queries and generally encouraging and advising the relevant assistants in addition to this role's own workload.It would be music to our ears if you were also:Proactive with a flexible approachAn enthusiastic music loverWhy join us?Here at Warner Music Group we're all about our people. We are one global company made up exclusively of knowledgeable, passionate, and creative people.It is the mission of every member of the WMG team around the world to create a nurturing environment for artists, songwriters, and the people behind the music – at every stage of their career. We strive to set WMG apart by embracing a philosophy of innovation 3 it's part of our company’s DNA.Consider a career at WMG and be a part of one of the most influential forces in culture today.

Title: Senior Finance Analyst (UK)
Description: ICE is the result of a big vision for the music industry that harnesses years of combined expertise in the multi-territory music market. We are the world's first integrated processing hub offering copyright, online licensing and processing services. We offer the music industry professional, reliable, accurate and efficient services that enable music societies, music publishers and the composers they represent to manage their copyright information and to receive royalties they are due for online exploitation of their works.Continue to article...As part of ICE, you will be working with some of the most knowledgeable and dedicated experts in the industry. We collaborate closely with our customers to continuously develop our products and services to ensure the best results. The markets we operate in are challenging, in terms of both their complexity and speed of change. ICE therefore requires its employees to adopt a flexible and positive approach to the developing requirements of their role and environment.We are currently looking for a: Senior Finance Analyst (Publisher Customers)This is a newly created role as a result of the company's growth, as Senior Finance Analyst, you will support the organisation in delivering tasks and processes in support of the critical monthly financial distributions to all our major publisher customers. This includes scheduling, forecasting and completing quality assurance checks on all distributions.You will support the organisation by producing the monthly management reporting packages for these customers including the reconciliation of distributions in line with specific commercial terms. In addition, you will be responsible for various analyses around revenue, costs and various non-financial information around these customers. Title: Manager, Fan Engagement (US)
Description: We are Warner Music Group, home to a broad roster of new stars and legendary artists through a collection of the best-known record labels in the music industry including Asylum, Atlantic, Big Beat, Canvasback, East West, Elektra, Erato, FFRR, Fueled by Ramen, Nonesuch, Parlophone, Reprise, Rhino, Roadrunner, Sire, Spinnin', Warner Records, Warner Classics, and Warner Music Nashville, as well as Warner Chappell Music, one of the world’s leading music publishers, with a catalog of more than one million copyrights worldwide.Continue to article...A little bit about our team:Warner Records is home to some of the most influential and innovative artists of our time. Our extraordinary roster of new and established recording artists is unparalleled, and our collective influence on music culture is without precedent. With a visionary approach toward discovering and nurturing musical talent, the Warner Records family of labels is currently home to an impressive generation of major artists.The mission of the Fan Engagement department (should you choose to accept it) is to acquire, engage, and retain fans across our digital ecosystems. We approach Digital Marketing in a fan-first, content-driven way and act as a 8creative agency' within the label. The team is responsible for any digital property that speaks directly to fans: social media, websites, email/CRM, mobile apps, and emerging technologies. Using content and creative marketing strategies, the Fan Engagement team keeps fans deeply connected to and invested in our artists.Why this could be your next big break:Do you have killer social media skills, a passion for content, and a deep knowledge of all things digital? Are you deep into urban music culture and artists? Can you take a campaign strategy from an idea on a whiteboard to a fully executed plan? Are digital best practices your best friend? Do you have a track record of delivering compelling, fan-centric digital marketing campaigns and an unmatched drive for creative excellence? Can you balance originality with the need to meet deadlines and bring to life a music artist's vision?Then Warner Records is the place for you!Warner Records is seeking an experienced and talented Fan Engagement manager to join our team. This role will strategize and execute compelling, artist-specific campaigns across all consumer-facing online touch-points (websites, email, social media platforms, etc). They will have a high degree of visibility across all company departments, artists, managers, and platform partners. They will manage timelines, budgets, content, influencers, and goals across a roster of 15-20 artists. Clear communication, excellent project management skills, and the ability to multi-task is a must!Here you'll get to:Oversee the day-to-day social media, website, and email accounts for a designated roster of Warner Records artistsDevelop, launch, and analyze digital campaigns for Warner Records artists, from inception through post-launch/sustainManage goals, strategy, and budget for each artistWork closely and collaboratively with management and internal departments to champion social-first ideas, develop editorial calendars, and execute integrated global activationsDevelop and execute paid advertising campaignsCollaborate with internal teams, including Commercial Sales, Marketing, Brand Licensing, Video Creation, and PublicityDevelop goals and performance metrics for campaigns, partnering with Analytics teams to derive insights about our artists and implement best practicesOversee creative ideation and development to deliver innovative, best-in-class campaigns for our artists and their fansPartner with social platforms to identify new opportunities and develop creative activationsMonitor campaigns regularly to understand effectiveness of strategy, making recommendations for continuous improvementReport weekly to senior staff and managers, providing actionable insights and campaign updatesWork with web/mobile development team to ensure consistent and impactful messaging across all web and mobile propertiesBuild and maintain relationships with social media platform representatives and participate in regular update meetingsBuild and maintain relationships with artists and managers, including communicating product updates and best practicesContinuously ideate and innovate within the social and digital spaceRockstar candidates will have:2-3 years professional experience in social media/digital marketing, preferably with a creative agency or entertainment companyPrior experience working across social channels, with email and web platforms, and on paid social media campaignsProven ability to build, execute and scale digital campaigns on a global levelIn-depth knowledge of social media platforms, email/CRM and web best practicesStrong organizational, project management, and decision-making skills with added attention to details and deadlinesExcellent writing, communication, and presentation skillsStrong interpersonal skills and ability to effectively work in a fast-paced environment with both creative- and business-oriented colleagues and partnersCreative thinker with passion for developing and delivering big ideas for our artistsAbility to think strategically but still execute tacticallyExtreme attention to detail with relentlessly high standardsInnate willingness to roll up your sleeves and get the job doneOperates with significant autonomyBachelor's DegreeIt would be music to our ears if you also had:Copywriting knowledgeExperience using Photoshop to edit and resize images

Title: Global Music Communication Manager (AUT)
Description: Our mission at Red Bull is to give wings to artists and music scenes around the world.Continue to article...The scope and scale of the music program is vast and as Music Communication Manager you'll play a strong role in communicating what we do in the realm of music to the world.Areas that play to your strengths (All the responsibilities we’ll trust you with) MUSIC AUDIENCEYour role will require you to understand the wide variety of music audiences and their media consumption behaviours. You will identify ways to maximize the impact of our artist and artist content projects working with your HQ colleagues and the Red Bull country network. You'll advise upon and implement activation strategies for our projects, provide regular updates on audience development, support and provide countries with guidance on their projects and develop education initiatives to increase specialised communications knowledge and competency across the Red Bull network.EARNED MEDIAIn this role, you'll lead the earned media promotion of music and artist-based content projects including digital, moving image and audio products and some event related projects. EARNED MEDIA: In this role, you'll lead the earned media promotion of music and artist-based content projects including digital, moving image and audio products and some event related projects. You'll be responsible for developing and managing the earned communication strategies for these projects, as well as project managing the development, launch and activation of the projects in collaboration with the content, programming, social, marketing and artist teams.
PROJECT MANAGEMENT: Your will manage the activations of artist and music content projects in collaboration with your counterparts, defining messaging and media angles around these projects, leading the earned media outreach with country communication teams, identifying opportunities for social influencer outreach and collaborating with the social media team to meet objectives on assigned projects.
COLLABORATION: By working closely with the insights team, you'll analyse and evaluate projects and provide meaningful learnings to the business. You'll establish strong collaboration with markets, to understand local priorities and needs. Furthermore, you'll evaluate local market communication plans, identify local optimization potentials and global release opportunities. Translating results and learnings from release plans into long term strategies and educational resources will be another focus of yours.
EXPERIENCE (Your areas of knowledge and: That matter most for this role:
3 years' of experience in a music-based communication or PR role
Experience working with music artists and entities
Clear understanding of global entertainment, music and culture industry 3 including best practices around audio and video campaigns
Ideally relationships in the global music and entertainment media landscape
Strong understanding of audience profiling, able to build relationships & to lead a network of consultants, agencies and counterparts
Well organized team player with a strong editorial and media understanding

Title: Receptionist (UK)
Description: Kobalt Music Group is looking for a receptionist to join us and to be the face of our London team. We are currently working in a sparkling office in Cannon Street and this is a fantastic opportunity for someone proactive, with strong interpersonal skills and a friendly attitude to become our front of house champion.Continue to article...First impressions count, and with an office of over 300 people, it is essential you are client focused and efficient, able to think on your feet and most of all able to remember a face to a name!! Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We're currently 600 people based across 13 offices and we're still growing. That's where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story!WHAT DOES A RECEPTIONIST AT KOBALT DO?First point of contact for all clients and visitors to the officeManaging the booking of meeting roomsManaging a large volume of calls in a professional and friendly mannerDealing with enquiries both internally and externallyGeneral administrative support to the facilities team when requiredBooking taxis for staffSorting and distributing post and deliveriesKeeping front of house looking fantastic!Prepping the beer fridge for Friday drinksWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Ability to provide professional 5* customer serviceOrganised and efficient with excellent verbal and written communication skillsAble to liaise with all staff members and clients, no matter their level of seniorityConfident but calm and in control at all times!Previous reception/administrative experience

Title: Label Manager (UK)
Description: Armada Music is a music company that operates on a global scale.Continue to article...Our aim is to be the best partner for artists in the electronic realm to work with, not only for the distribution and promotion of their music, but also in developing and shaping a solid career. As a brand, we aim to strengthen our position as a top supplier of the best music available in all electronic genres. Since 2003, our founding year, our headquarters are in Amsterdam. We started the U.S. office in 2016 and the U.K. office followed in 2017.Because of our growth, we are looking to expand our team in the U.K.We are therefore looking for a Label Manager U.K., who will play a key role in aligning the U.K. activities with the head office in Amsterdam as well as the NYC office.The main focus of the Label Manager U.K. is to devise a strategic plan for approaching the U.K. market as a music brand and self-sustainable hub for the promotion of dance music. At the same time, the Label Manager will contribute to the creation of promotional plans for singles and albums released by Armada Music. Detailed tasks would include:Product Development/ ReleasesCoordinate the production, marketing, distribution activities in relation to Armada Music's releases for U.K.-based/signed artists and labels;Maximize key opportunities and create marketing and promotional plans for global priority projects focused on the U.K. territory (and in general);Actively manage marketing and media strategy campaigns (i.e. press, television, radio etc.) with our Digital/Marketing departments;Liaise with the Amsterdam and New York-based promotions teams in order to align Armada Music's overall activities;Develop, maintain and circulate the label's U.K.-focused activities;Initiate and/or contribute to label/product meetings;Coordinate promo schedules, tours and events for the U.K. (together with promo and marketing manager);Collect and circulate sales/market data if and when available.Relationships and CommunicationWork closely with the A&R/digital/promotion and marketing teams in Amsterdam and New York;Manage relationships with Amsterdam/New York teams to ensure delivery of marketing and promo strategies for rest of world;Maintain contact with DSP partners and keep them updated on campaigns and priorities;Manage and maintain relationships with artist management and ensure that they are kept up to date on all aspects of the releases, as well as marketing and promotional activities;Provide regular updates to internal stakeholders on releases and campaigns;Keep a clear label/release administration.Business AwarenessEnsure awareness of the market (i.e. popular/dance music), music business developments, products and challenges and apply knowledge appropriately;Identify business opportunities such as possible collaboration with labels and artists, acquisitions of catalogs, initiate conversations etc.Monitor innovations and possibilities industry-wide, as well as specifically for U.K.Who is the person we're looking for?You are extremely passionate about electronic dance music;You have previously worked within the music industry;You have extensive knowledge of dance music, and know all about Armada Music and other labels' catalogs and artists in the U.K. and beyond;You are able to cope with pressure and changing deadlines exceedingly well (you can handle a fast-paced schedule of releases while working on multiple projects at the same time);You have excellent (verbal and written) communication, interpersonal and negotiation skills;You love working closely with the various departments of the company to get results quickly;You are inventive and creative, especially when looking to unlock new release strategies that could benefit a specific product.What will we offer you in return?You will be joining the ranks of a young and dynamic team;You will receive a monthly salary befitting your age and experience;We offer health care coverage and other favorable secondary working conditions.Can't contain your excitement? Send your resume and cover letter to jobs@armadamusic.com. Please make sure to put 8Label Manager U.K.' in the e-mail subject. We're looking forward to meeting you!ABOUT ARMADA MUSIC:Armada Music is the biggest independent dance music label in the world, based in Amsterdam, the Netherlands (HQ), London and New York (satellite offices). Founded in 2003, Armada Music represents labels and artists across the electronic music spectrum, including Afrojack, Andrew Rayel, Armin van Buuren, Erick Morillo, Fedde Le Grand, Gareth Emery, Kevin Saunderson, Lost Frequencies, Loud Luxury, Morgan Page and W&W.

Product Development/ Releases: Coordinate the production, marketing, distribution activities in relation to Armada Music's releases for U.K.-based/signed artists and labels;
Maximize key opportunities and create marketing and promotional plans for global priority projects focused on the U.K. territory (and in general);
Actively manage marketing and media strategy campaigns (i.e. press, television, radio etc.) with our Digital/Marketing departments;
Liaise with the Amsterdam and New York-based promotions teams in order to align Armada Music's overall activities;
Develop, maintain and circulate the label's U.K.-focused activities;
Initiate and/or contribute to label/product meetings;
Coordinate promo schedules, tours and events for the U.K. (together with promo and marketing manager);
Collect and circulate sales/market data if and when available.
Relationships and Communication: Work closely with the A&R/digital/promotion and marketing teams in Amsterdam and New York;
Manage relationships with Amsterdam/New York teams to ensure delivery of marketing and promo strategies for rest of world;
Maintain contact with DSP partners and keep them updated on campaigns and priorities;
Manage and maintain relationships with artist management and ensure that they are kept up to date on all aspects of the releases, as well as marketing and promotional activities;
Provide regular updates to internal stakeholders on releases and campaigns;
Business Awareness: Ensure awareness of the market (i.e. popular/dance music), music business developments, products and challenges and apply knowledge appropriately;
Identify business opportunities such as possible collaboration with labels and artists, acquisitions of catalogs, initiate conversations etc.
Monitor innovations and possibilities industry-wide, as well as specifically for U.K.
Who is the person we're looking for?: You are extremely passionate about electronic dance music;
You have previously worked within the music industry;
You have extensive knowledge of dance music, and know all about Armada Music and other labels' catalogs and artists in the U.K. and beyond;
You are able to cope with pressure and changing deadlines exceedingly well (you can handle a fast-paced schedule of releases while working on multiple projects at the same time);
You have excellent (verbal and written) communication, interpersonal and negotiation skills;
You love working closely with the various departments of the company to get results quickly;
You are inventive and creative, especially when looking to unlock new release strategies that could benefit a specific product.
What will we offer you in return?: You will be joining the ranks of a young and dynamic team;
You will receive a monthly salary befitting your age and experience;
We offer health care coverage and other favorable secondary working conditions.

Title: Broadcast Licensing Executive (UK)
Description: PPL is the UK's music licensing company for over 100,000 performers and recording rightsholders.Continue to article...We exist to help ensure people who dedicate their time, money and talent to making recorded music are fairly paid for their work. Our members include performers ranging from emerging grassroots artists through to established session musicians and influential global artists, as well as independent and major record companies. We have been licensing the use of recorded music in the UK since 1934.This includes when it is broadcast on radio, TV, and online, and when it is played in public (shops, bars, nightclubs, offices etc.). PPL's public performance licensing is now carried out on PPL's behalf by PPL PRS Ltd, a joint venture between PPL and PRS for Music.Through a network of international agreements with collective management organisations (CMOs) in other countries, PPL is also able to collect royalties for performers and recording rightsholders when their recorded music is played around the world. Our market-leading international collections business currently has 92 agreements around the world, covering over 90% of total global performance royalty value. In 2017 we collected £218.8 million in the UK and internationally and paid over 98,000 performers and record companies.Job SummaryThis is a customer facing, commercial role that involves issuing licences, dealing with queries and providing research and analysis to support PPL's/VPL's licensing activities. The role also involves managing relationships with a wide variety of radio, TV and online services, ensuring that licensing schemes are run smoothly and effectively.Team DescriptionPPL's Broadcast Licensing team is responsible for licensing TV and radio broadcasters to play recorded music as part of their programming. The team works with a wide range of broadcasters including the BBC, ITV, Channel 4, Five and Sky as well as commercial radio networks such as Capital, Heart and Absolute Radio.The team also develops PPL's tariffs by ensuring that they are fit for purpose in a world where the way businesses use music is constantly changing and that they strike a balance between a fair return for our members and a fair fee for users.Key requirementsExcellent written and oral communication skillsProficient in Microsoft Office (in particular, Excel)Proven record of delivering results/exceeding targets or KPIsMinimum two years' experience in a fast-paced, client-facing office or administrative roleTarget-driven with the ability to prioritise and re-prioritise according to team and wider business needsExceptional attention to detail, in both text and numbersKnowledge of and interest in the music and media industriesExperienced in delivering objectives within specified timeframesA persuasive nature with the ability to influence others to see and agree with a point of viewDuties and responsibilitiesManaging relationships with a wide portfolio of radio, TV and online services, ensuring that licensing schemes are run smoothly and effectivelyProactively identifying and contacting unlicensed broadcasters to ensure that they obtain the appropriate licence(s)Ensuring efficient administration of licensing activities by updating spreadsheets and maintaining databasesDeveloping and negotiating bespoke agreements and settlements with licensees within agreed parametersResponding to phone and e-mail enquiries regarding PPL licensing requirements for a wide variety of broadcast servicesBenefitsPPL employees have access to a wide range of benefits and discounts including a non-contractual bonus scheme and a comprehensive training and development scheme.Equality and diversity at PPLAt PPL, we are committed to ensuring that:our employees are engaged;we hire, develops and promotes the best people; andour talent pool is as wide as possible.We take equality and diversity seriously, intertwined with our people strategy, so that we can continue to develop and maintain a working culture that is fair and considerate to all.Apply todayPlease submit a CV and covering letter which answers the following question:What has motivated you to apply for this role?Unfortunately, due to the high number of applications received for every role at PPL we are only able to contact candidates we would like to progress to interview stage.

Title: Director, Digital Accounts (US)
Description: We are hiring a Director, Digital Accounts to join AWAL in Los Angeles. Reporting to the VP, Digital Accounts North America, the Director will cultivate and strengthen relationships with editorial, marketing and label relations teams at streaming services in the US.Continue to article...This role will contribute to the overall digital release strategy and work closely with the global AWAL Digital Accounts team in addition to the AWAL Marketing Directors and the Label Management teams.We are looking for a hard-working self-starter with excellent communication skills. You may be working for record label, streaming service, digital aggregator or distributor and have experience in digital accounts as well as a solid track-record of building trustworthy relationships with digital service providers. (break into two sentences). BACKGROUNDKobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the center of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL. Whether an artist is looking for the best digital distribution or a truly global campaign, AWAL has become the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers. This has created a high-quality path for artists to remain outside of the traditional label system while maintaining ownership of their masters. Our roster of clients includes artists such as Lauv, R3HAB, Timeflies, The Wombats, Betty Who, Rex Orange County, Tom Misch, and Bruno Major.We're currently over 600 people based across 13 offices and we're expecting to grow. That's where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Title: Project Manager (UK)
Description: Stones Throw is seeking a Project Manager to work in our London office.Continue to article...The project manager will work closely with the UK & Europe Label Manager to develop and deliver Stones Throw’s UK & European campaigns.The position will also provide general support in the day-to-day running of the label in the UK and Europe. The ideal candidate is a capable, enthusiastic self-starter with 2 years of relevant music industry experience, ideally at a record label or distributor, and a passion for great music.The candidate must be an excellent communicator, flexible, organized, hard-working, detail-oriented, and able to prioritize their workload. Stones Throw releases music in a range of different genres, so varied taste and broad understanding of the current musical landscape is important.The ability to hit the ground running will be important. We are a small and growing team so the position includes plenty of opportunity to get involved on all aspects of record campaigns.The position is based in our office in Hackney, East London. The salary is competitive.ResponsibilitiesProject managing select campaignsProviding general support to UK & Europe Label ManagerSetting up releases for digital and physical distributionPitching releases for digital placementUpdating our distribution partners and relevant retailers on our projectsDrafting copy such as sales notes and promo mailoutsSales reportingKeeping track of stock levels and movementsKeeping libraries of assets and contacts databases up to dateSome in-house PR and radio supportRepresenting Stones Throw at events such as gigs and label marketsSkills & ExperienceFamiliarity with Stones Throw Records and a genuine passion for the music we releaseAt least 2 years of relevant experience, ideally at either a label or distributorAn understanding of record release marketing campaigns, digital and physical retail, international markets, and distributionGood time management, with the ability to prioritize own workload and take initiativeExcellent verbal and written communication skillsDigital: MS Office, Google Apps, social media, some knowledge of the digital marketing landscapeApplicationPlease send a CV and short covering letter saying why you would be suitable for the role to jobs@stonesthrow.com with CProject Manager (UK)D in the subject line. The deadline is 1 August 2019.

Responsibilities: Project managing select campaigns
Providing general support to UK & Europe Label Manager
Setting up releases for digital and physical distribution
Pitching releases for digital placement
Updating our distribution partners and relevant retailers on our projects
Drafting copy such as sales notes and promo mailouts
Sales reporting
Keeping track of stock levels and movements
Keeping libraries of assets and contacts databases up to date
Some in-house PR and radio support
Representing Stones Throw at events such as gigs and label markets
Skills \u0026 Experience: Familiarity with Stones Throw Records and a genuine passion for the music we release
At least 2 years of relevant experience, ideally at either a label or distributor
An understanding of record release marketing campaigns, digital and physical retail, international markets, and distribution
Good time management, with the ability to prioritize own workload and take initiative
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Digital: MS Office, Google Apps, social media, some knowledge of the digital marketing landscape
Application: Please send a CV and short covering letter saying why you would be suitable for the role to jobs@stonesthrow.com with CProject Manager (UK)D in the subject line. The deadline is 1 August 2019.
