Title: Marketing Manager (NL/US)
Description: bitbird is a record label and creative company spearheaded by San Holo, based in The Netherlands.Continue to article...We’re looking for a Marketing Manager to strengthen our label team. Your responsibility will be ensuring the commercial success of our releases through the creation and execution of press campaigns, radio campaigns, and overseeing the general day to day marketing tasks. You will be creating well rounded and creative campaigns to create excitement around releases, activations and events. You’ll report directly to our Label Manager, work closely with bitbird’s Digital Marketing Manager and founders, and with Heroic, our closely affiliated artist management agency.Responsibilities:Creating marketing campaigns: Working closely with our Label Manager to create marketing campaigns for our releases fitting to the music’s commercial potential and style. Dividing tasks between internal and external teams and execution of the campaigns.Execute press release campaigns: Work with artists and their managers signed to bitbird to craft compelling narrative stories to go with their releases, creating press releases, writing artist bios, and pitching releases for press support/coverage.Outbound Marketing: Pitch new music to Youtube Music Channels, TikTok influencers, and any creative plans that are included in the marketing campaign.SoundCloud repost trading: coordinate with other labels, management teams, and artists to facilitate repost trading on SoundCloud for music releases.Spotify playlist trading: coordinate with our A&R team to facilitate submissions for consideration on bitbird playlists.Liaison for our external radio plugging teams: communicate weekly with our radio partners and helping strategize the best approach to each territory/market for high-priority releases.Creating compelling copy for various company purposes: Write copy for monthly newsletters, bitbird-affiliated pitch decks, maintaining upkeep on bitbird one-sheets.RequirementsMusic publicity or marketing experience. You have a strong background working in music or entertainment publicity in all aspects for the past 2 years; including pitching music releases for press coverage, writing biographies, Youtube Channel upload support, Spotify playlist trading, SoundCloud repost trading.Writing experience. You have a strong background in music media writing and have spent at least 2 years working on your skills as a music writer, contributing to music editorials, magazines, and blogs.Experience in the music industry. Having worked as an artist manager, blogger, marketing manager, publicist – anything that gives you context on our business.A solid understanding of dance radio territories is a bonus. This role requires you to liaise with our radio plugger partners for particular releases and an understanding of the key influential radio stations of these territories will be greatly beneficial in overseeing radio plugging.Love for bitbird, our message, artists and music.Fluent in English. Fluent in Dutch is a bonus.Work hard, play hard mentality. Our biggest market is the US and even though we’re based in The Netherlands, a lot of the action happens between 17:00 – 20:00 CET.Willing to try, not afraid to fail. You need a willingness to learn and master unfamiliar skills. We operate in a unique, non-traditional way and require you to adapt to our operations. We are after learning and embrace that failure is a part of that process.An excitement to learn. Social media and advertising is constantly changing. You need to have a finger on the pulse of the digital space and be excited to learn more as it evolves.Able to work 40 hours a week. Ability to come into our office in The Hague, or work with our staff in NYC is a huge bonus.Do you have what it takes?Submit your resume and a motivational letter explaining your history in the industry, experience with music marketing and publicity, why you think you’re a good fit for this role and our team and how you think we can improve upon our current marketing. We’ll get back to you shortly.

Responsibilities:: Creating marketing campaigns: Working closely with our Label Manager to create marketing campaigns for our releases fitting to the music’s commercial potential and style. Dividing tasks between internal and external teams and execution of the campaigns.

Execute press release campaigns: Work with artists and their managers signed to bitbird to craft compelling narrative stories to go with their releases, creating press releases, writing artist bios, and pitching releases for press support/coverage.
Outbound Marketing: Pitch new music to Youtube Music Channels, TikTok influencers, and any creative plans that are included in the marketing campaign.
SoundCloud repost trading: coordinate with other labels, management teams, and artists to facilitate repost trading on SoundCloud for music releases.
Spotify playlist trading: coordinate with our A&R team to facilitate submissions for consideration on bitbird playlists.
Liaison for our external radio plugging teams: communicate weekly with our radio partners and helping strategize the best approach to each territory/market for high-priority releases.
Creating compelling copy for various company purposes: Write copy for monthly newsletters, bitbird-affiliated pitch decks, maintaining upkeep on bitbird one-sheets.
Requirements: Music publicity or marketing experience. You have a strong background working in music or entertainment publicity in all aspects for the past 2 years; including pitching music releases for press coverage, writing biographies, Youtube Channel upload support, Spotify playlist trading, SoundCloud repost trading.

Writing experience. You have a strong background in music media writing and have spent at least 2 years working on your skills as a music writer, contributing to music editorials, magazines, and blogs.
Experience in the music industry. Having worked as an artist manager, blogger, marketing manager, publicist , anything that gives you context on our business.

A solid understanding of dance radio territories is a bonus. This role requires you to liaise with our radio plugger partners for particular releases and an understanding of the key influential radio stations of these territories will be greatly beneficial in overseeing radio plugging.
Love for bitbird, our message, artists and music.
Fluent in English. Fluent in Dutch is a bonus.
Work hard, play hard mentality. Our biggest market is the US and even though we’re based in The Netherlands, a lot of the action happens between 17:00 , 20:00 CET.

Willing to try, not afraid to fail. You need a willingness to learn and master unfamiliar skills. We operate in a unique, non-traditional way and require you to adapt to our operations. We are after learning and embrace that failure is a part of that process.
An excitement to learn. Social media and advertising is constantly changing. You need to have a finger on the pulse of the digital space and be excited to learn more as it evolves.
Able to work 40 hours a week. Ability to come into our office in The Hague, or work with our staff in NYC is a huge bonus.
Do you have what it takes?: Submit your resume and a motivational letter explaining your history in the industry, experience with music marketing and publicity, why you think you’re a good fit for this role and our team and how you think we can improve upon our current marketing. We’ll get back to you shortly.

Title: Product Manager (US)
Description: Mack Avenue Music Group is a critically acclaimed record company with offices in Los Angeles and Detroit.Continue to article...With a core emphasis on jazz and soul/R&B, Mack Avenue boasts a diverse roster of musical innovators such as Christian McBride, Cecile McLorin Salvant, Macy Gray, Billy Childs, Kirk Whalum, The Soul Rebels, Julian Lage, Jeffrey Osborne, Alfredo Rodriguez, Joey DeFrancesco, Chucho Valdez, Cyrille Aimee, Jonathan Butler, Kenny Garrett, Stanley Clark, Tia Fuller, Christian Sands, The Yellowjackets and many others.Mack Avenue Music Group is seeking a passionate and skilled music marketer, to be based in our Los Angeles office. The Product Manager is responsible for the timely creation and execution of marketing plans that align with the overall vision and strategy for assigned releases, maximizing sales and exposure; reflective of strategic goals, release-specific objectives and financial guidelines. And most importantly, to ensure the consistency of messaging and imaging between the artist and their music.Job Description:The Product Manager is the central point of contact responsible for planning and executing creative marketing initiatives across our roster of artist projects. This includes building concise marketing plans and budgets to achieve the goals that help drive the artist's vision & brand, all while managing resources responsibly and ensuring that all departments have the tools and information they need to keep both the artist and the company aligned with one consistent voice. This position will interact with all label departments, artists, artist management and executives of the company.Detailed Job Description/ Responsibilities:Develop and manage creative goal-oriented marketing plans with accompanying budgets.Act as the primary liaison between the artist/management and the label in all marketing related issues.Must have experience in all areas of digital marketing, social media, advertising, video, etc.Contribute both creatively and editorially to Mack Avenue owned media: website, socials and newsletter.Create and maintain content calendar for assigned artists with proper understanding of audience.Support the Commerce department to maximize relationships with key partners such as: Amazon, Apple, Deezer, Facebook, Instagram, Pandora, Spotify, Twitter and YouTube.Success in developing Direct-to-Consumer campaigns and targeted products including physical, merchandise, collectibles, bundles, etc.Maximize the use of research and insights to create custom presentations and pitches to prospective strategic partners for our artists and projects.Create, negotiate and manage relationships with key brand partners and agencies.Leverage data and insights to inform strategy across physical, digital and social channels.Conduct Cpost-mortemD analysis on marketing activations to draw conclusions and refine future initiatives.Support International department to ensure marketing plans and project needs are met and executed on a global basis.Liaison with Creative Services to ensure all deadlines are met. Initiate creation of materials, select and approve visual imagery, marketing tools and promotional product.Required Competencies/Skills:Minimum 2- 3 years of marketing experience in the music/entertainment industry.Thrives in a dynamic and fast-paced, high volume environment focused on both internal and external selling of ideas and concepts.Ability to develop strong professional relationships with key executives, including artists and artist managers.Must be highly organized and capable of developing and keeping to a project timeline. Ability to handle multiple projects and meet deadlines.Exceptional writing, verbal communication and negotiation skills.Creative, resourceful, proactive, self-starter, entrepreneurial spirit.Proven Project/Budget Management Skills.Skilled in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) or Apple (Pages, Numbers, Keynote) to create and manage multiple artist schedules, plans and budgets.Education Required/Preferred:4 year undergraduate degreeCompetitive salary and benefits. Learn more about us at www.mackavenue.com.Email your resume and cover letter to jobs@mackavenue.com. No phone calls, please.

Title: International Operations Specialist (UK)
Description: As a leading licensing business at the heart of the UK's music industry, PPL ensures those who invest their time, talent and money to make recorded music are compensated and recognised when their music is used by businesses or broadcast on TV, radio or online. Based in Soho our committed, passionate and dynamic culture will embrace your career with PPL as you help support our business as we deliver to our customers.Continue to article...To find out a little more here's a videoâ¦What you'll be doingâ¦The International Operations Specialist is responsible for providing high quality and complex administrative support to the International team. Collaboration creates great things; everyone's talent deserves recognition and each of our members is important to us. We are the professional face for our clients. You'll be surrounded by beautiful countryside so the ability to drive would be the easiest way of getting to work.Starting salary in the region of £20-25k, dependent on experience.Please send your CV by email, quoting PD0519 in the subject heading, and accompanied by a covering letter (with current salary) which demonstrates affinity with Music Concierge and playlisting, to vacancies@musicconcierge.co.ukNo phone calls or agencies please.www.musicconcierge.co.uk

Title: International Label Manager, ADA (UK)
Description: With its broad and diverse roster of new stars and legendary artists, Warner Music Group is home to a collection of the best-known record labels in the music industry including Asylum, Atlantic, East West, Elektra, FFRR, Fueled by Ramen, Nonesuch, Parlophone, Rhino, Roadrunner, Sire, Warner Bros., Warner Classics and Warner Chappell Music, one of the world’s leading music publishers with a catalogue of more than one million copyrights worldwide.Continue to article...For more than four decades, WMG has been an industry-leading force in providing a world-class array of services designed to help artists and labels grow their careers and their businesses.Artist & Label Services is the umbrella for WEA (Warner-Elektra-Atlantic) 3 the pioneering WEA distribution and marketing network 3 and Alternative Distribution Alliance (ADA) 3 the ground-breaking global distribution company for independent artists and labels.A little bit about our team:ADA's international department oversees the overall global business outside the US and UK market. Our role is to provide guidance, support and act as the central communications hub to our international distributed labels, UK/US teams and the international territories.We also bring in new and exciting music to ADA worldwide from across the globe. We plan and execute new release and catalogue strategy and equip the physical and digital sales teams with international marketing, promotion and sales information.We set up distribution strategies as well as design and execute label services in conjunction with the international affiliate frontline teams, our own staff and third parties.Why this could be your next big break:The person in this role will focus on two main areas; building ADA's footprint in Eastern Europe as well as managing the day to day relationship with our key Europe-based partners.As ADA continues to grow, we are expanding into new parts of the world every day. This role will be responsible for assisting in growing ADA's partners business in Europe, by managing and developing the wider WMG licensee relationships for ADA, and by supporting existing ADA partners in the region.Here you'll get to:Foster meaningful relationship with Europe-based 3rd party labels, artists and label partners, ensuring we are maximizing any revenue opportunities for their repertoire globally.Develop ADA's eastern European footprint, by working closely with the WMG licensee partners in the region and be the main point of contact for developing business countries in Europe, such as eastern European WM licensees with regards to ADA label partners and their releases.Update international ADA teams and WMG offices on new releases from (licensee) partners, in collaboration with the ADA International Label Management team and the ADA US / UK label management teams,Provide licensee companies' commercial teams with key international marketing & sales information on new releases, on-going priorities, & general ADA partner activity.Assist regional digital account management teams as well as licensee company commercial teams with assembling pitches, campaign execution and compiling recaps.Oversee and coordinate the label services offering for EE region as well as services offering of licensee labels in ROW territories, in conjunction with the ADA International team.Work alongside International Director, Int'l label managers and ADA int'l teams to coordinate local release plans for both distribution and label services.Work with int'l label managers and operations to support label onboarding in the region.About you:You will have a general understanding of physical and digital distribution globally, and a great understanding of this in (Eastern) Europe.You will have a track record of successfully building strong talent and management relationships.Have a broad knowledge of contemporary music scene and lifestyle.You will be motivated, creative, innovative, highly organized and detail orientated.You will be able to communicate effectively, and compassionately with all our stakeholders.You will be able to demonstrate and use your knowledge and experience in the European market place and territories.You will be passionate about independent music and record labels.You will be working across many different territories, it will be important for you to be flexible, dynamic and diplomatic so you can effectively adapt to business requirements.It would be music to our ears if you also had:5 years of music industry experience.Fluent in English. Anymore languages are a plus.University / Bachelor degree.Good working knowledge of Word, Excel, MS Office, and PowerPoint.Willing and able to travel internationally if business requires.Why join us?Here at Warner Music Group we're all about our people. We are one global company made up exclusively of knowledgeable, passionate, and creative people.It is the mission of every member of the WMG team around the world to create a nurturing environment for artists, songwriters, and the people behind the music – at every stage of their career. We strive to set WMG apart by embracing a philosophy of innovation 3 it's part of our company’s DNA.Consider a career at WMG and be a part of one of the most influential forces in culture today.Love this job and want to apply?Click the CApplyD link at the top of the page, or apply directly with your LinkedIn. Applying with LinkedIn will import all of the information you put in your profile, but will still allow you to upload a resume and cover letter.Don't be discouraged if you don't hear from us right away. We're taking our time to review all resumes, and to find the best people for WMG.Thanks for your interest in working for WMG. We love it here, and think you will, too.We are an equal opportunity employer that is committed to inclusion and diversity and will not discriminate against any applicant or employee on the basis of age, sex, sexual orientation, race, color, creed, religion, ethnicity, national origin, alienage or citizenship, disability, marital status, familial status, military or veteran status, or any other legal recognized protected basis under federal, state or local laws, regulations or ordinances.We are committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation because of a disability for any part of the application or employment process, please send an e-mail to Recruitment@wmg.com and let us know the nature of your request and your contact information.

complex cases while meeting short deadlines and managing interruptions.You will be customer-focused, always thinking a step ahead to keep the customer informed and anticipating future queries.You will be keen to go above and beyond to ensure your customers are looked-after, and to contribute actively to our close-knit, passionate team with your own ideas and vision.You will have previous experience with databases and understanding of a customer relationship systemYou will be self-motivated individual willing to go the extra mileYou will have strong administrative skills with good working knowledge of MS Office packages, preferably advanced excel skills.Excellent communication skills with the ability to influence and persuade.An excellent understanding of effective customer service, with respect to manner, tone, and approachAdvanced written and verbal communication skills and the ability to communicate professionally and empathetically with a variety of peopleExperience in dealing with data, in terms of maintenance, accuracy and basic analytics, and competency with finance and mathematics is desirableThe ability to self-motivate, work independently, and manage one's own Please click the 8Apply Now'Equality and Diversity at PPL PPL are committed to equal opportunities and inclusivity, therefore we welcome and encourage applicants from all sections of the community.Unfortunately, due to the high number of application received for every role here at PPL we are only able to contact candidates we'd like to progress to interview stage.Here at PPL we are proud to operate in a genuine, open and straightforward way while continuing to maximise all opportunities as they arise striving to represent PPL's members. Our job is to protect our members' rights, and make sure their talent and investment is rewarded fairly. We take an entrepreneurial approach, working together to create new ways to deliver better service and solve business challenges. Collaboration creates great things; everyone's talent deserves recognition and each of our members is important to us. We are the professional face for our clients. We serve with a smile and go the extra mile when representing them. We share a passion for music 3 that's why we do what we do.

Title: Head of Marketing, Digital Music (JP)
Description: We are looking for an experienced Head of Marketing, Amazon Music for our JP digital music business. As the Head of Marketing, Amazon Music, you will have responsibility supporting the digital music category in areas such as mass & direct marketing, traffic generation, web & device merchandising, creative/visual design, acquisition, retention, partner marketing, and brand strategy. This senior leadership position will manage a high performing team whose primary goal is to promote category awareness, drive acquisition and customer retention.Continue to article...This is an opportunity to directly influence and grow a business that is having a material impact on the digital consumer space. It requires a leader who will think and act big, exercising a bias for action coupled with thoughtful experimentation. The ideal candidate has strong product vision with unparalleled customer instincts and an ability to influence diverse teams. S/he will balance creativity with analytics and agility with operational discipline. Candidates should be both idea generators and builders, with a strong sense of accountability, and a keen focus on delivering great customer experiences and strong, measurable results. S/he should thrive in a culture of decisions based on analytics, be comfortable diving deep into the data, yet able to step-back and communicate both the strategic and immediate implications on priorities.Day-to-day the Head of Marketing, Amazon Music JP will:Be responsible for driving traffic to the digital music websites and device apps through a mix of untargeted and targeted marketing channelsDeliver new customer acquisition strategies along with churn management plansManage the look and feel of the different digital music storefronts, including daily operation and strategic planningClosely monitor customer feedback/needs through surveys and researchLiaise closely with the product development teams to drive innovation aimed at improving customer experience, and strengthening label relationshipsProvide input to defining the customer experience and customer satisfaction and represent that to senior executives as well as the digital music teamOvercome and solve complex obstacles that impact the digital music marketing & merchandising and product discovery, effectively escalate issues across all levels in the companyDrive sustainable process improvements across the functions which create year-over-year efficiency in the categoryIdentify new approaches for improving marketing & merchandising and category awareness to better manage the customer experience and the team over the long runFocus on talent management of the Marketing team which includes effectively hiring and developing the best talent, and creating an environment which motivates people to thrive in their expertise and deliver aggressive resultsDevelop and execute customer acquisition and churn management strategies for a subscription music service.Develop a clear customer segmentation and brand messaging strategy for Amazon Music.Drive customer engagement through targeted marketing and merchandising to convert existing Prime subscribers into active Prime Music customers, with increased listening frequency.Create measurable, scalable and innovative marketing campaigns 3 both offline and online 3 to meet goals around new customer acquisition.Develop an organizational structure that aligns the talent to the strategic objectives for the group. This leader will be responsible for continuing to build an extremely high caliber team.Establish strong industry relationships with key executives at the record labels, distribution companies and artist management.Reporting directly into Director, Amazon Music, and this role will be located in Tokyo, Japan.BASIC QUALIFICATIONSUniversity degree minimum; MBA or further engineering, marketing or analytical education will be advantageous.The ideal candidate will have demonstrated the ability to identify and solve ambiguous problems. In particular, he or she has a history of identifying customer needs and inventing new ways to meet those needs.This leader will be a big thinker who is perpetually focused on the strategy for the coming years yet is also tactical.He or she thrives in a high-energy, fast-paced environment.The right person will be known for his/her ability to communicate vision and drive execution and work across the team.He or she will have a strong reputation for attracting and developing the best talent.He or she will be someone who stays connected to the details of his or her business.Finally, this leader will be known for maintaining composure and steady vision in a fast moving, dynamic environment. Experience in the subscription Media Industry preferred.Specifically, he or she will have:Proven Product or Marketing leadership within a B2C ecommerce environmentDefined or marketed an online subscription programHighly analytical; with experience of taking data driven decisions and draw insights from data and testing to inform priorities and strategyDemonstrable success of building and launching online products.Clear evidence of managing and motivating a large team(s), both direct and indirect reportsTechnology affinity and track record of employing systems to drive simplification and changeAble to balance strategy with strong operational skills – able to understand, deconstruct, streamline and optimize complex, large-scale business processesPREFERRED QUALIFICATIONSProfessional traits that are not unique to this position, but necessary for Amazon leaders:Is a superb negotiator, at ease sitting down with top level executives with vendors and manufacturersIs highly analytical and structuredExhibits excellent business judgmentHires great people, develops great peopleHas relentlessly high standardsThinks strategically, but stays on top of tactical executionExpects and requires innovation of their teamThinks big and has convictionsIs action and results orientedHas the innate ability to inspire passion in others.

Day-to-day the Head of Marketing, Amazon: Be responsible for driving traffic to the digital music websites and device apps through a mix of untargeted and targeted marketing channels
Deliver new customer acquisition strategies along with churn management plans
Manage the look and feel of the different digital music storefronts, including daily operation and strategic planning
Closely monitor customer feedback/needs through surveys and research
Liaise closely with the product development teams to drive innovation aimed at improving customer experience, and strengthening label relationships
Provide input to defining the customer experience and customer satisfaction and represent that to senior executives as well as the digital music team
Overcome and solve complex obstacles that impact the digital music marketing & merchandising and product discovery, effectively escalate issues across all levels in the company
Drive sustainable process improvements across the functions which create year-over-year efficiency in the category
Identify new approaches for improving marketing & merchandising and category awareness to better manage the customer experience and the team over the long run

Focus on talent management of the Marketing team which includes effectively hiring and developing the best talent, and creating an environment which motivates people to thrive in their expertise and deliver aggressive results
Develop and execute customer acquisition and churn management strategies for a subscription music service.
Develop a clear customer segmentation and brand messaging strategy for Amazon Music.
Drive customer engagement through targeted marketing and merchandising to convert existing Prime subscribers into active Prime Music customers, with increased listening frequency.
Create measurable, scalable and innovative marketing campaigns 3 both offline and online 3 to meet goals around new customer acquisition.
Develop an organizational structure that aligns the talent to the strategic objectives for the group. This leader will be responsible for continuing to build an extremely high caliber team.
Establish strong industry relationships with key executives at the record labels, distribution companies and artist management.
Reporting directly into Director, Amazon Music, and this role will be located in Tokyo, Japan.
BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: University degree minimum; MBA or further engineering, marketing or analytical education will be advantageous.

The ideal candidate will have demonstrated the ability to identify and solve ambiguous problems. In particular, he or she has a history of identifying customer needs and inventing new ways to meet those needs.
This leader will be a big thinker who is perpetually focused on the strategy for the coming years yet is also tactical.
He or she thrives in a high-energy, fast-paced environment.
The right person will be known for his/her ability to communicate vision and drive execution and work across the team.
He or she will have a strong reputation for attracting and developing the best talent.
He or she will be someone who stays connected to the details of his or her business.
Finally, this leader will be known for maintaining composure and steady vision in a fast moving, dynamic environment. Experience in the subscription Media Industry preferred.
Specifically, he or she will have:: Proven Product or Marketing leadership within a B2C ecommerce environment
Defined or marketed an online subscription program
Highly analytical; with experience of taking data driven decisions and draw insights from data and testing to inform priorities and strategy
Demonstrable success of building and launching online products.
Clear evidence of managing and motivating a large team(s), both direct and indirect reports
Technology affinity and track record of employing systems to drive simplification and change
Able to balance strategy with strong operational skills , able to understand, deconstruct, streamline and optimize complex, large-scale business processes
PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: Is a superb negotiator, at ease sitting down with top level executives with vendors and manufacturers
Is highly analytical and structured
Exhibits excellent business judgment
Hires great people, develops great people
Has relentlessly high standards
Thinks strategically, but stays on top of tactical execution
Expects and requires innovation of their team
Thinks big and has convictions
Is action and results oriented
Has the innate ability to inspire passion in others.

Title: Finance Manager (UK)
Description: Established in 2007, Downtown Music Publishing is one of the world’s leading rights-management firms with offices in major music markets around the globe, including New York, Nashville, Los Angeles, London, Amsterdam, Tokyo, Paris, and Sydney.Continue to article...The company’s catalog spans over a hundred years of popular music, encompassing some of the world’s most beloved songwriters and artists including The Beatles, John Lennon & Yoko Ono, Ryan Tedder, Benny Blanco, Hans Zimmer, The Kinks, One Direction, MÃ¶tley CrÃ¼e, Robin Thicke, Naughty Boy, Phish, Hardwell, Jason Isbell, Sturgill Simpson, Santigold, and Tori Amos. Downtown Music Publishing is wholly owned by Downtown, which also owns New York-based Downtown Music Studios, AVL Digital Group and Songtrustâ, the world's largest technology platform for songwriting and music publishing royalties, which powers global royalty collection from over 120 territories and more than 20,000 unique income sources worldwide.Downtown is seeking a Finance Manager to join our team in the London office. Our ideal candidate possesses a degree in accountancy or a related major, an accountancy qualification, thrives in a fast-paced and agile organization, and has a strong interest in the music industry.What will you do:Perform all aspects of cash receipt and cash disbursement accounts operations, including daily reconciliation of cash balances and ensuring our vendors are paid on a timely basis.Work with our NY-based Finance team to develop and follow processes for monthly and quarterly closing of the ledger. Specifically, you will:Reconciling balance sheet accounts each monthPost inter-company journalsAnalyze and provide commentary on fluctuations/variances in our key accounts each monthConsolidate our non-US entities into a financial reporting package (balance sheet, profit and loss, cash flows)Prepare VAT filings and returns for our UK and IOM-based entities on a quarterly basis.Secure requisite tax withholding firms (e.g. CORs) on an annual basis.Manage the treasury function for our UK and International bank accounts, including analyses of multiple currencies to mitigate foreign exchange risk.Prepare annual statutory reports for Companies House and work with international accountancy firms to assist in preparation for non-UK statutory reporting.Work with our NY-based royalties team to assist in processing and accounting of royalties owed to clients.What skills do you have:A degree in Accountancy or related area of study.UK accountancy qualification (i.e. ACA, ACCA, or CIMA).3 years accounting experience, including: working in accounts payable/trade creditors function, managing a treasury/banking function with multiple currencies, and accounting for VAT.1 years managing an accounts function, including: execution of monthly or quarterly processes to close the ledger, reconciliation of accounts, posting inter-company journals, consolidation of multiple entities, and analyses of financial statements.Experience with preparing and submitting VAT returns and familiarity with standard tax withholding forms (e.g. CORs).Strong proficiency with Microsoft Excel, and a big plus if you have skills with Excel macros, Power Query, Power Pivot, and/or Power BI.Numerically obsessed, highly meticulous, and organized.Accountable and take ownership of projects you undertake.Analytical and curious – you ask a lot of questions.Ability to communicate financial information in a clear, professional manner.Why you want to work here:The chance to work in our amazing office in Covent GardensAmple opportunities to work collaboratively with a fantastic teamRich benefit programCareer growth opportunitiesCycle to work scheme and Season Ticket LoansInvitations from partners and clients to attend shows / eventsEnsuring a diverse and inclusive workplace where we learn from each other is core to Downtown’s values. We welcome people of different backgrounds, experiences, abilities and perspectives and encourage all to apply.

What will you do:: Perform all aspects of cash receipt and cash disbursement accounts operations, including daily reconciliation of cash balances and ensuring our vendors are paid on a timely basis.
Work with our NY-based Finance team to develop and follow processes for monthly and quarterly closing of the ledger. Specifically, you will:
Reconciling balance sheet accounts each month
Post inter-company journals
Analyze and provide commentary on fluctuations/variances in our key accounts each month
Consolidate our non-US entities into a financial reporting package (balance sheet, profit and loss, cash flows)
Prepare VAT filings and returns for our UK and IOM-based entities on a quarterly basis.
Secure requisite tax withholding firms (e.g. CORs) on an annual basis.
Manage the treasury function for our UK and International bank accounts, including analyses of multiple currencies to mitigate foreign exchange risk.
Prepare annual statutory reports for Companies House and work with international accountancy firms to assist in preparation for non-UK statutory reporting.
Work with our NY-based royalties team to assist in processing and accounting of royalties owed to clients.
What skills do you have:: A degree in Accountancy or related area of study.
UK accountancy qualification (i.e. ACA, ACCA, or CIMA).
3 years accounting experience, including: working in accounts payable/trade creditors function, managing a treasury/banking function with multiple currencies, and accounting for VAT.

1 years managing an accounts function, including: execution of monthly or quarterly processes to close the ledger, reconciliation of accounts, posting inter-company journals, consolidation of multiple entities, and analyses of financial statements.
Experience with preparing and submitting VAT returns and familiarity with standard tax withholding forms (e.g. CORs).
Strong proficiency with Microsoft Excel, and a big plus if you have skills with Excel macros, Power Query, Power Pivot, and/or Power BI.
Numerically obsessed, highly meticulous, and organized.
Accountable and take ownership of projects you undertake.
Analytical and curious , you ask a lot of questions.
Ability to communicate financial information in a clear, professional manner.

Title: Assistant Manager, Content Operations (UK)
Description: We're looking for an Assistant Manager, Content Operations specialist to join this critical function within AWAL's Digital Operations & Supply Chain team.Continue to article...You'll be responsible for getting our repertoire ready for release on streaming platforms, running the rule over new frontline submissions and catalogue products alike, as well as maintaining excellent operational relationships with our priority partner.You'll be comfortable dealing with large volumes of content and have an innate passion for debating the finer points of digital release metadata. AWAL is already an Apple Preferred partner and a Recommended Label Distributor at Spotify, come and help us be even better.BACKGROUNDKobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the centre of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists and label release music through AWAL.Whether an artist or label is looking for the best digital distribution or a truly global campaign, AWAL has become the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers alongside forward thinking and innovative labels.  The leading artists who have already chosen AWAL includes Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Lauv, Tom Misch, Rex Orange County, The Wombats, Bruno Major, Neneh Cherry as well as leading independent labels such as Glassnote and Downtown.We deliver audio and video to all of the key platforms using our bespoke supply chain system, powered by Kobalt's market leading technology, making thousands of deliveries per week.We're currently 600 people based across 13 offices and we're still growing.WHAT DOES ASSISTANT MANAGER, CONTENT OPERATIONS AT AWAL DO?QC release submissions in accordance with partner metadata specifications.Provide structured feedback for client-facing teams when releases fail checks.Carry out product metadata updates as required across the AWAL catalogue.Work with Product and Digital Supply Chain teams to continually review and optimise QC processes with a view to scale.Contribute to internal Content Operations documentation, including up-to-date DSP best practices and metadata style guide.Conduct monthly performance review, including analysis of our iTunes Provider Scorecard.Work with client-facing teams to develop tools and resources to increase first time success rate for uploaded content.WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? Experience in a Content Operations or Digital Supply Chain role, reviewing incoming content.Good knowledge of music metadata in a digital distribution context, particularly the iTunes and Spotify style guides.Ability to communicate effectively with non-technical audiences regarding complex metadata issues.Experience working with content management systems (iTunes Connect and Spotify preferred).Meticulous attention to detail.Bonus points for experience with command line tools, in particular iTunes Transporter.Extra bonus points for knowledge of audio-visual metadata requirements, and experience of reviewing short form music video.

BACKGROUND: Kobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the centre of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists and label release music through AWAL.
WHAT DOES ASSISTANT MANAGER, CONTENT OPE: QC release submissions in accordance with partner metadata specifications.
Provide structured feedback for client-facing teams when releases fail checks.
Carry out product metadata updates as required across the AWAL catalogue.
Work with Product and Digital Supply Chain teams to continually review and optimise QC processes with a view to scale.
Contribute to internal Content Operations documentation, including up-to-date DSP best practices and metadata style guide.
Conduct monthly performance review, including analysis of our iTunes Provider Scorecard.
Work with client-facing teams to develop tools and resources to increase first time success rate for uploaded content.
WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKIN: Experience in a Content Operations or Digital Supply Chain role, reviewing incoming content.
Good knowledge of music metadata in a digital distribution context, particularly the iTunes and Spotify style guides.
Ability to communicate effectively with non-technical audiences regarding complex metadata issues.
Experience working with content management systems (iTunes Connect and Spotify preferred).
Meticulous attention to detail.
Bonus points for experience with command line tools, in particular iTunes Transporter.
Extra bonus points for knowledge of audio-visual metadata requirements, and experience of reviewing short form music video.

This includes representing the company at industry functions and exhibitions across the UK and occasionally abroad if required.Your Profile.Experience working in a client facing or customer focused environment or experience working in a production music company / within the TV media industryStrong technical background, knowledge of MAC & PC functionalityExcellent interpersonal skills and the ability to easily build relationships with others and an impeccable phone mannerEnthusiastic and creative, keen to contribute idea'sSelf motivator and avid team playerOrganised with excellent eye for detailKeen interest in production music, film, TV and advertising 3 working experience in this area is a plus!Genuine interest and knowledge of a wide variety of music stylesWhat you should know about us.Will you fit in with the crowd at BMG Production Music? Well, we are made up of music lovers, makers and shakers! And we love to share our passion for music with as many broadcasting, film, advertising and interactive partners as possible.As a part of BMG 3 The New Music Company, BMG Production Music has quickly emerged as one of the biggest new players in the dynamic production and bespoke music business and is one of BMG's fastest growing business units, owning and representing some of the best production music catalogues out there.We are a division of the Bertelsmann Group which includes Penguin Random House, RTL Group and Arvato.Head to our Linkedin, Instagram and twitter channels to get a snippet into what It's like to work at BMG UK!Check out BMG Production Music here.Head to our site, Instagram and twitter channels to get a snippet into what It's like to work at Deep East Music!https://www.linkedin.com/company/bmg-production-music/https://www.instagram.com/bmgpmuk/Tweets by BMGPM_UK

Manage designated clients, building and nurturing the relationships to a high standard

Experience working in a client facing or customer focused environment or experience working in a production music company / within the TV media industry
Strong technical background, knowledge of MAC & PC functionality
Excellent interpersonal skills and the ability to easily build relationships with others and an impeccable phone manner
Enthusiastic and creative, keen to contribute idea's
Self motivator and avid team player
Organised with excellent eye for detail
Keen interest in production music, film, TV and advertising 3 working experience in this area is a plus!
Genuine interest and knowledge of a wide variety of music styles

Title: Director of Operations (US)
Description: Constant works with creative artists of all disciplines to help execute their artistic vision, amplify their voice and create long term, sustainable businesses around what they do best.Continue to article...We have offices in Los Angeles and New York, and currently represent Future Islands, Parra, Washed Out, and Explosions in the Sky, among others. Founded in 2004, Constant provides exclusive worldwide management and consulting services to an established roster of musicians, visual artists, producers, and composers. With an insatiable thirst for innovation and creative collaboration, we pride ourselves on working with the most talented artists in their respective fields.Director of Operations OverviewConstant is looking for an experienced Director of Operations to oversee day-to-day operations and supervise our team of 4-5 people working from remote locations.This position can be based in Los Angeles (first preference), New York City (second preference), or elsewhere (third preference, but open to ideas).You will report directly to the CEO and be responsible for a variety of client specific and company-wide projects, including directing workflow, overseeing the staff and HR-related duties, creating budgets / managing expenses, coordinating projects with external partners, and furthering business development initiatives.You will ensure that Constant's business is well-coordinated and productive by analyzing, managing, and improving its processes, and will be responsible for the overall efficiency of the business.A balanced understanding of big-picture concepts and goals in the creative arts, and the micro details that go into achieving those goals is essential.You will develop strategic plans by studying technological and financial opportunities, presenting conclusions and recommending objectives.You are fully committed to being an enthusiastic, active member of a small, busy team in an exciting period for the company.You have a solid grasp of our roster and align aesthetically with our vision: understanding the growth potential for our clients is essential.RequirementsThe ideal candidate is a proven leader who can play a major role in moving the business forward. The position is ever-evolving and requires strict attention to detail and creative problem solving — each of these characteristics is non-negotiable:3 years applicable experience in business administration, artist and/or project managementExcellent organizational and leadership abilitiesOutstanding communication and people skillsWorking understanding of data analysis and performance/operation metricsMust thrive on re-tooling processes and systems to make them more efficientSkilled in the art of delegation and follow throughTrack record of supporting multiple projects at once with tight deadlinesFamiliarity with G-Suite, Asana (or similar), FilemakerPro (or similar), and interest in becoming a power user of the software applications the company invests inExperience recruiting, interviewing, hiring and mentoring new talentUp-to-date with current marketing, advertising, and lifestyle trendsWe are a tech-friendly / tech-dependent company, so being aware of current technology trends / solutions / options is essentialThe following are not essential, but are preferred:4-year undergraduate degree in an applicable field of studyExperience with brand management, fundraising, and/or business developmentThe Fine PrintIf this role is a great fit for you, and Constant is where you want to be, we'd love to hear from you. We are constantly striving to be the best at what we do and want to talk to people who feel the same about what they do.Salary commensurate with experience and industry; benefits provided.Applicants who do not provide or address all of the following will not be considered for this position. Because the right candidate for us is acutely detail oriented, said candidate will be naturally inclined to send us everything we're requesting the first time, so as to not slow down the application process. Please, we beg of you, double check before hitting 8send' to avoid getting weeded out unnecessarily.Applicant ChecklistPlease e-mail the following to careers@constantartists.com with the subject header C2019 Director of Operations PositionD:Your one or two-page resumeOne page of references with contact informationOne page cover letter (these are actually read and reviewed!)Salary requirements and/or salary historyLinkedIn profile link (or a brief explanation of why you don’t have one)Due to the typically high volume of applicants we hear from when we are hiring new team members, we cannot respond to every application, but we do appreciate you reading this far, and look forward to hearing from you if you're the right match for this position.

Title: Global Head of Product Marketing (US)
Description: Know the user. Know the magic. Connect the two. At its core, marketing at Google starts with technology and ends with the user, bringing both together in unconventional ways. Our job is to demonstrate how Google’s products solve the world’s problems–from the everyday to the epic, from the mundane to the monumental. And we approach marketing in a way that only Google can–changing the game, redefining the medium, making the user the priority, and ultimately, letting the technology speak for itself.Continue to article...Whether you’re on a consumer product (like Gmail, Search, Maps, Chrome, Android) or a business product (Google Ads, AdSense, Google Marketing Platform, Analytics), you take part in a complete marketing experience as you lead every facet of the product’s journey. From determining positioning, naming, competitive analysis, feature prioritization and external communications, you help shape the voice of the product and help it grow a consumer base. This means you work with a cross-functional team across Sales, corporate communications, legal, webmasters, product development, engineering and more. In this role, you’ll be involved with product marketing strategy from beginning to end.ResponsibilitiesDrive strategic leadership within Marketing and senior-level cross-functional partners (e.g., Product Management, Engineering, Strategy, Finance, Partnerships, etc.) to grow our subscriptions business and make YouTube the best place for artists.Seek and deliver actionable consumer insights to inform product and marketing strategy, and advocate for the consumer across Marketing, Product, and partners.Act as a key leader for YouTube Product Marketing, utilizing robust consumer insights to build comprehensive marketing strategy and plans for new YouTube Music products and features.Play a key role in broad strategic initiatives, including helping to build YouTube Music's overall marketing strategy and plans, or building plans for new strategic opportunities.Drive insights, competitive analysis, product positioning, naming, objectives, and metrics. Work with a cross-functional team across Product Development, Engineering, Partnerships, Sales, Marketing Communications, PR, Legal, etc.Minimum qualifications:Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience.10 years of experience in product marketing.Preferred qualifications:Experience shaping product roadmaps and user experiences through actionable insights and recommendations. Experience in product branding, messaging, positioning, targeting, and multi-country product launches.Experience successfully collaborating with cross-functional teams.Experience leading teams. Track record of exceptional performance and high productivity.Ability to influence all levels and work effectively across functions and geographies.Ability to think strategically about complex issues, leading to thoughtful recommendations and action plans.Excellent quantitative reasoning, creativity, analytical, strategic, and interpersonal skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Responsibilities: Drive strategic leadership within Marketing and senior-level cross-functional partners (e.g., Product Management, Engineering, Strategy, Finance, Partnerships, etc.) to grow our subscriptions business and make YouTube the best place for artists.
Seek and deliver actionable consumer insights to inform product and marketing strategy, and advocate for the consumer across Marketing, Product, and partners.
Act as a key leader for YouTube Product Marketing, utilizing robust consumer insights to build comprehensive marketing strategy and plans for new YouTube Music products and features.
Play a key role in broad strategic initiatives, including helping to build YouTube Music's overall marketing strategy and plans, or building plans for new strategic opportunities.

Drive insights, competitive analysis, product positioning, naming, objectives, and metrics. Work with a cross-functional team across Product Development, Engineering, Partnerships, Sales, Marketing Communications, PR, Legal, etc.
Minimum qualifications:: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent practical experience.
10 years of experience in product marketing.
Preferred qualifications:: Experience shaping product roadmaps and user experiences through actionable insights and recommendations. Experience in product branding, messaging, positioning, targeting, and multi-country product launches.
Experience successfully collaborating with cross-functional teams.
Experience leading teams. Track record of exceptional performance and high productivity.
Ability to influence all levels and work effectively across functions and geographies.
Ability to think strategically about complex issues, leading to thoughtful recommendations and action plans.
Excellent quantitative reasoning, creativity, analytical, strategic, and interpersonal skills. Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Manager, Original Content Licensing (US)
Description: Spotify is seeking an exceptional candidate to join our licensing team in New York City to help support our original content team.Continue to article...The Licensing Manager's primary focus will be to assist the Head of Original Content Licensing to evaluate, structure, negotiate and execute content licensing transactions, with a particular focus on our podcast initiatives.The Licensing Manager will work closely with creative, marketing, and advertising executives to manage a slate of podcast deals, and to support Spotify's short and long term original content goals. The manager will also help with ongoing strategy development, communicate with key stakeholders and help operationalize new deals. The ideal candidate will have a background in the entertainment and/or digital content industries, with a proven track record of negotiating content development, production, talent services, and licensing agreements.Television business affairs or legal experience is a plus.What you'll doYou will structure and negotiate content acquisition deals with talent and studios, with a focus on original and exclusive podcast licensing.You will support licensing negotiations for global deals by analyzing performance of existing deals, potential of new deals and providing key data to build arguments around deal renewalsYou will collaborate with key members of the Spotify Studio team on content strategy, product and other experiences designed to evolve and grow Spotify's Original Content offeringsYou will work closely with internal teams including Finance, Insights, Legal, and Growth to help advise on deal structures & performance.You will collaborate with internal teams including, product, reporting and marketing to implement all deal termsWho you areYou have received your BA/BS (JD is a plus) and have a minimum of five (5) years of legal, business affairs or content licensing experience within a similar environment.You are familiar with legal and business issues relating to the development, production, and distribution of content. Television experience is a plus.You have experience in the tech and media sectorYou are a highly self motivated individual, comfortable in dynamic environments and rapidly evolving business models.You are skilled at crafting and implementing and organizing deal pipeline processes.You have demonstrated good negotiation and interpersonal skills, and have worked well in business environments with multiple stakeholders.You understand financial models and are able to craft and deliver presentations in Powerpoint and KeynoteYou are well organized with the ability to plan and deliver on tight deadlines with attention to detailYou are highly adaptable and are willing to adapt your role as the business grows and needs changeYou are willing to travel as neededWe are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. We strongly believe that diversity of experience, perspectives, and background will lead to a better environment for our employees and a better product for our users and our creators. This is something we value deeply and we encourage everyone to come be a part of changing the way the world listens to music.

What you'll do: You will structure and negotiate content acquisition deals with talent and studios, with a focus on original and exclusive podcast licensing.
You will support licensing negotiations for global deals by analyzing performance of existing deals, potential of new deals and providing key data to build arguments around deal renewals
You will collaborate with key members of the Spotify Studio team on content strategy, product and other experiences designed to evolve and grow Spotify's Original Content offerings
You will work closely with internal teams including Finance, Insights, Legal, and Growth to help advise on deal structures & performance.
You will collaborate with internal teams including, product, reporting and marketing to implement all deal terms
Who you are: You have received your BA/BS (JD is a plus) and have a minimum of five (5) years of legal, business affairs or content licensing experience within a similar environment.
You are familiar with legal and business issues relating to the development, production, and distribution of content. Television experience is a plus.
You have experience in the tech and media sector
You are a highly self motivated individual, comfortable in dynamic environments and rapidly evolving business models.
You are skilled at crafting and implementing and organizing deal pipeline processes.
You have demonstrated good negotiation and interpersonal skills, and have worked well in business environments with multiple stakeholders.
You understand financial models and are able to craft and deliver presentations in Powerpoint and Keynote
You are well organized with the ability to plan and deliver on tight deadlines with attention to detail
You are highly adaptable and are willing to adapt your role as the business grows and needs change
You are willing to travel as needed

Title: Global Artist Manager (AUT)
Description: To strengthen our Global Music Marketing Team, we´re looking for a Global Artist Manager.Continue to article...The Artist team works to transform the way Red Bull works with the music industry by driving innovative and fruitful partnerships with artists, managers, labels, and internally through Red Bull driven initiatives.Utilizing in-network promotion and cross-platform marketing, the Global Artist Manager sits at the center of an artist's opportunity to be most impactful, effective, and efficient when working with Red Bull.Alongside building key relationships with external partners, responsibilities encompass work across many of Reds Bull's key cross-functional verticals including original content, editorial, content marketing, licensing, and other opportunities.Areas that play to your strengths (all the responsibilities we’ll trust you with)Planning & CollaborationAs Global Artist Manager, you’ll enrich Global Culture Marketing business plan through proposal of innovative, and feasible, artist lead event & content projects. Furthermore, you’ll support the facilitation of artist deliverables for assigned projects to ensure optimal impact for the brand.RelationshipsYou’ll identify and maintain relationships with artists and music industry influencers to drive product trial, amplify our existing culture programs, and create unique marketing initiatives. In addition, you’ll work cross-functionally to develop, implement and track artist lead brand integration. Title: Head Of Publishing (UK)

Title: Head of AMP Production (US)
Description: The people here at Apple don't just create products 4 they create the kind of wonder that's revolutionized entire industries.Continue to article...It's the diversity of those people and their ideas that inspires the innovation that runs through everything we do, from amazing technology to industry-leading environmental efforts.Join Apple, and help us leave the world better than we found it. We’re growing and urge you to apply. We believe the role would place you in a highly-energized environment with some of the most talented creative individuals in the industry where you would be inspired to share your years of experience along with providing extraordinary support for the team.Job SummaryWe're looking for a skilled Senior Manager, Production, to lead a cross-functional team of content producers and platform publishers, responsible for managing the execution of all featured content and bespoke campaigns and programs across Apple Media Products, including Apple Music, App Store, iTunes Store, TV App, Apple Books, and Apple Podcasts. Department Summary: The Production Team implements and deploys on-service/in-store offerings across products (iTunes Store, Apple Music, App Store, TV App, Books Store) and platforms (Desktop, Apple TV, iOS).Key QualificationsStrong leadership and management experience running multiple functions and teams across a broad region.Extensive experience with organizational change management.Cool under pressure with excellent presentation skills.Ability to manage and motivate remote employees, ensuring they're aligned with wider organizational and business goals.Experience at developing and exercising cross-functional influence; translating between business owners, marketing, localization, creative teams, and technical teams.Ability to work collaboratively under pressure, with proven experience working on complex, international, large-scale, time-critical projects.Experience working with web-based Content Management Systems.A sharp attention to detail, and a knack for developing operational efficiencies.An understanding of the end-to-end workflow for managing featuring content, and how to constantly refine to meet the needs of the business.Excellent communication skills, both written and oral, and ability to communicate clearly and concisely to multiple audiences at all levels.Deep familiarity with the Apple ecosystem of services and hardware (Mac OS X, iOS, Apple Music, iTunes, App Store, iBooks, Podcasts, iTunes U, Trailers, Apple TV, etc.)Well versed in pop culture, media, and are passionate about entertainment.DescriptionThe Head of AMP Production, plays a critical function in that their organization is the main linchpin between business/editorial and all of the teams who contribute to featured content throughout all of our stores and services. This person will be managing multiple teams of cross-functional producers, organized by line of business, who project manage key campaigns, programs, and content, from deliverables that need to be turned on a daily basis to incredibly complex, longer-lead projects.This person will also be managing a team of platform publishers who manage, QA, and publish digital content assets across our stores and services and oversee execution-based programming for our storefronts. Both of these areas of the organization also play critical roles in identifying and managing launch deliverables for new product and feature launches, which requires a high degree of cross-functional collaboration across both creative and technical teams. Working closely with the Editorial, Business, and Design teams, the candidate in this role must be fully synced with the businesses' priorities, and ensure that the team is set up to produce and execute on every project on often shifting and very tight timelines.The person in this role must be adept at managing senior-level managers and someone who excels in high-pressure production environments. In this role you'll be responsible for the following: Acting as the main Production point person for the business and executive teams, understanding their priorities and making sure the team is aligned with the wider organization's goals.Managing multiple teams of producers who focus on music, video, apps, and more. Ensuring that your heads create, maintain, and socialize overarching project schedules, help them to redirect pitfalls and work with the team to develop better courses of action. Ensuring that all featured content and promotions are deployed on time and accurately, meeting if not exceeding the role of the business. Working cross-functionally and hand-in-hand with Production cohorts around the globe to drive new operations and production processes and streamline existing workflows. Constantly evaluating the requirements and resources to make the Production team successful.Driving a strong, open, supportive, and positive team culture. Integrating the team into the day-to-day business operations in the US, and collaborating with Production units around the world. Establishing clear goals and related KPIs that measure team performance, and regularly reporting these metrics to management. Making data-driven decisions and solid, data-supported cases for tooling and process evolution, working with the global Production management to drive change.Education & ExperienceBachelor's Degree and 10 to 15 years relevant experience.

Job Summary: We're looking for a skilled Senior Manager, Production, to lead a cross-functional team of content producers and platform publishers, responsible for managing the execution of all featured content and bespoke campaigns and programs across Apple Media Products, including Apple Music, App Store, iTunes Store, TV App, Apple Books, and Apple Podcasts. Department Summary: The Production Team implements and deploys on-service/in-store offerings across products (iTunes Store, Apple Music, App Store, TV App, Books Store) and platforms (Desktop, Apple TV, iOS).
Key Qualifications: Strong leadership and management experience running multiple functions and teams across a broad region.
Extensive experience with organizational change management.
Cool under pressure with excellent presentation skills.
Ability to manage and motivate remote employees, ensuring they're aligned with wider organizational and business goals.
Experience at developing and exercising cross-functional influence; translating between business owners, marketing, localization, creative teams, and technical teams.
Ability to work collaboratively under pressure, with proven experience working on complex, international, large-scale, time-critical projects.
Experience working with web-based Content Management Systems.
A sharp attention to detail, and a knack for developing operational efficiencies.
An understanding of the end-to-end workflow for managing featuring content, and how to constantly refine to meet the needs of the business.
Excellent communication skills, both written and oral, and ability to communicate clearly and concisely to multiple audiences at all levels.
Deep familiarity with the Apple ecosystem of services and hardware (Mac OS X, iOS, Apple Music, iTunes, App Store, iBooks, Podcasts, iTunes U, Trailers, Apple TV, etc.)
Well versed in pop culture, media, and are passionate about entertainment.
Description: The Head of AMP Production, plays a critical function in that their organization is the main linchpin between business/editorial and all of the teams who contribute to featured content throughout all of our stores and services. This person will be managing multiple teams of cross-functional producers, organized by line of business, who project manage key campaigns, programs, and content, from deliverables that need to be turned on a daily basis to incredibly complex, longer-lead projects.
Education \u0026 Experience: Bachelor's Degree and 10 to 15 years relevant experience.

Reservoir’s collection of film music includes rights to scores created by award-winning composer-producer Hans Zimmer, as heard in the motion pictures The Lion King, the Pirates of the Caribbean series, Gladiator, The Dark Knight Trilogy, and over 150 other titles.The company's roster of active writers and producers includes the award-winning James Fauntleroy, Ali Tamposi, Jamie Hartman, Lauren Christy, Dave Bassett, Nate CDanjaD Hills, and Statik Selektah, plus popular performing artists 2 Chainz, Joey Bada$$, WatchTheDuck, and Migos’ Offset and Takeoff. â¨Reservoir continues to grow through the selective acquisition of exceptional catalogs, and the signing and development of creative talent from around the world.

Title: Royalty Matching Assistant (UK)
Description: We are seeking an accurate candidate with a strong work ethic, total attention to detail and the ability to work at a sustained high level of performance to join the Neighbouring Rights Distribution Team in our London office.Continue to article...Kobalt has developed centralised, automated and efficient administration services supported by a scalable, proprietary, dedicated system using the latest technology.Because of Kobalt’s innovative approach, Kobalt offers a reduction in royalty collection time of up to 50%, more effective collection as well as substantially improved transparency.You will support the Distribution team in data matching processes required to enable accurate royalty payments to our clients. You will have strong numerical and organisational skills, high attention to detail, an affinity for technology and innovation, ability to solve loosely defined problems, a passion for music and Kobalt’s values.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast-growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We're currently over 600 people based across 13 offices globally and still growing! If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!WHAT DOES A ROYALTY MATCHING ASSISTANT IN NEIGHBOURING RIGHTS DO?Import and Convert incoming Societies' statementsProcess all royalty data (matching recordings, right types and usage dates)WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Good attention to detailAbility to be comfortable working on repetitive tasks for long periods of timeAbility to work to deadlinesGood level of written and spoken EnglishSPECIFICSBased at River Building, Cannon Street, London EC4R 3TEHours are 37.5 hours per week. Generally, these are taken between 9.30-6 with 1 hour for lunch but amendments to these hours can be agreed on a case by case basis with line manager approval

Title: Director, Finance (US)
Description: We are UMG, the Universal Music Group. We are the world's leading music company. In everything we do, we are committed to artistry, innovation and entrepreneurship.Continue to article...We own and operate a broad array of businesses engaged in recorded music, music publishing, merchandising, and audio-visual content in more than 60 countries. We identify and develop recording artists and songwriters, and we produce, distribute and promote the most critically acclaimed and commercially successful music to delight and entertain fans around the world. As an industry leader, we are constantly looking for talented individuals to join our team and continue to drive our company forward.UMG Nashville is looking for a Director, Finance.  The scope of this job consists of maintaining and reviewing for accuracy UMG Nashville financial statements (Income Statement, Balance Sheet and CFFO); analyze results and projections of UMG Nashville financial statements as related to approved yearly budgeting, quarterly forecasting projections, and recognition of monthly actuals, and liaise with North American Corporate Finance and Corporate Accounting regarding questions, coding, deadlines, and procedure as needed. The Director, Finance will assist in various needs within the finance department, including, but not limited to: Internal Audits and Sarbanes Oxley Compliance.UMG Nashville consists of Capitol Records Nashville, EMI Records Nashville, MCA Nashville and Mercury Nashville. Together their roster of artists reads like a who's who of Music Row stars, including Alan Jackson, Chris Stapleton, Darius Rucker, Dierks Bentley, George Strait, Eric Church, Kacey Musgraves, Little Big Town, Luke Bryan, Sam Hunt, Shania Twain and Vince Gill.Job Functions:Income Statement related:Review for accuracy and alignment with known monthly actualsBalance Sheet and Cash Forecasting:Prepare full Balance Sheet/ Cash Flow plan and forecasts, and upkeep for actuals with specific focus on:Accrued Marketing;Accrued Income;Third Party royalties;Payout/ Recoupment split and timing;Impact from internal drivers;Implications of deal type.Royalty Accounting Review (including JV):Utilize Artist ASL model to forecast updated recording recoveries, marketing recoveries and ending balances.Review and approve artists spend, recording recoveries, marketing recoveries and ending balance estimates from royalty group (request change when necessary).Update local artist balance file based on file spend/recoveries detail.Review deferred balances for accuracy and reasoning. Recommend any adjustments to write off.Provide Impairment analysis and STLT classification.Prepare JV estimates for forecasted months – recognizing both P&L and CFFO implications.Non-Record Income Business Segments:Responsible for accounting and forecasting of non-recorded income segments of UMGN P&L in coordination with applicable intercompany teams (including passive income monetization, D2C, Sponsorship/Branding, VIP Ticketing, etc.)Prepare Artist accountings at close of campaign and coordinate with legal, administration, NRI, finance and CAS/royalties teams, as necessary, to ensure timely reporting and payments.Ongoing Responsibilities:Complete various components of Month End financial book:Cash Flow Summary, AIP Summary, NRI detail, Advance provision, Spend  Recovery detail, Artist P&Ls (YTD/ RTD)Prepare Monthly Profitability summary for ExecutivesSupport finance department on Executive Presentation detail for Corporate GroupOversee Coordinator in sundry invoicing processing for ad hoc invoices.Review detail from Central Accounting Services:  Profit Participation Expense, Non-Recorded Income summary, Third Party Royalty analysis, International income/expense and Ticket spend by artist.In coordination with VP Finance, create project marketing budgets and forecasted phasing. Liaise with marketing teams to ensure proper execution.Monitor approvals across various UMG systems: Uniport, eTA, Egratis, Expensys and TicketBuy.Other duties and responsibilities as assigned.Skills/Abilities:Must be able to manage multiple tasks while prioritizing and meeting tight deadlines.Strong financial systems skills and proficient with Microsoft Office (advanced MS Excel).Internal Controls/SOX compliance testing and reporting.Proven high level of organization and attention to detail.Driven by accuracy of results.Excellent oral and written communication skills.Self-starter with ability to build effective and collaborative working relationships.Positive and professional attitude.Ability to learn fast and retain detailed information and instructions.Education & Experience:5 years of proven and relevant accounting experience, preferably with an entertainment company.Financial system experience (preferably in SAP and HFM).Experience in a large corporate setting actively participating in all aspects of the financial close and corporate consolidation processes.Exposure to an environment where internal controls are a high priority.Exposure to technical accounting.Bachelor's Degree in Accounting or Finance required.CPA certification a plus.Universal Music Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer.DisclaimerThis job description only provides an overview of job responsibilities that are subject to change.

Job Functions:: Income Statement related:Review for accuracy and alignment with known monthly actuals
Review for accuracy and alignment with known monthly actuals
