Title: VP Marketing (UK)
Description: Everything we do is rooted in the energy of club culture and its ability to bring people together. Open dancefloors; where music, ideas and people meet.Continue to article...Boiler Room started with a webcam taped to a wall, broadcasting from a warehouse in London, opening a keyhole to the city's underground. DIY at it's finest; raw uncut and homemade.As an independent music platform and cultural curator, we remain true to that history. Connecting club culture to the wider world, on screen and irl through parties, film and video.Since 2010 we built a unique archive; featuring over 4,000 performance, by more than 5,000 artists spanning 150 cities, enabling everyone regardless of where they live to enjoy the freedom it stands for.Our remit is to platform music in new ways 3 sound on for the voices and stories of the fringes of the mainstream.Boiler Room 3 watch, listen, dance.PURPOSE OF THE ROLEThe VP Marketing will be responsible for developing, articulating, driving and implementing key comms initiatives – both editorial & commercial – that serve our broader business goals.They will work as part of the senior leadership team to drive the business forward towards delivering on its strategic vision: of being the leading supporter of underground music & underground music artists globally. Within this, they will take responsibility for the positioning, packaging & promotion of key ideas – internally & externally – that push the company forward towards the realisation of this vision.Whilst we have an existing management structure in place for the operational side of the marketing team, it is the responsibility of the VP Marketing to lead, inspire & mentor this team.KEY RESPONSIBILITIESDevise comms narratives and drive the marketing strategy for Boiler Room priority projects & campaigns:Cementing Boiler Room's position as the leading supporter of underground music globally through promoting artists, playlists and contentCommunicating our goals & ambitions to the broader industry via B2B comms, plus represent the company at B2B industry events (i.e. panels & conferences)Accelerating the growth of our touring proposition & driving ticket salesFully establishing & maximising success of our apparel propositionOptimising the narrativization & performance of our commercial partnershipsDAY-TO-DAY DUTIESBuild strong relationships within the music industry – from record labels to DSPs & music media. Identifying opportunities that will serve our growth plansManage the external PR agency to activate and execute the strategyMentor the marketing team who will execute all marketing and comms campaigns across the marketing mixDrive audience growth & reach mechanics by developing new distribution ecosystems for commercial and original campaignsLeverage data & insights to inform marketing, brand, content & comms activity – as well as informing broader business decisionsEXPECTATIONSThe successful candidate will:Have substantial experience working at a senior level in a music / culture / media business, in developing & pitching comms strategiesHave a profile & be connected within the music industryHave a demonstrable passion for, and understanding of, the Boiler Room brand / visionBe able to galvanise & motivate the workforce around key comms initiatives, and oversee them coming to lifeBe adaptable and ready to work in a fast-paced environmentSKILLS / KNOWLEDGEDeep strategic thinkerExceptional communicatorBroad knowledge across all areas of marketing & comms, inc all the latest tools & softwareAbility to work autonomously & take the initiative on key projects, as well as able to inspire & lead project teamsDegree in Business, Marketing, PR / Comms, or Advertising preferred, but not essential

Title: Associate Director, Rights Clearance
Description: As Rights Clearance Associate Director, you will be responsible for rights clearances, on a worldwide basis.Continue to article...Your work will be focused on negotiating for the clearance of rights required for artist music and video content to be consumed on and off the Spotify platform.This includes third party content (such as music videos and live performance footage) as well as Spotify original content (such as Spotify video originals and Spotify original podcasts).Above all, your work will impact the way the world experiences music and podcasts.What you'll do:Personally and through your team, handling all audio and video synchronization clearances, including negotiating, drafting and finalizing all synchronization agreements and related clearance agreements with rights-holders.Building relationships with the key contacts internally as well as with labels, publishers, artists, managers and music libraries, worldwide.Working closely with, and supporting and advising our business teams 4 including our Music, Studios and Markets Teams, 4 on global licensing, production releases and other similar matters.Familiarity with the global PRO's, cue sheets and the ability to advise on public performance related agreements and issues.Who you are:A minimum of five years’ experience leading an in-house audio/visual rights clearance team or production counsel team with strong knowledge of common deal parameters and financial expectations.An effective negotiator able to handle a large pipeline of on-going deals at any given time.A proven ability to manage a team and collaborate effectively in a matrixed organization.Able to provide sound business and legal judgment.Confident public speaker who can provide concise and informative presentations around rights clearances for internal teams requiring guidance.Creative problem solver able to recommend changes to common deal terms and advocate for new processes that drive efficiencies.Must be self-motivated and able to independently identify work to be done without instructions.Team player with excellent organizational skills and ability to delegate effectively.Comfortable working in a company experiencing rapid growth.Great analytical and written/oral communication skills.We strongly believe that diversity of experience, perspectives, and background will lead to a better workplace for our employees and a better product for our users and our creators. This is something we value deeply and we encourage everyone to come be a part of changing the way the world listens to music.

What you'll do:: Personally and through your team, handling all audio and video synchronization clearances, including negotiating, drafting and finalizing all synchronization agreements and related clearance agreements with rights-holders.

An effective negotiator able to handle a large pipeline of on-going deals at any given time.
A proven ability to manage a team and collaborate effectively in a matrixed organization.
Able to provide sound business and legal judgment.
Confident public speaker who can provide concise and informative presentations around rights clearances for internal teams requiring guidance.
Creative problem solver able to recommend changes to common deal terms and advocate for new processes that drive efficiencies.

Title: Manager, Work of Art Management (US)
Description: Sony Music Entertainment is a global recorded music company with a roster of current artists that includes a broad array of both local artists and international superstars, as well as a vast catalog that comprises some of the most important recordings in history.Continue to article...Sony Music Entertainment is a wholly owned subsidiary of Sony Corporation of America.Sony Music is committed to providing equal employment opportunity for all persons regardless of age, disability, national origin, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, veteran or military status, marital and civil partnership/union status, alienage or citizenship status, creed, genetic information or any other status protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.The Day to Day (D2D) Manager position works closely with the Work of Art General Manager and the respective artists to manage every facet of the artists career.ResponsibilitiesCalendar: D2D is responsible for the Artist's Calendar. They oversee all business meetings, interviews, writing/recording sessions, travel, etc. (in alliance with the office coordinator and/or tour manager when on the road).Micro vs Macro 3 The D2D is in the weeds on all the micro. They know the details about every single entry in the calendar for the foreseeable future, while also having a strong grasp on the big picture (6-18 month plan).New Business Opportunities: seeking out new opportunities that can generate revenue and/or exposure to our artists. (artist collaborations, tour opportunities, private events, brand partnerships, technology alliances, etc)Social Media 3 Oversee the daily maintenance and content calendar for artist's social  web properties: managing content calendar, tour dates, brand partnerships, etc.Marketing 3 Overseeing all marketing efforts in relation to music promotion, live shows  tour, merchandise strategy, public relations.Merchandise3 maintaining and implementing strategy for growth and consumption in regards to the artist online webstore and tour merchandise.Asset Management 3 maintaining all photos, videos, songs, session files, publishing splits, label credits, etc. for the artists within Box, Dropbox, Google Suite, external hard drive or another asset management platform.In-Coming Requests 3 Each artist will receive an onslaught of daily requests. The D2D will vet and respond (green light or deny) any and all incoming requests.Supervising Interns 3 The D2D is responsible for supervising interns. Maintaining their workload, training/teaching based on the given task and proofing their completed work.Qualifications5 years in the music business (preferably within a management company)Very strong organizational skillsImpeccable work ethic (pride in what you do)Ability to use independent judgment and communicate effectively with all levels of an organizationStrong oral/written communication and interpersonal skillsPositive/enthusiastic attitudeTechnical Competency working within Box, Dropbox, Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Keynote, Wunderlist, Slack, Google Suite.Highly experienced with all social media platforms including, but not limited to Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, YouTube, Tumblr, Snapchat, TikTok.Highly experience with all digital service providers including, but not limited to Spotify, Apple Music, iTunes, Amazon, Deezer.

Responsibilities: Calendar: D2D is responsible for the Artist's Calendar. They oversee all business meetings, interviews, writing/recording sessions, travel, etc. (in alliance with the office coordinator and/or tour manager when on the road).

Micro vs Macro 3 The D2D is in the weeds on all the micro. They know the details about every single entry in the calendar for the foreseeable future, while also having a strong grasp on the big picture (6-18 month plan).

New Business Opportunities: seeking out new opportunities that can generate revenue and/or exposure to our artists. (artist collaborations, tour opportunities, private events, brand partnerships, technology alliances, etc)
Social Media 3 Oversee the daily maintenance and content calendar for artist's social  web properties: managing content calendar, tour dates, brand partnerships, etc.
Marketing 3 Overseeing all marketing efforts in relation to music promotion, live shows  tour, merchandise strategy, public relations.
Merchandise3 maintaining and implementing strategy for growth and consumption in regards to the artist online webstore and tour merchandise.

Asset Management 3 maintaining all photos, videos, songs, session files, publishing splits, label credits, etc. for the artists within Box, Dropbox, Google Suite, external hard drive or another asset management platform.
In-Coming Requests 3 Each artist will receive an onslaught of daily requests. The D2D will vet and respond (green light or deny) any and all incoming requests.
Supervising Interns 3 The D2D is responsible for supervising interns. Maintaining their workload, training/teaching based on the given task and proofing their completed work.
Qualifications: 5 years in the music business (preferably within a management company)
Very strong organizational skills
Impeccable work ethic (pride in what you do)
Ability to use independent judgment and communicate effectively with all levels of an organization
Strong oral/written communication and interpersonal skills
Positive/enthusiastic attitude
Technical Competency working within Box, Dropbox, Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Keynote, Wunderlist, Slack, Google Suite.
Highly experienced with all social media platforms including, but not limited to Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, YouTube, Tumblr, Snapchat, TikTok.
Highly experience with all digital service providers including, but not limited to Spotify, Apple Music, iTunes, Amazon, Deezer.

Title: Senior Content Marketing Strategist (US)
Description: The people here at Apple don't just create products 4 they create the kind of wonder that's revolutionized entire industries.Continue to article...It's the diversity of those people and their ideas that inspires the innovation that runs through everything we do, from amazing technology to industry-leading environmental efforts.Join Apple, and help us leave the world better than we found it.We’re growing and urge you to apply. We believe the role would place you in a highly-energized environment with some of the most talented creative individuals in the industry where you would be inspired to share your years of experience along with providing extraordinary support for the team. Apple is in search of a talented, ambitious and motivated content marketing and syndication strategist, with a minimum of 12 years of experience to join the marketing team.This person will be responsible for working cross-functionally to manage our content marketing strategy for Apple Music.This individual will help in the creation and management of a multi-platform content marketing strategy that is designed to reach potential and existing Apple Music audiences. This person will help drive cultural conversations that provide concrete reasons for consumers to dive deeper into Apple Music.This is a leadership position that will be an integral participant in the content we are creating for our Apple Music marketing campaigns. He/she will be responsible for how that content is surfaced off-platform, which marketing channels and partners we surface that content on, and why we choose to surface it there.It will be this person's responsibility to ensure that our marketing programs utilize content efficiently and effectively. Additionally, this position will be responsible for looking after how we build our amazing properties, including Beats 1, into broader Apple Music campaigns.Key Qualifications12 years of relevant content marketing and experience, with 7 years working on digital marketing campaigns.Deep understanding of the entertainment and music industry businesses preferred.Experience managing content and marketing production budgets.Highly skilled in managing consumer-first content marketing campaigns from start to finish.Experience in social content creation and syndication.Experience working with third party partners on content creation and marketing.Excellent relationship management abilities/strong interpersonal communication skills.Excellent organization skills, acute attention to detail, ability to handle multiple tasks in a fast-paced and time sensitive environment.Proven track record of meeting deadline and taking initiative to make confident and well-informed decisions.Possess a Ccan-doD attitude for all situations, no task too big or too small to handle.Excellent written and verbal interpersonal skills, and strong sense of professionalism.Ability to influence others; handle conflict appropriately, know when to delegate issues cross-cross-functionally, and when to apply common sense.Direct experience handling sensitive and confidential information with integrity, discretion, and upmost secrecy.Ability to prioritize, learn quickly, and be flexible.You work well under pressure, are results-oriented, upbeat, and focused, with a strong passion to learn and succeed.DescriptionOversee the creation and distribution of Apple Music marketing content across the digital ecosystem. Develop and execute multi-faceted content marketing campaigns that drive acquisition, engagement and retention for Apple Music. Work cross-functionally with the rest of the music marketing team to ensure we're creating the right content, for the right channels, with the right messaging, to better support business objectives. Work hand-in-hand with stakeholders to execute on our content marketing strategy, briefing in the right teams, and acting as the DRI cross-functionally. Responsible for looking after how we build our Beats 1 platform into our broader Apple Music campaigns. Build scaleable content syndication programs that achieve our objectives, and ladder up to broader business objectives, all while also supporting our partners and creators Relationship management and tactical business development responsibilities with agencies, and third party partners. Collaboration with PR to ensure we're telling the right stories at the right times, with our content. Work in concert with the Apple Music editorial team to ensure consistency across content created for on, and off, platform. Use data to help inform content marketing strategy, program strategy, and decisions on content creation and syndication. Write creative briefs and maintain the relationship with agencies both internal and external. Manage delivery timelines and internal and external creative asset approvals.Education & ExperienceBS/BA degree required, with strong content marketing and analytical background.

Key Qualifications: 12 years of relevant content marketing and experience, with 7 years working on digital marketing campaigns.
Deep understanding of the entertainment and music industry businesses preferred.
Experience managing content and marketing production budgets.
Highly skilled in managing consumer-first content marketing campaigns from start to finish.
Experience in social content creation and syndication.
Experience working with third party partners on content creation and marketing.
Excellent relationship management abilities/strong interpersonal communication skills.
Excellent organization skills, acute attention to detail, ability to handle multiple tasks in a fast-paced and time sensitive environment.
Proven track record of meeting deadline and taking initiative to make confident and well-informed decisions.
Possess a Ccan-doD attitude for all situations, no task too big or too small to handle.
Excellent written and verbal interpersonal skills, and strong sense of professionalism.
Ability to influence others; handle conflict appropriately, know when to delegate issues cross-cross-functionally, and when to apply common sense.
Direct experience handling sensitive and confidential information with integrity, discretion, and upmost secrecy.
Ability to prioritize, learn quickly, and be flexible.
You work well under pressure, are results-oriented, upbeat, and focused, with a strong passion to learn and succeed.
Description: Oversee the creation and distribution of Apple Music marketing content across the digital ecosystem. Develop and execute multi-faceted content marketing campaigns that drive acquisition, engagement and retention for Apple Music. Work cross-functionally with the rest of the music marketing team to ensure we're creating the right content, for the right channels, with the right messaging, to better support business objectives. Work hand-in-hand with stakeholders to execute on our content marketing strategy, briefing in the right teams, and acting as the DRI cross-functionally. Responsible for looking after how we build our Beats 1 platform into our broader Apple Music campaigns. Build scaleable content syndication programs that achieve our objectives, and ladder up to broader business objectives, all while also supporting our partners and creators Relationship management and tactical business development responsibilities with agencies, and third party partners. Use data to help inform content marketing strategy, program strategy, and decisions on content creation and syndication. Write creative briefs and maintain the relationship with agencies both internal and external. Manage delivery timelines and internal and external creative asset approvals.
Education \u0026 Experience: BS/BA degree required, with strong content marketing and analytical background.

Title: Senior Digital Account Manager, North America (US)
Description: The Domino Recording Company is looking for a Senior Digital Account Manager to be based in the Brooklyn, New York office to join our global digital team.Continue to article...The position will oversee key partnerships and initiatives across digital music and video consumer services (e.g. Apple Music, Spotify, Amazon, YouTube) for North America.Reporting jointly to the global Head of Digital and the US Label Manager, this fantastic opportunity will allow you to work with a diverse roster of independent artists.You will be responsible for leading relationships with key digital partners in market, shaping and implementing new and ongoing release campaigns, as well as building long-term programming strategies.Successful candidates should have a natural inclination to sales and marketing, focused on innovation and working creatively. As the role will call for monitoring and reporting on streaming data, sales and trends, an analytical mindset and an eye for numerical detail will be essential.CORE RESPONSIBILITIES:Lead digital account activities for North America, across multiple product platforms and business modelsRepresent Domino releases to DSPs for both frontline and catalogue campaignsMaximize opportunities for Domino artists through effective campaign building and communicating artist development strategies to our digital partners.Build and maintain strong relationships at the DSPsDrive revenue growth across the label by implementing innovative and ground breaking strategiesCollaborate with the rest of the Domino digital department and the wider marketing team to help define and deliver streaming and sales strategiesHone internal reporting and forecasting tools to identify opportunities and trends.Analyze performance of artist campaigns, including playlist and on-platform resultsserve as a liaison with artists and management providing logistical insight, planning and market feedbackLiaise with the Digital Operations Manager to ensure content delivery feeds are timely and completeCommunicate new developments and initiatives at the digital services to the wider team. REQUIREMENTS:We are seeking candidates that meet the following requirements and experience:Preferred 3 years music industry experience in the digital sector.Deep understanding of digital retail platformsExcellent written and verbal communication / interpersonal skills.Comprehensive knowledge of current and future trends in music marketingGlobal outlook and understanding of international markets and campaignsSuperior organizational and time management skills, attention to detail, and ability to prioritize and manage multiple projects and tasks.Proficient in Microsoft Office, particularly Microsoft Excel for sales and reporting analysisThe ability to travel on a regular basisFluency in Spanish would be a plus but not requiredMost importantly, a passion for music and understanding of the Domino rosterTO APPLY:Please send a cover letter stating how you meet the requirements, along with your resume to monique@dominorecordco.comDomino Recording Co. was founded in the UK in 1993 by Laurence Bell, who is still the primary creative force of the company. Initially focused on releasing singles and albums by American artists in the UK (Pavement, Bonnie “Prince” Billy, Sebadoh), the label has expanded over the past decade to include offices in New York, Los Angeles, Berlin, Paris and Sydney and has had considerable worldwide success, both commercial and artistic, with acts as diverse as Arctic Monkeys, Franz Ferdinand, Animal Collective, Dirty Projectors, Real Estate, Cat Power, Julia Holter, The Kills, Blood Orange, and Jon Hopkins to name a few.

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES:: Lead digital account activities for North America, across multiple product platforms and business models
Represent Domino releases to DSPs for both frontline and catalogue campaigns
Maximize opportunities for Domino artists through effective campaign building and communicating artist development strategies to our digital partners.
Build and maintain strong relationships at the DSPs
Drive revenue growth across the label by implementing innovative and ground breaking strategies
Collaborate with the rest of the Domino digital department and the wider marketing team to help define and deliver streaming and sales strategies
Hone internal reporting and forecasting tools to identify opportunities and trends.
Analyze performance of artist campaigns, including playlist and on-platform results
serve as a liaison with artists and management providing logistical insight, planning and market feedback
Liaise with the Digital Operations Manager to ensure content delivery feeds are timely and complete
Communicate new developments and initiatives at the digital services to the wider team.
REQUIREMENTS:: Preferred 3 years music industry experience in the digital sector.
Deep understanding of digital retail platforms
Excellent written and verbal communication / interpersonal skills.
Comprehensive knowledge of current and future trends in music marketing
Global outlook and understanding of international markets and campaigns
Superior organizational and time management skills, attention to detail, and ability to prioritize and manage multiple projects and tasks.
Proficient in Microsoft Office, particularly Microsoft Excel for sales and reporting analysis
The ability to travel on a regular basis
Fluency in Spanish would be a plus but not required
Most importantly, a passion for music and understanding of the Domino roster
TO APPLY:: Please send a cover letter stating how you meet the requirements, along with your resume to monique@dominorecordco.com

Title: Chief Executive (UK)
Description: PRS Foundation is the UK's leading charitable funder of new music and talent development across all genres. Interviews will be held 17 3 20 June.If you have not heard from us by the end of the day on Friday 14 June, you should assume that you have not been short-listed for interview. We regret that we will not be able to give feedback to those who are not short-listed.As part of PRS Foundation's dedication to promoting equality, diversity and inclusion we particularly welcome applications from female, LGBT, disabled and black and minority ethnic candidates.Registered charity 1080837.

Description: Kobalt is seeking a Senior level Business Partner to join a values-driven and growing People & Culture team.Continue to article...Reporting to the VP of People & Culture UK & Rest of World, this role will support circa 300 employees across the UK business where you will partner with the leadership teams and have global ownership of the Publishing Operations teams, AMRA (our digital music collection society) and Neighbouring Rights, as well as provide BU support for the Synchronisation & Creative teams and AWAL (our recordings function).The People & Culture department is a growing team of 20 made up of Business Partners (known internally as P&C Managers), Learning & Development, Employee Communications, Compensation & Benefits and Internal Recruitment.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model.Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized.  This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We're currently 650 people based across 13 offices and we're still growing.WHAT DOES A PEOPLE & CULTURE BUSINESS PARTNER AT KOBALT DO?Partner with leadership teams on people & culture and change management initiativesBuilds a strategic P&C plan on their business unit which supports the growth and challenges of their areaSupport staff by building a first-class employee experience, promoting development feedback, objective setting, employee engagementPromote and engage in well-being and diversity / inclusion initiativesManage all employee relation cases from initial advice through to completionWork with Learning & Development on learning programmes where necessaryRoll out and embed career progression framework and advise managers on compensation discussionsEnsure the HRIS data is up to date and changes are communicated to the P&C CoordinatorUse data and insights to influence and support business decisions and changesReport on P&C activity, including headcount, attrition, diversity, ERWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? Title: Senior Digital Marketing Manager (UK)

You will be joining this ambitious label as a core member of the digital team and be expected to craft and nurture the online presence of an assigned roster of acts. You will also be responsible for ensuring your acts are actively building and engaging with their fan bases through all online channels applicable to the artist. You should be a self starter who loves to think creatively about the challenges of grabbing an audience’s attention in the social space. developing artist fan bases and understand the audience better than anyone else.Devise and implement social media campaigns for a portfolio of artists across a broad range of social content environments.Work with the whole team to devise creative strategies for the continual growth & engagement of the artists’ fanbases.Brief & self-create both organic and paid for content.Work with marketing team and media buyers to set up ad campaigns.Lead the creation of all current and emerging social channels for new artists once signed.Work with artist & managers to write, build and send email communicationsHandle the updating of digital assets including banners and visual creative on all social properties.Set up and run social media training sessions with your development artists.Handle content uploads to YouTube ensuring correct tagging, SEO & optimisation.Provide reporting in artist and campaign meetings on the growth of artist fanbases, videos and online reaction.Handle link tagging for all artist releases to ensure we’re collecting remarketing data.Implement label strategy with regards to social properties e.g. YouTube advertising options, takedown policy and whitelisting of channels and content.About you:Experience in similar role within the music industry.Thorough understanding of the social media landscape.Clear knowledge of how to use social media functionality to generate engagement and stimulate interaction with both the artists and fans.Plan and implement an always on communications strategy both in release periods and off cycle to engage their fans.Articulate with excellent copy writing skills.An enthusiastic and innovative thinker who is able to multitask.Excellent analytical skills with results-oriented approach.The ability to communicate well (both verbally and in writing) to relay all undertaken and planned activity.Proficient with video & photo editing software.Good interpersonal skills in order to work directly with We are looking for a forward-thinking music professional with digital marketing experience, steeped in youth culture and excited by breaking new tracks and acts. Description: BMAT started in 2005 when a group of engineers with a passion for music decided to spin-off from an audio research lab in Barcelona.Continue to article...The company started with an idea 3 to index all the music in the world 3 and a mission 3 to better the music eco-system 3 and a team of nerds eager to learn how to build a successful business.Today the company has grown to more than a hundred people – spread across Barcelona, Buenos Aires, Lima, SÃ£o Paulo, London, Paris, Oslo, Tel-Aviv, Johannesburg, Singapore, Tokyo and Seoul – that team up to deliver daily 1 million identifications and 3 billion matches to our partners from all over the music industry.Our CultureWe love music. It makes our lives a zillion times better. And we want to give something back to her (yes, to us music is a lady).Make her life easier and her future brighter. We apply our scientific, data-infused brains to tell everyone what music has been played 3 so that creators get the recognition they deserve. We hear everything everywhere and tell everyone who wants to know. 8What plays around will come back around' we say. We believe by helping this happen we can make a positive change in the music ecosystem and enjoy talent for another million years.We are a team of pretty decent people who enjoy working with each other. We're open minded and well travelled, coming from all parts of the world. We like coffee, tea, chai, and mate all the same. We've got rappers, producers, cello players, DJs, and songwriters.The passion for music, the excitement for technology and Barcelona as a backdrop has proven to make a good combination. We say there is a BMAT touch. We believe it has to do with being passionate, obsessive, nice, generous, honest, self-motivated, entrepreneurial, intrepid, crazy, extraordinary, creative, agile, human, friendly, smart, witty, talented, naive, optimistic, surprising, sensual, and nerdy, but not very hipster (unfortunately). Title: Counter Records / Ninja Tune 3 Senior Product Manager (US)

Description: JOB DESCRIPTION:Continue to article...Work with all label departments & client partners, including agents & managers, to effectively plan & execute marketing campaigns with special focus on our key Counter Records artists.Take a key leadership role in coordinating and energizing various departments to proudly deliver clear, innovative & organized marketing campaigns.Develop a creative marketing strategy, and implement that strategy with a high degree of responsibility to drive consumption & sales and reach campaign goals.Oversee the bigger picture & ensuring all areas of a campaign management experience in the music industry.Bachelor's degree, or higher level of education, preferred.Passionate about music, specifically an awareness & appreciation of Ninja Tune.An excellent understanding of marketing campaigns & music industry generally.Excellent organizational skills.Ability to manage multiple campaigns at once, and manage a busy work schedule.Meticulous attention to relationship-management skills.Ability to handle difficult situations with tact and diplomacy.Excellent ability to problem solve and keep calm under pressure.Current awareness & understanding of social media platforms & trends.IT savvy with understanding of G Suite (Google Drive, Google Sheets, Google Docs)Hard working and enthusiasticABOUT US:Ninja Tune is an independent record label based in London, England, created in 1990 by the duo known as Coldcut: pioneers of the emerging hip-hop/electro scene in the eighties. Passionate about music, specifically an awareness & appreciation of Ninja Tune.

ABOUT US:: Ninja Tune is an independent record label based in London, England, created in 1990 by the duo known as Coldcut: pioneers of the emerging hip-hop/electro scene in the eighties. It has satellite offices in Los Angeles, Berlin and Paris. Ninja Tune and Counter Records have since released music from a myriad of artists such as Bonobo, ODESZA, Maribou State, Big Wild, Kasbo, What So Not and many more.

Title: Artist Community Manager (US)
Description: Sofar Sounds reimagines live events through curated, secret performances in intimate settings in more than 420 cities around the world.Continue to article...ROLE:At Sofar Sounds, we exist because of artists. We are all focused on connecting artists and those who support them on a deeper level. To date, over 30,000 artists have played a Sofar event somewhere in the world. As we continue to grow, we are investing even more in how we support artists. As the Artist Community Manager, you will ensure that every Sofar artist around the world feels heard, supported and connected. You will engage directly with the Sofar artist community and will serve as a leading artist advocate to inform Sofar’s further investment in supporting artists.POSITION TITLE:Artist Community ManagerREPORTING TO: CEOLOCATION:Ideally Boston. We will consider candidates in NYC as well.RESPONSIBILITIES:The Artist Community manager will work closely with a small, scrappy team to ensure every Sofar artist feels heard, supported and connected.Responsibilities include:Lead communication to Sofar artists around the world, across social channels, email and in-person sessions. Communicate with empathy and advocacy.Develop a deep understanding of the Sofar artist community, both qualitatively and quantitatively through in-person engagement and surveys.Recruit, develop and manage a small team of interns and full-time employees and full-time artist advocates to scale our artist outreach and engagement.Serve as an artist advocate internally, helping to set company priorities, product road map and other artist support initiatives.Set and achieve clear metrics to ensure success in artist engagement and sentiment.Stay in sync with other artist-facing teams (city operations, talent booking) to ensure clarity of operations and routing of specific artist needs to the right teams.Play a leading role in the rollout of new artist support programs, including education, tracking and measurement of such programs.Keep things loose and fun. Get out and see some shows.The Ideal Candidate:The ideal candidate brings together two must-have skills/experiences:Experience managing large communities with deep empathy, clarity of voice, efficiency and a strategic view toward the future you want to create. We’re open to all kinds of specific experience in this area, including community management, product marketing, social media management and evangelism. The key is a proven ability to engage with a big, diverse stakeholder group with clarity, consistency and empathy.You know what it’s like to be an artist and a performer — ideally, you’ve used a car as your green room before a show; you’ve suffered through more bass drum sound checks than you care to remember; you’ve crashed on couches and on the road between shows. Though experience as a performer is preferred, other experience evincing a deep empathy for working musicians (road management, artist management, etc) also considered.SKILLS & COMPETENCIES:Deep commitment to connecting to all members of the community to make everyone feel welcome and heard, especially those with a different background from your own. This includes self-awareness to watch for unconscious biases and proactive steps to continually improve.A track record of success in managing small, but diverse teams to ensure that every member of the community feels a sense of 1:1 connection.Excellent communications skills — public speaking, email communication, social media and other forms of external communicationsAbility to synthesize company goals and objectives with the goals and objectives of the community, to inform all aspects of strategy — products, services and company identity.Highly visible, empathetic and hands-on communicator with the ability to build and lead a highly motivated, small scrappy team of interns and artist advocates.Analytical enough to play a role in how we solicit and interpret feedback from the community and to draw actionable insights from the feedback.Enjoys bringing order and structure to a ‘no charts,’ frenetic growth-stage company environment. Stays cool under pressure, able to deal with high-emotion situations while staying cool and focused.Collaborative problem solver with low ego and high emotional intelligence, able to build consensus across teamsExperience working within a music or other creative community – focused company is a big plus.Cannot live without live music and knows how to have a good timeSofar Sounds is a community that creates and shares experiences. We are committed to being a humble partner to emerging artists and to create magical experiences for fans. We are growing quickly, working hard to help more musicians in more cities. As a result, we expect our people to get things done efficiently and to help other team members. We also seek people who learn from and make changes as a result of feedback from one another and our community. The right candidate should be efficient, upbeat and embody the Sofar experience while sharing our vision for discovering great new music and creating the best live event experience in the world.Sofar team culture is one of grit, efficiency, innovation, transparency and commitment to fostering a diverse, inclusive and welcoming environment. Values such as community-focus, humility and commitment to diversity of thought and background drive us forward.WORKING HERE:Sofar Sounds offers a comprehensive benefits package as well as opportunities for career growth and development.We’re a company of trust. What does that mean? If you need to take a break and go on a long walk, do it! If your family is in town and you want to spend the afternoon with them, do it! Need a vacation? Definitely do it! We trust our employees to take time when they need it.At Sofar, we embrace diversity and equal opportunity in a serious way. We are committed to building a team that represents a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and skills. We are inclusive. The more we live that, the better our work will be.COMPANY CULTURE:People who join Sofar Sounds have an excitement and energy about what we’re doing. They want to be a driving force of growth for emerging artists, committed to creating a magical experience for performers and fans alike, and this is reflected in the way they work.We are growing swiftly, helping more musicians in more cities. As a result, we expect our people to get things done efficiently and to help other team members, whether a specific task is their role or not. Also to learn from, and make changes as a result of, feedback from one another and our community.The right candidate should be efficient and upbeat, and needs to live the Sofar experience while sharing our vision for discovering great new music and creating the best live event experience in the world.If you’re not based in Boston or NYC but are still interested in applying, please do! We are open to relocating someone if they are a rock star.

ROLE:: At Sofar Sounds, we exist because of artists. We are all focused on connecting artists and those who support them on a deeper level. To date, over 30,000 artists have played a Sofar event somewhere in the world. As we continue to grow, we are investing even more in how we support artists. As the Artist Community Manager, you will ensure that every Sofar artist around the world feels heard, supported and connected. You will engage directly with the Sofar artist community and will serve as a leading artist advocate to inform Sofar’s further investment in supporting artists.
LOCATION:: Ideally Boston. We will consider candidates in NYC as well.
RESPONSIBILITIES:: The Artist Community manager will work closely with a small, scrappy team to ensure every Sofar artist feels heard, supported and connected.
The Ideal Candidate:: Experience managing large communities with deep empathy, clarity of voice, efficiency and a strategic view toward the future you want to create. We’re open to all kinds of specific experience in this area, including community management, product marketing, social media management and evangelism. The key is a proven ability to engage with a big, diverse stakeholder group with clarity, consistency and empathy.

You know what it’s like to be an artist and a performer — ideally, you’ve used a car as your green room before a show; you’ve suffered through more bass drum sound checks than you care to remember; you’ve crashed on couches and on the road between shows. Though experience as a performer is preferred, other experience evincing a deep empathy for working musicians (road management, artist management, etc) also considered.
SKILLS \u0026 COMPETENCIES:: Deep commitment to connecting to all members of the community to make everyone feel welcome and heard, especially those with a different background from your own. This includes self-awareness to watch for unconscious biases and proactive steps to continually improve.
A track record of success in managing small, but diverse teams to ensure that every member of the community feels a sense of 1:1 connection.
Excellent communications skills — public speaking, email communication, social media and other forms of external communications
Ability to synthesize company goals and objectives with the goals and objectives of the community, to inform all aspects of strategy — products, services and company identity.
Highly visible, empathetic and hands-on communicator with the ability to build and lead a highly motivated, small scrappy team of interns and artist advocates.
Analytical enough to play a role in how we solicit and interpret feedback from the community and to draw actionable insights from the feedback.
Enjoys bringing order and structure to a ‘no charts,’ frenetic growth-stage company environment. Stays cool under pressure, able to deal with high-emotion situations while staying cool and focused.
Collaborative problem solver with low ego and high emotional intelligence, able to build consensus across teams
Experience working within a music or other creative community , focused company is a big plus.
Cannot live without live music and knows how to have a good time
WORKING HERE:: Sofar Sounds offers a comprehensive benefits package as well as opportunities for career growth and development.
COMPANY CULTURE:: People who join Sofar Sounds have an excitement and energy about what we’re doing. They want to be a driving force of growth for emerging artists, committed to creating a magical experience for performers and fans alike, and this is reflected in the way they work.

Title: Junior Marketing Manager, TVC (UK)
Description: Employed by Brown Cow, we are an organisation specialising in sourcing professional events support staff and placing them within the organisations that require specific support for ad-hoc projects.Continue to article...We are now keen to source a Junior Marketing Manager or Marketing Manager, working with one of our clients, an exciting well recognised media organisation.This is a full time, fixed term contract for 11 months and as such you need to have great impact to deliver across a large cross-functional team, ranging from product, policy, legal and mixed partnerships. You should have a minimum of 2 years' marketing experience, a marketing degree level qualification or relevant practical experience with the ability to manage multiple projects. We need you to have experience of working with agencies and vendors.It goes without saying that we expect a combination of excellent communication and interpersonal skills as you will need to support the EMEA Go to Market Lead and Local Product marketing Managers, so experience here is key.We want you to have a voice, so you can give everyone else a voice though your work. Look at our attached job description and person specification for more details and get in touch if you are who Brown Cow London are looking for. If interested, please email your CV and covering letter directly to sueross@dittonHR.co.uk explaining why you would suit the role. Please also state your salary expectations.Know the user. Know the magic. Connect the two. At its core, marketing starts with technology and ends with the user, bringing both together in unconventional ways.Our job is to demonstrate how our products solve the world’s problems–from the everyday to the epic, from the mundane to the monumental. And, we approach marketing in a way that only we can–breaking the rules, redefining the medium, making the user the hero, and ultimately, letting the technology speak for itself.YouTube's mission is to give everyone a voice and a chance to succeed, bring people together, and show them the world. With over 1 billion monthly active users, our ambition is to be the leader in all types of video. Music is a critical part of YouTube’s business, and YouTube is one of the top music brands globally. Every day millions of music fans come to YouTube to listen to the latest hits, find that song they can't find anywhere else, get more connected to an artist they love, learn more about one they just discovered, or be part of the latest music cultural phenomenon.Purpose of the roleIn this role, you will need to combine operational and executional rigour, and excellent communication and interpersonal skills to support the EMEA Go to Market (GTM) Lead and Local Product Marketing Managers in the development, tracking and execution of their YouTube Music and Premium go-to-market with a focus on the launch and sustain moments. The role aims to establish YouTube Music as the most local and loved music service and capture the attention of the industry, artists and consumers.The role holder will work with a large cross-functional team across product, partnerships, communications, policy, legal, and agency partners, enabling collaboration and ensuring that teams stay focused. You will be operating in a fast, ever-changing environment that requires you to be open to new influences and inspirationMain duties and responsibilitiesSupport EMEA GTM Lead and Local Product Marketing Managers in the development of through the line go-to-market strategy for priority and scale EMEA markets rolling out in 2019, defining launch and sustain phase approach leveraging local moments.In conjunction w/ the local xfn team, uncover local insights including competitive landscape and market context to help influence go-to-market approach and marketing positioning for YouTube Music & Premium.Support execution of local programs, capturing and evangelizing scalable best practices, and tracking spend and performance compared with other marketing activities. Local programs to include but not limited to: events, influencer marketing, media & brand partnerships.Collaborate with EMEA Growth Marketing team to manage the transcreation and local validation processes for the global evergreen acquisition and retention marketing touchpoints and programs enabling the launch and scaled promotion of YouTube Music & Premium.Responsible for defining, evangelizing and maintaining the launch playbooks, codifying best practices and recommending approach for priority and scaled markets.Main EMEA marketing point of contact with global and regional cross-functional team supporting operational needs to ensure all minimum launch requirements are met by maintaining up-to-date project reports, tracking and mitigating issues, and providing stakeholders with timely updates.Support management of regional and local events and marketing agencies, ensuring delivery timelines are defined and met.Applicable to all Brown Cow London employeesContribute to the effective and efficient running of Brown Cow London.Participate, as appropriate, in meetings.Adhere to all Brown Cow London's policies and procedures.Represent Brown Cow London in a professional manner.Treat all colleagues, Brown Cow London members, Clients and members of the public with dignity and work within and adhere to Brown Cow London's equal opportunities statement and policies.Carry out any reasonable requests made that are within the broad remit of this rolePERSON SPECIFICATIONQualifications/TrainingBA/BS degree or equivalent practical experience.Demonstrable experience in a marketing, product or GTM role, either client or agency side.Previous experience2 years proven experience within marketingProven ability to manage multiple projects.Experience working with agencies and vendors, and a variety of markets across Western Europe, Eastern Europe and Middle EastProven ability to build consensus and work effectively within a cross-departmental team.Key skillsA persuasive communicator, adept at packaging disparate or complicated information into a concise, compelling story, no matter the audience.Ability to work efficiently on multiple projects simultaneously, in a fast paced dynamic environment.Strong organizational and project management skillsProven ability to set goals and prioritize many initiatives and opportunities with minimal guidance.CompetenciesAdaptability/Flexibility: maintains effectiveness in a changing environment. Able to be flexible in approach to work without losing sight of key objectives.Attention to detail: accomplishes tasks with thoroughness, accuracy and reliability.Team working: builds and develops appropriate relationships with colleagues, member and suppliers at all level of seniority. Committed team player, with the ability to motivate others.Communication: expresses ideas effectively and conveys information appropriately and accurately both verbally and in writing.Resilience: maintains performance under pressure and / or opposition.Analysis/Reasoning: examines data in order to grasp issues, draw conclusions and solve problems.

Purpose of the role: In this role, you will need to combine operational and executional rigour, and excellent communication and interpersonal skills to support the EMEA Go to Market (GTM) Lead and Local Product Marketing Managers in the development, tracking and execution of their YouTube Music and Premium go-to-market with a focus on the launch and sustain moments. The role aims to establish YouTube Music as the most local and loved music service and capture the attention of the industry, artists and consumers.
Main duties and responsibilities: Support EMEA GTM Lead and Local Product Marketing Managers in the development of through the line go-to-market strategy for priority and scale EMEA markets rolling out in 2019, defining launch and sustain phase approach leveraging local moments.

In conjunction w/ the local xfn team, uncover local insights including competitive landscape and market context to help influence go-to-market approach and marketing positioning for YouTube Music & Premium.

Support execution of local programs, capturing and evangelizing scalable best practices, and tracking spend and performance compared with other marketing activities. Local programs to include but not limited to: events, influencer marketing, media & brand partnerships.

Collaborate with EMEA Growth Marketing team to manage the transcreation and local validation processes for the global evergreen acquisition and retention marketing touchpoints and programs enabling the launch and scaled promotion of YouTube Music & Premium.
Responsible for defining, evangelizing and maintaining the launch playbooks, codifying best practices and recommending approach for priority and scaled markets.

Main EMEA marketing point of contact with global and regional cross-functional team supporting operational needs to ensure all minimum launch requirements are met by maintaining up-to-date project reports, tracking and mitigating issues, and providing stakeholders with timely updates.
Support management of regional and local events and marketing agencies, ensuring delivery timelines are defined and met.
Applicable to all Brown Cow London emplo: Contribute to the effective and efficient running of Brown Cow London.
Adhere to all Brown Cow London's policies and procedures.
Represent Brown Cow London in a professional manner.
Treat all colleagues, Brown Cow London members, Clients and members of the public with dignity and work within and adhere to Brown Cow London's equal opportunities statement and policies.
Carry out any reasonable requests made that are within the broad remit of this role
PERSON SPECIFICATION: BA/BS degree or equivalent practical experience.

Title: Product Designer, Car & Wearables (SE)
Description: We are looking for an experienced product designer to join our Platform & Partner Experience team who are tasked with considering how to extend our core mobile experience across multiple contexts and device ecosystems, including native desktop, tablets, web player, automobiles, wearables, televisions, speakers, software integrations, and much more.Continue to article...What you'll doBe responsible for the design of Spotify's Partner Platform Experiences; to deepen relationships between artists and fans by extending Spotify into the car via an embedded experience.Work with small multi-disciplinary teams made up of representatives from engineering, product, product insights, and marketing, among others.You will help set the tone for our partnerships (Google, Amazon, GM, etc.) and advocate for quality when it comes to the Spotify experience as it is represented on external platforms.Take into account existing insights, technical constraints, business needs, driver distraction guidelines and specific platform demands to produce data-informed solutions.You'll take full ownership of your work and total responsibility for every last detail, every step of the way, from whiteboard sketching to high-fidelity final treatments.Rapidly produce multiple concepts and prototypes, understanding when deeper exploration is warranted and when it is best to change paths and try another approach.Who you areYou have at least 7 years of experience designing at consumer-focused tech or media companies; experience designing in car is a plusYou are comfortable working closely with teams from companies such as Google and Amazon.You're happy to travel the world to collaborate with our international partners and can be flexible with your working hours.You are a talented designer with strong UX and visual design skillsYou have the communication skills to help set up frameworks and brainstorm initial design concepts. Also instrumental is the ability to speak confidently to your own design work and convince others of your reasoning.You have a people-focused design mentality and want to design and build products/features that truly have meaningful impact.You have an online portfolio, or PDF that you can share with us. A link to your online portfolio, or a PDF, is required. Applications without a portfolio are unlikely to be considered.You are able to provide a brief explanation in your application of why you are excellent for this role, while highlighting your portfolioWe are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

What you'll do: Be responsible for the design of Spotify's Partner Platform Experiences; to deepen relationships between artists and fans by extending Spotify into the car via an embedded experience.
Work with small multi-disciplinary teams made up of representatives from engineering, product, product insights, and marketing, among others.
You will help set the tone for our partnerships (Google, Amazon, GM, etc.) and advocate for quality when it comes to the Spotify experience as it is represented on external platforms.
Take into account existing insights, technical constraints, business needs, driver distraction guidelines and specific platform demands to produce data-informed solutions.
You'll take full ownership of your work and total responsibility for every last detail, every step of the way, from whiteboard sketching to high-fidelity final treatments.
Rapidly produce multiple concepts and prototypes, understanding when deeper exploration is warranted and when it is best to change paths and try another approach.
Who you are: You have at least 7 years of experience designing at consumer-focused tech or media companies; experience designing in car is a plus
You are comfortable working closely with teams from companies such as Google and Amazon.
You're happy to travel the world to collaborate with our international partners and can be flexible with your working hours.
You are a talented designer with strong UX and visual design skills

You have the communication skills to help set up frameworks and brainstorm initial design concepts. Also instrumental is the ability to speak confidently to your own design work and convince others of your reasoning.
You have a people-focused design mentality and want to design and build products/features that truly have meaningful impact.
You have an online portfolio, or PDF that you can share with us. A link to your online portfolio, or a PDF, is required. Applications without a portfolio are unlikely to be considered.
You are able to provide a brief explanation in your application of why you are excellent for this role, while highlighting your portfolio

Title: Global Head of Commercial Development, Music Publishing (US)
Description: The people here at Apple don't just create products 4 they create the kind of wonder that's revolutionized entire industries.Continue to article...It's the diversity of those people and their ideas that inspires the innovation that runs through everything we do, from amazing technology to industry-leading environmental efforts. Join Apple, and help us leave the world better than we found it.Apple is seeking a senior expert in rights in musical compositions to build and lead a team to manage the commercial relationships relating to rights in musical compositions.This is a key role to support the rapid expansion and growth of Apple Music, iTunes, and all use of musical compositions across the Apple ecosystem. This position is in the new Global Music Publishing group and is based in Los Angeles. Are you a self-starter who is dedicated and demonstrates creative and critical thinking abilities?Do you possess a passion for music, and the craft of songwriting while also having a superior commercial mind?We’re growing and urge you to apply. We believe the role would place you in a highly-energized environment with some of the most talented creative individuals in the industry where you would be inspired to share your years of experience along with providing extraordinary support for the team.Key QualificationsA minimum of 10 years experience working in senior commercial and/or licensing roles within the music industry, ideally within music publishers, collecting societies and/or digital music servicesWe believe an extraordinary understanding of and passion for the global music publishing licensing landscape will be critical to your successYou have an intimate understanding of digital music and the content distribution industryYou possess excellent business development, negotiation, and partnership management skillsYou have a consistent record of working collaboratively within international, cross-functional teams and a high-growth environmentYou are willing to travelExcellent written and verbal communication skills and the ability to build and deliver impactful presentations at all levels of the organizationExcellent organizational skills and attention to detailAn ability to see the big picture and handle a high level of ambiguityConfidence in calling out issues and presenting to senior management on project status and schedulesHoned analytical skills, with an ability to parse through extensive data to extract the key elementsThe ability to build and deliver persuasive arguments in favour of complex concepts.You work well within a fast-paced, time-sensitive environment with proven experience working on multi-faceted, international, large-scale, time-critical projects.DescriptionIn this role, you will build and manage a team that will be responsible for business negotiations with music publishers and collecting societies around the world, building and maintaining relationships with their commercial and business affairs teams to enable the use of musical compositions within Apple as needed. You will work in partnership with the Legal team and the Operations branch of the Music Publishing team on negotiating complex licenses with publishing rights holders to support expansion to new markets, product features, pricing, and commercial programs designed to accelerate the growth of Apple Music. You will also collaborate closely with other elements of the Music Publishing team, as well as other facets of the Apple business, such as the Business Development team to facilitate and build business models for the use of musical compositions throughout the Apple business. A great mind for numbers will stand you in good stead for this role.Education & ExperienceBA/BS degree in Business or related subject, MBA or equivalent degree preferred.

Title: Senior Manager, Label Integrations (UK)
Description: As part of Kobalt's significant expansion of AWAL – our recorded music division – the Senior Manager, Integrations will be responsible for managing the day to day processes of the Integrations Operation team to deliver a best in class client experience, ensuring smooth onboarding of the client from contract closure to go live.Continue to article...Reporting to the Senior Director, Label Integrations, the successful candidate will put the client at the heart of the process and deliver outstanding support levels, through a strong knowledge of project implementations and by maintaining excellent relationships with external partners.You may be working for a label or distributor with experience in digital ops, supply chain and release management processes as well as deep expertise of physical media and digital music metadata, audio, artwork & video asset management and content management systems, as well as a passion for pushing things forward in this space.Most important is that you have great interpersonal skills and can cope with a high-pressure/fast-paced environment but remain optimistic & persistent to make things happen as well as have a passion for music and Kobalt!BACKGROUNDAWAL is the Recordings division of Kobalt, and provides a range of marketing, distribution and other services to artists and labels, empowering recording owners whilst enabling them to retain ownership and control of their masters. AWAL works with more than 12,000 clients and our roster includes labels including Glassnote Records, 30th Century Records, Loop, Chrysalis, Lex Records and SideOneDummy Records amongst many others.We're expecting our Catalogue and Label business to grow fast. That's where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!We're currently 600 people based across 12 offices and we're still growing.WHAT DOES A SENIOR MANAGER, LABEL INTEGRATIONS DO? Work closely with Senior Director, Label Integrations to onboard all catalogue and label clients.The day-to-day management of Integrations team, motivating staff through projects and prioritising workloads.Manage the support the Integrations team gives to clients throughout the onboarding journey, ensuring any advice given sets expectations, is timely, accurate and consistent.Work with external clients previous Distribution partners to coordinate the transfer of physical and digital manifests, metadata and digital assets.Establish & build upon excellent relationships with external distributors and across multiple internal business areas.Manage logistical processes including Change of Distribution notices for moves of physical catalogues with third party physical distribution partners.Maintain multiple systems and reports to ensure accurate reporting to the business of integrations, including Go Live dates, slippage, revenue recognition, non-starters etc and be able to provide a schedule of product go live dates with accurate timescales.Take ownership of client issues / complaints and work with team to resolve them in a timely manner, taking ownership for case resolution and closure, whilst liaising with the appropriate internal departments.Post Go Live delivery checks and continued metadata and asset enhancement.Administer and manage termination processes (Offboarding).WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Experience in managing people in operations or onboarding or other relevant business areas in the music industry is essential.Catalogue BACKGROUND: AWAL is the Recordings division of Kobalt, and provides a range of marketing, distribution and other services to artists and labels, empowering recording owners whilst enabling them to retain ownership and control of their masters. AWAL works with more than 12,000 clients and our roster includes labels including Glassnote Records, 30th Century Records, Loop, Chrysalis, Lex Records and SideOneDummy Records amongst many others.
WHAT DOES A SENIOR MANAGER, LABEL INTEGR: Work closely with Senior Director, Label Integrations to onboard all catalogue and label clients.
The day-to-day management of Integrations team, motivating staff through projects and prioritising workloads.

Work with external clients previous Distribution partners to coordinate the transfer of physical and digital manifests, metadata and digital assets.
Establish & build upon excellent relationships with external distributors and across multiple internal business areas.
Manage logistical processes including Change of Distribution notices for moves of physical catalogues with third party physical distribution partners.

Take ownership of client issues / complaints and work with team to resolve them in a timely manner, taking ownership for case resolution and closure, whilst liaising with the appropriate internal departments.
Post Go Live delivery checks and continued metadata and asset enhancement.
Administer and manage termination processes (Offboarding).

Advanced knowledge of music metadata in a digital distribution context, particularly the iTunes and Spotify style guides.
Previous experience of stock management and stock moves essential.
Expertise with content management systems (iTunes Connect & Spotify preferred).
Meticulous attention to detail and advanced analytical skills.
Ability to communicate effectively to a variety of stakeholders at all levels.
Highly proficient in spoken and written English.
Numerate and IT literate.

Title: General Manager (UK)
Description: The Music Publishers Association is seeking a General Manager to implement a clear strategy which will deliver the best possible results on matters of music copyright protection and industry related policies.Continue to article...The work of the General Manager will influence long term sustainability and an awareness of the importance of music publishing on behalf of its members within the UK.The role will include constant engagement with members, politicians, policy makers, industry stakeholders and with the general public, when necessary, on the MPA's work on policy, professional development, education and membership & outreach activities.The General Manager will implement the MPA's strategic and operational plans and lead, motivate and develop a small, but dynamic team to provide the MPA's Membership with the best possible service and results.The successful candidate will be an experienced manager, with a passion for music publishing and experience of working in the industry.They will support the growth of our Membership, oversee the effective delivery of a multitude of member meetings, projects and long-standing initiatives, as well as proactively seeking out new opportunities for the organisation.Responsibilities will include:Devising, developing and delivering the MPA strategic plan; in partnership with the CEO and MPA Board, communicating the vision of the organisation clearly to all relevant stakeholders.Responsibility for Board, Committee & Member Meetings;working with the team toconstruct interesting, relevant agendas and preparing papers in liaison with Chairs.Overseeing the planning and implementation of the annual programme of events; in particular leading on the development of all MPA training and professional development.Working with the DOO and CEO on Financials; including setting the MPA's annual budget, reviewing monthly management accounts and staff salaries and co-ordinating membership subscriptions.Generating revenue and sponsorship opportunities; overseeing the compilation of the annual Sponsorship Brochure, and hitting targets set in conjunction with the CEO and team.Representing the MPA and enhancing the reputation of the organisation; promoting the business and building strong and constructive relationships with other industry stakeholders including UK Music, UK Music members (including PRS for Music, BPI, AIM, The Ivors Academy), other relevant trade bodies and organisations such as the British Copyright Council and outreach organisationsPresenting at MPA Events and Public Speaking Engagements; moderating panels and training courses, delivering speeches and presentations to membership and external audiences.Engaging the membership in the work of the MPA and key Policy issues; working with the team to ensure that the interests of MPA members are promoted to government, policy makers and opinion makers anticipating new developments which may impact businesses.Developing and maintaining relationships with current and potential members; responding to Members needs by implementing new initiatives and maximising value for money for membership.The successful candidate will be a strong, focussed leader with significant experience of managing teams.They must have exceptional interpersonal skills and the ability to deal with individuals from all levels of the music industry, forging strong relationships and demonstrating excellent diplomatic skills to bring together diverse views to obtain consensus.They will have a capacity to thrive under competing demands on time and to enjoy prioritising a demanding workload with meticulous attention, able to set and action short, medium and long-term company strategies.The successful candidate will be joining a young, lively team at an exceptionally exciting moment in the history of music publishing and this role sits at the centre of it all.Application Process:To apply, please send your CV together with a covering letter to Lorna Greenwood 3 lorna.greenwood@mpagroup.com by Midday on Tuesday 7th May.Please note you must have the right to work in the UK. We are unable to consider applicants who do not have the right to work in the UK.

What do Music Promotion assistants do?: Music promotions assistants work on behalf of artists to help secure airplay, playlists and editorial support for a single or album. They often pitch directly to Radio, TV, Press and streaming services.
What does Artist Development mean?: Artist Development is to work with talent to cultivate and develop their career both artistically and financially, it requires a deep passion for music and good knowledge of brand and audio production.
Duties:: Writing press releases and setting up artist campaigns.
Maintenance and management of our contacts database.
Compiling weekly reports for clients based on promotional metrics (i.e. Airplay).
Pitching music to radio, TV, press and streaming platforms.
Discovering new talent for our Artist Development roster.
Required Competencies/Skills:: Music nut and a can-do
Excellent organisational ability, great communicator and a skilled writer.
Outstanding IT skills and social media
Passionate about finding new talent and helping artists to succeed.
Highly desirable:: Journalistic background or experience within radio/tv.
Previous experience within a PR team
Why join us?: We love technology and our team are absolutely mad about music. We're passionate about supporting artists in their career. This role will challenge you and provide the right candidate with real insight to the music industry and many opportunities to learn. We're also a really fun team.

Title: Artist Services & Marketing Coordinator (UK)
Description: Kartel are a Modern Music Services Agency representing a diverse roster of specialist, cultural and front-line artists, managers, labels and brands.Continue to article...Our passionate team provide a range of product, marketing, creative and distribution services to an incredible roster of both emerging and established artists and labels.We are looking for an Artist Services & Marketing Coordinator to join our growing team in Hackney, London. They will have experience working in digital marketing, artist management or record label environment. demonstrated track record of creative problem solving; thinks big, starts small, grows fast.Analytical and quantitative skills, ability to use hard data and metrics to back up assumptions and develop business cases. Ability to clearly communicate these data insights to others.High attention to detail including proven ability to manage multiple, competing priorities simultaneously.Proactive attitude; willingness to roll up sleeves and dig deep to achieve goalsProven experience developing and implementing multi-channel marketing campaigns that support market segment share growth.Amazon is an Equal Opportunity-Affirmative Action Employer – Minority/Female/Disability/Vet. Proactive attitude; willingness to roll up sleeves and dig deep to achieve goals
Proven experience developing and implementing multi-channel marketing campaigns that support market segment share growth.
