Title: Label Relations, Insights Lead (US)
Description: Imagine being a part of an agile team where your ideas have the potential to reach millions.Continue to article...Picture working on cutting-edge consumer-facing products, where every single team member is a critical voice in the decision-making process. Envision being able to leverage the resources of a Fortune-500 company within the atmosphere of a start-up. Welcome to Amazon Music, where ideas are born and come to life as Amazon Music Unlimited, Prime Music, and so much more.Everyone on our team has a meaningful impact on product features, new directions in music streaming, and customer engagement. We are looking for new team members across a variety of job functions including software engineering/development, marketing, design, ops and more. Come join us as we make history by launching exciting new projects in the coming year.Our team is focused on building a personalized, curated, and seamless music experience. We want to help our customers discover up-and-coming artists, while also having access to their favorite established musicians. We build systems that are distributed on a large scale, spanning our music apps, web player, and voice-forward audio engagement on mobile and Amazon Echo devices, powered by Alexa to support our customer base. Amazon Music offerings are available in countries around the world, and our applications support our mission of delivering music to customers in new and exciting ways that enhance their day-to-day lives.Come innovate with the Amazon Music team!We are searching for an exceptional talent to join Amazon Music's Label Relations team to help guide our ongoing strategy and day-to-day operations through the provision of actionable insights, derived through deep data analysis across a diverse range of business-critical areas.The Label Relations team is primarily responsible for innovating on the content in the service and managing strong relationships with our external Music partners.Specifically, we own the relationships with our record label partners and seek to drive collaborative initiatives that i) provide the best music experience for our customers and ii) grow our respective businesses. The Label Relations team also works closely with a wide range of internal departments; including Marketing, Merchandising, Content Acquisition, Operations, and works extensively with our global network of Amazon Music offices around the world.This position will drive informed decision making through rigorous analysis of service wide consumption trends, user flow data, audience development tools, voice related insights, new release (and catalogue) campaign performance, content insights etc. The business is evolving very quickly, and we need a self-starter who can address not just obvious, or prescribed data studies, but also take ownership and 8look under the hood' at the vast amount of consumption related data being generated daily, to determine action plans in order to further maximize our potential.In short, the overarching objective of this role will be to lead insights and analysis within the Label Relations team, providing actionable and strategic guidance to the organization. This is a unique opportunity to play a vital role on a fast-growing team and to own high visibility/impactful projects. The role will report to the Head of Label Relations (US & CA), and will also work extremely closely with Amazon Music's Business Intelligence team.The role requires a commitment to goals, proactive examination, and a relentless focus on the customer. The right candidate will thrive in an entrepreneurial, fast-changing, and collaborative environment where people are driven, enthusiastic, collaborative and not afraid to take risks. The ideal candidate will have proven experience delivering results both within and outside his/her organization.Additionally, the successful applicant will possess a track record of delivering scalable data solutions and impactful insights that drive growth and change. We are seeking an entrepreneurial, metrics-driven individual with strong ownership and focus, and someone who can prioritize effectively in a rapidly-moving environment, and communicate clearly and persuasively, distilling complex data points into actionable insights for the global team.If this sounds like you 3 we are looking forward to hearing from you!About YouConsumer obsessed, with deep curiosity to understand types of customers, qualitatively and analyticallyCommunicate with superior verbal and written skills across all levels of the organization. Title: Head of Sales and Retail (UK)
Description: Warp Records is a leading fully independent label with offices in London, New York, Los Angeles and Sheffield. We release music by ground-breaking and influential artists across genres with global audiences including Aphex Twin, Flying Lotus, Danny Brown, Brian Eno, Kelela, Yves Tumor, Boards of Canada, Oneohtrix Point Never and many more.Continue to article...We also provide services to a small family of labels including On U Sound, LuckyMe, Duophonic, Black Focus and Fade to Mind. This includes working with physical retail and distributors, DSPs, as well as planning and delivering exciting D2C promotions both within and outside record release campaign cycles.The successful candidate will have at least 3 years of experience in music or merchandise sales or distribution. They will be commercially minded with a proven track record of delivering sales and profitable campaigns.They will have the drive to innovate and deliver high-quality D2C products and experiences to fans of our artists. They will have a good understanding of streaming and the digital music landscape. They will have a passion for the artists and labels that we work with. They will be both highly collaborative, to work closely with the wider team on campaign activity, and highly self-sufficient and entrepreneurial to drive longer-term strategies. They will work closely with campaign managers in the UK and US, international marketing teams, production, promotions and our internal technology team. This position will be based in our London office.Principle ResponsibilitiesEnhancing our relationships with retailers, DSPs and distributors to work with them to deliver successful new release and catalogue campaigns.Creating and delivering successful global D2C strategies for our artists through their own stores, Bleep and other platforms. This includes music, merchandise and other products.Pitching new releases to DSPs and co-ordinating strategy with international teams.Developing our artists and label merchandise business.Working with campaign managers and marketing manager on delivering these elements as part of release campaigns, including where appropriate pre-orders, bundles, ticket promotions, merch drops, special editions and value adds.Managing product set up, sales notes, priority sheets, pricing and stock levels across stores, distributors and territories.Co-ordinating global retail initiatives and assets between UK, US and international departments.Regular reporting to label, artists and management.Required SkillsA thorough and up to date understanding of retail, the streaming environment and D2C.The ability to translate ambition and creativity into, efficient, effective campaignsA knowledge of, and passion for the artists and labels that we work with.Commercially driven, with a high level of numeracy and confidence in budgeting and pricing.An exceptional communicator and organizer, who hits deadlines and is able to prioritize their own efforts and that of a wider team.Experience in running successful D2C campaigns.Great attention to detail and commitment to quality.To ApplyPlease send a CV and covering letter to jobs@warprecords.com with Head of Sales and Retail as the subject.Closing date: 1st April 2019

Role Summary: We are looking for an experienced person to manage sales and retail for our artists across all labels. This includes working with physical retail and distributors, DSPs, as well as planning and delivering exciting D2C promotions both within and outside record release campaign cycles.

Title: Junior Manager, Digital Accounts (US)
Description: We are hiring a Junior Manager of Digital Accounts in our Los Angeles office. Reporting to the Senior Director, Digital Accounts North America, the Junior Manager will support the North American account management team with their day to day responsibilities.Continue to article...BACKGROUNDKobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the center of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL. Whether an artist is looking for the best digital distribution or a truly global campaign, AWAL has become the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers. This has created a high-quality path for artists to remain outside of the traditional label system while maintaining ownership of their masters. Our roster of clients includes artists such as Lauv, R3HAB, Timeflies, The Wombats, Betty Who, Rex Orange County, Tom Misch, and Bruno Major.We're currently over 500 people based across 10 offices and we're expecting to grow. That's where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!WHAT DOES A JUNIOR MANAGER, DIGITAL ACCOUNTS AT AWAL DO?Prepare and distribute pitch submission forms to streaming partnersPrepare DSP newsletter and artist updatesCoordinate the delivery of assets to external partnersCreate sales and streaming reports for key campaignsLiaise with global account managers and provide supportMaintain the DSP contact database, ensuring information is correct and up to dateAssist with the creation of account presentations for stakeholders and internal teamsForecast sales and streams across all digital and streaming partnersMonitor streaming activity and identify opportunitiesEffectively summarize, analyze, explore, and communicate data to AWAL organizationWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Bachelor's Degree in Music, Marketing and/or relevant field2 years' working experience in the music industry, ideally within digital sales or marketingAn understanding of the current music industry landscapeFamiliarity with major digital download and streaming platformsExcellent communication skillsStrong organizational skills and attention to detailProactive self-starter who is both reliable and willing to take responsibilityAbility to set and consistently meet deadlines

Title: Copyright Assistant (UK)
Description: Joining the Publishing Copyright department in our London office, the primary role of the Asia Copyright Assistant will be to assist in managing our catalogue by ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly with our sub-publishers in Asia and liaising directly with the collection societies where required.Continue to article...A successful candidate will need to have excellent communication skills when dealing with general enquiries from internal and external sources and can deal with pressure but remain optimistic and persistent to complete tasks accurately and efficiently.Founded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We're currently over 600 people based across 13 offices globally and still growing! If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!WHAT DOES A COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT AT KOBALT DO?Ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at collection societiesDealing with general enquiries regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreements.Handling song disputes and duplicate claimsProcessing new works and agreements as the company acquires rights to new cataloguesProcessing royalty statements received from collection societiesProcessing Label Copy received from Major and Local Asian labels.Analysing the royalties received to identify possible underpayment or delayed payment, and working with the societies to resolve these issuesYou may be asked to take on extra responsibilities 3 please be aware that the above list is not exhaustiveWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? Excellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to audienceTrack record of excellent organization and prioritisation skillsStrong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-setHigh level of written and spoken EnglishAnalytical skills as well as experience working with ExcelPrevious admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable

WHAT DOES A COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT AT KOBAL: Ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at collection societies
Dealing with general enquiries regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreements.
Handling song disputes and duplicate claims
Processing new works and agreements as the company acquires rights to new catalogues
Processing royalty statements received from collection societies
Processing Label Copy received from Major and Local Asian labels.
Analysing the royalties received to identify possible underpayment or delayed payment, and working with the societies to resolve these issues
You may be asked to take on extra responsibilities 3 please be aware that the above list is not exhaustive
WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKIN: Excellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to audience
Track record of excellent organization and prioritisation skills
Strong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-set
High level of written and spoken English
Analytical skills as well as experience working with Excel

The range of music we work with is both comprehensive and exciting, providing a challenging and rewarding experience for all our employees.As the Head of Sales & Marketing for the UK, you will be responsible for managing The Orchard’s local and global repertoire in the UK market as well as driving a talented team of local account managers. You will also be front facing, meeting labels, managers and artists, providing the opportunity to guide and develop their UK strategy. contacts, especially North America 3 some international travel may be required in the course of this role, as is flexibility for out of hours working.

Title: Senior Manager, Label Integrations (UK)
Description: As part of Kobalt's significant expansion of AWAL – our recorded music division – the Senior Manager, Integrations will be responsible for managing the day to day processes of the Integrations Operation team to deliver a best in class client experience, ensuring smooth onboarding of the client from contract closure to go live.Continue to article...Reporting to the Senior Director, Label Integrations, the successful candidate will put the client at the heart of the process and deliver outstanding support levels, through a strong knowledge of project implementations and by maintaining excellent relationships with external partners.You may be working for a label or distributor with experience in digital ops, supply chain and release management processes as well as deep expertise of physical media and digital music metadata, audio, artwork & video asset management and content management systems, as well as a passion for pushing things forward in this space.Most important is that you have great interpersonal skills and can cope with a high-pressure/fast-paced environment but remain optimistic & persistent to make things happen as well as have a passion for music and Kobalt!BACKGROUNDKobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the centre of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL.Whether an artist is looking for the best digital distribution or a truly global campaign, AWAL has become the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers.This has created a high-quality path for artists to remain outside of the traditional label system while maintaining ownership of their masters. The leading artists who have already chosen AWAL includes Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Lauv, Tom Misch, Rex Orange County, The Wombats, Bruno Major, Neneh Cherry as well as leading independent labels such as Glassnote and Downtown.We're currently over 600 people based across 13 offices globally and still growing! If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!WHAT DOES A SENIOR MANAGER, LABEL INTEGRATIONS DO? Work closely with Senior Director, Label Integrations to onboard all catalogue and label clients.The day-to-day management of Integrations team, motivating staff through projects and prioritising workloads.Manage the support the Integrations team gives to clients throughout the onboarding journey, ensuring any advice given sets expectations, is timely, accurate and consistent.Work with external clients previous Distribution partners to coordinate the transfer of physical and digital manifests, metadata and digital assets.Manage the pre and post signed deal physical and digital data reviews, quality-checks and research.Establish & build upon excellent relationships with external distributors and across multiple internal business areas.Manage logistical processes including Change The leading artists who have already chosen AWAL includes Lauv, Nick Cave, Deadmau5, Die Antwoord, Verite, Tom Misch, Bruno Major and Rex Orange County.Kobalt is currently over 600 people based across 10 offices and we're continuing to grow. That's where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! The Role: This is a newly created role within the Legal & Business Affairs team, based in New York. You will be a key member of the team leading negotiations with record labels, publishers and collecting societies in the U.S. and Europe.

Your role will be to partner closely with internal business counterparts to negotiate and deliver partnerships with record companies, music publishers, collecting societies and other stakeholders across the music industry.

Your work will include business modelling, deal proposals, negotiation of commercial terms, and drafting and negotiation of contracts for SoundCloud’s unique business model. You will need to be a seasoned dealmaker who is capable of leading complex negotiations and driving towards solutions, often in challenging circumstances. A deep understanding of the digital music rights landscape and experience of negotiating international licensing deals for or with digital service providers and/or UGC platforms are prerequisites.

You will be a subject matter expert within the Company, and will be expected to share your knowledge and experience within the team and across the wider organisation to help educate and inform your colleagues. You will give clear and commercially focused advice to other teams on matters relating to music rights
PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES: Support SoundCloud's overall business by licensing in music rights for various business initiatives
Draft and negotiate synch licenses, waivers and other similar terms with labels and publishers
Negotiate and draft partnership agreements with music industry stakeholders
Coordinate with product and engineering teams in connection with product development related to music licensing
Collaborate with other members of the Legal & Business Affairs team to ensure business needs are met
Represent SoundCloud towards external stakeholders in the music industry, both as part of your day-to-day work and potentially in public forums
Your qualifications: Qualified lawyer* with at least 8 years’ relevant experience
Good understanding of the digital music rights landscape in the US & Europe
Experience in negotiating complex rights deals in multiple territories
Experience of working in a dynamic, fast-paced environment
Experience of building and leading a team
Strategic thinker and creative problem solver
Comfortable dealing with uncertainty and ambiguity
Good relationships within the music, media, and/or consumer tech industry
Fluent in English, and ideally one or more other European languages
Diversity at SoundCloud:: CSoundCloud is for everyone. Diversity and open expression are fundamental to our organization they help us build a social platform and global community where anyone can create, discover, and share sounds. We acknowledge the challenges in our industry, and strive to develop an inclusive culture where everyone can contribute.D

The Role:: Working in conjunction with the Head Of International and local Label Managers & Product Managers, the global digital marketing and data team to build and develop global marketing, promotional and commercial plans for all relevant Caroline repertoires – direct signings and label deals – and all deal types.
Artist/Label Relationship Management:: Provide leadership, advice and support to internal and external label partners in respect of their release schedule in the areas of sales, distribution, marketing and promotional plans as appropriate to their deal type.
Provide a first-class knowledge of the music industry to support advice given.
Oversee the release schedule process and ensure that all aspects of it are properly managed and scheduled in a timely manner.
Responsible for stock forecasting, internal sales reporting, chart reports, ensuring chart eligibility of Caroline releases.
Project Management:: Market assigned artists and labels on a global level.
General problem solving for all aspects of releases.
Forge strong working relationships with Labels, Artists, Artist Management, Marketing colleagues and Territories in the Caroline structure, travel to oversee large promo events when necessary.
Ensure timely delivery of assets from external partners, and internal delivery into supply chain, such that marketing timelines are met – preorders, instant grats etc go live on time.
Monitor international programmes on key projects and provide regular detailed written reports on priority projects as determined by Head Of International.
To devise and direct a promotional strategy for artists to ensure maximised sales in line with marketing objectives.
To work on own initiative to ensure all opportunities are maximised – identify artists labels in the Caroline system that merit marketing and promotional focus.
Person Specification:: Passion for UK and US urban music.

Proven experience in a similar International, label management or digital role; ideally within Music Industry or Music Agency with a track record of liaising effectively with both Managers, Labels and Artists.
Experience of working, influencing and persuading at all levels of an organisation (upwards and downwards).
The ability to learn quickly and adapt to the changing market.
Flexible and able to prioritise own workload and self-motivated; hard working and enthusiastic.
Prepared to travel internationally as required.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, above average presentation skills.
Team player with a flexible approach, willing to defer when appropriate.

Title: Label Manager, Australia (AUS)

Title: Frontend Engineer, f/m/d (DE)
Description: SoundCloud is the world’s leading audio platform, allowing everyone to share and discover unique content anywhere, anytime, on the web and on mobile. We’re currently seeking a product engineer with a frontend focus to support us and help us grow here in Berlin.Continue to article...You have a background in web development and JavaScript, experienced with React or other Javascript application architectures.Experience in developing for the server would be desirable, for example, with Ruby, NodeJS or Scala. You have a keen interest in creating polished user interfaces by working closely with designers, and producing high quality code with an emphasis on automated testing. You are interested in solving problems that you can connect to business value, and are excited by the opportunity to work with cutting-edge browser capabilities and new technologies such as ES2015, Typescript and React Native.More broadly, you are a smart, engaging person who cares about solving business problems across multiple code bases and can help our team keep a robust but sustainable pace.You have the maturity and skill that comes with practical, hands-on technical experience, you understand the importance of balancing technical debt with delivery, and you have the confidence to express your opinions but the humility to learn from your experience.You have pair-programmed or have some interest in trying it. A formal computer science degree is not necessary: we’re interested in your skills and attitude, not your degree.If this sounds like you, we'd love to talk to you.Diversity at SoundCloud:SoundCloud is for everyone. Diversity and open expression are fundamental to our organization; they help us build a social platform and global community where anyone can create, discover, and share sounds.We acknowledge the challenges in our industry, and strive to develop an inclusive culture where everyone can contribute.

Title: North America Label Manager (US)
Description: Warp Records is a leading fully independent label with offices in New York, Los Angeles, London and Sheffield.Continue to article...We release music by groundbreaking and influential Artists across genres with global audiences – Aphex Twin, Flying Lotus, Danny Brown, Brian Eno, Kelela, Yves Tumor, Boards of Canada, Oneohtrix Point Never and many more. Title: Music Editor, Japan (JP)
Description: Apple Music, the world’s fastest-growing subscription music streaming service, offers an incredible catalog of tens of millions of songs, combined with your entire iTunes library, which you can stream or download for offline listening all completely ad-free. We are looking for a passionate and talented individual to program Apple Music in Japan. This is an editorial role, reporting to the Head of Music for Japan. If you are passionate about music, enjoy working and collaborating across a number of cross functional teams, and are well organized to meet targets and deadlines, we’re keen to hear from you.Continue to article...Do you have at least three years experience and knowledge in radio, retail, A&R, editorial, record labels, and/or any other music related field(s)?Are you culturally savvy with a good sense of judgment on the appropriateness of content?We’re looking for somebody with excellent interpersonal and communication skills, and excellent collaboration skills.We’d love to find somebody with a self-motivated, driven attitude.If you have experience in copy-writing, that will also be useful.As the Music Editor, you will build an editorial strategy and decide editorial priorities. This will take into account product release info, demographic targeting, partner promotions and marketing promotional opportunities etc. You’ll have responsibility for programming featured content within our content management system on a regular basis. You will ensure the editorial voice of Apple Music is clear and well-balanced, between innovative promotions and the discovery of great new music.We’ll need you to support an overall Apple Music editorial strategy that seeks to synchronise marketing and editorial activities across media types (apps, music, TV, movies, podcasts, Apple Books, etc.) in a co-ordinated fashion. Research user activity using internal Apple Music reporting tools to refine editorial selection. You’ll work closely with the Marketing team to maximise the reach of promotion opportunities, and also liaise with the Production team on a weekly basis to ensure that design, audio and copy requirements are delivered in a timely fashion and are always consistent with our brand values. Cross-functional work with Apple Music/iTunes Music, TV, movies, and apps teams is another key area of responsibility.BS/BA or equivalent work experience required.

Title: Independent Label Relations Representative (US)

They are responsible for handling relationships, developing content and improving revenue opportunities for independent label partners.Continue to article...We are the point contact for our independent label partners. Title: Senior Policy Manager, Trust & Safety (US)
Description: We are seeking an outstanding Senior Policy Manager to join our growing Trust & Safety team. And lest we forget, we’re stocked up on an enormous amount of the music you love.Can’t contain your excitement? Send your cover letter and resume to jobs@armadamusic.com.Make sure to put ‘A&R Manager Amsterdam’ in the e-mail subject. We’re looking forward to meeting you!About ARMADA MUSIC:Armada Music is the biggest independent dance music label in the world, based in Amsterdam, the Netherlands (HQ), London and New York (satellite offices). Founded in 2003, Armada Music represents labels and artists across the electronic music spectrum, including Afrojack, Andrew Rayel, Armin van Buuren, Erick Morillo, Fedde Le Grand, Gareth Emery, Kevin Saunderson, Lost Frequencies, Loud Luxury, Morgan Page and W&W.

What will you be doing?: You will scout and sign talented artists making waves on a global scale
You will maintain existing relationships with artists and nurture/develop your own acts;
You will actively search for artists, writers, producers, toplines, remixers and other specialized talent able to contribute or add to the quality of our releases and our repertoire;
You will match the right people by making perfect use of your connections within the music industry;
You will visit performances, showcases, club nights and similar events to discover new talent and maintain existing relationships;
You will chase information and details to pass on to our Legal department;
You will create and oversee release plans made in tandem with the assigned Product Manager (and the PR, Creative and Digital teams);
You will use your knowledge of dance music to help releases realize their potential;
You will assist the Product team in developing marketing assets, strategies, marketing plans and campaigns.
Who are we looking for?: You are extremely passionate about dance music;
You have at least three to five years of qualifying work experience in a similar role;
You know all there is to know about dance music and Armada Music, and you are extremely knowledgeable about the discographies of relevant artists and catalogs from many other labels worldwide
You know all about the main music distribution partners (from physical distribution to digital distribution and streaming);
You know how record labels operate, especially in terms of product marketing, business occasions, A&R administration and more;
You’re able to cope with pressure and changing deadlines extremely well; you’re able to keep your head straight while working on multiple projects at the same time;
You are fluent in Dutch and English, in speaking and writing, and you’re the boss when it comes to negotiating deals;
You love working in tandem with all of the company’s different departments to get results fast;
You have a broad musical taste and are well-known with dance music’s many different sub genres;
You are resourceful, creative and you live and breathe dance music.
What will you get in return?: You will be joining the ranks of a young and dynamic team;
You will receive a monthly salary befitting your age and experience;
We offer favorable secondary working conditions, including 25 days off (per year) and an appealing pension scheme;

And if that doesn’t convince you, know that we’ve got our own in-office club for our wild office parties and Friday afternoon drinks, a gym open to anyone who wants to blow off some steam after (or before) working hours and colleagues so fun you probably don’t even want to head back home at the end of the day. And lest we forget, we’re stocked up on an enormous amount of the music you love.

Our team provide bespoke, personalised services to an incredible roster of both emerging and established artists and labels.Continue to article...We are looking for a Global Sync Manager to join our team in North America.REQUIREMENTSThe ideal candidate will have a strong instinct for the music services environment and be passionate about supporting professional creators in todays’ ever evolving recorded music landscape. They will need day to day licensing experience, ranging from deal negotiation, client meetings, music clearance, music pitching, contract drafting, and quote system entry.JOB DESCRIPTIONPro-actively maintain, expand and develop a network of relationships within the global Film, TV, Advertising, Game and Music Supervisor community.Prepare day-to-day sync pitches on demand, reacting to worldwide music searches.Respond to quote requests and handle pricing and deal negotiation.Communicate with Kartel team and clients to ensure collection of assets and copyright information ensuring catalogue is “pitch ready”Assist with the Management of sync catalogue platform, ingesting new releases, development of best practice metadata management and rights management.Regularly update existing sync clients with catalogue in order to maximise revenuesCreation of sync reports and newsletters to demonstrate past success and share best practice guidelines with existing clients.Work with senior management on sync and licensing business development plans.REQUIREMENTSYou will be a passionate music expert with diverse musical knowledge across multiple genresYou must have an extensive sync and licensing network and understanding of music licensingExcellent general music industry knowledge keeping up with trends, innovation and opportunities in sync, licensing and the wider industryMust have a demonstrable track record securing music sync opportunitiesStrong networking skills, confident communicator, great interpersonal skills and attention to detail are a prerequisiteCompetent negotiator and diligent record keeperThis is a unique and challenging opportunity to join an ambitious and versatile music business shaping the future of consumer music marketing. REQUIREMENTS: The ideal candidate will have a strong instinct for the music services environment and be passionate about supporting professional creators in todays’ ever evolving recorded music landscape. They will need day to day licensing experience, ranging from deal negotiation, client meetings, music clearance, music pitching, contract drafting, and quote system entry.

Title: Royalty Tracker (UK)
Description: Concord Music Publishing is looking to employ a full time Royalty Tracker in its London office.Continue to article...Company and Department OverviewConcord Music is the independent, worldwide leader in the development, acquisition and management of recorded music, music publishing and theatrical performance rights.With offices in Los Angeles, New York, Nashville, London, Miami and Berlin, Concord Music has a diverse and vibrant roster of active recording artists, composers, and songwriters, and a vast and historic catalog of works by some of the most treasured names in music history.THE ROLEThe music publishing business is afflicted by the issue of missing royalty income due to errors in the registration of songs or due to under-reporting or misallocation by societies and agents. The Royalty Tracker role is tasked with maximising client royalty income through the tracking of missing royalty income. A good understanding of music societies and royalty collection is therefore paramount.This role requires someone who can organise and manage multiple activities simultaneously. The Royalty Tracker will work closely with internal managers, clients and third-party providers. They need to be confident, articulate and determined to claim and collect royalty income.Key responsibilities of the role include:Analysis of digital, streaming and physical products salesFilm and television broadcast investigationsLive performance tracking and inter-company project workClaiming missing royalties from societiesAnswering client queriesWorking closely with internal departments to ensure completeness of society registrations and royalty processingEXPERIENCE AND SKILLS3 years of experience in music publishingStrong knowledge of music society processes across multiple royalty streamsGood working relationships with music industry professionalsEnthusiastic and self-motivated with confidence to work on own initiativeStrong multi-tasking skillsExcellent communication skillsRoyalty tracking background preferableExceptional research skillsBENEFITSSalary £30,000 – £35,000 per annum, depending on experience.Health insurance28 days holiday per annumCompetitive pension schemeConcord Bucks – for related gigs and merchandiseAPPLICATION DETAILSPlease send a covering letter and CV to Concord HR c/o jane.monksfield@concord.com by the 1st February 2019.

Company and Department Overview: Concord Music is the independent, worldwide leader in the development, acquisition and management of recorded music, music publishing and theatrical performance rights.
THE ROLE: The music publishing business is afflicted by the issue of missing royalty income due to errors in the registration of songs or due to under-reporting or misallocation by societies and agents. The Royalty Tracker role is tasked with maximising client royalty income through the tracking of missing royalty income. A good understanding of music societies and royalty collection is therefore paramount.

This role requires someone who can organise and manage multiple activities simultaneously. The Royalty Tracker will work closely with internal managers, clients and third-party providers. They need to be confident, articulate and determined to claim and collect royalty income.
Key responsibilities of the role include: Analysis of digital, streaming and physical products sales
Live performance tracking and inter-company project work
Working closely with internal departments to ensure completeness of society registrations and royalty processing
EXPERIENCE AND SKILLS: 3 years of experience in music publishing
Strong knowledge of music society processes across multiple royalty streams
Good working relationships with music industry professionals
Enthusiastic and self-motivated with confidence to work on own initiative
BENEFITS: Salary £30,000 , £35,000 per annum, depending on experience.
Health insurance
28 days holiday per annum
Competitive pension scheme
Concord Bucks – for related gigs and merchandise
APPLICATION DETAILS: Please send a covering letter and CV to Concord HR c/o jane.monksfield@concord.com by the 1st February 2019.

Title: Copyright Assistant (UK)
Description: Joining our Publishing Copyright department in Kobalt’s London office, the primary role of the Copyright Assistant will be to support the team with managing our catalogue in the US by ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly.Continue to article...The successful candidate will need to have attention to detail and research skills as well as excellent communication skills when communicating internally and with external sources and can deal with pressure but remain optimistic and persistent to complete tasks accurately and efficiently.Founded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model.Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We're currently 600 people based across 10 offices and we're still growing.WHAT DOES A US COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT AT KOBALT DO?Ensuring song details are correctly registered in Kobalt's favour at the US collection societiesInvestigating possible matches and duplicate work details at the US collection societiesManaging a shared inbox dealing with general inquiries from all US societies regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreementsYou may be asked to take on extra responsibilities 3 please be aware that the above list is not exhaustiveWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Strong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-setExperience working with large data setsExcellent communication skills and high level of written and spoken EnglishTrack-record of excellent organization and prioritisation skillsPrevious admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirablePrevious admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable.Most important is that you are ambitious and flexible and can adapt to a growing and changing department!

WHAT DOES A US COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT AT KO: Ensuring song details are correctly registered in Kobalt's favour at the US collection societies
Investigating possible matches and duplicate work details at the US collection societies
Managing a shared inbox dealing with general inquiries from all US societies regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreements
You may be asked to take on extra responsibilities 3 please be aware that the above list is not exhaustive
WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKIN: Strong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-set
Experience working with large data sets
Excellent communication skills and high level of written and spoken English
Track-record of excellent organization and prioritisation skills
Previous admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable
Previous admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable.
Most important is that you are ambitious and flexible and can adapt to a growing and changing department!

Title: Marketing and Sales Operations Coordinator (US)
Description: West One Music Group (WOMG) seeks a Marketing and Sales Operations Coordinator based in Los Angeles, CA.Continue to article...Reporting to: Brad Burnside, VP of Partnerships & OperationsTerritories Covered: US, Canada, and Latin AmericaLevel of Experience: 1-2 yearsBelow are the key areas of responsibility in the listed territories that this role covers:MarketingWOMG’s music users demand authenticity and we want to position ourselves as integral, creative partners in what they do. This should be communicated in all our interactions – online, in-person and any events / touch points we share.You’ll be responsible for supporting the VP of Partnerships & Operations and our global marketing team in driving the transactional side of marketing.Our goal is to represent and increase the usage of our constantly-expanding repertoire: West One Music, Fired Earth Music, The Scoring House, Refuel Music, SOMOS, EDS, And Then Some, Zero Degrees, and any new labels that we create over time.You’ll help execute on our North American marketing plan in line with the overall brand from HQ in London. This includes: social media presence, tone of voice control for “Americanizing” appropriate communications, events planning, local marketing material creation, merchandise and swag orders and other things relevant to the territory.Sales OpsBehind every great client-facing team is a fully integrated and supportive operations team. Sales Ops helps the administrative process line up with our overall vision and strategy for expanding business by establishing best practices and meaningful data reporting for the sales team.You’ll be responsible for supporting the VP, the Business Development and the Account Management teams in sales operations for North America. The administrative and strategic systems you will help coordinate will lead to the successful staging for our outbound sales team.You will be encouraged to closely align with sales opportunities and constantly evolve how to internally support successful sales cycles from lead generation to client retention.Along with an external support person in Salesforce operations, you will be responsible for representing and facilitating CRM best practices for the internal team.This includes managing database and logged activities through periodic data cleans and dashboard review, enabling monthly progress reviews with the sales team, supporting sales team in implementing effective lead generation, database management, and reporting, and ensuring we’re using Salesforce to the best of its potential, measured by mid-month progress on follow up reports.Practical SkillsMarketing automation software (Pardot), CRM (Salesforce), detail obsession, time management, major social media platform management, copyright, vendor negotiation, basic photoshop/in design abilities a plus.Character TraitsA successful candidate will have a positive, can-do attitude that lends itself to our general mantra of being smart, brave, and captivating. You will play a key part in supporting our sales team to deliver the WOMG umbrella brand and its various catalogs to establish strong market share across the US, Canada, and Latin America. For this specific role, this could apply as follows:Smart: interested in overall marketing and sales cycles and their successful application to a growing business. You want to help organize our back of house operations and will constantly seek to improve our workflows.Brave: constantly curious about innovative ways to generate and activate new and existing leads / client bases. You like to test the limit and will be fearless in helping stake our claim in a crowded market.Captivating: commanding a fresh and effective writing style with solid eye for briefing compelling and impactful design. You will use your skills to masterfully encapsulate our mission so that people will be excited about our expansion efforts.Further to being smart, brave, and captivating, our team ethos is to consistently be:Proactive (always seeking out new leads under your own motivation andsources)Generative (always seeking to generate activity and business opportunity from inside of you, rather than being micro-managed)Understanding (having a deep grasp of our industry, the cultural practices and practical landscape of broadcasting and music use)Entrepreneurial (thinking outside the box, taking risks, following through and ultimately delivering everything you commit to)Intuitive (rolling with the punches, responding appropriately, being creative)Supportive (sharing successes, being communicative, owning a willingness to dive into whatever is necessary to get the job done)Professional (maintaining a code of business that includes excellent relationships, personal integrity and a mindset that delivers on commitments)Helpful (being enthusiastically up for doing what’s needed rather than staying stuck in your version of your role)Creative (being willing to try new things and think around problems for intelligent solutions)Organized (maintaining a high standard of recording of data and intelligence from your day to day so that there is ultimate transparency in all you do)Are you excited about this incredible opportunity?Candidates should apply to Lizzie Winn, Global Head of Human Resources, at jobs@westonemusic.com with cover letter stating why you think you’d be a great fit and resume including a low-res photograph.

Marketing: You’ll be responsible for supporting the VP of Partnerships & Operations and our global marketing team in driving the transactional side of marketing.

Our goal is to represent and increase the usage of our constantly-expanding repertoire: West One Music, Fired Earth Music, The Scoring House, Refuel Music, SOMOS, EDS, And Then Some, Zero Degrees, and any new labels that we create over time.

You’ll help execute on our North American marketing plan in line with the overall brand from HQ in London. This includes: social media presence, tone of voice control for “Americanizing” appropriate communications, events planning, local marketing material creation, merchandise and swag orders and other things relevant to the territory.
Sales Ops: You’ll be responsible for supporting the VP, the Business Development and the Account Management teams in sales operations for North America. The administrative and strategic systems you will help coordinate will lead to the successful staging for our outbound sales team.
You will be encouraged to closely align with sales opportunities and constantly evolve how to internally support successful sales cycles from lead generation to client retention.
Along with an external support person in Salesforce operations, you will be responsible for representing and facilitating CRM best practices for the internal team.

This includes managing database and logged activities through periodic data cleans and dashboard review, enabling monthly progress reviews with the sales team, supporting sales team in implementing effective lead generation, database management, and reporting, and ensuring we’re using Salesforce to the best of its potential, measured by mid-month progress on follow up reports.
Practical Skills: Marketing automation software (Pardot), CRM (Salesforce), detail obsession, time management, major social media platform management, copyright, vendor negotiation, basic photoshop/in design abilities a plus.
Character Traits: Smart: interested in overall marketing and sales cycles and their successful application to a growing business. You want to help organize our back of house operations and will constantly seek to improve our workflows.

Brave: constantly curious about innovative ways to generate and activate new and existing leads / client bases. You like to test the limit and will be fearless in helping stake our claim in a crowded market.

Captivating: commanding a fresh and effective writing style with solid eye for briefing compelling and impactful design. You will use your skills to masterfully encapsulate our mission so that people will be excited about our expansion efforts.
Further to being smart, brave, and capti: Proactive (always seeking out new leads under your own motivation andsources)
Generative (always seeking to generate activity and business opportunity from inside of you, rather than being micro-managed)
Understanding (having a deep grasp of our industry, the cultural practices and practical landscape of broadcasting and music use)
Entrepreneurial (thinking outside the box, taking risks, following through and ultimately delivering everything you commit to)
Intuitive (rolling with the punches, responding appropriately, being creative)
Supportive (sharing successes, being communicative, owning a willingness to dive into whatever is necessary to get the job done)
Professional (maintaining a code of business that includes excellent relationships, personal integrity and a mindset that delivers on commitments)
Helpful (being enthusiastically up for doing what’s needed rather than staying stuck in your version of your role)
Creative (being willing to try new things and think around problems for intelligent solutions)

partners.Persuasive and effective at interacting with all levels of the partner organization, from analyst to Director to C suite level with partner relationships.Results-focused with strong attention Title: Director, International Marketing (UK)
Description: AWAL is looking for a flexible, confident and highly organised International Marketing Director, based out of our London office. Reporting to the General Manager, the role will involve working directly with our artists, managers and label clients and alongside our network of International Marketing Managers, distribution partners and licensees to plan.Continue to article...This is an exciting time to be joining AWAL, Kobalt’s Recordings division, and to be successful in the role, we need a confident, skilled international marketer who can build and deliver successful and innovative international campaigns, driving projects through the company worldwide.Adding to your international network of contacts, you will also be build long lasting relationships with our international partners.BACKGROUNDKobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the centre of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL. Whether an artist is looking for the best digital distribution or a truly global campaign, AWAL has become the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers. This has created a high-quality path for artists to remain outside of the traditional label system while maintaining ownership of their masters. The leading artists who have already chosen AWAL includes Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Lauv, Tom Misch, Rex Orange County, The Wombats, Bruno Major, Neneh Cherry as well as leading independent labels such as Glassnote and Downtown.We’re currently over 500 people based across 11 offices and we’re expecting to grow. That’s where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!WHAT DOES DIRECTOR, INTERNATIONAL MARKETING AT AWAL DO?Work closely with your roster of AWAL Recordings artists and label clients to construct & Implement Global Marketing Plans & StrategiesManage territorial budgets & set sales/streaming targets with international partnersWork closely with and drive our global network of Marketing Managers and distribution partners to deliver top-grade resultsBring innovation and creativity to campaignsCommunicate release information and pitching materials to the Digital Accounts Team and set playlist targets and strategiesGenerate campaign reports and analyse streaming data to identify trends and opportunitiesOversee the collection of initial stock orders, promotional items, and sales figures from our international networkWork with team to schedule promo trips and phoner schedulesBuild and maintain good working relationships with AWAL’s International network of label managers, distribution partners, licensees, and promo companiesDistribute assets & tools to the international teamsWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? A minimum of 5 years’ experience working in the International Department of a record label or distributorA deep understanding of International markets, retail, digital & media outlets is essentialExperience working closely with artists & managersYou must be highly organised & able to prioritise and have an interest in data & analyticsAbility to build and maintain relationships with international partnersWhen preparing your application, please include in your cover letter what recent music marketing initiatives or campaigns you’ve found interesting and why.

BACKGROUND: Kobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the centre of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL. Whether an artist is looking for the best digital distribution or a truly global campaign, AWAL has become the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers. This has created a high-quality path for artists to remain outside of the traditional label system while maintaining ownership of their masters. Reports to: Director of UK Marketing & Label Strategy
Responsible for: Marketing support with primary responsibilities for translating artist strategies into action plans and budgets, data analysis, trend interpretation and providing general assistance across the UK label team as required.
Position Summary: The role supports all aspects of marketing and the work of the Director, from the creation of the artist’s marketing plans and budgets through to the roll out of artist releases.
The role is also expected to provide key analytical insight and support to the UK and European team through engagement with Nettwerk’s business intelligence and analytical systems.
Main Duties:: Working with the Director, assist with development and roll out of all aspects of an artist’s release campaign
Assist in the coordination of the work of both internal team members and outside service providers
Working directly with the production team, manage artist release schedules and ensure adherence to production requirements and timelines
Participate in label and artist meetings as determined by the Director
Artist Release Support: Working with the Director, assist with development and roll out of all aspects of an artist’s release campaign
Assist in the coordination of the work of both internal team members and outside service providers
Working directly with the production team, manage artist release schedules and ensure adherence to production requirements and timelines
Participate in label and artist meetings as determined by the Director
General Marketing:: Assist the team in analyzing data and reports either directly from the Company’s business intelligence systems or from reports published by the Company’s analytics department; help the team identify and act on trends found in the data

Research and develop an understanding of trends in the UK marketplace as it pertains to aspects of streaming platforms. This includes analysis of local market playlist uptake, evaluating the impact of influencers and measuring the effect of marketing campaigns.

Assist the office’s marketing team members to create evaluations and summaries on various initiatives, including internet marketing, radio and promo campaigns. Distribute relevant reports that support both the UK team and the broader European and North American teams.
Liaise under the direction of the Director, with managers, artists and outside PR and other companies as required to set up and carry out marketing and promotion plans for Nettwerk artists in the UK.

Monitor the state of spending against budgets on active releases
Other: Other reporting and administrative support as required by the Director
Qualifications: Experience in the marketing of product or services and providing analytical support to help drive such efforts is an asset
Proven experience in dealing directly with clients/artists is also an asset
Exposure to social media marketing and promotional practices
Exposure to data reporting and business intelligence systems such as Tableau

Positive/enthusiastic attitude
Proven organizational skills and attention to detail
Strong business writing skills
Strong interpersonal and communication skills

The Position: We are looking for a Senior Manager, Income Tracking Systems to join the Group Income Tracking team based in our Paddington office.
BMG’s Income Tracking team are the company’s ‘royalty detectives’ hunting down monies which are due to our clients with a combination of attention to detail, endless tenacity and insight.

Self-starter

25 days annual leave plus 3 days between Christmas & New Year

Title: Director of Marketing (US)
Description: Blue Élan Records is an independent record label based in Los Angeles (Westwood) California.Continue to article...For us, it starts with the music. Good music deserves to be heard, no matter who it’s by, no matter the age, no matter the gender, no matter the faith, no matter the sexual orientation—no matter any of that.Good music by committed artists. That’s what our Blue Élan family is all about.Voices that deserve to be heard for the pleasure and the pure enjoyment that good music brings.With this goal in mind, we are looking to expand our team with the addition of an experienced Director of Marketing, to lead our marketing team and expand our reach and opportunities for our artists.Position: Director of MarketingLocation: Los Angeles (Westwood), CA, with some travel requiredRequirements for Application:2 years of music industry experience preferred and 5 years in a digital and social media marketing role required;Strong knowledge of and experience with primary ad platforms, including AdWords, Facebook, and Youtube; working relationship with and knowledge of digital agencies a plus;Strong organizational skills and attention to detail with ability to multi-task and thrive on a small team with minimal guidance;Demonstrated critical thinking and the ability to jump from the creative side of marketing to the analytical, including ability to adapt strategies based on analytics; andPassion for music and technology/digital trends and an interest in innovating and growing in the music marketing space.Job Description:Devise, define, structure, plan, implement, and analyze marketing initiatives for Blue Élan and each Blue Élan artist;Coordinate and execute campaigns for artists and events hosted, promoted, or supported by Blue Élan, including developing brand strategy, directing the development of marketing collateral, and implementing local market advertising plans;Analyze consumer demographics, consumer preferences, and competitor data, and generally evaluate the effectiveness and performance of marketing campaigns;Oversee Blue Élan staff, including the marketing sub-team, regarding marketing agendas and campaigns;Compose advertising copy and various marketing materials for use by Blue Élan and its artists, including drafting copy for e-mail marketing and social media marketing;Derive and implement inbound marketing agendas for Blue Élan and each Blue Élan artist to attract new customers and supporters by creating awareness and raising interest through non-traditional and emerging marketing initiatives, such as podcasts, blogs, newsletters, and innovative social media strategy, including performing and analyzing requisite research through market analysis to define target audiences and consumer interests;Pitch and secure strategic partnerships with websites and digital media companies in coordination with Blue Élan’s PR team(s);Assist artists and their teams with new media issues, including website maintenance, basic coding, social media verifications and e-mail list cultivation; andDevelop and expand Blue Élan’s direct-to-consumer efforts in conjunction with Blue Élan’s account managers to implement artist-specific pre-order offerings and expand ongoing offerings.

Requirements for Application:: 2 years of music industry experience preferred and 5 years in a digital and social media marketing role required;
Strong knowledge of and experience with primary ad platforms, including AdWords, Facebook, and Youtube; working relationship with and knowledge of digital agencies a plus;
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail with ability to multi-task and thrive on a small team with minimal guidance;
Demonstrated critical thinking and the ability to jump from the creative side of marketing to the analytical, including ability to adapt strategies based on analytics; and
Passion for music and technology/digital trends and an interest in innovating and growing in the music marketing space.
Job Description:: Devise, define, structure, plan, implement, and analyze marketing initiatives for Blue Élan and each Blue Élan artist;

Coordinate and execute campaigns for artists and events hosted, promoted, or supported by Blue Élan, including developing brand strategy, directing the development of marketing collateral, and implementing local market advertising plans;
Analyze consumer demographics, consumer preferences, and competitor data, and generally evaluate the effectiveness and performance of marketing campaigns;
Oversee Blue Élan staff, including the marketing sub-team, regarding marketing agendas and campaigns;
Compose advertising copy and various marketing materials for use by Blue Élan and its artists, including drafting copy for e-mail marketing and social media marketing;

Derive and implement inbound marketing agendas for Blue Élan and each Blue Élan artist to attract new customers and supporters by creating awareness and raising interest through non-traditional and emerging marketing initiatives, such as podcasts, blogs, newsletters, and innovative social media strategy, including performing and analyzing requisite research through market analysis to define target audiences and consumer interests;
Pitch and secure strategic partnerships with websites and digital media companies in coordination with Blue Élan’s PR team(s);
Assist artists and their teams with new media issues, including website maintenance, basic coding, social media verifications and e-mail list cultivation; and
Develop and expand Blue Élan’s direct-to-consumer efforts in conjunction with Blue Élan’s account managers to implement artist-specific pre-order offerings and expand ongoing offerings.

Ability to construct and implement innovative marketing strategies and ideas

Title: Finance & Strategy Manager (US)

Title: General Manager (UK)
Description: Established in 2007, Downtown Music Publishing is one of the world’s leading rights-management firms with offices in major music markets around the globe, including New York, Nashville, Los Angeles, London, Amsterdam, Tokyo, Paris, and Sydney.Continue to article...The company’s catalog spans over a hundred years of popular music, encompassing some of the world’s most beloved songwriters and artists including The Beatles, John Lennon & Yoko Ono, Ryan Tedder, Benny Blanco, Hans Zimmer, The Kinks, One Direction, Mötley Crüe, Robin Thicke, Naughty Boy, Phish, Hardwell, Jason Isbell, Sturgill Simpson, Santigold, Carla Bruni, and Social Distortion.Downtown Music Publishing is wholly owned by Downtown & Company, which also owns New York-based Downtown Music Studios and Songtrust, the world’s largest technology platform for songwriting and music publishing royalties, which powers global royalty collection from over 120 territories and more than 20,000 unique income sources worldwide.Downtown is looking for a General Manager, for its UK office. In addition to their primary responsibilities for the office, the GM will have a role in helping to coordinate the workflow between the Companies offices around the world in support of all Downtown business units. This role will report directly to the Chief Operations Officer based in New York.What does the General Manager do:Work with COO and other senior executives to establish formal workflow between the NYC, London and other European offices to achieve the goals of Downtown and its two primary business units, Downtown Music Publishing and Songtrust;All UK based employees (other than MD) would have dotted line reporting to GM, with GM assuming full leadership of the UK staff with regards to day to day issues. The GM would also work in tandem with solid line hiring managers and Head of People for CORE sessions (i.e., performance reviews) for all UK employees (other than MD);Lead office related responsibilities with support of administrative staff and local office manager (i.e., office space issues, insurance contracts, miscellaneous vendors, back-office administrative support in foreign markets, etc.);Work as logistics lead in establishing new foreign offices in the territory (i.e. set up legal entity, office space, banking). May be called upon to do same for a small number of offices outside of the territory;Establish best practices in London office w/r/t to key business processes;Responsible for administration of office budgets (e.g. T&E oversight, capital expenditures, functions and events outside of the creative/marketing budget);Liaise with General Counsel and outside legal and tax advisors regarding maintaining corporate good standing and tax complianceWhat skills do you have:10 years of working at a publisher or CMO, or other relevant experience.Hands on knowledge of how music royalties are collected and distributed globallyAppreciation for technology and the opportunity it presents to improve global royalty collectionAble to travel internationally from time to time to New York and other European offices.

We aim to revolutionize the way people consume content, and we need some very talented people to help us achieve our mission.Continue to article...We are seeking an outstanding Legal Counsel to join our growing Legal team in New York.As Legal Counsel for Spotify you will partner with and provide legal guidance to our Licensing and Business Development teams with a particular focus on the Business Development side of the business. Our deals are state of the art, groundbreaking and always evolving so it will be up to you to continuously assess and advise the teams on legal issues using a creative and pragmatic approach.What you´ll doReview, draft and manage a wide range of commercial agreements, including hardware agreements, global partnership deals, content licences and reseller/payment service provider agreementsImprove our standard form agreements and legal processes to help us scale globally with a consistent but locally relevant approachWork closely with our business teams to summarize and communicate legal concepts clearly and effectively and help them synthesize legal frameworks in developing their business strategies.Advise our teams about relevant legal contexts, issues and risks, complex agreement structures, approval processes, and company policies and proceduresProvide support to other legal and business teams globally on an as-needed basisWho you areLaw Degree (or equivalent) from top-tier universityAbility to practice in the United StatesCombined 4-7 years of experience working in a top tier law firm and/or in-house legal departmentTeam player with an ability to work autonomouslyPrevious experience partnering with global stakeholdersStrong track record of working with a wide range of commercial agreements with a technology, business development, content licensing, media and IP focusExperience working in technology sector; recorded music and publisher licensing experience a bonusWe are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. We strongly believe that diversity of experience, perspectives, and background will lead to a better environment for our employees and a better product for our users and our creators. This is something we value deeply and we encourage everyone to come be a part of changing the way the world listens to music.

What you\u00B4ll do: Review, draft and manage a wide range of commercial agreements, including hardware agreements, global partnership deals, content licences and reseller/payment service provider agreements
Improve our standard form agreements and legal processes to help us scale globally with a consistent but locally relevant approach
Work closely with our business teams to summarize and communicate legal concepts clearly and effectively and help them synthesize legal frameworks in developing their business strategies.
Advise our teams about relevant legal contexts, issues and risks, complex agreement structures, approval processes, and company policies and procedures
Provide support to other legal and business teams globally on an as-needed basis
Who you are: Law Degree (or equivalent) from top-tier university
Ability to practice in the United States
Combined 4-7 years of experience working in a top tier law firm and/or in-house legal department
Team player with an ability to work autonomously
Previous experience partnering with global stakeholders
Strong track record of working with a wide range of commercial agreements with a technology, business development, content licensing, media and IP focus
Experience working in technology sector; recorded music and publisher licensing experience a bonus

artists, as well as the future of the growing management business of !K7.It will suit someone with a strong entrepreneurial attitude and the interest to work in a focussed international team.

The role requires:: Taking lead in all areas of artists’ core businesses, including international touring, recording campaigns, brand & commercial partnerships, business affairs and beyond
Sound knowledge of various areas of an artist’s business affairs – legal and financial – as well as current issues and topics affecting the wider music industry
Daily artist management tasks supported by an assistant as well as broader strategic thinking
A well curated international network of industry contacts
Well honed artist liaison skills as well as proven communication skills with wider internal and external team members
At !K7 we value within our team:: Ability to work within a team of advisors and to contribute as one to achieve the goals of our artists and business together
Interest in ever improving our systems and working practices collectively at the company
A moral-minded, professional and forward thinking outlook for the businesses of our artists and therefore of !K7.
!K7 can offer the successful candidate:: The opportunity to work with significant current artists at various levels of development, with full team support and resource, including experienced colleagues based internationally, and team assistants.
Expected travel with artists when required and for meetings and conferences internationally.

The insight into working in a forward thinking management model where artists are able to build and keep their own rights, promote their own material, and remain in control of their careers , with us as their partners.
The opportunity to grow an artist roster further from within K7’s business model: we are always growing.
More about !K7:: !K7 Music is an international music services and label group founded in 1985, with offices in Berlin, London and New York, and an international team of 30 music industry professionals.

!K7 Artist Management supports a diverse range of artists with innovative and creative approaches across the music value chain, working closely with the other departments of the company including Marketing, Sales & Distribution, and Licensing. !K7’s current roster includes Tricky, Mykki Blanco, DJ Tennis, Lafawndah, FaltyDL, Niklas Paschburg, Hania Rani, Marquis Hawkes, Bad Breeding, Nathan Micay and the Juan Maclean.
