Key responsibilities:: Prepare a timeline and write a marketing plan for each project in conjunction with Label Head.
Maintain an up-to-date budget on each project in conjunction with the Label Head.
Work with artists and managers to come up with an agreed creative direction, including but not limited to video commissioning, artwork commissioning, photo shoots and general asset creation, such as
EPK’s and online content.
Work closely with Label Head and artwork designer to deliver the artwork as the artist has approved, including thorough artwork checks.
Source label copy, working with production to bring the product to market to the correct spec and on budget.

Communicate with 3rd party team and internal team on all elements of the project and progress of the campaign; including organising regular team meetings, taking and distributing notes and following up on action points raised in conjunction with the Label Head.

Work with the Label Head on planning and creative concepts for campaign advertising and then following through with external media agencies and the internal digital media agency (Fused) regarding delivery of ads and artwork for TV, outdoor, print and digital campaigns.

Liaise with the Caroline digital marketing team and artist management on creative ideas for the artist’s online and social media presence, D2C store, website and asset creation, ensuring Caroline digital campaigns always incorporate the latest innovations formats and platforms to connect artists with audiences and artist website and social media remain up-to-date and on message at all times.
Update the commercial sales team on all project developments and supplying them with the tools they need to best sell our products e.g. POS/exclusives/D2C bundles/artwork and accurate sales notes.

Liaise with the promo team on all UK artist promo runs including radio sessions, TV performances, press interviews, ensuring that costs are closely managed within budget and making travel and logistical arrangements (gear hire etc), as required.
Liaise with international marketing team to keep them up to date on the progress of all UK projects.

Liaise with artist management and the internal sales team on tour stock orders for live dates, ensuring all stock is ordered in a timely manner for shows and where possible, barcode scanners are supplied so that sales can be UK chart eligible.

Plan ticket buys for London and regional shows and festivals, in consultation with the Label Head and liaising with the Marketing Assistant on guest-lists, ensuring there is an adequate provision for all key senior management, promo, marketing, sales, synch and brand partnership team members and their clients.
Plan and organise regular campaign planning meetings for active projects, including preparing a meeting agenda and debriefing with the Label Head in advance of the meeting.

Assist Caroline to maximise promotional and marketing opportunities for all the artists, by keeping abreast of all new developments both inside and outside the industry that could help us to maximise sales.
Essential:: Passion for UK and US urban music.
Previous relevant experience in the music industry.
Good understanding of the release process within a major label.
Understanding of how UK music marketing works.
Relevant marketing and creative contacts.
Excellent communication skills – written and verbal.
Excellent organisation skills and attention to detail.
Efficient and able to work independently to a high standard.
Highly organised.
The ability to learn quickly and use own initiative.
Ability to remain calm when working under pressure and to deadlines.
Flexible and able to prioritise a large workload.
Hard working and enthusiastic team player.
Self-motivated with the ability to deal with the routine tasks as well as the more interesting aspects of the role.
Keen to develop within the field of marketing.
Passion for contemporary culture and creative work relevant to Caroline’s roster e.g. artwork and packaging design, photography, music video.
Desirable:: Record company experience, particularly in production and or digital/social media area would be an advantage.
An established contact network within the UK urban music scene would be an advantage also.

Title: International Communications Manager (DE)
Description: The International Communications Manager is responsible for supporting local markets on the development and execution of the communications campaigns and individual communications activities connected to their Red Bull Music (RBM) events, primarily Red Bull Music Festivals and large-scale signature concepts.Continue to article...They are the first point of contact for countries' teams with regards to RBM festivals and responsible for representing Red Bull and Red Bull Media Network's international strategic priorities in the field.Overall, they are also responsible for improving the communications output by mentoring and supporting regional communications teams and up-leveling quality, reach and engagement of regional communications activities with the support of the Red Bull Media Network (RBMN).Key Responsibilities, Tasks and Activities:Country SupportGuide country communications and marketing teams on the development of a communications plan.Be the first point of contact in the development of overall communications plan and be responsible for connecting the relevant vertical media specialists once stories and concepts are on strategy and ready for development. Vertical specialists include (but are not limited to) social media, editorial, moving image, live broadcast and audio portfolio.Support the local team from development of plan through to delivery. Ensure all major milestones are met, communications opportunities are maximized and team is operating effectively. Ensure planning and development is done with a suitable timeline to allow for improvement and upleveling (where relevant).Develop reporting standards for the communications campaigns and activities around RBM experiences and ensure countries and the teams responsible for communications campaigns and activities deliver reports within a defined time period.Report on project, campaign and overall results to the international RBM and RBMN teams when required.Highlight best practices, learning and examples and share with other countries and the international team to continue the improvement of RBM experience communications.Develop tools, processes and other opportunities for countries to deliver better communications campaigns and activities around RBM experiences.Provide insights from working with the countries to help ideate how teams could be improved and where there is an opportunity for better output through training or more effective collaboration with the international marketing and RBMN teams.Global OpportunitiesIdentify international Earned, Social and Owned communications opportunities from local RBM experiences that can be expanded beyond the host country.Manage international media visits to country experiences, either personally or through an agreed process with the host country. Manage media requests and access to talent.Provide insights from the industry and music landscape to help improve RBM output and results globally.Deliver improved Business Planning guidelines and support tools for countries for event communications to enable them to deliver a stronger plan and ultimately better results year-on-year.REQUIREMENTS5-6 years of experience working in music communications, marketing, and/or public relations.Confident with project management.Ability to handle multiple projects at different phases simultaneously.Skilled at building and relationships across many different cultural lines.Interested and knowledgeable about music and creative culture relevant to our work.Highly-organized, diligent, and comfortable presenting.Willing to travel internationally.Proficiency with Google Docs, Word, Excel, Powerpoint, etc.Very high level written and spoken English (an additional language would be a bonus but is not required).

Country Support: Guide country communications and marketing teams on the development of a communications plan.

Be the first point of contact in the development of overall communications plan and be responsible for connecting the relevant vertical media specialists once stories and concepts are on strategy and ready for development. Vertical specialists include (but are not limited to) social media, editorial, moving image, live broadcast and audio portfolio.

Support the local team from development of plan through to delivery. Ensure all major milestones are met, communications opportunities are maximized and team is operating effectively. Ensure planning and development is done with a suitable timeline to allow for improvement and upleveling (where relevant).

Develop reporting standards for the communications campaigns and activities around RBM experiences and ensure countries and the teams responsible for communications campaigns and activities deliver reports within a defined time period.
Report on project, campaign and overall results to the international RBM and RBMN teams when required.
Highlight best practices, learning and examples and share with other countries and the international team to continue the improvement of RBM experience communications.
Develop tools, processes and other opportunities for countries to deliver better communications campaigns and activities around RBM experiences.

Provide insights from working with the countries to help ideate how teams could be improved and where there is an opportunity for better output through training or more effective collaboration with the international marketing and RBMN teams.
Global Opportunities: Identify international Earned, Social and Owned communications opportunities from local RBM experiences that can be expanded beyond the host country.
Manage international media visits to country experiences, either personally or through an agreed process with the host country. Manage media requests and access to talent.
Provide insights from the industry and music landscape to help improve RBM output and results globally.
Deliver improved Business Planning guidelines and support tools for countries for event communications to enable them to deliver a stronger plan and ultimately better results year-on-year.
REQUIREMENTS: 5-6 years of experience working in music communications, marketing, and/or public relations.
Confident with project management.
Ability to handle multiple projects at different phases simultaneously.
Skilled at building and relationships across many different cultural lines.
Interested and knowledgeable about music and creative culture relevant to our work.
Highly-organized, diligent, and comfortable presenting.
Willing to travel internationally.
Proficiency with Google Docs, Word, Excel, Powerpoint, etc.
Very high level written and spoken English (an additional language would be a bonus but is not required).

Title: Copyright Assistant, German Speaking (UK)
Description: Joining the Publishing Copyright department in our London office, the primary role of the Copyright Assistant will be to assist in correctly registering works and agreements in Germany, Austria and Switzerland at the local collection societies and carrying out all related tasks to maintain and manage our catalogue to highest standards, including working with royalties.Continue to article...The successful candidate will need to have excellent communication skills when dealing with general enquiries from internal and external sources and can deal with pressure but remain optimistic and persistent to complete tasks accurately and efficiently.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model.Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized.This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We’re currently 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re still growing.WHAT DOES A COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT AT KOBALT DO?Ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at collection societiesDealing with general enquiries regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreementsHandling song disputes and duplicate claimsProcessing new works and agreements as the company acquires rights to new cataloguesProcessing royalty statements received from collection societiesAnalysing the royalties received to identify possible underpayment or delayed payment, and working with the societies to resolve these issuesYou may be asked to take on extra responsibilities – please be aware that the above list is not exhaustiveWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Excellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to audienceHigh level of written and spoken German as well as EnglishTrack-record of excellent organization and prioritisation skillsStrong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-setAnalytical skills as well as experience working with ExcelPrevious admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable.Most important is that you are ambitious and flexible and can adapt to a growing and changing department!

BACKGROUND: Founded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model.
WHAT DOES A COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT AT KOBAL: Ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at collection societies
Dealing with general enquiries regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreements
Handling song disputes and duplicate claims
Processing new works and agreements as the company acquires rights to new catalogues
Processing royalty statements received from collection societies
Analysing the royalties received to identify possible underpayment or delayed payment, and working with the societies to resolve these issues
You may be asked to take on extra responsibilities – please be aware that the above list is not exhaustive
WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKIN: Excellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to audience
High level of written and spoken German as well as English
Track-record of excellent organization and prioritisation skills
Strong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-set
Analytical skills as well as experience working with Excel
Previous admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable.
Most important is that you are ambitious and flexible and can adapt to a growing and changing department!

Title: Copyright and Licensing Administrator, Music Publishing (US)
Description: Another Victory Inc., located in Chicago, Illinois, is seeking an experienced Copyright and Licensing specialist.Continue to article...The ideal candidate will have a minimum of 3-5 years’ expertise in music publishing administration, including registration of song titles, a full understanding of intellectual property rights, dealing with Performing Rights Organizations and working with our Finance department regarding publishing statements.Additionally, the ability to creatively and successfully assist current staff with exploiting our catalog and maximizing copyright revenues with ad agencies, music supervisors, cable and television networks etc.You should have an intense passion for the music/publishing business, a substantial list of contacts and relationships as well as a true entrepreneurial desire to be part of a growing and important part of our business.Candidate must have in depth knowledge of copyright administration and copyright law, proven experience as a royalty administrator with a music publishing company, exceptional Microsoft Office skills, a strong understanding of the music industry particularly the administrative aspects of music publishing, contractual experience and the ability to work independently in a highly communicative, team oriented environment.Excellent benefits – medical, dental, life and 401K.If you think you are the ideal candidate for the position or may know someone who would be ideal for this exciting opportunity please send your cover letter and resume which will be in the strictest of confidence to: work@victoryrecords.com.

Title: Tax Senior (US)

Title: Streaming Lead (UK)
Description: Areas of responsibilityContinue to article...Attend streaming partner meetings to present and pitch our artists and releases for editorial, marketing and wider partner support. Including Spotify / Apple Music / Amazon / Deezer / YouTube and others as they enter the UK marketplace.Use data from each platform to present the strongest and most compelling argument on the performance of our artists and their tracks to deliver partner playlist support and growth.Communicate off-platform activity where relevant to help build partner's understanding of the overall success and momentum of each act.Deliver weekly and ad-hoc reports on playlist additions & track performance across DSPs for label.Lease with each streaming services editorial, marketing, events, brands and label / artist support teams rather than just their editorial playlist teams to build the deepest and most productive relationship with each partner.Devise creative content based marketing strategies in conjunction with the label's marketing and creative teams to drive engagement of an artist's material on streaming services.A key part of the role will be to drive consumption of an artist's overall catalogue of music on streaming services. Including creative content and marketing executions to remind fans and casual audiences of previous releases and drive consumption of this material. Including working with artist and management to educate them on the value of focussing on their entire body of work rather than just the latest release.Find creative ways to use regular moments in the yearly calendar and moments outside of music to deliver a connection to an artist's streaming catalogue.Build artists audiences and monthly listenership on streaming services to allow us to be less reliant on 3rd party editorial playlists and decisions for the success of our releases. Including optimisation of algorithmic programming for maximum effect.Find innovative and effective ways to build streaming partners into all facets of an artist campaign. Including social support, earned and paid media, bespoke video assets, integration into outdoor and other traditional marketing assets, live event integration, ticket pre-sales and more.Keep artist streaming profiles up to date including images, bios and other relevant information. Ensure an artists' streaming profiles are reflecting and clearly signposted on all artist channels and marketing assets with relevant market team.Liaise with internal playlist service on pitching artists for our UK and International network of playlists.Forensic analysis of a tracks' performance in specific playlists and the growth across each platform.Utilise in-house tools and external to inform data-driven decisions 3 Tableau, Spotify and Apple Artist Dashboards, Google Analytics, Facebook/Instagram Insights 3 both first and third party tools.Establish and deliver performance growth targets across stream, social engagement, followers etc.Develop an understanding of how radio and other forms of non-streaming support including paid and earned media impact the streaming performance of a song and artist. Title: Digital Marketing Manager (UK)
Description: We’re looking for a Digital Marketing Manager to join AWAL – Kobalt’s recordings division and work with our incredible and growing roster of emerging and already established talent from all over the world.Continue to article...Based out of our London office and reporting to the VP, Digital Marketing you will be working closely with UK & International marketing teams as well as our US-based digital marketing counterparts.As our digital expert, you will be responsible for conceiving and executing best-in-class digital marketing campaigns and initiatives working across multiple platforms to grow audiences & followers as well as maximize engagement for new and developing Kobalt Music Recordings artists.You are most likely already working in the music industry in a similar role with excellent understanding of all digital platforms and abreast of current music industry developments. You also need to be highly organised and methodical, but able to bring creativity, enthusiasm, and fresh ideas to the table.As much as this position requires a great deal of strategic thinking and planning, you are still a “doer” and someone who is very hands-on in their approach to work.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model.Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We’re currently 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re still growing.WHAT DOES A DIGITAL MARKETING MANAGER AT KOBALT DO?Conceive industry leading digital marketing initiatives & social media strategies, using experience and expertise to formulate original and ground-breaking ideasSet up and manage digital advertising campaigns whilst ensuring spends are effectively maximized to achieve campaign goalsOriginate assets as required for social media and advertising campaignsCompile and analyse reports, using data from Kobalt’s streaming analytics platform alongside 3rd party platforms and dashboards, and present back to the marketing and management teamsRun digital health checks at the outset of campaigns, advise clients on how to optimize their online channels and profilesDrive digital best practices through the companyManage and develop relationships with digital platformsWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Advanced experience working closely with artists and managers, digital platforms, and Digital Marketing in the music industryExperience in sourcing, executing and measuring influencer marketing campaigns either in the UK and/or global territoriesProven track-record in building social numbers and followersOrganized self-starter with the capacity to manage an often intense workload and work in a high-pressure environmentExcellent stakeholder management and communication skills at all levels is keyPresence as well as ambition to continuously improve and with a drive to approach each campaign with enthusiasm and innovative/creative mind-setStrategic in approach but hands-on and able to implement with rigourCollaborative, with a “can do” attitude, providing solutions to any problems

Title: Registrations Assistant (UK)
Description: To enable accurate royalty collections on behalf of our clients, we need to make sure that collection societies have all the necessary copyright information in their systems to license and match claims for the use of music in streaming, radio, TV etc.Continue to article...Joining our fast-growing Publishing Operations team in a brand-new role, the Registrations Assistant will support the team with the end-to-end process of work registrations.We are looking for someone to become a go-to-person for a range of work registrations areas, and become an essential part of the wider copyright team.This means that your role will be dynamic both in terms of level of responsibility but also in collaboration across several teams at Kobalt.It’s therefore crucial that you are a quick-learning self-starter, someone with an analytical mind-set, is tech-savvy and cares about all the little details.You should also have an exceptional attention to detail and a genuine interest in data and the importance of accurate data in the music industry.To be successful in the role, you need to deal with the pressure of having a high volume of repetitive tasks but remain optimistic and persistent to complete tasks accurately and efficiently as well as excellent time management and confident in asking questions.An interest in admin and copyright as well as music publishing is highly desirable but most important is that you are ambitious, hardworking and take pride in the work you do!BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model.Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We’re currently 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re still growing.WHAT DOES A REGISTRATIONS ASSISTANT AT KOBALT DO?To provide support to Registrations Manager in overseeing the work registrations processBuild good working relationships with teams involved in registration processAssist with improving reporting and monitoring processesAssist in management of priority work registrationsAssist in documenting the registration processesSupport the Copyright team with generating registrationsAssist with management of ad-hoc registration tasksWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Excellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to audienceExcellent organization and prioritisation skills working in a ever-evolving, fast-paced environmentStrong attention to detail, accuracy, and a detail-driven mind-setHigh level of written and spoken EnglishComfortable working with large data sets as well as using ExcelTech savvy, generally interested in new technology and can pick things up quicklyYou may have a background in any music industry role, but ideally have experience working in fast paced office environments in coordinator/researcher/operational/financial and/or customer support roles working with large data sets with internal & external email correspondence.

Title: Press Officer and Radio Plugger opportunities (UK)
Description: We are delighted to be expanding our team and are currently looking for both a Press Officer and a Radio Plugger.Continue to article...Yes Please are a music publicity company based in the buzzing heart of Shoreditch.We’re small but perfectly formed, creative and open-minded, and always looking for people with great ideas, a fantastic network, and a strong work ethic to join our team.Our current roster includes: elbow, Cherry Glazerr, Primal Scream, Ry X, Run The Jewels, De La Soul, Weyes Blood and Lauv amongst others.We work with a range of labels, artist management companies, charities and live events.We’re always up for speaking to new people, and currently have brilliant opportunities to join us as:Press OfficerThe ideal candidate will be a self-motivated and passionate music PR with previous proven experience in a similar role, a thorough knowledge of print and online press campaigns, and a strong existing network in music PR will be key.Radio PluggerJust like the Press role, the ideal candidate will be a self-motivated and passionate Radio Plugger, with previous proven experience in this field, providing them with a thorough knowledge of radio and an excellent network of contacts across stations.Because we’re a small team, we keep an open mind when it comes to working patterns and flexibility.We believe in hiring great people who want to work on projects they’re passionate about, and support them in their career development.Whether you have a wealth of existing knowledge and can bring your own clients to the table, or maybe you’ve got just a year or two under your belt, but have a burning desire and drive to learn and grow, why not get in touch?We’d love to hear from you!Please send your CV to hello@biypeople.co.uk with the subject heading “Press Officer – Yes Please” or “Radio Plugger – Yes Please” before December 8, 2018.

Press Officer: The ideal candidate will be a self-motivated and passionate music PR with previous proven experience in a similar role, a thorough knowledge of print and online press campaigns, and a strong existing network in music PR will be key.
Radio Plugger: Just like the Press role, the ideal candidate will be a self-motivated and passionate Radio Plugger, with previous proven experience in this field, providing them with a thorough knowledge of radio and an excellent network of contacts across stations.

Title: Project Manager, Music Platforms (AUT)
Description: Red Bull Media House is a global media company that produces, distributes and publishes authentic content and inspirational entertainment programming in the sports and lifestyle genres.Continue to article...From film to television, print to digital media, music and games, the company’s products can be experienced on the latest devices and platforms, fascinating audiences worldwide.Long Story ShortIn this role, you'll guarantee delivery of Red Bull's music repertoire across all in-house and external platforms.You'll drive the development of all existing and future music platforms and ensure their maintenance. Moreover, you'll assure metadata consistency across platforms, analyze data for business intelligence, and create and conduct educational workshops around music platforms.Internal PlatformsYou'll take over the project management of our Red Bull Audio Library digital product developments and maintenance. Providing first level technical support for Audio Library users and communicating insights by monitoring repertoire performance, as well as production reports for management, will be main tasks in your role as Music Platforms Manager.External platformsAnother aspect of this role will be to ensure availability of our owned and controlled music repertoire on B2B and B2C platforms. You'll manage operations of 3rd parties facing Red Bull Media House platforms and push further technical development of our RBMH platforms.other platformsMaintaining other music platforms for 3rd party deliveries (e.g. Source Audio, Harvest Media, YouTube), ensuring the availability of exploitable content and managing deliveries to distribution partners and other 3rd parties will be part of your role as well.ExperienceMinimum experience of 5 years in a product or project management position.Highly skilled in technical project management.Product development experience.Experiences with Content Management Systems.Familiar with delivery standards for DSPs, YouTube Content ID and finger-printing technologies.Background in web and database programming with a strong affinity to database library management.Highly developed sense of data accuracy.Knowledge about audio visual content production workflows / music cue sheet reporting.General knowledge about the music industry, music production processes and music rights management.Other requirementsBachelor degree or equal practical experienceFluent in English, German beneficialApplyIn your cover letter, please include an explanation of why your experience is specifically relevant to this role at Red Bull. We want to see your personal style 3 what makes you tick and why you think your next opportunity is here with us.

Long Story Short: In this role, you'll guarantee delivery of Red Bull's music repertoire across all in-house and external platforms.
Internal Platforms: You'll take over the project management of our Red Bull Audio Library digital product developments and maintenance. Providing first level technical support for Audio Library users and communicating insights by monitoring repertoire performance, as well as production reports for management, will be main tasks in your role as Music Platforms Manager.
External platforms: Another aspect of this role will be to ensure availability of our owned and controlled music repertoire on B2B and B2C platforms. You'll manage operations of 3rd parties facing Red Bull Media House platforms and push further technical development of our RBMH platforms.
other platforms: Maintaining other music platforms for 3rd party deliveries (e.g. Source Audio, Harvest Media, YouTube), ensuring the availability of exploitable content and managing deliveries to distribution partners and other 3rd parties will be part of your role as well.
Experience: Minimum experience of 5 years in a product or project management position.
Highly skilled in technical project management.
Product development experience.
Experiences with Content Management Systems.
Familiar with delivery standards for DSPs, YouTube Content ID and finger-printing technologies.
Background in web and database programming with a strong affinity to database library management.
Highly developed sense of data accuracy.
Knowledge about audio visual content production workflows / music cue sheet reporting.
General knowledge about the music industry, music production processes and music rights management.
Other requirements: Bachelor degree or equal practical experience
Apply: In your cover letter, please include an explanation of why your experience is specifically relevant to this role at Red Bull. We want to see your personal style 3 what makes you tick and why you think your next opportunity is here with us.

Description: Based at the iconic Capitol Tower in the heart of Hollywood, Capitol Music Group is home to some of the best known labels in modern music; encompassing the jazz icons of Blue Note, Capitol’s generations of pop stars, and the legendary recording of Motown to name but a few.Continue to article...We produce, distribute and promote some of the most critically acclaimed and commercially successful music in the industry. Ability to read an audience and develop communication strategy depending on that audience.Develop and execute content calendars for artists.Build innovative campaigns to further our artists’ data capture, sales and advertising goals, and react to social analytics in order to improve the success of these campaigns.Manage artist web properties, reading and tweaking content strategy for those properties in reaction to analytics.Create tracking links for campaigns, and communicate learnings as appropriate.Experience:3 years experience working in the music or entertainment fields. Established relationships with digital and social partners.Expert, native user of social media platforms.Effective collaboration with other members of the Digital Marketing team (as well as teams throughout the company)Able to read and react to trends in the social and online space.Basic HTML knowledge preferred.Intermediate user of image editing & design software (e.g. Photoshop, InDesign).Quick learner who is able and excited to jump into and master new platforms, apps and services.Able to read basic analytics and develop plans and content in reaction to what the data observed is telling them.Strong presentation skills (both written and spoken), creativity and adaptability.Demonstrated ability to develop content for online use.Experience writing copy and creation of photo/video content.Education:BA or BS required.Universal Music Group is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Title: Work Entry Assistant (UK)
Description: Kobalt Music Publishing is looking for a Work Entry Assistant to be based in the Kobalt office in London, and to work within the Copyright Department as a song processor.Continue to article...The primary roles of a copyright department are registering claims to songs at the relevant collection societies globally and processing the royalties which result from the exploitation of those songs.This enables us to collect and distribute royalties due to the copyright owners. But before all of this can be done, we need to import into our system the songs we receive from our clients.This is the all-important task of our department, and accuracy is absolutely key as all subsequent tasks rely on the accuracy and efficiency with which this task is performed.The successful candidate will have a well-supported imitation and training process.Founded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model.Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized.This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.WHAT DOES A WORK ENTRY ASSISTANT AT KOBALT DO?To ensure that all works delivered to Kobalt Music Publishing are entered with 100% accuracy and within the deadlinesDaily input of a substantial amount of data entryBeing able to comply with complex procedures and rules of work entryTo have an impeccable knowledge of all work entry issues and able to help and advice members of the copyright department regarding work entry mattersTo liaise in a professional way with clients and colleagues regarding issues or queriesTo maintain learning and professional development, including new technical and copyright developmentsWhere required, to be proactive, foresee issues and suggest improvements in the system and procedureWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Previous copyright experience, a good understanding of music publishing, or a music-related degree, is highly desirableGood communication skills are very important: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally, we are looking for someone who is able to adapt communication to audienceCreative Problem Solving: experience in creating and implementing new processesDecisiveness: effective and timely decisions, but perceives the impact and implications of decisionsPerson with resilience and tenacity: someone who effectively deals with pressure but remains optimistic and persistent to enter works accurately and efficientlyTrack record of good organization and prioritisation skillsHigh level of written and spoken EnglishHigh level of numeracyStrong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-set

Title: Label Manager (UK)
Description: We’re currently working with an incredible and growing roster of emerging talent via artist and label relationships across the globe.Continue to article...AWAL is looking for it’s next addition to the label management team, based in our London headquarters and working closely with various account, marketing and label management teams globally. In this role, you will be responsible for a roster of high profile relationships across label and artist accounts, driving operational efficiency, creativity, visibility and value.It’s a very exciting time for the company as a whole and particularly within the label management team where we’re really proud to be looking after so many of the hottest records being released right now.At AWAL we’re doing things differently and are well placed to make the most of the changing face of the industry. Your role managing some of the hottest properties, in a cutting-edge team will be an exciting challenge.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2004, AWAL is the trusted partner of independent artists and labels globally, from new artists at the beginning of their career to global successes.With local offices across the world, AWAL offers services such as distribution, YouTube monetization, playlist promotion, member support from industry experts, and real-time data analytics through the AWAL Portal and App.Our client roster includes both established and emerging artists such as Moby, Placebo, Chip, Gabrielle Aplin, Todd Terje, Tom Misch, R3hab and Vérité, and labels including B3SCI, Chrysalis Records, Marathon Artists, Kid Coconut, Independiente and mau5trap.At Kobalt, we’re currently over 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re expecting to grow.That’s where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!WHAT DOES A LABEL MANAGER AT KOBALT DO?Reporting to the Senior Director of Label Management in the UK, your main focus will be to represent a core roster or AWAL’s key client partnerships to drive visibility, revenue and relationships whilst adding value and expertise to numerous partners and their campaigns.We expect our label managers to work in close collaboration with marketing, A&R and management to identify and develop priority artists and releases in order to upscale towards full label services, project management and other business lines.ResponsibilitiesManage a roster of priority clients spanning independent artists and labels across both digital and physical supply chains as necessarySupport these clients with distribution and release planning advice and best practices working in tandem with marketing, sales and project management as required on higher-profile campaigns.Onboard new clients in tandem with A&R, integrations and other teams as necessary.In tandem with management and A&R, regularly review and audit rosters, offering insight to those partners that should be upstreamed for broader services and to streamline workflow to current priorities.Mentor more junior team members as required and act as a buddy to new staff as the AWAL team expandsProvide reporting and feedback to line managers and executives as required.Represent AWAL at artist showcases & gigs, plus relevant industry conferences and eventsContinually strive to offer ideas for improvement, development and entrepreneurship across your own role and that of the team.WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Passionate about music. With minimum 2 years’ music industry experience within a progressive label or distributor, in a label management or product management capacityExcellent working knowledge of digital music distribution, supply chain and DSP processesExcellent understanding of the needs of artists and labels for building and maximising their release campaignsExcellent interpersonal skills and ability to communicate effectively at all levels, internally and externally.Flexible, adaptable and strategic thinker.Knowledge of international industry landscape is highly desirableSolid understanding/experience of digital music marketing and promotion would be an advantageUnderstanding of physical supply chain and distribution would be desirable.The role will involve daily working with international based contacts, especially North America – some international travel may be required in the course of this role, as is flexibility for out of hours working.IMPORTANT NOTICEApplicants must be eligible to work in the United Kingdom.Verification checks will be conducted during the interview process and a full DBS check on acceptance of offer.Kobalt is an equal opportunity employer encouraging diversity in the workplace.All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, national origin, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or any other status or characteristic protected by law.

BACKGROUND: Founded in 2004, AWAL is the trusted partner of independent artists and labels globally, from new artists at the beginning of their career to global successes.

Title: Copyright Assistant, Portuguese Speaking (UK)
Description: Joining the Publishing Copyright department in our London office, the primary role of the Copyright Assistant will be to assist the team in managing our catalogue in Brazil by ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at the collection societies.Continue to article...This is an entry-level position for an individual with strong data management experience.A successful candidate will need to have excellent communication skills when dealing with general enquiries from internal and external sources and can deal with pressure but remain optimistic and persistent to complete tasks accurately and efficiently.Kobalt is the leading technology-powered music services company, offering end-to-end solutions for over 50,000 artists and songwriters and 800 labels and publishing companies.Through Kobalt technology and services, creators benefit from more money, better reporting, and maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized.With the award-winning Kobalt desktop portal and app, Kobalt clients get real-time insight into their work and activity.Collection of royalties has traditionally been time-consuming and costly.Kobalt has developed centralised, automated and efficient administration services supported by a scalable, proprietary, dedicated system using the latest technology.Because of Kobalt’s innovative approach, Kobalt offers a reduction in royalty collection time of up to 50%, more effective collection as well as substantially improved transparency.We’re currently over 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re expecting to grow.That’s where you come in. Description: Independent Label and Publisher Communion Music is growing its A&R team, and looking for A&R professionals at all levels.Continue to article...As well as having a natural affinity with our creative and working culture, successful candidates will demonstrate the following skills and attributes:The ability to critically analyse recorded music right through from demo submissions to finished recordings from multiple angles: creatively, structurally, technically and in relationship to the marketplace and cultural context.A genuine passion for artist development: the capacity to engage with lyrics in a meaningful way, display empathy for the artistic process and be able to relate to artists socially and creatively.The appetite to monitor specialist radio, blogs and other media sources as well as having and maintaining relationships with music lawyers, managers etc. and to have an appetite for attending live music events on a weekly basis.A first-hand understanding of one or more of the following creative processes: song-writing, producing, engineering, performing/general musicianship.Exceptionally high levels of drive and initiative, first rate communication skills, attention to detail and the proven capacity for original thinking.And, depending on the level of hire:A good current working knowledge of UK producers, producer managers, mixers, and an understanding of the record making process from inception to delivery of masters.A detailed understanding of relevant commercial trends, and in general of the broader music industry and its practices.About Communion:Communion is an Independent Record Label, Publisher and Live Promoter, based in London and established in 2006.We work with artists including Bear's Den, Lucy Rose (Communion Records), Catfish and the Bottlemen, Peace (Communion Publishing), George Ezra, James Bay, Maggie Rogers, Sam Fender (Communion Presents).

Description: Caroline is the label services division of Universal Music – offering individually tailored solutions on a global scale, including, but not limited to marketing, promotion, sales and distribution – assisting artists and labels to retain their independence whilst leveraging the strengths of a major label.Continue to article...Key Responsibilities:You will work in conjunction with the Head of International and local Label Managers & Product Managers, the global digital marketing and the data team to build and develop global marketing, promotional and commercial plans for all relevant Caroline repertoires – direct signings and label deals – and all deal types.Artist/Label Relationship Management:Provide leadership, advice and support to internal and external label partners in respect of their release schedule in the areas of sales, distribution, marketing and promotional plans as appropriate to their deal type.Provide a first-class knowledge of the music industry to support advice given.Oversee the release schedule process and ensure that all aspects of it are properly managed and scheduled in a timely manner.Responsible for stock forecasting, internal sales reporting, chart reports, ensuring chart eligibility of Caroline releases.Project Management:Market assigned artists and labels on a global level.Create and implement release strategies.Attend planning and scheduling meetings.General problem solving for all aspects of releasesForge strong working relationships with Labels, Artists, Artist Management, Marketing colleagues and Territories in the Caroline structure, travel to oversee large promo events when necessary.Ensure timely delivery of assets from external partners, and internal delivery into supply chain, such that marketing timelines are met; pre-orders, instant grats etc go live on time.Monitor international programmes on key projects and provide regular detailed written reports on priority projects as determined by Head of International.To devise and direct a promotional strategy for artists to ensure maximised sales in line with marketing objectives.To work on own initiative to ensure all opportunities are maximised – identify artists labels in the Caroline system that merit marketing and promotional focus.Person Specification:Essential:Proven experience in a similar International, label management or digital role; ideally within Music Industry or Music Agency with a track record of liaising effectively with both Managers, Labels and Artists.Experience of working, influencing and persuading at all levels of an organisation (upwards and downwards).The ability to learn quickly and adapt to the changing market.Flexible and able to prioritise own workload and self-motivated; hard working and enthusiastic.Prepared to travel internationally as required.Excellent verbal and written communication skills, above average presentation skills.Team player with a flexible approach, willing to defer when appropriate.Equal Opportunities:Universal Music is an equal opportunities employer. We embrace diversity and are committed to a working environment where no one will be treated less favourably on grounds of their sex, race, disability, sexual orientation, religion, belief or age.Disclaimer:The company presents this job description as a guide to the major areas and duties for which the jobholder is accountable.However, the business operates in an environment that demands change and the jobholder’s specific responsibilities and activities will vary and develop.Therefore, the job description should be seen as indicative and not as a permanent, definitive and exhaustive statement.

Key Responsibilities:: You will work in conjunction with the Head of International and local Label Managers & Product Managers, the global digital marketing and the data team to build and develop global marketing, promotional and commercial plans for all relevant Caroline repertoires – direct signings and label deals – and all deal types.

To work on own initiative to ensure all opportunities are maximised – identify artists labels in the Caroline system that merit marketing and promotional focus.

Experience of working, influencing and persuading at all levels of an organisation (upwards and downwards).
The ability to learn quickly and adapt to the changing market.
Flexible and able to prioritise own workload and self-motivated; hard working and enthusiastic.
Prepared to travel internationally as required.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, above average presentation skills.
Team player with a flexible approach, willing to defer when appropriate.

Title: Administrative Assistant, Sync (UK)
Description: Kobalt Music is looking for an enthusiastic, bright and organised administrative assistant with a passion for music to support the SVP, Synch & Brand Partnerships and the wider UK based sync team.Continue to article...As a natural extension of the supported executive and the Sync team in their day-to-day activity you will need to be an excellent organiser and communicator, able to build a trustworthy relationship among internal teams and external contacts.The successful candidate will have previous support experience in a fast-paced, ever-evolving environment as well as a track record of managing ever-changing schedules, conflicting priorities and planning international travel across multiple time zones.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model.Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized.This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We’re currently 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re still growing.WHAT DOES AN ADMINISTRATiVE ASSISTANT, SYNC AT KOBALT DO?Provide administrative support, including:Heavy scheduling and calendar managementBooking regular department meetings, internal and external.Maintaining, organising and submitting expensesManaging call volumePrioritising to do lists and call logsCoordinating travelManaging department resourcesAssisting with organising departmental eventsAssisting the Sync team as neededAttending meetings and circulating meeting notes, as neededLiaise with other departments at Kobalt for various projects and meetingsAssist in creating and distributing various synch reports for Kobalt clientsSend weekly synch activity reports to the global companyMaintain a system for reviewing pending deals, payments and other synch licensing activityMarket the Kobalt catalog, including sending weekly newsletters to the music supervisor communityAssist with presentations, reports and other tasks as assignedProvide back-up administrative support to the department as neededMaintain a strict level of confidentialityProvide additional administrative and creative support as needed, such as database logging, sourcing audio, tagging music, servicing TV production companies with rights information and instrumentals, TV blanket licensing enquiries.Attend gigs and events as and when required. We have many live shows every month and you will be required to be present at some of these. This is not a 9 to 5 roleWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? Bachelor’s Degree preferredMinimum of 2 years’ administrative experience, preferably supporting and/or interacting with senior level executives;Music industry experience with knowledge of the recorded and music publishing businesses preferredPA experience desirableA passion for musicA team playerProficiency in Gmail and Gsuite; Proficiency with Google Drive, Photoshop, Mailchimp and iMovie also preferredAbility to work independently, handle multiple projects, adapt to changing priorities, and deal with stress in a productive mannerGreat initiative with the ability to be collaborative, demonstrate strong judgment, professionalism and poiseConfident self-starter, able to take own initiative and act in a timely manner.Strong verbal, written and interpersonal communication skillsStrong organizational skills and ability to be resourceful are imperativeHighly productive and proactiveMust maintain the highest level of confidentialityComfortable working in a fast paced, high volume & evolving environmentAbility to work overtime when necessarySPECIFICSBased at River Building, Cannon Street, London EC4R 3TEHours are 37.5 hours per week.Generally, these are taken between 9.30-6 with 1 hour for lunch but amendments to these hours can be agreed on a case by case basis with line manager approval

The Role: WMA is looking for an experienced Marketing Strategy Manager to join the global team, based in either our Nashville or London office.

The individual is expected to be a creative Marketing expert, with proven experience in delivering first class marketing campaigns within music and/or the wider entertainment space and be fully immersed in the ever-changing digital landscape.
The position will support the global Marketing Strategy team, executing best in class campaigns for WMA’s clients, which will include artists, labels, brands and other entertainment businesses.
KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: Manage a variety of Marketing Strategy projects and clients, whilst working with key stakeholders to deliver campaign support and ideation in line with the overall marketing objectives.
Create and execute exciting and innovative marketing strategies for clients whilst ensuring all stakeholders are kept informed and objectives are kept front of mind.
Maintain up-to-date marketing plans, timelines and project documents for each project, regularly communicating updates and news across key stakeholders (both internally and externally).
Assist with pitches for playlist consideration (either directly using appropriate tools or providing the relevant information to the distributor/label team as needed).
Able to write creative briefs for assets (video tools, outdoor and promotional advertising, marketing one-sheets, or social creative assets).
Copywriting in correct tone of voice for official sites, e-newsletters and tools including marketing one-sheets.
Manage the entire campaign life cycle from strategic planning to tactical activities of assigned roster.
Represent clients across all communications with correct tone, content and style.
Support artist development with a fresh approach and creative strategies for all projects, that can be implemented for little or no budget.
Propose creative ways to engage and develop online communities.
Have an eye on trends and developments and be immersed in the music marketing world.
Have a strong handle on all major social platforms including and not limited to Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, Snapchat, YouTube, Pinterest and Spotify.
Have an understanding of email marketing, digital merch sales and ticketing/touring.
Confidently work with all relevant team players such as artist, management, label, live, pluggers etc to execute plans effectively.
Build and maintain relationships with local partners (DSPs, media outlets, blogs and influencers) to ensure they are aware of relevant WMA clients.
Regularly attend local events, gigs and networking opportunities, to integrate into the community and help bring in new business.
Drive innovation to position artists, brands and WMA as industry leaders.
Work closely with the other departments within WMA to offer a bespoke streamlined service, amplify activity and refer new business.
SKILLSET: Demonstrable knowledge of and passion for traditional, digital and social marketing tactics and trends within music and entertainment.
Ability to multi-task in a high energy environment.
Ability to manage time effectively and adhere to deadlines.
Ability to work autonomously whilst keeping open and clear communication with the global Marketing team.
Creative thinker and a self-starter. Highly motivated.
Proficient with budgets and mindful of efficient money management.
Knowledge and understanding of Paid Media strategy and ad offerings.
Ability to work with a broad range and level of people; to share and convey updates, ideas and vision with clarity and tailored to the audience.
Excellent relationship building skills – get to know clients including artists and management in a friendly but professional way, maintaining credibility.
Ability to maintain professionalism and a positive attitude at all times.
Excellent communication skills – verbal & written.
Excellent people skills , confident & proactive.
Team player – cross departmental teamwork required.
Can take on and solve problems in an effective, positive, calm manner.
Highly organized with exceptional attention to detail.
Highly creative with the ability to recognise and research new opportunities.
EDUCATION \u0026 EXPERIENCE: Proven experience in marketing within the music industry, ideally within a major or independent label.
Experience working within a high energy environment, working with team members of different levels.
Proven experience of delivering creative and strategically driven marketing campaigns.
Experience dealing with artists and managers in a professional environment.
Experience reviewing and analysing data to provide insight and deliver strategies
Ideally graduate level with a degree in marketing, PR, music or related field.
WHO WILL YOU BE WORKING WITH?: Directly with clients to discuss briefs, campaigns, timings and budgets • The Global Social and PR Teams
Artists / Bands (major to unsigned) and Brands
Contact: jobs@wma.agency

Description: Concord Music seeks an experienced Marketing Project Manager to support the music publishing and brand management business. With offices in Los Angeles, New York, Nashville, London, Berlin, and Cleveland, Concord Music has a diverse and vibrant roster of active recording artists, composers, and songwriters, and a vast and historic catalog of works by some of the most treasured names in music history.Concord Music’s publishing business was transformed in 2017 when it acquired Imagem Music Group and integrated it with Concord’s existing publishing operation, The Bicycle Music Company. In 2018, Concord Music added the renowned musical theatre library, Tams-Witmark to its publishing business. Boosey & Hawkes, Rodgers & Hammerstein, and Tams-Witmark will remain as individual business units under the Concord Music umbrella.

Title: Business Affairs Manager (UK)
Description: JOB SUMMARYContinue to article...An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Business Affairs Manager to join Kobalt’s Business Affairs team in its master rights division in London servicing the needs and development of Kobalt’s fast-growing recordings business, AWAL.The role will involve hands on negotiating and drafting of our digital distribution and wider label services agreements, as well as supporting and assisting the team with more complicated deals and organisational and operational elements critical to the smooth running and rapid evolution of AWAL.It will provide an excellent opportunity for you to develop both professionally and personally in an unusual and highly progressive music company.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is building the music company of the future.Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model.Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to retain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.Kobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the centre of everything we do.We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL. Whether an artist or independent label is looking for the best digital distribution or a truly global campaign, AWAL has become the partner of choice for innovative artists and entrepreneurial labels at all stages of their career or expansion.This has crafted a high-quality path for artists and music entrepreneurs to remain outside of the traditional label system while retaining ownership of their masters or master rights.The leading artists who have already chosen AWAL include Lauv, Nick Cave, Deadmau5, Bruno Major, Little Simz, Verite, Tom Misch, You Me At Six and the Wombats.We’re currently 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re still growingWHAT DOES A BUSINESS AFFAIRS MANAGER AT AWAL/KOBALT DO?Negotiating, drafting and handling the signature and the implementation (along with internal teams) of various client (both artist and B2B) agreements and side letters originating in the world ex. US for AWALNegotiating, drafting and running the signature and implementation (along with internal teams) of commercial agreements and licences connected to AWAL’s business including physical distribution licences, exclusive territorial licences, DSP licenses, sponsorship and branding agreements, compilation licences, promotional uses, tech development agreements and other third party licencesAdvising and supporting our AWAL marketing, operations, creative/A&R and Management teams on a variety of matters including without limitation on deal terms, IP matters, GDPR/data use-related matters, dispute resolution, dealflow processes, best legal and business practice and general BA queriesMaintaining good relationships with external lawyers – especially at Associate levelWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?2 years PQE with relevant experience from a top music/entertainment private practice law firm and/or in-house role within a Major or indie record label.Individuals with in-house music publishing understanding and passion for the recorded music part of the industry will also be eligible for considerationStrong negotiation and drafting experienceExcellent communication skills (both verbal and written) and the ability to disseminate complicated legal and contractual principles to non-lawyer staff in a coherent and precise mannerA good existing music industry network of contacts and an interest in going to gigs with the AWAL team to solidify professional relationships (both internal and external) would be a bonusExcellent analytical skills and attention to detailExcellent organisational skills and ability to prioritise workload in a role that at times can be pressured or working to tight deadlinesSPECIFICSBased at River Building, Cannon Street, London EC4R 3TEHours are 37.5 hours per week. Generally, these are taken between 9.30-6 with 1 hour for lunch but amendments to these hours can be agreed on a case by case basis with line manager approval

Title: VP, East Coast (US)
Description: Audio Network is a global music company that licenses and supplies authentic and beautifully crafted music to a diverse community of creators who make television, film and advertising for all screens.Continue to article...Collaborating with over 1,000 composers, artists and producers, Audio Network built and owns a world-class catalogue of more than 150,000 tracks of original music spanning every conceivable style, mood and instrumentation.Audio Network has proven itself a leader in the creation of music for video with considerable opportunities for growth in terms of new customer segments, deeper engagement and international reach.At its core, Audio Networks is built onAuthentic Music – Audio Network only records with the best and most authentic artists, musicians and composers at top recording facilities around the world.Effortless Global Licensing – All music is pre-cleared for multiple platforms worldwide in perpetuity and is liberated for use in a wide array of content including; television, film, advertising and digital videos.World-Class Service – Audio Network is obsessed with delivering personalized service for all core segments (Television, Advertising, Enterprise, Digital Creators) with best-in-class music search & discovery and platform-agnostic delivery to wherever customers work.Crafted with creatives in mind, Audio Network’s music is used around the world by some of the media industry’s biggest names and brands including the BBC, ITV, NBC/Universal, Viacom, Coke, Nike, Havas and Vice; to name a few.The RoleBased in Manhattan and reporting to the CEO (London), the VP, East Coast is a key strategic appointment that will play an integral role in leading the execution of the company’s commercial strategy in licensing Audio Network’s world-class music to television, advertising, enterprise and digital media customers.The role will manage the East Coast sales and creative team to maximize revenue opportunities through synchronization licensing and generating performance royalties by driving usage with a focus on key strategic partners in advertising & branded content.The VP, East Coast will help drive awareness of the Audio Network brand in the US and be a key contributor to the company’s overall strategy across its core customer segments (advertising & branded content).The role will require leadership and mentorship of the East Coast Sales, creative and publishing team members. Reporting to the CEO, this role will also comprise part of the ANW’s executive team which includes senior executives from Music, Marketing, Product and Finance.This is a rare, career-defining and highly rewarding opportunity for the right executive to make their mark on a truly disruptive company which, through delivering businesses (and ultimately consumers) with access to cutting edge music on a global scale and through multi-channels, is fundamentally transforming the global music licencing landscape.Key responsibilities of the role include – but are not limited toOverall management of the East Coast team and operations including; sales forecasting, budgeting, expense tracking/approvals and sales/marketing initiatives.Commercial leadership in delivering Group strategy and driving US revenues through synchronization licensing and performance royalties in ANW’s core segments (TV, Advertising, Enterprise and Digital Media) with a key focus on advertising & branded content.Leading the sales and creative teams to execute commercial strategies, pipeline management and performance plans ensuring they are supported by the right structure, talent and process.Identifying and developing new partnership opportunities while maintaining current and existing client relationships.Partner-level, customer relationship management, in supporting key US-based global media companies, The Role: Based in Manhattan and reporting to the CEO (London), the VP, East Coast is a key strategic appointment that will play an integral role in leading the execution of the company’s commercial strategy in licensing Audio Network’s world-class music to television, advertising, enterprise and digital media customers.

The VP, East Coast will help drive awareness of the Audio Network brand in the US and be a key contributor to the company’s overall strategy across its core customer segments (advertising & branded content).

The role will require leadership and mentorship of the East Coast Sales, creative and publishing team members. Reporting to the CEO, this role will also comprise part of the ANW’s executive team which includes senior executives from Music, Marketing, Product and Finance.

This is a rare, career-defining and highly rewarding opportunity for the right executive to make their mark on a truly disruptive company which, through delivering businesses (and ultimately consumers) with access to cutting edge music on a global scale and through multi-channels, is fundamentally transforming the global music licencing landscape.

Commercial leadership in delivering Group strategy and driving US revenues through synchronization licensing and performance royalties in ANW’s core segments (TV, Advertising, Enterprise and Digital Media) with a key focus on advertising & branded content.

Title: Director, Corporate Development (US)
Description: SoundExchange develops business solutions to benefit the entire music industry. Title: Music and Events Specialist (UK)
Description: The Music and Events Specialist (FTC, 12 months) is a specialist role and key player in War Child’s wellknown Music and Events team.Continue to article...The Music and Events team creates powerful experiences that bring people together to inspire and entertain and is driving War Child’s place as one of the leading charities within the UK music industry.Building on War Child’s 25-year history of working with the best from UK music, the Music and Events Specialist will use their network and live events experience to deliver a portfolio of creative, innovative and income generating events for War Child.Based in London, the post-holder will have exceptional relationship management skills, strengthening and growing our relationships within the music industry and showcasing the power of music to do good.The ideal candidate will be able to start by early December in order to get up to speed ahead of our flagship BRITS Week shows in February.About usWar Child, the charity for children affected by war. This is a vision that can only be realised through the collective actions of children themselves, communities and their leaders, organisations like War Child, governments and key decision makers.Your roleUsing their networks and specialist expertise of the music industry, the Music and Events Specialist will work with the Head of Music and Events to develop a portfolio of live events and other fundraising products that leverage our relationship with the music industry to raise valuable funds for War Child.The Music and Events Specialist is responsible for being a key liaison with the music industry, stewarding our relationships with some of our highest profile supporters.They will use their creativity and commercial know-how to approach new partners and develop and deliver some of our highest profile live events ultimately cultivating War Child’s unique support to raise funds via the music industry.Your responsibilitiesAccountable for delivering live events safely in line with the concept, plan and budget; using your learning and expertise to contribute to an overall balanced portfolio of live music events for the department.Responsible for identifying and putting forward appropriate and viable opportunities and new partnerships for the music industry to further support our work and raise valuable funds.Act as an ambassador for War Child within the UK music industry, building and maintaining strong relationships with, artists, labels, sponsors, promotors, media partners, venues and agents, amongst others. Manage multiple relationships across all levels of seniority whilst being able to negotiate their support for War Child. Steward key relationships with high profile supporters that are long-lasting and raise profile and funds for War Child.Work with other Fundraising and Marketing teams and global War Child agencies to identify and deliver ways in which the music industry can enhance their work.Build and maintain strong internal relationships with departments such as Press and PR, Digital and Advocacy so that they maximise the value of our music industry partners to War Child.Recruit, manage and develop the Music and Events Officer to provide assistance in delivering the team plan, with a particular focus on ensuring that the post-holder builds their own profile and network within the UK music industry.Responsible for ensuring our events and products are delivered safely and their brand identity is protected and enhancedCreate, develop and manage media, brand and sponsorship partnerships for our live events, ensuring that War Child’s intellectual property, creative assets and rights are reasonably used in line with our Brand Guidelines and Communication PolicyEnsure that relevant data about our supporters, events and products is captured to feed a culture of learning and continuous improvement within the teamThese duties provide a framework for the role and should not be regarded as a definitive list.
