About us: War Child, the charity for children affected by war. We are the only specialist charity for children in conflict, delivering high-impact programmes that are rebuilding lives across Afghanistan, Iraq, Jordan, Uganda, the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), Central African Republic and Yemen.
Your role: Using their networks and specialist expertise of the music industry, the Music and Events Specialist will work with the Head of Music and Events to develop a portfolio of live events and other fundraising products that leverage our relationship with the music industry to raise valuable funds for War Child.
Your responsibilities: Accountable for delivering live events safely in line with the concept, plan and budget; using your learning and expertise to contribute to an overall balanced portfolio of live music events for the department.
Responsible for identifying and putting forward appropriate and viable opportunities and new partnerships for the music industry to further support our work and raise valuable funds.

Act as an ambassador for War Child within the UK music industry, building and maintaining strong relationships with, artists, labels, sponsors, promotors, media partners, venues and agents, amongst others. Manage multiple relationships across all levels of seniority whilst being able to negotiate their support for War Child. Steward key relationships with high profile supporters that are long-lasting and raise profile and funds for War Child.
Work with other Fundraising and Marketing teams and global War Child agencies to identify and deliver ways in which the music industry can enhance their work.
Build and maintain strong internal relationships with departments such as Press and PR, Digital and Advocacy so that they maximise the value of our music industry partners to War Child.

Recruit, manage and develop the Music and Events Officer to provide assistance in delivering the team plan, with a particular focus on ensuring that the post-holder builds their own profile and network within the UK music industry.
Responsible for ensuring our events and products are delivered safely and their brand identity is protected and enhanced

Create, develop and manage media, brand and sponsorship partnerships for our live events, ensuring that War Child’s intellectual property, creative assets and rights are reasonably used in line with our Brand Guidelines and Communication Policy
Ensure that relevant data about our supporters, events and products is captured to feed a culture of learning and continuous improvement within the team
These duties provide a framework for the role and should not be regarded as a definitive list. Other reasonable duties may be required consistent with the grade of the post.
You are: Networked within the UK music industry with the ability to build and maintain strong relationships with artists, labels, sponsors, promotors, media partners, venues and agents, amongst others
Expert in the workings of the music industry including key figures in the industry, promotional and touring cycles, artist marketing campaigns and market trends

Experienced in producing and delivering sector-leading live music events that are proven to deliver against their concept, budget and plan and manage risk appropriately in order maintain War Child’s reputation
A highly competent negotiator with the ability to negotiate successful agreements with artists, labels, sponsors, promotors, media partners, venues and agents, amongst others

Able to work effectively as a team leader within a fast-paced event delivery environment, with the ability to quickly problem solve, make logistical decisions to maintain the integrity of an event and stay calm under pressure
Able to meticulously plan and budget for events as well as manage your workload and your team’s workload to meet deadlines, manage priorities and hit targets
Able to recruit, develop and manage line reports, empowering them to deliver against the annual plan and budget
A confident presenter with the ability to pitch War Child passionately, concisely and professionally to potential supporters across all levels within the UK music industry
Always demonstrating a passion for music, proactively meeting contacts and willing to attend events in the evenings and weekends
Child safeguarding: Our work with children to keep them safe is the most important thing we do.
Contact information: To explore the post further or for any queries you may have, please contact: Samantha Robertson, Acting Head of Music and Events, samanthar@warchild.org.uk
How to apply: Please download the application pack from https://www.warchild.org.uk/who-we-are/jobs
All applications must be sent to recruitment@warchild.org.uk by 11:59pm on 31/10/2018
Due to limited resources, we will contact only the shortlisted candidates
Interviews will be held the week commencing 05/11/18
Pre-employment checks: A satisfactory police record check to include a Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) check and/or an International Criminal Record Check
Proof of eligibility to work in the UK (roles based in London only)

Title: Financial Controller (UK)
Description: ICE is an international world class service centre for the global management and administration of music rights.Continue to article...We offer the music industry professional, reliable, accurate and efficient services that enable music societies, music publishers and the composers they represent to manage their copyright information and to receive royalties they are due for online exploitation of their works.The markets we operate in are challenging, in terms of both their complexity and speed of change.ICE therefore requires its employees to adopt a flexible and positive approach to the developing requirements of their role and environment.We have a new and exciting opportunity for a Financial Controller looking for a career to join this dynamic, fast-moving, venture based in Kings Cross, London.The Financial Controller will be responsible for a wide range of key duties, including: consolidating the UK monthly management information packs, multiple currency cashflow forecasts, reviewing and analysing costs and various non-financial information, and improving the reporting tools and processes.The roleIn the role of Financial Controller you will support the Head of Finance and team with the delivery of Budget, Actuals and forecast across ICE, the development of planning and reporting systems that integrate efficiently with other areas of Finance and the wider business, and the delivery of long term financial plans.The Financial Controller be responsible for preparation of monthly reporting packs for key stakeholders, including monthly multi-currency cashflow forecasts. They will also assist in preparation for the quarterly forecasts and the annual budget across the ICE Hub for Board level approval.The Financial Controller will lead the internal and external year end audits which may include coordinating any external audit assistance with third party suppliers.They will also provide support and input into the design, testing and implementation process of system improvements and support change initiatives throughout the business.They will be play an active role in risk identification and management, providing decision support on process improvements substantiated with relevant analysis and evidence.RequirementsThe ideal candidate must be fully qualified (CIMA, ACA, ACCA) or obtain an equivalent accountancy qualification.They will have strong knowledge of the UK statutory GAAP and desirably IFRS/US GAAP knowledge.A minimum of 3 years’ experience engaging with key stakeholders and experience in the use of accounting systems such as, Cognos BI, SAP and any financial consolidation tools would also be highly advantageous.ApplicationsIf you are a self-motivated, analytical individual with great organisational and communication skills with strong attention to detail, we’d love to hear from you. A more detailed job description is available on request.If this sounds like you and you have a passion for making a difference, please apply by sending your CV and covering letter to recruitment@iceservices.com by Friday 26 October 2018.We recognise, welcome and value diversity and are committed to creating a workplace where all employees are given the opportunity to reach their full potential, feel valued and work together effectively to achieve our goals

The role: In the role of Financial Controller you will support the Head of Finance and team with the delivery of Budget, Actuals and forecast across ICE, the development of planning and reporting systems that integrate efficiently with other areas of Finance and the wider business, and the delivery of long term financial plans.

The Financial Controller be responsible for preparation of monthly reporting packs for key stakeholders, including monthly multi-currency cashflow forecasts. They will also assist in preparation for the quarterly forecasts and the annual budget across the ICE Hub for Board level approval.
The Financial Controller will lead the internal and external year end audits which may include coordinating any external audit assistance with third party suppliers.
They will also provide support and input into the design, testing and implementation process of system improvements and support change initiatives throughout the business.
They will be play an active role in risk identification and management, providing decision support on process improvements substantiated with relevant analysis and evidence.
Requirements: The ideal candidate must be fully qualified (CIMA, ACA, ACCA) or obtain an equivalent accountancy qualification.
They will have strong knowledge of the UK statutory GAAP and desirably IFRS/US GAAP knowledge.

A minimum of 3 years’ experience engaging with key stakeholders and experience in the use of accounting systems such as, Cognos BI, SAP and any financial consolidation tools would also be highly advantageous.
Applications: If you are a self-motivated, analytical individual with great organisational and communication skills with strong attention to detail, we’d love to hear from you. A more detailed job description is available on request.

Title: Head of Artist & Label Marketing (UK)
Description: At Spotify, we’re focused on providing the right music for every moment. We’ve connected millions of people to their favorite songs and created a service that people use to discover and share music they love. We’re currently in 61 markets and growing fast!Continue to article...As the Head of Artist & Label Marketing (ALM) team the UK you’ll ensure that every artist, manager and label within the genre sees Spotify as the #1 partner for artist development and monetization by building and deploying impactful strategies and by upholding best in class industry relationships.We are seeking an experienced artist marketing strategist with excellent people skills and strong label and artist management contacts who are used to working in a dynamic environment to lead our ALM efforts in the UK.This position is based in London reporting to the Managing Director, UK. You will work with the ALM management team to develop the local content strategy as well as execute against the Global content strategy and support key aspects of company-wide objectives. Above all, your work will impact the way the world experiences music.What you’ll doBuild and maintain relations with key partners at senior levels across the community across the countryYou will work on and develop new content strategies for the regionIdentify strategic artist projects to partner withConceive and execute extraordinary artist marketing campaignsYou will collaborate cross-functionally with other internal teams to fulfill company strategic goals and advocate for your market’s needs at senior level internallyYou will have a team to lead, develop and inspireExplain and champion the Spotify business model with partners across the artist and label community.Who you areYou have a consistent record in marketing, strategy and operations, and a strong music and technology background. You have significant experience of successfully developing a teamYou have an academic degree and significant management experience with an established digital music service, record label or artist management companyYou are a strong communicator with excellent negotiating and people skillsYou possess strong analytical skills and ability to analyze and interpret data, using it to set performance targetsYou are well organized, with the ability to plan and deliver on tight deadlines and pay exceptional attention to detailYou will bring an extensive network of music industry contacts—rights holders, managers, agents, promoters, and artistsWe are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. We strongly believe that diversity of experience, perspectives, and background will lead to a better environment for our employees and a better product for our users and our creators. This is something we value deeply and we encourage everyone to come to be a part of changing the way the world listens to music.

What you\u2019ll do: Build and maintain relations with key partners at senior levels across the community across the country
You will work on and develop new content strategies for the region
Identify strategic artist projects to partner with
Conceive and execute extraordinary artist marketing campaigns
You will collaborate cross-functionally with other internal teams to fulfill company strategic goals and advocate for your market’s needs at senior level internally
You will have a team to lead, develop and inspire
Explain and champion the Spotify business model with partners across the artist and label community.
Who you are: You have a consistent record in marketing, strategy and operations, and a strong music and technology background. You have significant experience of successfully developing a team
You have an academic degree and significant management experience with an established digital music service, record label or artist management company
You are a strong communicator with excellent negotiating and people skills
You possess strong analytical skills and ability to analyze and interpret data, using it to set performance targets
You are well organized, with the ability to plan and deliver on tight deadlines and pay exceptional attention to detail
You will bring an extensive network of music industry contacts—rights holders, managers, agents, promoters, and artists

Title: Manager, Content Operations (US)
Description: Symphonic Distribution is a 100% independent music distribution and marketing company based in downtown Tampa.Continue to article...We focus on distributing electronic, hip-hop, and urban music in general. We pride ourselves in finding the newest, most talented up-and-coming bands, producers, rappers, managers, and labels with promise of developing long careers.Symphonic Distribution is looking for an experienced, reliable, energetic and tech-savvy individual to be its Manager, Content Operations based in New York.A Manager, Content Operations collaborates with the Client Team, the Technology Team, and our DSP (Digital Service Provider) partners including Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon, Pandora and others to ensure the successful flow of content from our clients to our DSP network.The day-to-day responsibility of this role is managing content deliveries to Symphonic’s DSP network, troubleshooting any failures, and maintaining our relationships with DSP partners. The larger objectives include growing the DSP network by onboarding new DSP partners, improving our delivery success metrics by analyzing errors, and keeping Symphonic informed of changes in the DSP landscape.We’re looking for a highly organized, positive, and energetic individual to join our team. We will depend on you to ensure that our flow of deliveries from Symphonic to the DSP’s is done so effectively and correctly as well as ensure that our departments have all the up to date information at the DSP level.Essential Duties & ResponsibilitiesMonitor delivery pipelines to DSP partners and troubleshoot delivery errors.Document the ingestion process of each DSP partner.Collect, organize, and keep up-to-date all DSP documentation, including XSD and XML specs.Execute scripts or manual workflows when necessary to repair errors.Create, modify or run SQL queries to gather metrics or investigate errors.Track the quality of deliveries to key DSPs and report to management.Liaise with DSP partners to gather feedback on potential delivery improvements.Respond to client inquiries related DSP delivery issues as needed.Improve delivery procedures and suggest delivery system improvements wherever possible.Lead onboarding of new DSP partners, including running tests and reporting status.Act as the internal expert on our DSP network, knowing all pain points, opportunities, and major events.Meet regularly with the Product team to inform overall product strategy.RequirementsMust have at least 5 years of experience performing and managing or directing content related operations.Must have experience managing direct reports.Must have experience with FTP, HTML and/or XML language.High School Diploma required.

Essential Duties \u0026 Responsibilities: Monitor delivery pipelines to DSP partners and troubleshoot delivery errors.
Document the ingestion process of each DSP partner.
Collect, organize, and keep up-to-date all DSP documentation, including XSD and XML specs.
Execute scripts or manual workflows when necessary to repair errors.
Create, modify or run SQL queries to gather metrics or investigate errors.
Track the quality of deliveries to key DSPs and report to management.
Liaise with DSP partners to gather feedback on potential delivery improvements.
Respond to client inquiries related DSP delivery issues as needed.
Improve delivery procedures and suggest delivery system improvements wherever possible.
Lead onboarding of new DSP partners, including running tests and reporting status.
Act as the internal expert on our DSP network, knowing all pain points, opportunities, and major events.
Meet regularly with the Product team to inform overall product strategy.
Requirements: Must have at least 5 years of experience performing and managing or directing content related operations.
Must have experience managing direct reports.
Must have experience with FTP, HTML and/or XML language.
High School Diploma required.

Title: International Project Manager (UK)
Description: We currently have an exciting opportunity for an International Project Manager to join our growing team, based at Tileyard / Kings Cross in London.Continue to article...Marathon Artists is home to some of the most exciting new music including Courtney Barnett, Pond, Max Jury, Jamie Isaac and Baaba Maal.We are focused on discovering, developing and breaking amazing artists on a global scale. Our mission is to create careers whilst never removing art from its culture. Our passion is to partner with artists in realising their vision, making music heard by as many as possible, for as long as can be. We’re in it for the long run.Marathon Artists is also home to imprint labels House Anxiety, MOVES Recordings and Mahogany Records.House Anxiety launched in 2008, with releases from King Krule to Forth Wanderers, Childhood to The Big Pink.MOVES launched in 2017 with the release of the first official UK Afrobeats compilation, MOVES: The Sound of UK Afrobeats. The label has released music from Belly Squad, Afro B, Skengdo x AM, Finesse Foreva, IQ, Harlem Spartans, Rai-Elle with many more exciting projects in the pipeline.Mahogany is one of the best music discovery platforms in the world delivering cinematic live sessions, unique live concerts and a carefully curated blog and since their launch in 2017 now releasing music on their label Mahogany Records (Kawala, Scott Quinn and Model Man).As well as the record label, publishing and management arms, Marathon Artists is powered by a special ability to navigate music’s ever-evolving relationship with technology, showcased by its own start-up incubator, Marathon Artists Labs.Our ideal candidate will have previous International Project Management experience within a fast-paced label environment, with proven experience of successfully rolling out impactful international marketing campaigns for developing acts.DutiesAnalyse data and contextualize artists’ works to identify opportunities.Devising and planning international marketing strategies and campaigns with UK and global teams.Work closely / liaise with Marathon Artists’ global teams and distributors and artists, artists’ managers, agents.Overseeing and executing: international marketing plans, reporting back, budget control, marketing spend, retail planning, overseeing stock orders and accounting.If you have the relevant experience, a passion for developing artists with integrity, and want to be part of our team at Marathon, please send your CV to hello@biypeople.co.uk with the subject heading “International Project Manager – Marathon” before Friday, October 12 2018.

Duties: Analyse data and contextualize artists’ works to identify opportunities.
Devising and planning international marketing strategies and campaigns with UK and global teams.
Work closely / liaise with Marathon Artists’ global teams and distributors and artists, artists’ managers, agents.
Overseeing and executing: international marketing plans, reporting back, budget control, marketing spend, retail planning, overseeing stock orders and accounting.

Title: Legal & Membership Affairs Manager (UK)
Description: Reporting to the Head of Legal & Global Digital Licensing in London, the Legal & Membership Affairs Manager will be an essential part of a small legal team, and be involved in negotiation and drafting of inter-society and digital licensing agreements.Continue to article...As a primary responsibility and working closely with the CEO of AMRA, you will also be instrumental in all legal and administrative matters connected with AMRA taking on and managing new writer members.AMRA is the first of its kind — a global digital music collection society, built on technology and trust. AMRA is designed to maximize value for songwriters and publishers in today’s digital age, while providing the highest level of transparency and efficiency.Through cutting edge technology and a direct global digital collections model, AMRA is able to optimize digital revenue collections across all platforms, with full transparency to its clients.If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!WHAT DOES A LEGAL & MEMBERSHIP AFFAIRS MANAGER AT AMRA DO?Supporting the writer member acquisition work of the CEO of AMRA (based in New York)Compiling and sending offer letters and term sheetsLiaising with potential AMRA writer members (and their professional advisers) to give them information on AMRA, its services and answering any questions they may have associated with changing collection society and/or signing up with AMRANegotiating and drafting membership agreements (and any other associated legal documents, such as data waivers and NDAs) with potential AMRA writer membersCarrying out all necessary administration relating to the successful on-boarding of new AMRA writer members, and managing paymentsLiaising with appropriate internal members of the AMRA team to ensure that new AMRA writer members are correctly claimed on the IPI database and that their songs are fully and accurately registeredKeeping up to date lists of writer members and their deal terms, and providing updates on “in progress” writer negotiations to the management teamProviding ongoing query management/being the first point of contact for all active AMRA writer members (including legacy members)Communicating regularly with writer members to ensure their needs are being met and to identify any issuesOrganising and minuting the Annual General Membership Meeting/Board Meetings and managing the vote for Writer/Publisher members to have seats on the AMRA BoardNegotiating/drafting inter-society agreements and managing other inter-society correspondence (including notifications of AMRA’s digital licences and AMRA repertoire changes) as requiredAssisting the Head of Legal with digital licensing – this will include working with the Legal & Global Digital Licensing Manager to manage AMRA’s licences with the applicable DSPs, drafting of deal summaries and maintaining the team’s global DSP rate cardAssisting with other day-to-day legal matters that arise within the organisation both internally and externallyOtherwise assisting the Head of Legal and Global Digital Licensing, Legal & Global Digital Licensing Manager, COO and CEO as requiredWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Experienced paralegals or LPC graduates as well as newly qualified lawyersShould be confident and have a friendly, outgoing personality so that they can interact positively with AMRA’s writer members but they should also be highly organised and detail orientated in order to accurately carry out the legal work involvedSome experience dealing with music industry legal agreements, preferably in a songwriter, music publishing or collection society setting would be idealA passion for music (and the digital music market) is essentialSPECIFICSBased near Cannon Street / Monument / Bank, London EC4R 3TEWorking hours are 9.30am-6pmTo apply, please send your CV and cover letter to jobs@amra.com

WHAT DOES A LEGAL \u0026 MEMBERSHIP AFFA: Supporting the writer member acquisition work of the CEO of AMRA (based in New York)
Compiling and sending offer letters and term sheets

Liaising with potential AMRA writer members (and their professional advisers) to give them information on AMRA, its services and answering any questions they may have associated with changing collection society and/or signing up with AMRA
Negotiating and drafting membership agreements (and any other associated legal documents, such as data waivers and NDAs) with potential AMRA writer members
Carrying out all necessary administration relating to the successful on-boarding of new AMRA writer members, and managing payments
Liaising with appropriate internal members of the AMRA team to ensure that new AMRA writer members are correctly claimed on the IPI database and that their songs are fully and accurately registered
Keeping up to date lists of writer members and their deal terms, and providing updates on “in progress” writer negotiations to the management team
Providing ongoing query management/being the first point of contact for all active AMRA writer members (including legacy members)
Communicating regularly with writer members to ensure their needs are being met and to identify any issues
Organising and minuting the Annual General Membership Meeting/Board Meetings and managing the vote for Writer/Publisher members to have seats on the AMRA Board
Negotiating/drafting inter-society agreements and managing other inter-society correspondence (including notifications of AMRA’s digital licences and AMRA repertoire changes) as required

Assisting the Head of Legal with digital licensing , this will include working with the Legal & Global Digital Licensing Manager to manage AMRA’s licences with the applicable DSPs, drafting of deal summaries and maintaining the team’s global DSP rate card
Assisting with other day-to-day legal matters that arise within the organisation both internally and externally
Otherwise assisting the Head of Legal and Global Digital Licensing, Legal & Global Digital Licensing Manager, COO and CEO as required
WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKIN: Experienced paralegals or LPC graduates as well as newly qualified lawyers

Should be confident and have a friendly, outgoing personality so that they can interact positively with AMRA’s writer members but they should also be highly organised and detail orientated in order to accurately carry out the legal work involved
Some experience dealing with music industry legal agreements, preferably in a songwriter, music publishing or collection society setting would be ideal
A passion for music (and the digital music market) is essential
SPECIFICS: Based near Cannon Street / Monument / Bank, London EC4R 3TEWorking hours are 9.30am-6pm

Title: Distribution Manager (UK)
Description: We live to create. We live for the creatives.Continue to article...Platoon is a platform for fearless creatives. Founded by veteran entrepreneurs from the creative and tech industries, our mission is to discover the very best emerging talent (musicians, artists, writers, filmmakers, designers) and provide them with the platform to create, distribute and commercialise exceptional creative work.WHAT YOU WILL DO AS PART OF OUR TEAM:Digital distribution supply chainYou will manage the end-to-end distribution process for all audio/video content to all DSP’s (Digital Service Providers) via FUGAYou will be the point of contact for artists & managers for all things distribution, analytics and royalty accountingYou will liaise with artists & managers and manage the onboarding process and delivery of all metadata and assetsAnalyticsYou will be comfortable working with analytics and data tools to provide regular updates to the team and to managersRoyalty AccountingYou will use our royalty accounting software to run monthly and weekly artist statements and manage artist queries around thisPreferably, you will have some experience working with digital content delivery, basic finance and accounting and/or financial tracking.WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT FROM YOUR NEW TEAMMATES:A deep focus on and passion for the individuals that we work withStrong conviction that the artists we work with will build exceptional global careers and businessesA real love and enthusiasm for discovering talent and for providing them with the tools to growA family – for artists, partners, teammates and stakeholdersWE TRY AND MAINTAIN A STRONG AND OPEN CULTURE. THESE ARE SOME OF OUR CORE VALUES:Exceptionally Creative – in everything we do and the individuals we work withThink Big – We’re crazy ambitious so we’re always thinking as big as we can. Bigger thinking leads to bigger challenges which leads to working with artists at the top of their game.Be Curious. Peel things apart. Sniff and prod and poke. Tug at the loose bits and chew on the fat. Look for better ways of doing everything. Mission = Possible.Move your body, your mind and your boundaries. Look after the bones that house you. Go hiking, do some yoga, take a swim. Always keep the blood pumping. Move your mind as much as you possibly can. Reimagine, reinvent, rethink everything. Always move your limits. Relentlessly push yourself, scale the mountains and set fire to the comfort zonePlay the long game. Play with friends and family and laugh as much as you possibly can, even when the sky is falling down. Play with ideas. Play with possibilities.Just say No. Walk away from the things that don’t serve you or you don’t believe in. Be precious with your time and energy. Give your knowledge to those who are ready for it. Know your worthChocolate & Tequila – are two things that can fix almost anything. Drink well and always with friends

Title: Senior A&R Manager (UK & Europe)
Description: Who are we?Continue to article...We’re a music company first and foremost. We love music, and it shows in the creativity, talent and breadth of our roster, and the craft that goes into making our music. Simply put, we create original, high-quality music for brands, broadcasters, filmmakers and creatives, all over the world and we work with a roster of more than 1000 artists, including renowned composers, respected singer songwriters and both known and emerging artists, streaming on all major platforms.Audio Network has more than 150,000 tracks in its catalogue, spanning every genre and mood imaginable.From Coca-Cola and Saatchi & Saatchi to HBO…We’ve helped them tell their iconic stories. Will you help shape ours?Who are we looking for?We are seeking a Senior A&R Manager (UK & Europe) to work in the A&R team (within Audio Network’s Music Team), being based in our London office.Your RoleReporting to the Head Of A&R, you will have a primary focus on the implementation of Audio Network’s UK and European A&R and wider music strategy. Your role will be balanced between sourcing the best artist, composer and producer talent whilst maintaining album project commissioning and delivery levels, artist project management and overseeing key commercial releases.Primary responsibilities Contribute to the global A&R team strategy in scouting and signing appropriate / target artists and composers to the catalogue from the UK and European territories.Build strong talent and management relationships and deliver the ANW message to the creative community.Contribute to agreed A&R and music commissioning targets.Overall responsibility for artist relations, efficient project management and production of owned projects in UK and Europe; ensuring that they are successfully executed, within budget and on time whilst maintaining Audio Network’s high standard of quality and authenticity.Influence our commissioning with insight into current and emerging music trends by keying into record labels, DSP’s, radio, press/online tastemakers and music specialists.Using industry knowledge and a creative ear to inform the team in A&R meetings and music team critical listening sessions giving both a commercial and creative perspective on the music delivered to Audio Network and ensuring that the company’s very high standards are maintained.Collaborating across marketing and sales teams on B2B Artist/Composer campaigns.Play a key role in Audio Networks growing commercial music strategy – be at the heart of the team responsible for executing B2C campaigns for our commercially released music.Regularly attending gigs, showcases and all relevant new music / A&R events and conferences when necessary. International travel will be required.Representing the music team internally and playing a part in the effective internal communication of our music to ANW global staff with the objective of representing our music, artists / composers, campaigns and stories.Required experience8 years experience with a proven track record in a similar role at either a record label, music publisher or related music company.Commercial awareness of the market, new music trends, music business developments, products and challenges.Should be immersed in the Music Industry, have an end to end understanding of how it works and have a contact base that reflects this – in particular, a strong relationship with key stakeholders at DSP’s is beneficial.An understanding of sync and the use of music in film, television and advertising.An awareness of music and media trends in the online / digital landscape.An obvious passion for a broad range of music and exemplary critical listening skills.Your Special SkillsExcellent communication skills with the ability and desire to thrive in a team and as part of a wider company culture.Excellent organisational skills, time management and an ability to prioritise tasks.Experience managing recording budgets.Calm and methodical approach to work in a sometimes pressurised environment.Passion for music across a wide range of genres. Base of knowledge in music theory and critical listening skills preferable.Comfortable in analysing music and discussing with colleagues and artists using technical and shared / specialist language. Confident presenting and discussing projects and associated successes and challenges in a group. Competent user of Microsoft office applications.

Who are we looking for?: We are seeking a Senior A&R Manager (UK & Europe) to work in the A&R team (within Audio Network’s Music Team), being based in our London office.
Your Role: Reporting to the Head Of A&R, you will have a primary focus on the implementation of Audio Network’s UK and European A&R and wider music strategy. Your role will be balanced between sourcing the best artist, composer and producer talent whilst maintaining album project commissioning and delivery levels, artist project management and overseeing key commercial releases.
Primary responsibilities: Contribute to the global A&R team strategy in scouting and signing appropriate / target artists and composers to the catalogue from the UK and European territories.
Build strong talent and management relationships and deliver the ANW message to the creative community.
Contribute to agreed A&R and music commissioning targets.

Overall responsibility for artist relations, efficient project management and production of owned projects in UK and Europe; ensuring that they are successfully executed, within budget and on time whilst maintaining Audio Network’s high standard of quality and authenticity.
Influence our commissioning with insight into current and emerging music trends by keying into record labels, DSP’s, radio, press/online tastemakers and music specialists.

Using industry knowledge and a creative ear to inform the team in A&R meetings and music team critical listening sessions giving both a commercial and creative perspective on the music delivered to Audio Network and ensuring that the company’s very high standards are maintained.
Collaborating across marketing and sales teams on B2B Artist/Composer campaigns.
Play a key role in Audio Networks growing commercial music strategy – be at the heart of the team responsible for executing B2C campaigns for our commercially released music.
Regularly attending gigs, showcases and all relevant new music / A&R events and conferences when necessary. International travel will be required.

Representing the music team internally and playing a part in the effective internal communication of our music to ANW global staff with the objective of representing our music, artists / composers, campaigns and stories.
Required experience: 8 years experience with a proven track record in a similar role at either a record label, music publisher or related music company.
Commercial awareness of the market, new music trends, music business developments, products and challenges.

Should be immersed in the Music Industry, have an end to end understanding of how it works and have a contact base that reflects this – in particular, a strong relationship with key stakeholders at DSP’s is beneficial.
An understanding of sync and the use of music in film, television and advertising.
An awareness of music and media trends in the online / digital landscape.
An obvious passion for a broad range of music and exemplary critical listening skills.
Your Special Skills: Excellent communication skills with the ability and desire to thrive in a team and as part of a wider company culture.
Excellent organisational skills, time management and an ability to prioritise tasks.
Experience managing recording budgets.
Calm and methodical approach to work in a sometimes pressurised environment.
Passion for music across a wide range of genres.
Base of knowledge in music theory and critical listening skills preferable.
Comfortable in analysing music and discussing with colleagues and artists using technical and shared / specialist language.
Confident presenting and discussing projects and associated successes and challenges in a group.
Competent user of Microsoft office applications.

Position Summary: The Director A&R will shape the creative direction and raise the profile of the publishing business at eOne. The successful hire will identify, sign and develop songwriters, writer/producers, and writer/artists across popular genres including pop, urban, hip hop and R&B. Experience working with songwriters, artists and music producers to take songwriters to “the next level.”
Hands-on comfort level in managing all steps of the A&R process.
Strong connections and reputation in the music industry that can be leveraged for the benefit of our songwriters.
Track record of relevant credits in popular music as an A&R, manager or creative director for successful writers and artists.
A creative professional who is genuinely collaborative, hard-working, high integrity and reliable.
Equal Opportunity: Entertainment One is an equal opportunity employer committed to supporting diversity and inclusion within its work environment.

Only successful candidates will be contacted.

Title: Repertoire Data Team Manager (UK)
Description: As one of the leading Collective Management Organisations (CMOs) at the heart of the UK’s music industry, PPL ensures those who invest their time, talent and money to make recorded music are compensated when their music is used in public by businesses or broadcast on TV or radio. Based in Soho our committed and passionate culture where we place our members and licensee customers at the heart of what we do, will offer a dynamic environment to showcase your skills.Continue to article...PPL RepertoireThe Repertoire department is a data operations function reporting into PPL’s Chief Operating Officer. In short, the department’s main responsibilities are processing and managing all recording, rights and performer line-up data pertaining to sound recordings owned by rightsholders and CMOs, and then processing data from PPL’s licensees (e.g. BBC) and other CMOs to track when and where those sound recordings are being used.Closely liaising with the Member Services, International, Distribution, Business Services, Business Change, IT and Insight & Innovation teams, the department is integral to PPL’s core role of distributing airplay royalties accurately and efficiently to its members. Additionally, the Repertoire department carry out several data processing tasks in support of services provided by PPL’s International and Business Services departments.What you’ll be doing…This role will be responsible for delivering the day to day Repertoire data management activities to maintain and improve the validity and completeness of all repertoire data – from recording information such as the recording title and where it was recorded, through to the performer line-up that made the recording and the rights ownership data. Coordinating the prioritisation of work-loads across the team is a key part of this. You will provide clear, summarised management information to senior management and, where appropriate, engage with external stakeholders to help improve the quality of their repertoire data processes.Key Accountabilities…Working under the direction of Repertoire Operations Manager to deliver the objectives set for the Repertoire Data Quality teamTo provide key BAU Data management and maintenance activities to the agreed budgets (where appropriate), timetables and quality;Leading the management of large and complex repertoire and member datasets (including from external sources) to maintain and improve its quality in accordance with agreed Data Governance procedures;Working with Distribution, Member Services, Insight & Innovation, and IT to define and make use of management information to enable effective communication and informed decision making;To lead and support the Repertoire Data Team in carrying out any activities necessary to contribute to the achievement of agreed deadlines and/or to reduce activity backlogs;What you’ll need…You’ll be an enthusiastic self-starter with an eye for detail with the following:Educated to a minimum of a degree level (or equivalent) in relevant technical and/or analytical subject. Or qualified through experienceProven track-record of successfully managing and developing team members working specifically with data management / data quality related BAU and project activitiesBroad experience of working in complex, high volume, data rich environmentsKnowledge and understanding of MDM and data management methodologiesProven experience of communicating with and presenting to a variety of audiences including Senior Executive level to simply convey complex concepts and informationProficient user of MS Office (Word, Excel & PowerPoint)What we can offer you…Apart from the competitive salary, generous pension scheme, monthly social events and free fruit; we offer a fun and highly spirted working environment with great people. When you come on board at PPL you can expect to be rewarded for your hard work in many ways, not just financially. Employees are entitled to a wide range of benefits which supports their lifestyles and needs.

PPL Repertoire: The Repertoire department is a data operations function reporting into PPL’s Chief Operating Officer. In short, the department’s main responsibilities are processing and managing all recording, rights and performer line-up data pertaining to sound recordings owned by rightsholders and CMOs, and then processing data from PPL’s licensees (e.g. BBC) and other CMOs to track when and where those sound recordings are being used.
What you\u2019ll be doing\u2026: This role will be responsible for delivering the day to day Repertoire data management activities to maintain and improve the validity and completeness of all repertoire data – from recording information such as the recording title and where it was recorded, through to the performer line-up that made the recording and the rights ownership data. Coordinating the prioritisation of work-loads across the team is a key part of this. You will provide clear, summarised management information to senior management and, where appropriate, engage with external stakeholders to help improve the quality of their repertoire data processes.
Key Accountabilities\u2026: Working under the direction of Repertoire Operations Manager to deliver the objectives set for the Repertoire Data Quality team
To provide key BAU Data management and maintenance activities to the agreed budgets (where appropriate), timetables and quality;
Leading the management of large and complex repertoire and member datasets (including from external sources) to maintain and improve its quality in accordance with agreed Data Governance procedures;
Working with Distribution, Member Services, Insight & Innovation, and IT to define and make use of management information to enable effective communication and informed decision making;
To lead and support the Repertoire Data Team in carrying out any activities necessary to contribute to the achievement of agreed deadlines and/or to reduce activity backlogs;
What you\u2019ll need\u2026: Educated to a minimum of a degree level (or equivalent) in relevant technical and/or analytical subject. Or qualified through experience
Proven track-record of successfully managing and developing team members working specifically with data management / data quality related BAU and project activities
Broad experience of working in complex, high volume, data rich environments
Knowledge and understanding of MDM and data management methodologies
Proven experience of communicating with and presenting to a variety of audiences including Senior Executive level to simply convey complex concepts and information
Proficient user of MS Office (Word, Excel & PowerPoint)
What we can offer you\u2026: Apart from the competitive salary, generous pension scheme, monthly social events and free fruit; we offer a fun and highly spirted working environment with great people. When you come on board at PPL you can expect to be rewarded for your hard work in many ways, not just financially. Employees are entitled to a wide range of benefits which supports their lifestyles and needs.

Title: VP of Business Development (US)
Description: Artist Growth is growing! We’re expanding our team to drive interest in our offerings to the live entertainment business.Continue to article...Artist growth is a fast growing software company that’s reinventing the way music artists and their supporting organizations manage their touring business. Our cloud platform addresses the needs of artists and tour teams as well as the management companies, agencies and labels that support their live business. We are poised to disrupt the $25 billion touring segment of the entertainment industry by being THE touring platform, delivering a valuable range of services to our customers.We are a young company which means no politics and a get-stuff-done culture where you can make your mark and take as much responsibility as you can handle. Come prove yourself — there’s no glass ceiling and no regimented org structure.The RoleIn this role, you will be focused on evangelizing AG Finance. AG Finance is a unique tour finance program that Artist Growth is offering artists in partnership with Pinnacle Financial Partners. AG Finance is a key element of Artist Growth’s vision of being the primary platform that touring artists go to launch and grow their touring business.This is primarily a business development role, targeting decision makers at large artist management companies, business management companies, music agencies and record labels. You will leverage your own existing network as well as AG’s sales infrastructure, to engage with these key decision makers to evangelize AG Finance.Your mission is to drive and close AG Finance tour advance loans to artists about to go on tour. You will do this by evangelizing the program to a wide audience of influencers, leading you to the artist business managers who will ultimately make the decision to apply for a tour advance loan through AG.This role can be based in Los Angeles or New Your City, where you will be working from a virtual office. You should expect frequent travel between the two cities. Most of your meetings should be face to face with key influencers.About YouYou have deep experience in the music businessYou have worked at a major agency or artist management companyYou have long and deep connections in the industryYou live the music cultureYou are tenacious on the phone, able to engage busy prospectsYou have comfort with ambiguity and with having less than 100% of information all of the timeYou have a lot of energy, enthusiasm and ambition to work with a strong desire to get betterYou have high acumen for selling and driving complex enterprise dealsResponsibilitiesIn this role you will:Initiate contact and gain audience with key targets:Leveraging relationships and research to find qualified prospectsPreparing action plans and schedules to engage specific targetsTraveling to shows and meeting with prospective clientsFollowing up on new leads and referrals resulting from field activityCoordinate between business manager and Pinnacle Bank on the negotiation of deal terms as well as any contractual terms.Manage accounts through quality checks and other follow-upMaintain an up-to-date level of industry and product expertiseTrack all activity in AG’s Sales CRMAbout AGArtist Growth is a comprehensive live entertainment artist management software platform. The AG platform connects artists, tour managers, artist managers, business managers, agents, labels and tour service providers to deliver all that a tour business needs to be successful. And through AG Finance powered by Pinnacle Financial Partners, Artist Growth also provides artists with the capital they need to fund their tours.The primary offering includes:1. Tour Management: managers/tour managers and label tour and promo departments use AG to run the operations of a tour with online collaboration across the entire tour team including third party providers2. Roster Tracking/Management: managers, business managers, agents and label teams are able to share a roster-wide 360-view of tour events, bookings and financials.3. Tour Financing: we facilitate artist tour advances underwritten against their contracted tour bookings that are in our system (and soon will offer credit cards).Ready for us?Think you can be a top-performing driver of our business and a key member of our quickly-growing company? Drop us your resume and let us know why you will crush it.

Title: Lecturer or Professional Practice Fellow in Music, Voice (NZ)
Description: Music is located in the Department of Music, Theatre and Performing Arts and offers undergraduate and postgraduate courses in music performance, composition, ethnomusicology and musicology, studio production and music technology, and music and theatre. Continue to article...Voice has long been an extremely important part of Otago’s Music programme: its many distinguished graduates include winners and place-getters in the nation’s leading vocal contests, and singers pursuing international careers as opera and concert performers.Over the years, the focus on voice has expanded considerably with the introduction of, first, rock and pop vocals and, more recently, musical theatre voice. Therefore, classical voice is fundamental not only to the classical music programme, but also to the teaching of singing per se. It builds synergies between the various voice streams.The RoleWe are seeking to appoint either a permanent, full-time (confirmation path) Lecturer in Music (Voice) OR a Professional Practice Fellow in Music (Voice) at full-time or part-time hours. Appointment will be made depending on skills and experience.The position provides the opportunity to work within a strong and forward-looking department dedicated to excellence in music, theatre, dance and performing arts education. In this role, you will undertake performance and teaching primarily in the area of vocal music performance and teach other departmental courses including summer school where needed. You will also be required to perform with colleagues and visiting performers.This is an exciting opportunity to join our team as the Department intends to establish a university choir, which the successful candidate will be expected to organise and/or direct.Lecturer:As a Lecturer you will also be expected to actively engage in research, which may include and be largely focused on performance per se and Performance-as-Research. You will be actively involved in the recruitment of new students and in applying for and receiving external research funding.You will be expected to have:Doctoral degree (DMA or PhD) or equivalent, and therefore be eligible to supervise postgraduate students.Significant profile as a current performer.Relevant vocal teaching experience.Professional Practice Fellow: As a Professional Practice Fellow you will undertake your duties under the overall supervision of the co-coordinator of the classical music performance programme. We are open to the possibility of the position being shared between two people.You will be expected to have:Masters degree (MMus or MA), or equivalent.Significant profile as a current performer.Relevant vocal teaching experience and professional qualification, and therefore be eligible to participate in the supervision of all postgraduate students.Further DetailsThe salary range for a Lecturer is $78,445 to $93,821 per annum and for a Professional Practice Fellow is $59,386 to $113,519 per annum (pro rata). Appointment will be made at an appropriate step in these ranges depending on qualifications and experience.ApplicationCandidates are requested to submit the following:A CV.A cover letter.Three referee contact details.Applications quoting reference number 1801681 will close on Sunday, 14 October 2018.Equal opportunity in employment is University policy.

The Role: We are seeking to appoint either a permanent, full-time (confirmation path) Lecturer in Music (Voice) OR a Professional Practice Fellow in Music (Voice) at full-time or part-time hours. Appointment will be made depending on skills and experience.

Title: International Social Media Manager (UK)
Description: The Zildjian Company has been synonymous with setting the standard for the development and manufacture of high performance musical instruments since 1623.Continue to article...As the world’s leading maker of cymbals, drumsticks and percussion mallets, Zildjian products are sold across the globe, under the Zildjian and Vic Firth brands, and are the standard to which all other cymbals, drumsticks and mallets are measured.From the beginner to the world’s greatest rock stars, jazz performers and concert percussionists, amateurs and pros alike chose Zildjian and Vic Firth products to allow them to experience and share the joy of music.Headquartered in Norwell, Massachusetts with offices in Los Angeles, CA and London, UK, Zildjian products are sold globally through distributors and via a network of dealers.All Zildjian products are made in the USA at our cymbal factory in Norwell, MA and drumstick/mallet factory in Newport, ME.We are looking for an International Social Media Manager to grow, engage, and retain our follower base across existing owned channels; drive earned media; and test, learn and scale within paid social media channels.Reporting to the Director of Digital Marketing, s/he will have direct responsibility for the execution of our social media marketing plan for the Zildjian family of brands including Zildjian, Vic Firth, and Mike Balter.S/he will be a digital athlete with a passion for being up-to-date on the latest digital technologies and social media trends.S/he will also be one part passionate brand builder and one part consumer champion – someone who thrives on spreading their passion and excitement for our brands with consumers in multiple segments around the world while continuously driving performance improvement by leveraging analytics to better understand our consumers and optimize our engagement with them.The successful candidate will have a high-level of creativity, attention to detail, and project management skills, with a talent for writing custom and engaging content.Our team is at the heart of our culture. We value family, innovation, and accountability. We strive to hire and develop the best and foster a culture of collaboration, communication and transparency. We are a driven and hardworking group of individuals that have a lot fun growing these brands!Key Responsibilities:Understand consumer personas, attitudes, behaviors and trends. Understand and leverage the consumer journey to optimize engagement and impact.In conjunction with the Director Digital Marketing, collaborate with Education and Content Strategy team, Category Management and Artist Relations to design and execute a social media strategy that delivers against business goals and objectives.Build and maintain our social media profiles and presence – writing and posting social optimized content and responding to followers on Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, Snapchat, Pinterest, Google, YouTube and other social media channels.Identify new and innovative ways to engage existing and new consumers through exploring new social media channels that reach our target audiences.Ensure all content is aligned with brand standards.Monitor and report on relevant social content about our brands, our competitors and other industry influencers.Attend consumer and trade events to capture and create content.Develop, recommend, and execute optimization strategies and tactics based on analytics insights and reports.Execute the SEO action plan; ensure best practices are implemented across our owned channels.Stay up to date with current technologies, best practices and trends in social media, design tools and applications and share across the team to continuously improve.Build, maintain and distribute a detailed a master consumer marketing calendar for each brand and region.Track social media performance against KPIs to ensure our plans and content are achieving relevant metrics relative to all paid, owned and earned efforts. Oversee test & learns and scaled media analytics to ensure the delivery of optimal results and impact.Develop, recommend, and execute optimization strategies and tactics based on analytics insights and reportsActively listen to consumers; monitor and report on relevant insights about our brands and products, our competitors and other industry influencersAttend consumer and trade events to capture and create contentStay up to date with current technologies, best practices and trends in social media, design tools and applications and share across the team to continuously improveBuild, maintain and distribute a detailed a master consumer marketing calendar for each brand and region.Collaborate with the US team to build a holistic view of our marketing activities and impact.RequirementsBachelor’s Degree in marketing or communications with 5 years of experience in social media marketing or digital first PR.A social media power user with passion for social media marketing and trends; first-hand experience activating in and optimizing placement and execution on Google, YouTube and leading social media platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter.Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with demonstrated experience writing content for social media.Ability to conceptualize, create and deliver content (text, image, video)A solid understanding of SEO techniques.Excellent project management skills and ability to multi-task and track a wide variety of activities under deadline, paying close attention to detail.Must be analytical – be creative and also have the know how to drive results/reportingBackground in drumming or percussion.Ability to travel and work remotely is needed.Ability to work/live in the Greater London area.Zildjian does not accept/pay fees for unsolicited resumes from third party agencies/vendors. As part of our dedication to the diversity of our workforce, Zildjian is committed to Equal Employment Opportunity without regard for race, ethnicity, gender, protected veteran status, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or religion.Please send resume/CV to Andread@Zildjian.com

Key Responsibilities:: Understand consumer personas, attitudes, behaviors and trends. Understand and leverage the consumer journey to optimize engagement and impact.

In conjunction with the Director Digital Marketing, collaborate with Education and Content Strategy team, Category Management and Artist Relations to design and execute a social media strategy that delivers against business goals and objectives.

Build and maintain our social media profiles and presence – writing and posting social optimized content and responding to followers on Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, Snapchat, Pinterest, Google, YouTube and other social media channels.
Identify new and innovative ways to engage existing and new consumers through exploring new social media channels that reach our target audiences.
Ensure all content is aligned with brand standards.
Monitor and report on relevant social content about our brands, our competitors and other industry influencers.
Attend consumer and trade events to capture and create content.
Develop, recommend, and execute optimization strategies and tactics based on analytics insights and reports.
Execute the SEO action plan; ensure best practices are implemented across our owned channels.
Stay up to date with current technologies, best practices and trends in social media, design tools and applications and share across the team to continuously improve.
Build, maintain and distribute a detailed a master consumer marketing calendar for each brand and region.

Track social media performance against KPIs to ensure our plans and content are achieving relevant metrics relative to all paid, owned and earned efforts. Oversee test & learns and scaled media analytics to ensure the delivery of optimal results and impact.
Develop, recommend, and execute optimization strategies and tactics based on analytics insights and reports
Actively listen to consumers; monitor and report on relevant insights about our brands and products, our competitors and other industry influencers
Attend consumer and trade events to capture and create content
Stay up to date with current technologies, best practices and trends in social media, design tools and applications and share across the team to continuously improve
Build, maintain and distribute a detailed a master consumer marketing calendar for each brand and region.
Collaborate with the US team to build a holistic view of our marketing activities and impact.
Requirements: Bachelor’s Degree in marketing or communications with 5 years of experience in social media marketing or digital first PR.

A social media power user with passion for social media marketing and trends; first-hand experience activating in and optimizing placement and execution on Google, YouTube and leading social media platforms such as Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with demonstrated experience writing content for social media.
Ability to conceptualize, create and deliver content (text, image, video)
A solid understanding of SEO techniques.
Excellent project management skills and ability to multi-task and track a wide variety of activities under deadline, paying close attention to detail.
Must be analytical – be creative and also have the know how to drive results/reporting
Background in drumming or percussion.
Ability to travel and work remotely is needed.
Ability to work/live in the Greater London area.

Title: Junior Digital Account & Product Manager (DE)
Description: As the streaming landscape is continuing to grow and change, Kobalt is looking for a personable as well as analytical Junior Digital Account & Product Manager to join our Berlin-based AWAL team.Continue to article...This amazing opportunity will allow you to work with an incredibly diverse roster of up and coming as well as established acts, helping to drive their playlisting and profiles across digital services.Reporting to the Marketing Director, GSA, you will help with the day to day management of new release information and pitching materials to ensure new and ongoing releases are presented to digital accounts.To be successful in the role, we need someone with a natural incline towards sales, but with a humble approach, as well as a true people person who takes great care of Kobalt contacts.You will be the primary contact for selected platforms and therefore responsible for monitoring and reporting on sales and trends using various analytics tools, so it’s pivotal that you have an analytical mindset, an eye for details and love digging the data!Additionally, you will assist as Junior Product Manager working with our international artists and label clients and their representatives to implement the global strategy into the local market (GSA).For GSA clients your role includes supporting the onboarding process via UK, building a good relationship with them, and finding out everything you need to know to support their career fully.This role is ideal for someone with an incredible drive, enthusiasm & passion for music!This is an exciting time to be joining Kobalt’s Recordings division, and to be successful in the role, we need a confident, quick learner who is not afraid of asking questions but also great at taking control of situations.The Berlin office is a small, tight-knit unit so we are looking for an exceptional team player, with a track-record of working proactively in teams, is a safe pair of hands and takes initiative as well as goes above and beyond to support the team!Kobalt is the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers as we transform the way music is released through AWAL and Kobalt Music Recordings by creating a high-quality path for artists to remain outside of the traditional label system while maintaining ownership of their masters.The leading artists who have already chosen Kobalt includes Lauv, Nick Cave, The Kooks, The Wombats, You Me At Six, Courtney Barnett, Tom Misch, Hugo Helmig and Vérité. Working at Kobalt, you will help build the music services company of tomorrow!What would you do as a Junior Digital Account & Product Manager?Represent AWAL releases (catalogue as well as frontline) to selected DSPs & assist with the day to day relationship with key accounts in GSALiaise with project marketing leads to help build digital sales and streaming strategiesAssist with the collection and processing of pitching information across the AWAL rostersPrepare presentations for playlist and editorial pitching meetingsGenerate weekly reports on the status of artist campaigns including playlist and on-platform profile resultsAnalyse performance of releases using Kobalt’s analytics toolsAssist artists, labels and their representatives as point of contact for any queries related to releases/dealsAs a Junior Product Manager you work largely independently on our releases and you coordinate all processes and measures of marketing:Creative exchange with promotion agencies as well as managements, artists and UK AWAL office to ensure a successful release/ artists campaigns for GSAYou control your campaigns on a data-driven basis and are constantly looking for new marketing strategies.You’re working closely with UK team to develop and implement digital marketing strategies as well as innovative digital conceptsYou work closely with the global AWAL team and you are in constant exchange of knowledgeIt is also important to establish and cultivate contacts with artists, artist managers, tour organizers and media partnersPitch videos & translate shorter PR-texts, mailout of CDs, research sales and streaming dataCompilation and administration of newsletter contacts as well as mailout of newsletterArtist support on promotion days as well as concert visitsYou will ideally have……knowledge of current and future trends in music and the digital services landscape…global outlook and understanding of markets…excellent written and verbal communication skills with experience of building and maintaining client relationships…organized self-starter, able to multi-task, adapt to changing priorities, and work in a high-pressure environment…interpersonal skills and the ability to build relationships throughout the organization as well as with external parties…fluency in German and English…excellent attention to detail, an analytical & detail-driven mind-set…professional experience using Microsoft office suite…good understanding of flexible entrepreneurial work environments and growing businesses…proactive, both reliable and willing to take responsibility…friendly, enthusiastic and results-oriented personality

Title: Digital Content Producer (UK)
Description: AWAL Recordings is seeking a dynamic creative superstar eager to join a cutting-edge independent record label based in London.Continue to article...The ideal candidate is passionate about internet trends, technology, and multimedia content with a creative flair. Doesn’t just look outside the box but wants to create the next evolution of the digital landscape in music.This person will be leveraging their experiences to drive creative ideations on both digital and social media platforms for our artists.You’ll help define and execute strategy while producing content yourself as well as discover, recruit and manage external creative suppliers working in all disciplines of digital media.WHAT DOES A DIGITAL CONTENT PRODUCER AT KOBALT DO?Develop short-form visual content, GIF’s, memes, animated artwork, short-form videos and dynamic media assets.Collaborate with AWAL’s marketing & digital marketing team to create shareable content that excites fans and sets our artists apart in an increasingly crowded marketplace.Analyze KPI’s to ensure the success of each channel content strategy across multiple campaigns.Manage and create artist-driven strategies with an eye to the future and to keep pushing the boundaries of digital creation.Engage with a wider creative community to help leverage leading-edge creative content strategies for AWAL artists.Collaborate in developing social campaigns for music artists across various genres, channels and platforms.Utilize popular trends and Internet memes; incorporate them into digital campaigns, make recommendations on new channels and strategies to incorporate them.WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? 2-5 years of developing cutting edge digital content – Final Cut, Photoshop, InDesignExperience with creating or managing social channels; social campaigns, supporting digital marketingKnowledge of online social performance analytics with ability to make creative decisions based on this dataExcellent verbal and written communication skillsAbility to manage multiple projects simultaneously and work autonomously in a fast-paced, high-growth environmentPassion for creative workA love of music!

WHAT DOES A DIGITAL CONTENT PRODUCER AT: Develop short-form visual content, GIF’s, memes, animated artwork, short-form videos and dynamic media assets.
Collaborate with AWAL’s marketing & digital marketing team to create shareable content that excites fans and sets our artists apart in an increasingly crowded marketplace.
Analyze KPI’s to ensure the success of each channel content strategy across multiple campaigns.
Manage and create artist-driven strategies with an eye to the future and to keep pushing the boundaries of digital creation.
Engage with a wider creative community to help leverage leading-edge creative content strategies for AWAL artists.
Collaborate in developing social campaigns for music artists across various genres, channels and platforms.
Utilize popular trends and Internet memes; incorporate them into digital campaigns, make recommendations on new channels and strategies to incorporate them.
WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKIN: 2-5 years of developing cutting edge digital content , Final Cut, Photoshop, InDesign
Experience with creating or managing social channels; social campaigns, supporting digital marketing
Knowledge of online social performance analytics with ability to make creative decisions based on this data
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and work autonomously in a fast-paced, high-growth environment
Passion for creative work
A love of music!

Title: Promotions Coordinator (UK)
Description: Warp Records has an exciting opportunity for a UK Promotions Coordinator to be based in their London office.Continue to article...The full-time role is part of the UK team, supporting creative and effective campaigns for the label’s roster of ground-breaking artists.Duties may include; liaising with media, research, coordination of events, distribution of promotional materials, handling mailing lists and reporting.The ideal candidate will have a huge passion for and be deeply immersed in music culture with a good knowledge of Warp’s artists.A good understanding of the UK music and media landscape is a must. Title: Communications and PR Manager (UK)
Description: As a leading licensing business at the heart of the UK’s music industry, PPL ensures that those who invest their time, talent and money to make recorded music are compensated and recognised when their music is used by businesses or broadcast on TV, radio or online.Continue to article...What you’ll be doing…Under the direction of the Senior Communications and Marketing Manager, you will be responsible for planning, implementing and measuring external communications and PR activities on time and on budget to support the company’s business activities.The Communications and PR Manager will be responsible for seeking opportunities proactively to ‘sell’ PPL across appropriate B2B and B2C platforms to key target audiences, including the company’s members (existing and new), the broadcast sector, overseas music licensing companies, the wider music industry, government, and the general public.This coverage is not limited to media publications but includes speaking opportunities, drafting engaging newsletter content and identifying appropriate opportunities across other public platforms.Key accountabilities…Implement strategic campaigns, which raise awareness of the organisation, drive business, and position the company as a leader.Manage the sell-in of stories to the media, creatively communicating angles that are relevant to journalists and publications to maximise coverage potential.Plan, write and manage the delivery of corporate materials from press releases, interview briefing notes, and CEO quarterly updates, to internal and external presentations, newsletter stories, Annual Reviews, and opinion pieces.Advise on and select the best use of media and overall communications channels (i.e. TV, radio, print, online) as required for the successful execution of campaigns.Build a network of journalist contacts proactively in a range of national, regional and trade publications that can help the company to extend its coverage potential.Liaise proactively with staff throughout PPL to ensure that all communications materials and information relating to campaigns is up-to-date and consistent, whilst complying with the company’s mission, corporate values and policies.Monitor and report on the effectiveness of all media campaigns, always looking to develop the metrics and formats of presenting this information.What you’ll need…Along with the key accountabilities you will also need the following:At least five years’ experience of working in a corporate communications and PR role, preferably in-house.Solid knowledge of PR, marketing and social media disciplines through relevant degree and/or professional qualification.Strong time management skills with the ability to prioritise, meet deadlines and effectively manage competing organisational demands and a heavy workload.Experience of utilising traditional tools (press, networking etc.) as well as new tools (social media, blogs etc.) in order to maximise media coverage and awareness opportunities.Strong interpersonal skills and ability to engage with a wide range of stakeholders at all levels to drive results.Experience of working flexibly in a time-pressured and challenging environment with multiple deadlines.Highly IT literate.Experience of working in the music industry would be beneficial.What we have to offer…When you come on board at PPL you can expect to be competitively rewarded for your hard work. You will be entitled to 25 days’ annual leave. You will also be entitled to a wide range of benefits to support your needs and lifestyle, including Private Medical Care, Season Ticket Loan, Childcare Vouchers, Life Assurance, Discounted Gym Memberships and many more…You will also be entitled to an annual discretionary bonus, depending on PPL and personal performance.Next Steps…If this sounds like you, then we’d love to hear from you! Please apply with your CV and cover letter as to why you feel this is the right role for you.Equality and Diversity at PPLPPL are committed to equal opportunities and inclusivity, therefore we welcome and encourage applicants from all sections of the community.Unfortunately, due to the high number of application received for every role here at PPL we are only able to contact candidates we’d like to progress to interview stage

Title: Senior PR Agent (NL)
