The label looking for an experienced Senior PR Agent to join its PR team full-time, based in Hilversum in the Netherlands. RESPONSIBILITIESBuilding strategic PR campaigns for music releases on Spinnin’s (sub)labels, writing press releases and contriving story ideas for various projects.Building a relationship with Spinnin’s artists and coordinate campaigns to raise their profiles to a next level.Generate widespread press coverage in line with the artists’ goals and wishes.Working closely with various teams within the Spinnin’ brand to create new and unique campaigns to promote the label and its artists, including setting up promotional strategies along with our Global Partnerships department.REQUIREMENTSAt least three years experience in music PR, setting up print and online media campaigns.Up to date with current developments in (electronic) dance music, its artists, labels and events.An extensive list of contacts within (inter)national music media.Understanding and able to incorporate the importance of social media into a PR campaign.Excellent written and verbal skills in both Dutch and English.A passionate music lover, team player and sparkling personality.WHAT DO WE OFFER YOU?And what do we offer you? Well, very much! A versatile job at one of the key players on the market.The company offers an inspiring, energetic working environment and a competitive salary. In addition, we offer good employee benefits and we give you the chance to fulfill the best job in the world!Are you interested? We would like to receive your motivation and CV. You can send all this to jobs@spinninrecords.nl to Benita van BinsbergenAcquisition for this vacancy is not appreciated.

RESPONSIBILITIES: Building strategic PR campaigns for music releases on Spinnin’s (sub)labels, writing press  releases and contriving story ideas for various projects.
Building a relationship with Spinnin’s artists and coordinate campaigns to raise their profiles  to a next level.
Generate widespread press coverage in line with the artists’ goals and wishes.

Working closely with various teams within the Spinnin’ brand to create new and unique  campaigns to promote the label and its artists, including setting up promotional strategies  along with our Global Partnerships department.

Up to date with current developments in (electronic) dance music, its artists, labels and  events.
An extensive list of contacts within (inter)national music media.

Excellent written and verbal skills in both Dutch and English.
A passionate music lover, team player and sparkling personality.
WHAT DO WE OFFER YOU?: And what do we offer you? Well, very much! A versatile job at one of the key players on the market.

Title: Publicity & Communications Director (US)
Description: Red Bull Records is a full-service independent record label dedicated to long-term artist development, with a diverse roster across a variety of musical genres. Title: Radio & TV Plugger (UK)
Description: You will oversee the National Radio campaigns for a broad roster, working closely with the Senior Radio Promotions Manager at all times. You will also work alongside the Head of TV Promotions to help them implement TV Promotions campaigns. You will initiate, coordinate and implement the promotional strategy for artists on your roster in conjunction with the Senior Radio Promotions Manager.Continue to article...Key responsibilities:Develop & maintain relationships with producers, music teams & presenters across a full range of National Radio stations via regular phone contact, combined with meetings.Develop & maintain relationships with artists management & external labels.Work closely with all internal & external teams to maximise artist’s potential across all National Radio channels.Keep up to date with all new and existing promotional opportunities across National Radio.Book impactful & strategic promotional opportunities across National Radio for artists.Oversee the organisation and execution of promotional opportunities across National radio.Create & consult on radio edits where appropriate.Maintain awareness of Radio industry trends.Maintain awareness of presenter tastes & trends.Provide regular, thorough updates to internal & external teams.Create radio single timelines for artists in conjunction with the Senior Radio Promotions Manager, applying promotional strategy to all National Radio campaigns.Get key radio contacts to see showcases/live gigs; arrange appropriate hospitality for contacts around said events.Creating promotional assets for physical mailouts (where appropriate).Maintain a broad knowledge of Social Media opportunities to assure maximum utilisation of National Radio promotion.Ensure online coverage on the relevant websites when radio promotion is booked and circulate it to the relevant internal teams.Help manage artist promotion budgets in conjunction with the Senior Radio Promotions Manager.Attend the weekly Caroline Intl planning meeting as well as individual artist meetings, reporting on the radio plot for the relevant artists, commenting on forthcoming opportunities, and contributing ideas to all aspects of the campaign.Clip & distribute spot plays to relevant teams.Help maintain TV contacts database in conjunction with the Head of TV Promotions.Assist the Head of TV Promotions with artist promotional bookings.Oversee servicing of video content to TV platforms in conjunction with the Head of TV Promotions. Including: ingestion of videos to Promostream and Video Submission Forms.Person Specification:EssentialPrevious relevant experience in the music industry.Good understanding of National Radio campaigns.A strong list of contacts across National Radio.Excellent communication skills – written and verbal.Excellent organisation skills and attention to detail.Efficient and able to work independently to a high standard.The ability to learn quickly and use own initiative.Ability to remain calm when working under pressure and to deadlines.Flexible and able to prioritise a large workload.Hard working and enthusiastic team player.Self-motivated with the ability to deal with the routine tasks as well as the more interesting aspects of the role.Keen to develop within the field of Radio & TV PR.Passion for music and understanding of how UK media works.Passion to maintain & create new relationships.Comfortable in a networking environment.Be confident – have a voice and be able to communicate ideas.DesirableRecord company experience, particularly in a Promotions department.Willingness to attend regular gigs. Diversity and Inclusion:Universal Music is an equal opportunities employer. We embrace diversity and are committed to a working environment where no one will be treated less favourably on grounds of their sex, race, disability, sexual orientation, religion, belief or age.Disclaimer:The company presents this job description as a guide to the major areas and duties for which the jobholder is accountable. However, the business operates in an environment that demands change and the jobholder's specific responsibilities and activities will vary and develop. Therefore, the job description should be seen as indicative and not as a permanent, definitive and exhaustive statement.

Develop & maintain relationships with artists management & external labels.
Work closely with all internal & external teams to maximise artist’s potential across all National Radio channels.
Keep up to date with all new and existing promotional opportunities across National Radio.

Good understanding of National Radio campaigns.
A strong list of contacts across National Radio.
Excellent communication skills – written and verbal.
Excellent organisation skills and attention to detail.
Efficient and able to work independently to a high standard.
The ability to learn quickly and use own initiative.
Ability to remain calm when working under pressure and to deadlines.
Flexible and able to prioritise a large workload.
Hard working and enthusiastic team player.
Self-motivated with the ability to deal with the routine tasks as well as the more interesting aspects of the role.
Keen to develop within the field of Radio & TV PR.
Passion for music and understanding of how UK media works.
Passion to maintain & create new relationships.
Comfortable in a networking environment.
Be confident – have a voice and be able to communicate ideas.

Title: Copyright & Royalties Manager (UK)
Description: Manners McDade is looking for an experienced Copyright & Royalties Manager for a full time, permanent position.Continue to article...Flexible working is considered, salary is according to experience and the role will be based at our office in Farringdon, London.Manners McDade is an independent specialist publisher with a catalogue of instrumental and electronic music including the works of artists such as Nils Frahm, Max Cooper & Poppy Ackroyd and TV composers Ben Foster (Happy Valley) Miguel d’Oliveira (First Dates) & Jon Opstad (Thirteen).Reporting to the Managing Director, the successful candidate will manage all aspects of copyright and royalties including liaison with writers and composers and regular communication with our network of international sub-publishers and our account manager at PRS.Essential skills:Two years’ copyright and royalties experienceWorking knowledge of Music Maestro/VistexAbility to work within a small teamAbility to be flexible and collaborativePreferred skills:Ability to read musicSpoken French and/or GermanKnowledge of film and TV music including cue sheets and clock numbersApply with CV and covering letter to Catherine Manners, Director catherine@mannersmcdade.co.ukClosing date for applications is Friday 21st September and interviews will be held on Friday 28th September.

Essential skills:: Two years’ copyright and royalties experience
Ability to work within a small team
Ability to be flexible and collaborative

Title: Digital Account Manager (UK)
Description: Glassnote Records is the proud home to a broad roster of artists, including Grammy Award winning Phoenix, Brit Award Nominated Childish Gambino, BBC Sound Of 2018 long listed Jade Bird, IDER, Dylan Cartlidge, Hamilton Leithauser, Flo Morrissey, Half Moon Run, Flight Facilities, Mansionair, James Hersey, Mosa Wild & William Prince.Continue to article...Based in our busy central London office, the Digital Account Manager will work closely with DSPs, digital platforms and content creators to develop and implement innovative, creative and meaningful digital campaigns, as well as devising effective streaming and digital marketing strategies for our wide roster of artists. The successful candidate will have excellent account management skills, strong existing relationships with DSPs and be as analytical as they are creative. Previous proven experience in audience engagement and growth is also essential. Initiating and developing new relationships will be key, along with excellent project management, presentation and problem solving skills. We’re looking for an organised, adaptable team player with a passion for music, digital and artist development to join our like-minded team.To apply, please email your CV to jobs@glassnotemusic.com with the subject heading “Digital Account Manager” by Friday 21st September.

Title: Director of Operations (UK)
Description: We’re hiring! UK Music is looking for a new Director of Operations.Continue to article...UK Music is a campaigning and lobbying organisation that represents the British music industry.An exciting opportunity has opened up to become our Director of Operations. Based in our central London office, you will report directly to the CEO.You will need experience juggling multiple projects, working to tight deadlines and have the ability to ensure the smooth running of a busy office.The Director of Operations will be responsible for:Office management; including facilities and building contacts and contracts, staff health and safety/first aid, office licences etc.IT management; liaison with IT providers, responsible for software contracts and office security procedures.CEO diary managementCreating and keeping up to date the UK Music activity gridRunning weekly staff meetingsBoard management; collating and sending out all board papers, fixing and minuting annual board meeting dates and ensuring funders and members are all engaged and supported.Finance management; collating and sending out all Governance and Finance papers, fixing annual Governance and Finance meetings, processing and getting sign off for all incoming invoices and management of UK Music credit cards.Company Secretarial; keeping Companies House records up to date, processing Annual Statutory Accounts, managing lease agreements and company insurances.HR; responsible for all staff HR issues, manage all staff holiday and sick leave, manage UK Music benefits and lead on new starters and leavers processes.Member engagement and relationship managementManaging UK Music Business and Design Support officerSalary is dependent on experience.Please apply with your CV and cover letter as to why you feel this is the right role for you.UK Music is committed to equal opportunities and inclusivity, so we welcome all applicants.Please send applications to: ele.hill@ukmusic.orgUnfortunately, due to the high number of applications, only candidates shortlisted for interview will be contacted.Closing date for applications: 6pm Wednesday September 19.

Title: Head of Music Publishing Partnerships, YouTube and Google Play (SP)
Description: Continue to article...Google’s line of products and services to our clients never stops growing. The Partnerships Development team is responsible for seeking and exploring new opportunities with Google’s partners.Armed with your business acumen and extensive product knowledge, you are right on the front line of interacting with our partners, and helping them find ways to grow using Google’s newest product offerings.Your knowledge of relevant verticals and relationships with key industry players will help shape our great applications and content for products such as YouTube, Google TV and Commerce.As the Head of Music Publishing Partnerships Music-focused group of the APAC Content Partnerships team, you’ll be responsible for a portfolio of established and new YouTube publishers, collection society as well as songwriters/composers.You will be responsible for the management of new licensing agreements, the renewal of existing ones as well as the management of theses partnerships on a day-to-day basis.You will cultivate your partner relationships to ensure they can take full advantage of Youtube and Google’s ever-growing suite of offerings, optimize their channel on YouTube and identify growth opportunities.At the same time, you will also keep your Google and YouTube teammates up to speed on the complex and evolving music publishing business landscape in the region to ensure that our products and practices continue to lead and support the market.ResponsibilitiesManage and execute partnerships with content partners primarily for compositions rights.Manage day-to-day management of strategic composition rights partners in APAC, such as local branches of music publishers and the various collecting societies.Prioritize agreement and business line development through analysis and understanding of YouTube’s product roadmap.Define content acquisition vertical strategies, decide where to focus efforts, and choose mix of acquisition channels/methods.Initiate and maintain relations with content owners and/or creators, develop and/or lead scalable content acquisition programs, and help build content metrics and business infrastructure tools.QualificationsMinimum qualifications:BA/BS degree in Law or Business Administration, or equivalent practical experience.13 years of relevant business experience in music and/or licensing music rights for digital music, broadcast, or internet businesses.Experience leading complex negotiations and managing strategic partnerships in digital music and/or media.Experience in rights management; including sync rights, performance licenses (and withdrawals), collection societies and publisher/label relationships.Preferred qualifications:Experience working with data management and YouTube’s Content Management System.Experience negotiating complex music agreements.Ability to think strategically and convert complex issues into straightforward, timely action and thrive in a fast-paced, fluid, and collaborative environment.Established relationships with senior level executives in publishing music industry in the region.Fluency with, or willingness to learn, technological concepts, financial models and product features.

Responsibilities: Manage and execute partnerships with content partners primarily for compositions rights.
Manage day-to-day management of strategic composition rights partners in APAC, such as local branches of music publishers and the various collecting societies.
Prioritize agreement and business line development through analysis and understanding of YouTube’s product roadmap.
Define content acquisition vertical strategies, decide where to focus efforts, and choose mix of acquisition channels/methods.
Initiate and maintain relations with content owners and/or creators, develop and/or lead scalable content acquisition programs, and help build content metrics and business infrastructure tools.
Qualifications: BA/BS degree in Law or Business Administration, or equivalent practical experience.
13 years of relevant business experience in music and/or licensing music rights for digital music, broadcast, or internet businesses.
Experience leading complex negotiations and managing strategic partnerships in digital music and/or media.
Experience in rights management; including sync rights, performance licenses (and withdrawals), collection societies and publisher/label relationships.
Experience working with data management and YouTube’s Content Management System.
Experience negotiating complex music agreements.
Ability to think strategically and convert complex issues into straightforward, timely action and thrive in a fast-paced, fluid, and collaborative environment.
Established relationships with senior level executives in publishing music industry in the region.
Fluency with, or willingness to learn, technological concepts, financial models and product features.

Title: Digital Marketing Manager (UK)
Description: Based in our London office, Domino Recording Company is seeking a full time Digital Marketing Manager.Continue to article...The Digital Marketing Manager is our central conduit for all digital marketing and advertising initiatives. Working closely with the UK Project Management and Marketing teams, they will originate the digital identity of our campaigns, developing comprehensive marketing plans and the assets to execute them, and manage our UK and International digital advertising activity.We are seeking an individual who lives and breathes digital culture, and will bring a constant source of digital and technological ideas to the team. Alongside a strong strategic background and a practical skillset in running advertising campaigns, candidates with experience driving special projects, API builds and creative digital initiatives will be highly regarded.Great interpersonal skills, a self-starting attitude and the ability to work in an open and creative group environment are key to this hire. Two years’ practical digital marketing experience in a music focused environment is preferable. Alternatively outstanding candidates from the field of film, media or TV will be considered.Duties include:Conceive and oversee digital marketing endeavors for Domino’s project roster, working closely with the Project Management and Marketing teams.Lead UK & International digital advertising campaigns that are complimentary to campaign marketing objectives, working closely with the UK & International teams to effectively phase campaign activity and budgets.Buying, trafficking & optimising advertising across various platforms including Facebook Business Manager (including Facebook and Instagram), Google Ads (including Search, Display and YouTube), Snapchat and more, with support from a coordinator.Drive briefs for digital marketing tools in collaboration with internal and external designers/developers including: campaign websites and API builds, banner advertisements, bespoke social media assets, pre-roll video ads and more.Run social media campaigns and fan focused engagement initiatives, speaking to and leveraging our artists’ ecosystems to best serve our campaigns.Share digital knowledge within the team via demographic and engagement reports, social media snapshots, YouTube/VEVO data and advertising results.Stay abreast of and advise the team on the latest developments in online advertising, digital media, D2C and music/technology partnerships.Build and maintain relationships in the digital marketing realm, with a particular focus around social network partners including Facebook, Twitter, YouTube and more.Requirements:Minimum of two years’ digital advertising and campaign experience in the music industry or related fieldRegular user of Facebook, Instagram, YouTube and Twitter, with proven experience using social media to effectively enhance marketing campaignsExperience in International digital marketing highly regardedExcellent written and verbal communication skillsAttention to detail & ability to multitaskBachelor’s degree in related field preferredDomino Recording Co. was founded in the UK in 1993 by Laurence Bell, who is still the primary creative force of the company. Initially focused on releasing singles and albums by American artists in the UK (Pavement, Bonnie “Prince” Billy, Sebadoh), the label has expanded over the past decade to include offices in New York, Berlin, Paris and Sydney and has had considerable worldwide success, both commercial and artistic, with acts as diverse as Arctic Monkeys, Franz Ferdinand, Animal Collective, Blood Orange, Dirty Projectors, Real Estate, The Kills, and Jon Hopkins to name a few.Please send a covering letter stating how you meet the requirements along with your CV to vacancy@dominorecordco.com before 5pm on September 28th, 2018.

Duties include:: Conceive and oversee digital marketing endeavors for Domino’s project roster, working closely with the Project Management and Marketing teams.

Lead UK & International digital advertising campaigns that are complimentary to campaign marketing objectives, working closely with the UK & International teams to effectively phase campaign activity and budgets.

Buying, trafficking & optimising advertising across various platforms including Facebook Business Manager (including Facebook and Instagram), Google Ads (including Search, Display and YouTube), Snapchat and more, with support from a coordinator.

Drive briefs for digital marketing tools in collaboration with internal and external designers/developers including: campaign websites and API builds, banner advertisements, bespoke social media assets, pre-roll video ads and more.
Run social media campaigns and fan focused engagement initiatives, speaking to and leveraging our artists’ ecosystems to best serve our campaigns.
Share digital knowledge within the team via demographic and engagement reports, social media snapshots, YouTube/VEVO data and advertising results.
Stay abreast of and advise the team on the latest developments in online advertising, digital media, D2C and music/technology partnerships.
Build and maintain relationships in the digital marketing realm, with a particular focus around social network partners including Facebook, Twitter, YouTube and more.
Requirements:: Minimum of two years’ digital advertising and campaign experience in the music industry or related field
Regular user of Facebook, Instagram, YouTube and Twitter, with proven experience using social media to effectively enhance marketing campaigns
Experience in International digital marketing highly regarded
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Attention to detail & ability to multitask
Bachelor’s degree in related field preferred

Title: Coordinator, Content Operations (UK)
Description: We’re looking for a Content Operations Coordinator to join this expanding team within AWAL’s Digital Operations department. The successful candidate will be responsible for maintaining the quality of our release deliveries, ensuring our metadata is best in class. Experience with digital service metadata requirements and content management systems is required. An obsessive eye for detail and encyclopedic knowledge across a variety of genres would be a definite plus too!Continue to article...BackgroundKobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the center of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL. Kobalt is the leading technology-powered music services company, offering end-to-end solutions for over 50,000 artists and songwriters and 800 labels and publishing companies. Through Kobalt technology and services, creators benefit from more money, better reporting, and maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. With the award-winning Kobalt desktop portal and app, Kobalt clients get real-time insight into their work and activityWhether an artist is looking for the best digital distribution or a truly global campaign, AWAL has become the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers. This has created a high-quality path for artists to remain outside of the traditional label system while maintaining ownership of their masters. The leading artists who have already chosen AWAL includes Lauv, Nick Cave, Deadmau5, Die Antwoord, Verite, Tom Misch and Ray BLK.AWAL is a preferred supplier to the major DSPs, holding Direct Live status with Apple. We deliver audio and video to all of the key platforms using our bespoke supply chain system, powered by Kobalt’s market leading technology, making thousands of deliveries per week.We’re currently over 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re expecting to grow. That’s where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!What does a Content Operations Coordinator at AWAL do?QC audio release submissions in accordance with partner metadata specifications.Provide structured feedback for client-facing teams when releases fail checks.Carry out metadata updates as required across the AWAL catalogue.Work with internal teams to continually review and optimise content ingestion processes with a view to scale.Contribute to Content Operations documentation, including metadata style guide.What Skills and Experience are we looking for?Experience in a Content Operations, Digital Supply Chain or similar role at a label or distributor.Good knowledge of music metadata in a digital distribution context, particularly the iTunes and Spotify style guides.Experience working with content management systems (iTunes Connect and Spotify preferred).Meticulous attention to detail.Passion for a range of musical genres with bonus points for Classical or Bollywood knowledge.

Background: Kobalt has revolutionized the music publishing business by putting the creator at the center of everything we do. We are now taking the same approach and transforming the way recording artists release music through AWAL. Kobalt is the leading technology-powered music services company, offering end-to-end solutions for over 50,000 artists and songwriters and 800 labels and publishing companies. Through Kobalt technology and services, creators benefit from more money, better reporting, and maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. With the award-winning Kobalt desktop portal and app, Kobalt clients get real-time insight into their work and activity
What does a Content Operations Coordinat: QC audio release submissions in accordance with partner metadata specifications.
Provide structured feedback for client-facing teams when releases fail checks.
Carry out metadata updates as required across the AWAL catalogue.
Work with internal teams to continually review and optimise content ingestion processes with a view to scale.
Contribute to Content Operations documentation, including metadata style guide.
What Skills and Experience are we lookin: Experience in a Content Operations, Digital Supply Chain or similar role at a label or distributor.
Good knowledge of music metadata in a digital distribution context, particularly the iTunes and Spotify style guides.
Experience working with content management systems (iTunes Connect and Spotify preferred).
Passion for a range of musical genres with bonus points for Classical or Bollywood knowledge.

Title: Senior Copyright Assistant (UK)
Description: Joining the Publishing Copyright department in our London office and being responsible for our Sub-Publisher relationships, this newly created Senior Copyright Assistant position will primarily assist in correctly registering works and agreements at many of our Sub-Publishers in varying territories, as well as carrying out all related tasks to maintain and manage our catalogue to highest standards, including working with royalties. The successful candidate will need to have excellent communication skills when dealing with general enquiries from internal and external sources and can deal with pressure but remain optimistic and persistent to complete tasks accurately and efficiently.Continue to article...BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We're currently 600 people based across 10 offices and we're still growing.WHAT DOES A SENIOR COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT WORKING WITH SUB-PUBLISHERS AT KOBALT DO?Ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at sub-publishersDealing with general enquiries regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreementsHandling song disputes and duplicate claimsProcessing new works and agreements as the company acquires rights to new cataloguesProcessing royalty statements received from sub-publishers in different territoriesAnalysing the royalties received to identify possible underpayment or delayed payment, and working with the sub-publishers to resolve these issuesYou may be asked to take on extra responsibilities 3 please be aware that the above list is not exhaustiveWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Excellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to audience and tailor communication effectively to different cultures and international nuancesHigh level of written and spoken EnglishTrack-record of excellent organization and prioritisation skillsStrong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-setAnalytical skills as well as experience working with ExcelAble to work independently and manage time and workload effectivelyPrevious admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable. Most important is that you are ambitious and flexible and can adapt to a growing and changing department!IMPORTANT NOTICEApplicants must be eligible to work in the United Kingdom. Verification checks will be conducted during the interview process and a full DBS check on acceptance of offer. Kobalt is an equal opportunity employer encouraging diversity in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or any other status or characteristic protected by law.

BACKGROUND: Founded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.
WHAT DOES A SENIOR COPYRIGHT ASSISTANT W: Ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at sub-publishers
Dealing with general enquiries regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreements
Handling song disputes and duplicate claims
Processing new works and agreements as the company acquires rights to new catalogues
Processing royalty statements received from sub-publishers in different territories
Analysing the royalties received to identify possible underpayment or delayed payment, and working with the sub-publishers to resolve these issues
You may be asked to take on extra responsibilities 3 please be aware that the above list is not exhaustive
WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKIN: Excellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to audience and tailor communication effectively to different cultures and international nuances
High level of written and spoken English
Track-record of excellent organization and prioritisation skills
Strong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-set
Analytical skills as well as experience working with Excel
Able to work independently and manage time and workload effectively

Previous admin and copyright experience in a similar role as well as a good understanding of music publishing is highly desirable. Most important is that you are ambitious and flexible and can adapt to a growing and changing department!
IMPORTANT NOTICE: Applicants must be eligible to work in the United Kingdom. Verification checks will be conducted during the interview process and a full DBS check on acceptance of offer. Kobalt is an equal opportunity employer encouraging diversity in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or any other status or characteristic protected by law.

Title: Digital Account Manager, Streaming (US/SE)
Description: At Epidemic Sound we are reinventing the music industry. Our carefully curated music library, with over 30,000 tracks, is tailored for storytellers no matter what their story is. Countless customers around the world, from broadcasters, productions companies and YouTubers rely on our tracks to help them tell their stories.Continue to article...Epidemic Sound’s music is heard in hundreds of thousands of videos on online video platforms such as YouTube, Facebook, Twitch, and Instagram, and our music is streamed by million fans around the world on services such as Spotify and Apple Music. Our HQ is located in Stockholm with offices in NYC, LA, Hamburg, Amsterdam, and Madrid. We are growing fast, we have lots of fun, and we are transforming the music industry.We are now looking for a for a Digital Account Manager with strong interpersonal and organizational skills to manage our relationships with the digital platforms. Streaming is one of Epidemic’s fastest growing business areas. If you enjoy a fast-paced environment with lots of potential to shape our business, this is the position for you.Job descriptionYour main focus will be to manage day-to-day relationships with digital music streaming platforms to ensure close relationships are maintained. You will pitch and promote our releases to Spotify, Apple, Amazon, Fuga, Deezer, Tidal and similar services. You will work closely with our team of music curators, A&Rs, artists/musicians and the marketing team with the aim of ensuring maximum exposure and impact of our releases.A few examples of tasks included in the role;Prepare pitch-material and updates of our releases for submission to digital music streaming platformsDrive strategy pitches, best practice and processes with retail accounts to ensure our repertoire receives maximum promotion and exposureInfluence the release schedule to ensure optimized pitching routines are establishedCreate metrics and case studies around content performance and visibility, impact of marketing initiatives and retail response to music promotionProvide best practice for release planning and schedulingCommunicate streaming, playlisting and digital sales results throughout the organizationIdentify and execute high-revenue music streaming opportunitiesIdentify and approach strategic music streaming platforms and negotiate new deals to ensure our catalogue is widely available around the worldThis is a full-time position, preferably based at our HQ in Stockholm or one of our offices in New York or Los Angeles. You’ll be reporting to the Streaming Manager, and we would like you to start as soon as possible.What are we looking for?In order for you to thrive and succeed in this role, we believe that you have minimum 2 years of music industry experience, preferably at a record label or digital music service within retail marketing and/or digital account management. You also have strong knowledge of leading digital music platforms (Spotify, Apple, Amazon, Deezer, YouTube etc) and good understanding of sales strategy and how it can be used to strengthen a retail marketing campaign. We want you to have experience of building and maintaining client relationships and have great interpersonal skills. Your written & verbal communication skills in English are excellent.As a person you are friendly, enthusiastic and results-oriented. You pay excellent attention to details and have strong organizational skills with the ability to initiate and drive improvements. You are great at prioritizing and work effectively under pressure, as well as maintaining a professional manner.

hiring and developing the best talent, and creating an environment which motivates people to thrive in their expertise and deliver aggressive resultsDevelop and execute customer acquisition and churn management strategies for a subscription music service.Develop a clear customer segmentation and brand messaging strategy for Amazon Music.Drive customer engagement through targeted marketing and merchandising to convert existing Prime subscribers into active Prime Music customers, with increased listening frequency.Create measurable, scalable and innovative marketing campaigns 3 both offline and online 3 to meet goals around new customer acquisition.Develop an organizational structure that aligns the talent to the strategic objectives for the group. This leader will be responsible for continuing to build an extremely high caliber team.Establish strong industry relationships with key executives at the record labels, distribution companies and artist management.Reporting directly into Director, Amazon Music, and this role will be located in Tokyo, Japan.BASIC QUALIFICATIONSUniversity degree minimum; MBA or further engineering, marketing or analytical education will be advantageous.The ideal candidate will have demonstrated the ability to identify and solve ambiguous problems. Title: Work Entry Assistant (UK)
Description: Kobalt Music Publishing is looking for a Work Entry Assistant to be based in the Kobalt office in London, and to work within the Copyright Department as a song processor.Continue to article...The primary roles of a copyright department are registering claims to songs at the relevant collection societies globally and processing the royalties which result from the exploitation of those songs. This enables us to collect and distribute royalties due to the copyright owners. But before all of this can be done, we need to import into our system the songs we receive from our clients. This is the all-important task of our department, and accuracy is absolutely key as all subsequent tasks rely on the accuracy and efficiency with which this task is performed.The successful candidate will have a well-supported imitation and training process.Founded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.WHAT DOES A WORK ENTRY ASSISTANT AT KOBALT DO?To ensure that all works delivered to Kobalt Music Publishing are entered with 100% accuracy and within the deadlinesDaily input of a substantial amount of data entryBeing able to comply with complex procedures and rules of work entryTo have an impeccable knowledge of all work entry issues and able to help and advice members of the copyright department regarding work entry mattersTo liaise in a professional way with clients and colleagues regarding issues or queriesTo maintain learning and professional development, including new technical and copyright developmentsWhere required, to be proactive, foresee issues and suggest improvements in the system and procedureWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR? Previous copyright experience, a good understanding of music publishing, or a music-related degree, is highly desirableGood communication skills are very important: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally, we are looking for someone who is able to adapt communication to audienceCreative Problem Solving: experience in creating and implementing new processesDecisiveness: effective and timely decisions, but perceives the impact and implications of decisionsPerson with resilience and tenacity: someone who effectively deals with pressure but remains optimistic and persistent to enter works accurately and efficientlyTrack record of good organization and prioritisation skillsHigh level of written and spoken EnglishHigh level of numeracyStrong attention to detail, accuracy and a detail-driven mind-set

Title: Senior Director, Label Management (UK)
Description: We’re currently working with an incredible and growing roster of emerging talent and already established labels from all over the world. We’re looking for someone to be responsible for the day to day management of our UK AWAL Label Management team, operating out of the London office. The role also works closely with our North America label management team and provides support to label managers in Europe and Australia. You’ll be looking after a small roster of the higher profile clients while also managing the label managers.Continue to article...It’s a very exciting time for the company as a whole and particularly within the label management team where we’re really proud to be looking after so many of the hottest records being released right now. At AWAL we’re doing things differently and are well placed to make the most of the changing face of the industry. You role leading the growing team will be very exciting challenge.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2004, AWAL is the trusted partner of independent artists and labels globally, from new artists at the beginning of their career to global successes. With local offices across the world, AWAL offers services such as distribution, YouTube monetization, playlist promotion, member support from industry experts, and real-time data analytics through the AWAL Portal and App. Our client roster includes both established and emerging artists such as Moby, Placebo, Chip, Gabrielle Aplin, Todd Terje, Tom Misch, R3hab and Vérité, and labels including B3SCI, Chrysalis Records, Marathon Artists, Kid Coconut, Independiente and mau5trap.At Kobalt, we’re currently over 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re expecting to grow. That’s where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!Main job focusLeading and supporting the UK label management team to develop their client-facing skills and industry knowledge to ensure that we are providing excellent service to our evolving client base as we scaleClose collaboration with our A&R and marketing teams on identifying and developing priority artists and releases as they upscale towards full label services and project management.Working closely with the LA-based SVP Client Development and the North America Label Management team to build a strong, unified global team, and developing and driving best practices for serving our client base and training new label managers.ResponsibilitiesProvide leadership and development for the UK AWAL label managers, with particular focus on improving campaign and marketing support, setting excellent standards for artist /client communication and maintain excellent client service levels across all client types and tiers.Provide strong support to SVP, Operations and SVP, Client Development and the global label management team in developing, standardising and implementing best practices and consistent global processes.Manage a roster of priority clients – both independent artists and labels. Support these clients with distribution and release planning advice and best practices.Deliver ongoing training and development for team members to continually improve the skills and knowledge of the team to support client campaign and marketing needs.Support UK label management team with onboarding of new clients, with particular focus on growing area of label/catalogue clientsSupport incoming Label and B2B business, including servicing needs around physical distribution and catalogue management as required.Regular client roster audits to ensure team focus is on current priority clients.Provide training to new UK Label Managers as the team expands and oversee hiring of new label managers as we scale.Work in conjunction with A&R teams on identifying AWAL clients to upstream to label services, and help to manage that process seamlessly.Provide reporting to AWAL senior management as requiredRepresent AWAL at artist showcases & gigs, plus relevant industry conferences and events.Person specificationPassionate about music. With minimum 5 years music industry experience within a progressive label or distributor, in a label management or product management capacityExcellent working knowledge of digital music distribution, supply chain and DSP processesExcellent understanding of the needs of artists and labels for building and maximising their release campaignsProven experience in team leadership and developing/nurturing team membersGood understanding of physical supply chain and distributionStrong focus on developing processes, best practices and training toolsExcellent interpersonal skills and ability to communicate effectively across all levels of the organisationStrong strategic thinking to help drive growth and excellence in our team and global label management areaFlexible and adaptable approach to growing a team and adapting processes as the business continues to grow and client-base evolves rapidlyKnowledge of international industry landscape highly desirableSolid understanding/experience of digital music marketing and promotion would be an advantageThe role will involve daily working with international based contacts, especially North America – some international travel will be required in the course of this role, as is flexibility for out of hours working.

With local offices across the world, AWAL offers services such as distribution, YouTube monetization, playlist promotion, member support from industry experts, and real-time data analytics through the AWAL Portal and App. Our client roster includes both established and emerging artists such as Moby, Placebo, Chip, Gabrielle Aplin, Todd Terje, Tom Misch, R3hab and Vérité, and labels including B3SCI, Chrysalis Records, Marathon Artists, Kid Coconut, Independiente and mau5trap.

Excellent interpersonal skills and ability to communicate effectively across all levels of the organisation

Solid understanding/experience of digital music marketing and promotion would be an advantage

Title: Executive Director (US)
Description: The Clyde Theatre, located in Fort Wayne, IN has recently opened its doors to nationally recognized bands, artists and tours.Continue to article...We have built an amazing team that is passionate about providing the nation’s best concert experience and we’re looking for an experienced leader to serve as the Executive Director of this incredible venue and team.Powered by Sweetwater Sound, the nation’s #1 online music retailer of pro audio and music instruments, the Clyde Theatre was formerly a 1950’s art deco themed movie theater.It has now been now transformed into a state of the art music venue with world class sound and lighting systems and amenities.With a total renovation cost of $9 million dollars, there wasn’t a single corner that was cut to make sure guests of The Clyde receive the best concert experience possible.World-renowned acoustician, Russ Berger (Russ Berger Design Group) was brought in to provide the venue with the industry’s best acoustics. Title: Artist Marketing Manager (UK)
Description: The Orchard has an immediate opening for the position of Artist Marketing Manager, reporting into the Director of Artist Marketing. Continue to article...Working alongside the Director of Artist Marketing the Artist Marketing Manager will effectively and creatively manage all aspects of marketing campaigns for assigned Orchard artists as approved artist services campaigns.The candidate will manage all marketing activity in line with commercial objectives and approved budget. Build and maintain strong relationships with artist management, key internal departments and external agencies/suppliers.Supporting Director on preparation of campaign plans for assigned artists in line with best marketing and planning processes, as well as managing own campaigns as assigned.Effectively manage marketing spend in line with approved budget and project KPIs.Generating and supervising integrated, cost effective and profitable D2C campaigns.Ensure timely delivery of all aspects of the marketing campaign in line with core project and approval route milestones.Expedite ‘best practise’ in all aspects of campaign execution. Timely delivery and being on budget are as important as creativity.Keep aware of the trends and opportunities that provide us with a chance to market the artist effectively.Work effectively to deliver an overall campaign that reflects the vision and goals of the artist, management or label alongside the strategy of the agreed marketing plan.Communicating with management and artists on a regular basis to address concerns, manage expectations and generally maintain a good working relationship.Work cross functionally with other departments (such as label management, interactive marketing, retail marketing, physical, international, sync) to ensure flow of information throughout the campaign and to provide new business opportunities.Supply physical and digital sales teams with all materials necessary for selling releases into retail with maximum impact.Sharing information around acts with press & promotion departments so that each side has necessary tools for maximising impact across media.As part of the team, maximise possibilities for international release through careful co-ordination of plan with international department.Be aware of changes and developments in the music market, with particular emphasis on competitor activity.Represent The Orchard in meetings with existing and potential artists & artist management.Represent The Orchard, including attending gigs and spreading the word.Qualifications / ExperienceExperience within a label or artist management dealing with frontline release campaignsKnowledge and experience of artist and label services including campaign management, budget management and physical distribution.Excellent knowledge of the digital space, including music retail, analytics and social media, with an ability to adapt to a constantly changing landscape.Highly advanced organizational skills with the ability to multi-task and prioritise to tight deadlines.Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to speak effectively across all levels of business, both internally and externally.Resourceful, accountable, and takes initiative – an expert troubleshooterPassionate about independent music and artist developmentThe Orchard is a pioneering music and video distribution company operating in more than 35 global markets. We provide innovative and comprehensive sales and marketing solutions for content owners working with such diverse artists such as Skepta, The Cribs, Jason Isbell, BTS and Run The Jewels. With an industry-leading technology platform, The Orchard’s creative and tailored approach streamlines its clients’ business complexity while amplifying reach and revenue across hundreds of digital and physical outlets around the world. The Orchard was founded in 1997 to foster independence and creativity in the music industry. For further information, please visit www.theorchard.com.

Qualifications / Experience: Experience within a label or artist management dealing with frontline release campaigns
Knowledge and experience of artist and label services including campaign management, budget management and physical distribution.
Excellent knowledge of the digital space, including music retail, analytics and social media, with an ability to adapt to a constantly changing landscape.
Highly advanced organizational skills with the ability to multi-task and prioritise to tight deadlines.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to speak effectively across all levels of business, both internally and externally.
Resourceful, accountable, and takes initiative , an expert troubleshooter
Passionate about independent music and artist development

Title: Project Manager (UK)
Description: Secretly Group is looking for a motivated, ambitious and enthusiastic Project Manager to join its London team. 2-4 years of music industry experience are essential. The candidate must have a passion for music, be keen to contribute creative ideas to our global marketing strategy, and an ability to communicate effectively with artists and managers.Continue to article...A strong knowledge of electronic-orientated music is a bonus. Secretly Group is made up of the Dead Oceans, Jagjaguwar and Secretly Canadian record labels, headquartered in Bloomington, Indiana, USA. We release music by artists such as Bon Iver, Angel Olsen, Slowdive, Shame, Whitney, and serpentwithfeet.Principle ResponsibilitiesManaging album campaigns across UK and Europe, working closely with a US PM to develop global campaignPlanning and coordinating European promo and promo tripsWorking with the marketing team to create European wide plansManaging and overseeing project budgetsInitiating and leading regular international project meetingsUsing stats and analysis to help shape marketing campaignsCoordinating press, radio, TV, retail and digital marketing teams.Helping to coordinate assets across a campaign including music videos, remixes, live sessions, photoshoots, digital assets.Liaising with live agents, promoters and artist managers on a live plotCollating label copy and data for European based artistsLiaising with local staff and distributors in European territories on retail and promotional stock requirementsLiaising with our digital teams and digital retailersSome international travel is required.The role is full-time, 40 hours per week, and will require some out-of-hours activity, such as attendance at shows, accompanying artists on promotional activities etc.Required SkillsExcellent communication and organizational skills are essential.Must possess strong problem solving and analytical skills.Must be able to demonstrate independent thought and action.Ability to set priorities and meet deadlines.Effectively manage multiple concurrent projects.Background of 2-4 years in music industry.**We’ll be accepting CV’s until 27 August, 2018.Contact: To apply or to recommend a qualified candidate send email directly to Jessie LaJoie, Secretly’s HR Director at applications@secretlyservices.com

Principle Responsibilities: Managing album campaigns across UK and Europe, working closely with a US PM to develop global campaign
Planning and coordinating European promo and promo trips
Working with the marketing team to create European wide plans
Managing and overseeing project budgets
Initiating and leading regular international project meetings
Using stats and analysis to help shape marketing campaigns
Coordinating press, radio, TV, retail and digital marketing teams.
Helping to coordinate assets across a campaign including music videos, remixes, live sessions, photoshoots, digital assets.
Liaising with live agents, promoters and artist managers on a live plot
Collating label copy and data for European based artists
Liaising with local staff and distributors in European territories on retail and promotional stock requirements
Liaising with our digital teams and digital retailers
Some international travel is required.
The role is full-time, 40 hours per week, and will require some out-of-hours activity, such as attendance at shows, accompanying artists on promotional activities etc.
Required Skills: Excellent communication and organizational skills are essential.
Must possess strong problem solving and analytical skills.
Must be able to demonstrate independent thought and action.
Ability to set priorities and meet deadlines.
Effectively manage multiple concurrent projects.
Background of 2-4 years in music industry.

Title: Product Manager, Discovery (DE)
Description: What’s next in music happens on SoundCloud first. As the worlds largest open audio platform, SoundCloud is powered by a connected community of creators, listeners and curators who share, discover and influence whats new, now and next in music and audio.Continue to article...Were looking for a Product Manager, Discovery to join our team in Berlin. Working directly with a world-class engineering team, youll develop and own the roadmap for content discovery on SoundCloud. Making our catalogue of over 180 Million tracks from over 30 million artists feel like its personalised for every user on SoundCloud is no small feat and will require a strong vision and tight collaboration with many different domain experts to deliver and go to market with a variety of algorithmic and editorial solutions.Key responsibilitiesCommunicate product vision and strategy for your product internally and externallyInterpret and refine strategic business goals into actions towards product changesUse various data sources to inform your decisionsPlan for and perform post-release analysis to measure customer and business impactPrioritize features, improvements and bugs based on customer impact and business goalsDetermine best areas of improvement, taking into considering implementation time, return on investment and trade offsRequired skills and experienceBachelor’s degree or work experience in a relevant field with 2 years of product management and/or business experience, experience with agile methodology and ideally distributed teams. Proven record of delivering successful products & servicesFamiliarity with best practices for content discovery, recommendation systems, and editorial strategies is a huge plusStrong analytical and quantitative skills; ability to use hard data and metrics to back up assumptions, recommendations, and drive actionsExtremely thorough, detail-oriented and organized with ability to manage multiple parallel projectsOutstanding verbal and written communication, as well as a high level of comfort leading workshops and presentations with a diverse group of stakeholders and teams.Diversity at SoundCloudSoundCloud is for everyone. Diversity and open expression are fundamental to our organization; they help us build a social platform and global community where anyone can create, discover, and share sounds. We acknowledge the challenges in our industry, and strive to develop an inclusive culture where everyone can contribute.

Key responsibilities: Communicate product vision and strategy for your product internally and externally
Interpret and refine strategic business goals into actions towards product changes
Use various data sources to inform your decisions
Plan for and perform post-release analysis to measure customer and business impact
Prioritize features, improvements and bugs based on customer impact and business goals
Determine best areas of improvement, taking into considering implementation time, return on investment and trade offs
Required skills and experience: Bachelor’s degree or work experience in a relevant field with 2 years of product management and/or business experience, experience with agile methodology and ideally distributed teams. Proven record of delivering successful products & services
Familiarity with best practices for content discovery, recommendation systems, and editorial strategies is a huge plus
Strong analytical and quantitative skills; ability to use hard data and metrics to back up assumptions, recommendations, and drive actions
Extremely thorough, detail-oriented and organized with ability to manage multiple parallel projects
Outstanding verbal and written communication, as well as a high level of comfort leading workshops and presentations with a diverse group of stakeholders and teams.
Diversity at SoundCloud: SoundCloud is for everyone. Diversity and open expression are fundamental to our organization; they help us build a social platform and global community where anyone can create, discover, and share sounds. We acknowledge the challenges in our industry, and strive to develop an inclusive culture where everyone can contribute.

Title: Senior Communications Manager (US)
Description: What’s next in music happens on SoundCloud first. As the world’s largest open audio platform, SoundCloud is powered by a connected community of creators, listeners and curators who share, discover and influence what’s new, now and next in music and audio.Continue to article...We are currently seeking a Senior Communications Manager to lead our consumer-focused communications efforts. The role will be based in SoundCloud’s New York office, and will be an integral member of the SoundCloud Communications team.In this role, you’ll support the development and execution of communications strategies that proactively tout SoundCloud’s unique platform and its vibrant community of creators and curators to consumer-focused media across culture, music, entertainment and lifestyle. This will include everything from crafting press materials such as press releases, pitches, and briefing documents, to working closely with cross-functional teams in different time zones to identify, shape and tell stories that demonstrate the creative vibrancy and cultural impact the SoundCloud community of creators and music fans are having in the world.Working closely with the Manager, Communications (Business and Trade), you’ll determine the best speaking engagements, profiles, and Op-Eds to successfully position SoundCloud as thought leaders in the music industry.About youYou are passionate about SoundCloud, and you know the platform inside and out.You love to pitch — you have tons of media contacts and are on a first name basis with top consumer-focused writers who cover culture, entertainment, lifestyle and music.You are deeply passionate and knowledgeable about the music landscape, from new artists that are emerging, to the events and moments that matter most. You live and breathe music and culture.You are flexible, adaptable and don’t mind moving quickly to keep up with the demands of busy, yet exciting work environment.You are a problem solver, resourceful and able to work independently, but also a strong collaborator and team player.You might have been a copywriter in your past life, and your attention to detail and writing skills are impeccable.Experience in a global or international role a plus.Big points if you have experience working with influencers too.Requirements5-7 years experience in a consumer-PR focused role, ideally within a content platform, music, culture or entertainment company with demonstrated success securing media coverage across various platforms (tv, digital, print, web, radio).Diversity at SoundCloud:“SoundCloud is for everyone. Diversity and open expression are fundamental to our organization; they help us build a social platform and global community where anyone can create, discover, and share sounds. We acknowledge the challenges in our industry, and strive to develop an inclusive culture where everyone can contribute”.

About you: You are passionate about SoundCloud, and you know the platform inside and out.
You love to pitch — you have tons of media contacts and are on a first name basis with top consumer-focused writers who cover culture, entertainment, lifestyle and music.
You are deeply passionate and knowledgeable about the music landscape, from new artists that are emerging, to the events and moments that matter most. You live and breathe music and culture.
You are flexible, adaptable and don’t mind moving quickly to keep up with the demands of busy, yet exciting work environment.
You are a problem solver, resourceful and able to work independently, but also a strong collaborator and team player.
You might have been a copywriter in your past life, and your attention to detail and writing skills are impeccable.
Experience in a global or international role a plus.
Big points if you have experience working with influencers too.
Requirements: 5-7 years experience in a consumer-PR focused role, ideally within a content platform, music, culture or entertainment company with demonstrated success securing media coverage across various platforms (tv, digital, print, web, radio).
Diversity at SoundCloud:: “SoundCloud is for everyone. Diversity and open expression are fundamental to our organization; they help us build a social platform and global community where anyone can create, discover, and share sounds. We acknowledge the challenges in our industry, and strive to develop an inclusive culture where everyone can contribute”.

Title: Release Management Coordinator (UK)
Description: As part of Kobalt’s significant expansion of AWAL – Kobalt’s recorded-music division, the Coordinator will support the growing global Release Management team by ensuring accurate and timely build of all AWAL Recordings releases for digital and physical distribution.Continue to article...Reporting to the Senior Director of Release Management, the Coordinator will assist all our Release Managers based in London, New York & Stockholm with all aspects of product build, release set up and scheduling to ensure all AWAL products meet release date.If you’re looking to build a career in a music and tech focussed company, this role could be just right for you.Maybe you have some experience of music metadata, or have even worked directly within a supply chain in the music industry?You also have to have laser sharp attention to detail, take real pride in what you do and have great interpersonal skills and ability to cope with a high-pressure/fast-paced environment but remain optimistic and persistent to make things happen.There’s a lot of cross-checking of info, carrying out repetitive, yet totally essential tasks. So, don’t apply for this role if you’re not ready to get your head down and focus or don’t like spreadsheets or tech!Working at Kobalt, you will help build the music services company of tomorrow, to empower creators in today’s complex and change the music landscape – ideal for someone with an incredible drive, enthusiasm & passion for music!What would you do as a Release Management Coordinator?Assisting with collation, management and storage of metadataSetting up video releases & delivering to Ops teamEntering relevant data and assets into Kobalt internal systemsChecking technical aspects of assets received from clientsAssisting with registration of tracks and products for Performance & BroadcastAssisting with co-ordination of physical format productionCollating physical manufacture costsAssisting with stock control management, stock orders, splits and shippingCreating POs for stock deliveries, from both storage facility and manufacturersCoordinating CD promo manufacture, including checking artwork and creditsManagement of our promo stock cupboardAssisting with release schedule maintenance and distributionChecking of chart registrationsMinute taking in meetingsYou will ideally have…excellent written and verbal communication skills with highly proficient spoken and written English…a tech-savvy mind-set, detail driven with working knowledge of Excel & Google Sheets with high level of accuracy…ability to concentrate on tasks for a long period of time following strict industry guidelines…strong organisational and prioritisation skills – carefully plans and structures their work load but can be flexible as priorities frequently changeApplicants must be eligible to work in the United Kingdom. Verification checks will be conducted during the interview process and a full DBS check on acceptance of offer.Kobalt is an equal opportunity employer encouraging diversity in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or any other status or characteristic protected by law.

Title: Trainee Financial Accountant (UK)
Description: The MPA Group of Companies (MPA, MCPS and PMLL) is seeking a trainee financial accountant to assist the Director of Operations with all of the finance functions across the three companies as well as providing assistance with other finance related projects. Continue to article...This is a fantastic opportunity to work in a forward-thinking music company and would suit a qualified accounting technician (AAT or similar) who is studying for a professional accountancy qualification and looking to further their development in an industry specific environment. An interest in music and the developing technologies surrounding the music business is a must.Responsibilities will include:Finance functions including invoicing, processing petty cash and credit card expensesPreparation of monthly management accounts and MPR reports across the groupAssisting with the preparation of annual budgets for the groupAnalysis of income, expenditure and statementsManaging MPA membership renewal process and subscription payments Assist with PMLL distributions to members Assist with the preparation of Finance, Audit & Risk Committee papersDealing with both internal and external customer queries including reconciliation of accounts, production of statements, etc. to the highest level of service.The successful candidate will be a dynamic, flexible individual with the intellectual agility to join the dots between the various issues affecting the MPA Group. They will have experience and knowledge of online banking systems and cash payment methods, as well as the preparation of management accounts and budgeting / reforecasting. They will have meticulous attention to detail and the ability to evaluate information to identify potential errors, as well as the foresight to see opportunity beyond immediate tasks and identify more efficient ways of working.  Applicants should be motivated to work individually, exercising initiative and progressing matters independently, as well as collaboratively in a team.Application Process: To apply please send your CV together with a covering letter, indicating how you fulfil these requirements to Raymi Haile – raymi.haile@mpagroup.com. Please also provide your salary expectations. Please note you must have the right to work in the UK. We are unable to consider applicants who do not have the right to work in the UK. All applications to jobs at the Music Publishers Association are subject to the MPA’s data privacy notice for applicants, which is available here.
