Title: Director, Digital Marketing (US)
Description: BMG is a new kind of music company. Our mission is clear; to help artists and songwriters make the very most of their songs and recordings in the digital age. That’s why we offer the best creative support, provide the best access to platforms and work hard to maximize the value of each and every copyright, ensuring all our clients receive all payments promptly and accurately.Continue to article...We are not a technology company – though we use the best technology. We are not just a music publisher or a record label – although we offer both services. BMG is a company with service, transparency and fairness at its heart and a commitment to being the best international music company for songwriters, artists and everyone who works with us.The RoleBMG Recorded Music is a modern and global industry leader successfully investing in a number of today’s top new and established artists across multiple genres. We strive to CREATE high value creation for our artists in an extremely competitive environment that sets BMG apart from other market players.In line with our unique and focused approach to artist marketing, we are looking for a passionate and highly qualified individual to lead our digital marketing efforts with the goal to drive streaming, downloads, physical, and other non-traditional sources of income for BMG artists.What You’ll Be DoingLead the digital marketing dept by planning and fully executing comprehensive and innovative campaigns across key digital platforms to drive revenue on artist releases.Create and develop digital marketing timelines and campaigns around artist releases including singles, albums, and music videos.Set goals, define strategy, execute accordingly, and manage the digital marketing team to be held accountable for all campaign efforts.Cultivate and foster strategic partnerships with major social media platforms (Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, Snapchat, Facebook, etc…)Possess The ability to lead, develop, and support junior members of the digital marketing team to ensure campaign goals are met.Work closely with the creative content team making The Most effective use of Key digital assets.Budget, activate, and manage online advertising campaigns.Build and develop promotional opportunities across artist and 3rd party social platforms to optimize fan engagement.Develop direct relationships and communication channels with artists and management teams to fully optimize marketing campaigns.Understand the streaming landscape and how creative digital marketing efforts can effectively support and drive streaming initiatives.Analyze digital marketing campaigns across social and other online platforms, to the Internal BMG marketing/promotion/Executive teams, and others as needed; ensure audience development and growth with measurables.Build and organize calendar system to meet deadlines for social posts around single, album launch, video premieres, etc…Must Haves:5 Years at a record label, artist management firm, or 3rd party company in digital marketing with strong managerial experience leading a team.Candidates should demonstrate passion, creativity, leadership, a high organizational skill set, great communication skills, problem solving ability, and also able to multi-task and prioritize in a fast paced environment with a high volume of releases.Strong existing relationships at key digital platforms (Instagram, Twitter, Facebook, Giphy, Shazam, Musical.ly, Tumblr, etc..)Demonstrate a deep knowledge and experience in strategizing anD executing comprehensive digital marketing campaigns, as well as pitch proposals for prospective artist signings. (Please be prepared to discuss past campaigns/examples you have worked on in previous position)You have an in-depth understanding of today’s music streaming landscape and have a clear understanding how it intersects with social media and other forms of digital marketing with the goal of driving revenue for our artists.You have a high level of knowledge of all social platforms and current best practices for social media marketing.Encompass a high level of knowledge of the leading digital advertising platforms and the various ad units available. You know how to create, track, and analyze the performance of a campaign.Previous Experience in influencer marketing. Pre-existing relationships in this area are a plus.You have a strong passion for new technology and keeping up with the latest digital trends with the goal of finding new ways to market music and connect artists with their fans.Experience in creating and managing budgets for the departmentDemonstrate that you have the ability to build, lead, motivate, and inspire your team.What’s In It For YouRobust medical/dental/vision packagesTwo weeks’ vacation in year one; three weeks’ vacation in year two$500 fitness reimbursementDiverse possibilities of personal and professional development including trainings and access to Lynda.comDollar for dollar match of up to 6% into 401(k) after your first year with the companyA fun and respectful community of people with a passion for music

The Role: BMG Recorded Music is a modern and global industry leader successfully investing in a number of today’s top new and established artists across multiple genres. We strive to CREATE high value creation for our artists in an extremely competitive environment that sets BMG apart from other market players.
What You\u2019ll Be Doing: Lead the digital marketing dept by planning and fully executing comprehensive and innovative campaigns across key digital platforms to drive revenue on artist releases.
Create and develop digital marketing timelines and campaigns around artist releases including singles, albums, and music videos.
Set goals, define strategy, execute accordingly, and manage the digital marketing team to be held accountable for all campaign efforts.
Cultivate and foster strategic partnerships with major social media platforms (Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, Snapchat, Facebook, etc…)
Possess The ability to lead, develop, and support junior members of the digital marketing team to ensure campaign goals are met.

Build and develop promotional opportunities across artist and 3rd party social platforms to optimize fan engagement.
Develop direct relationships and communication channels with artists and management teams to fully optimize marketing campaigns.
Understand the streaming landscape and how creative digital marketing efforts can effectively support and drive streaming initiatives.

Candidates should demonstrate passion, creativity, leadership, a high organizational skill set, great communication skills, problem solving ability, and also able to multi-task and prioritize in a fast paced environment with a high volume of releases.
Strong existing relationships at key digital platforms (Instagram, Twitter, Facebook, Giphy, Shazam, Musical.ly, Tumblr, etc..)

Demonstrate a deep knowledge and experience in strategizing anD executing comprehensive digital marketing campaigns, as well as pitch proposals for prospective artist signings. (Please be prepared to discuss past campaigns/examples you have worked on in previous position)

You have an in-depth understanding of today’s music streaming landscape and have a clear understanding how it intersects with social media and other forms of digital marketing with the goal of driving revenue for our artists.
You have a high level of knowledge of all social platforms and current best practices for social media marketing.
Encompass a high level of knowledge of the leading digital advertising platforms and the various ad units available. You know how to create, track, and analyze the performance of a campaign.
Previous Experience in influencer marketing. Pre-existing relationships in this area are a plus.
You have a strong passion for new technology and keeping up with the latest digital trends with the goal of finding new ways to market music and connect artists with their fans.
Experience in creating and managing budgets for the department
Demonstrate that you have the ability to build, lead, motivate, and inspire your team.

A fun and respectful community of people with a passion for music

Title: Client Manager, Hip Hop (US)
Description: Symphonic Distribution is a 100% independent music distribution and marketing company with headquarters in Tampa, FL. We focus on distributing electronic, hip-hop, and urban music in general. We pride ourselves in finding the newest, most talented up-and-coming bands, producers, rappers, managers, and labels with promise of developing long careers.Continue to article...We are currently looking for a full time Client Manager (Hip Hop) to join our team!If you have worked at a label, distributor, publisher, or management company, and have had continued success developing emerging artists or labels; if you are deeply rooted in the hip-hop community in New York, and can maintain a healthy balance of the job that must be done during the day with the connection to the artists and music that happens at night, then this job may be for you!Reporting to the Chief Client Officer and based in New York City, the Client Manager (Hip-Hop) will manage relationships with Symphonic’s artist and label clients, spearheading key signings, priority release development, and will act as the primary go-to for a key segment of our client base.Responsibilities //Nurture healthy and productive relationships with prospective and existing artist and label clients.Collaborate with Symphonic’s global team to plan and implement form-fitting marketing strategies.Manage the daily logistics of release management (metadata standards, production, ingestion, take-downs, etc.)Plan, manage, and analyze the financial success of our client relationships.Anticipate problems and implement communication and tactical strategies to solve them.Requirements //High school diploma or GED. Bachelor’s degree in music or business-related field preferred.Must have a deep love and knowledge of many sub-genres within the Hip-Hop genre.Must 2 years working in a key client relationship role at a record label, distributor, management company, or publisher, or agency.Must have At least a foundational understanding of the intricacies of streaming music retailers, metadata, and pricing models.Must have exceptional written and verbal communication skills with experience giving presentations.Must have very strong analytical and quantitative skills with a demonstrated ability to explain complicated financial and technical matters to artistsMust have proficiency using Google’s Suite of productivity applications.Must be extremely detail oriented with excellent organizational skills.Must have strong collaboration and influencing skills, with the ability to successfully partner with multiple stakeholders.Must be able to go out and see shows and meet with clients at night, sometimes multiple times a week.Must be able to travel on a regular basis (potentially 25% of the time)About Symphonic Distribution //Symphonic Distribution is a 100% independent music distribution and marketing company founded by music producer Jorge Brea in 2006. Headquartered in downtown Tampa, FL with offices in major music markets throughout the world, Symphonic provides thousands of new and established record labels, managers and individual artists with global digital music distribution to hundreds of retail and streaming platforms, playlist pitching, release promotion, and a comprehensive suite of label services. Symphonic enables content creators to fully maximize their music’s revenue and grow their global audience.

Responsibilities //: Nurture healthy and productive relationships with prospective and existing artist and label clients.
Collaborate with Symphonic’s global team to plan and implement form-fitting marketing strategies.

Who are we looking for?: We are seeking an Artist Project and A&R Coordinator to work in the music / A&R team, being based in our London office. This will be a one-year fixed term contract to cover maternity leave.

Preparing insight, trend and data reports on commercial album / artist sales performance

Understanding of the commercial label and publisher world with broad knowledge of latest trends

Highly organised and happy working and problem solving in an environment where internal timelines are often affected by aspects of the creative process and working with 3rd party composers and musicians
Collaborative attitude and will thrive in a team environment
Confident presenting and discussing projects and associated successes and challenges in a group
Competent user of Microsoft office applications

Title: Product Manager (NL)
Description: Do you eat note-shaped cereal for breakfast? Does listening to dance music make you happier than a small child in a toy store? Are you able to do an amazing job despite – or maybe even due to – being a wee bit crazy? If your answer to all of these questions is a firm “YES”, you’ll fit right in here at Armada Music. And as faith would have it, we have just the job opening for you. In other words, we’re looking at you to become our next…Continue to article...PRODUCT MANAGER (FULL-TIME)As our brand-new PRODUCT MANAGER, you will be part of the Product department. You will report to the Head of Product management and will work closely with – among others – specialists in the field. What will you be doing (exactly)?You will create on/offline promo and strategic marketing plans for our releases and artists;You will execute marketing plans and do your utmost to meet its objectives;You will follow new trends and technological developments closely and indulge in conceptual thinking;You will build marketing plans based on market analysis and research;You will keep a tight grip on the marketing & promotion budgets and will report the cost balances to the Head of Product;You will keep the artists (and/or their management teams) up to date on planning, progress, content and chart success. Who is the person we’re looking for?You’ve acquired a relevant degree, at least ‘hbo’ (Dutch standard) or a bachelor degree;You have – at least – 3-5 years of experience in project management within the music/entertainment industry;You are punctual, organized and efficient when it comes to creating schedules and plans;You are creative and a real team player;You are used to working with strict deadlines and thrive under pressure;You are on top of – and an active user of – all forms of music consumption and social media (and its most recent developments);You are analytical, ambitious, decisive and a problem solver;You have a relevant network within the music industry;You are (near) fluent in written and spoken English. If you’ve mastered the Dutch language as well, you are at an advantage. What will we offer you in return?You will be joining the ranks of a young and dynamic team;You will receive a monthly salary befitting your age and experience;We offer favorable secondary working conditions, including 25 days off (per year) and an appealing pension scheme; And if that doesn’t convince you, know that we’ve got our own in-office club for our wild office parties and Friday afternoon drinks, a gym open to anyone who wants to blow off some steam after (or before) working hours and colleagues so fun you probably don’t even want to head back home at the end of the day. And lest we forget, we’re stocked up on an enormous amount of the music you love. Can’t contain your excitement? Send your resume and cover letter to jobs@armadamusic.com. Please make sure to put ‘Product Manager’ in the e-mail subject. We’re looking forward to meeting you!

Title: Business Affairs Manager (US)
Description: Vevo is the world’s leading all-premium music video and entertainment platform with over 25 billion monthly views globally. Vevo delivers an expertly curated experience for audiences to explore and discover music videos, exclusive original programming and live performances from the artists they love.Continue to article...Take a look inside Vevo: http://vevo.ly/cz8ykPWe are seeking a Manager, Business Affairs to join our small team of talented lawyers who are working to help grow Vevo’s business. This position will report directly to the Global Head of Licensing and will be a member of the broader Business Affairs / Business Development team.The Business Affairs team plays a key role in the company’s evolving strategy, ensuring that Vevo enters into smart, innovative agreements. As Manager, Business Affairs, you will provide business and legal counsel in connection with the company’s ongoing development efforts and day-to-day activities.You will also support the company’s overall strategy and work cross-functionally with our Content & Programming, Marketing, Finance, and Business Development teams. Eventually, you will manage your own deals directly with Vevo’s internal and external stakeholders. This role requires an individual who is an effective communicator and negotiator, is adaptable, and has the ability to spot issues, all while supervising a high volume of deals from inception through execution.As a member of the Business Affairs team at Vevo, you’ll be working alongside incredibly talented individuals who will both inspire you and challenge you to think about issues such as how best to navigate the intellectual property landscape, monetize content, and balance risk to create successful long-term partnerships.We often take time as a team to review complex legal issues and new advancements in the music and technology industries, and to think critically about various developments around us that may impact our overall strategy. We have a phenomenal unit made up of fun and accomplished individuals with diverse backgrounds, and we’re excited to have you join us!This is a full-time position and is based at our New York City headquarters.AS A MEMBER OF OUR TEAM, YOU WILLReview, draft, negotiate and execute a variety of agreements, with particular focus on: (1) music publishing/PRO/collecting society agreements (2) master use/content licensing (3) distribution (via online and mobile syndication, mobile applications, IPTV and other IP-connected devices and platforms (4) original programming and production, (5) live events, (6) ad sales and strategic marketing, (7) software and technologyAssist the Global Head of Licensing with all matters related to the licensing of music publishing rights and master use/videoAssist in the enforcement and protection of Vevo’s intellectual property rights, including the management of the takedown claim process for videos distributed on the Vevo platformAssist in the management of litigation/claims in conjunction with outside counselDevelop and maintain standard form agreementsDirect and work closely with outside counselCollaborate with other members of the legal team to ensure business needs are metTHIS DESCRIBES YOUYou share our passion for music and digital media and have a genuine curiosity for emerging digital technology.You have a track record of questioning the status quo to achieve positive results.You are comfortable with ambiguity and an ever-changing landscape and thrive in high pressure situationsYou can see the ‘big picture’ and translate high-level strategy to day-to-day implementationYou have ideally 2-4 years’ experience at a top tier law firm or entertainment, media and/or technology company in music licensing, including hands-on experience negotiating label, publisher, and PRO deals in the digital space.You understand international copyright law, and is up to date on the various licensing structures and processes that are customary in those territories in which Vevo does business.REQUIREMENTS2-4 years’ experience in contract law, intellectual property, media, technology and/or entertainmentAbility to keep information confidentialAbility to work independently and as part of a team in a dynamic, creative environment.Willing to work extended hours, as neededHighly proficient in Microsoft OfficeBA/BS and JD from top-tier programsINTERESTED? GREAT! YOU MIGHT LIKE TO KNOWWe’re a fun, energetic, and tight knit team.We really enjoy music and technology.We have excellent compensation and benefits packages.We have in-office and external happy hours.We have premier access to music content and new releases of original media content.We love to give away concert tickets to live music – not to mention live Vevo-produced musical performances.We offer a 401k match.We have a fully stocked kitchen.We are in an upscale New York city office with stunning views of Times Square.

AS A MEMBER OF OUR TEAM, YOU WILL: Review, draft, negotiate and execute a variety of agreements, with particular focus on: (1) music publishing/PRO/collecting society agreements (2) master use/content licensing (3) distribution (via online and mobile syndication, mobile applications, IPTV and other IP-connected devices and platforms (4) original programming and production, (5) live events, (6) ad sales and strategic marketing, (7) software and technology
Assist the Global Head of Licensing with all matters related to the licensing of music publishing rights and master use/video
Assist in the enforcement and protection of Vevo’s intellectual property rights, including the management of the takedown claim process for videos distributed on the Vevo platform
Assist in the management of litigation/claims in conjunction with outside counsel
Develop and maintain standard form agreements
Direct and work closely with outside counsel

Title: Royalty Manager (UK)
Description: Sentric Music are looking for a Royalty Manager to join our Finance Team.Continue to article...Based in the creative areas of the Baltic Triangle in Liverpool – or in Soho, London – the Royalty Manager will be integral to managing the accounts for our international client base.Company & Recruitment BackgroundSentric Music is a rapidly growing music publishing company with 50 employees based in our offices in the UK, Germany, New York, LA, Netherlands and Spain. The company handles copyright and royalty administration for a large client base consisting of songwriters, record labels and music catalogue owners.We are now looking for an enthusiastic and ambitious Royalty Manager to manage the full cycle of royalty distribution to our clients; from cash receipt from international collection societies, to matching income with relevant songs, preparing artists’ statements and coordinating payments.The company is in an exciting phase of launching an industry-challenging platform that will transform royalty collection for our ever-growing client base. This is a unique opportunity to take part in the design aspect of our new royalty accounting software, which will offer fair and transparent solutions to the music industry.Job DescriptionManage all aspects of distribution of royalties, including:Log cash receipts on daily basis across all internal companies and sourcesRetrieve related statement for cash receipt and analyse/split/reconcileComplete accounting cycle required of each source of money, i.e: Reconcile to amount received in the bank; Split the file between clients/companies; Identify writers and PRS tunecodes linked to account; Convert international income to local currency at correct FX rate; Book in ledger as appropriate; Prepare upload files for royalty system(s); Prepare raw data for manual statementsAnalyse all royalty income in-depthPrepare royalty statement and coordinate the distribution to our clientsManage the onboarding of royalty accounts for new clientsAssist with design of our bespoke royalty accounting software to be launchedAssist with migration from various systems to our new software once completeManage the workload of Finance Analyst to assist with all stages of royalty accountsAssist with any other ad-hoc tasks as required by Head of FinanceExperienceEssentialEducated to degree level (Economics or similar) or completed relevant Accounting qualificationMinimum 3 years experience of working with finance in a commercial environmentExperience of working to set deadlinesAdvanced user of ExcelExperience of managing accounts for multiple and complex streams of incomeMeriting Experience of working with music publishing royalty accounts is highly desirableExperience of designing new procedures and routines for accountingPerson SpecificationEnthusiastic about working with a forward-thinking company in the music industryEnjoy working both independently and as part of a teamExcellent attention to detailHighly organised, with the ability to plan and manage your own – and others – workloadTransparent and willing to collaborate with other departmentsAbility to maintain a calm and methodical approach whilst working under pressureA keen eye to identify improvements and efficiency of accounting proceduresEmployment PackageCompetitive salary based on experienceInspiring and creative office premises with a music profileOffice pool table, break-out areas and company socialsPension schemePerkbox Employee Benefits SchemeSalary sacrifice schemes incl. Cycle to work, Childcare vouchers, Nursery, Discounted gym membershipApplication submission:​Please email your CV and cover letter to careers@sentricmusic.com , subject line “Royalty Manager”Application deadline: ​9.00 AM Monday 13 August 2018 but interviews may commence sooner

Title: Music Licensing & Sync Manager (UK)
Description: Songtradr is looking to hire an experienced, client-facing Music Licensing & Sync Manager to join its fast growing UK business.Continue to article...The ideal candidate will have a successful track record in sourcing and delivering a broad spectrum of music licensing opportunities and an established network of key contacts across all of agency, advertising, brand, sync and music supervision communities.Reporting to the UK MD, she/he will also be working closely with counterparts in the US and internationally to drive our UK Sync business.About SongtradrSongtradr is the world’s fastest growing music licensing platform connecting the music and media industries into a single marketplace and community.Our mission is clear, we want to take the pain out of licensing whilst not compromising on creative quality nor devaluing the rights of the artists we represent.Established in 2016, Songtradr represents over 250,000 curated artists, songwriters and catalogues from over 180 countries. We are passionate about revolutionizing the process of licensing rights and are not shy in our ambitions.ResponsibilitiesProactively seek licensing opportunities with content producers and other creatives with a particular focus on advertising agencies and brandsWork with CRM database, building an extensive sales pipeline and tracking licensing opportunities to close.Negotiate clearance and license fees with clients, based on current market value, providing budget estimates and alternative suggestions of music to fit within a specified budget.Assist in the review and execution of music licenses.Candidate ProfileYou will have:A minimum of 5 years client-facing music sync experience with agencies and supervisorsAn extensive network of contacts at the decision-maker level within agency, advertising, brand, sync and music supervision communitiesA trusted reputation for quality, capability, delivery and successA desire and aptitude to work in an entrepreneurial, fast paced environmentA can-do attitude and be able to hit the ground runningPlease send CV and case study examples of successes to steve@songtradr.comApplicants must be eligible to work in the United Kingdom.Verification checks will be conducted during the interview process.Songtradr is an equal opportunity employer encouraging diversity in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, national origin, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or any other status or characteristic protected by law.

Title: Software Engineer (ES)
Description: Continue to article...At BMAT, we like to sing ‘put the right letters together and make a better day’. Our international team, full of problem solvers and music lovers, work to put the right chords together and make a better music scene.As users (and we use it a lot), we want it more accessible. As musicians, we need it fairer. And as music researchers, we squeeze our brains to develop one of the most widespread systems to track music and identify audio.Currently, we report music usage from all over the world to PROs, labels, publishers and broadcasters, so that artists get the recognition they deserve. However, we only feel totally fulfilled when we can sneak a couple of funny jokes while talking about this.Job summaryAs part of the Digital Engineering Team, your responsibilities will be to build and improve the products that power one of the most complete music data processing systems in the world.We are connected to more than 45 DSPs (Digital Service Providers) such as Spotify, YouTube, Apple Music, Deezer or Amazon that provide DSRs (Digital Sales Reports) that we process, store, reconcile and match in order to provide insight to our partners, standardize formats, enrich information and compute royalty statements, among others.In addition, we also provide on-demand audio identification services, that enable our partners to be in control of user-uploaded content (e.g. YouTube, Facebook, SoundCloud) and increase revenue collection from videos containing their music.With Python as the main development technology, our system processes 650 million lines per month in a horizontally scalable architecture powered by Amazon Web Services.Essential duties and responsibilities:We are looking for a resourceful, action-driven and talented coder to help us with the evolution, maintenance and operation that a system of this magnitude requires.As part of the engineering team, you will work side by side with the operations team in order to offer the best user experience to our clients and develop creative solutions to satisfy their business needs. You must have:Proficiency in Python or similarAn instinct for listening, asking, learning and communicatingHands-on experience with SQL (MySQL, PostgreSQL)A strong belief that you get the most out of your time when working in a teamLove for uncertainty – you think it creates opportunity rather than stressBasic knowledge of BashLots of fun explaining our back-end to your grandparentsExperience with Linux It would be great if you also have:Responsive web design experienceUX/UI experienceHands-on experience with Python web frameworks (Django, Flask)Experience with JavaScript frameworks (Vue, React)Basic knowledge of dockerFrequently worked/developed/deployed on the cloud and in automated environmentsExperience with software design life cycle and best practices (code reviews, testing)Experience designing and developing RESTful APIsWhat we offer:Full-time jobFlexibility on working hours and home officeFree coffee, fruit and language classesFree snacks, beers and jam sessions on Friday afternoons!Becoming part of a young and international team based in Barcelona obsessed with bettering the music industry

Job summary: As part of the Digital Engineering Team, your responsibilities will be to build and improve the products that power one of the most complete music data processing systems in the world.

Title: Senior Coordinator Copyright Administration (US)
Description: BMG is a new kind of music company. Our mission is clear; to help artists and songwriters make the very most of their songs and recordings in the digital age.Continue to article...That’s why we offer the best creative support, provide the best access to platforms and work hard to maximize the value of each and every copyright, ensuring all our clients receive all payments promptly and accurately.We are not a technology company – though we use the best technology. We are not just a music publisher or a record label – although we offer both services.BMG is a company with service, transparency and fairness at its heart and a commitment to being the best international music company for songwriters, artists and everyone who works with us.As the Senior Coordinator, Copyright Administration you will be responsible for the following items:USCO Copyright Registration & RecordationComplete and submit Copyright Registrations to the United States Copyright Office for all BMG-controlled compositions and recordings that are delivered to the Copyright department for the purpose of USCO registration.Record assignments and/or other relevant documents with the United States Copyright Office.Recoupable Cost Tracking & AnalysisTrack, calculate, and submit recoupable costs to Royalties and Finance departments for all USCO-related Registration and Recordation costs.USCO IT Development CoordinationAssist and coordinate with IT team on ongoing process to develop solutions and new tools to make the registration process more efficient.Data Maintenance & Procedure MonitoringFile Uniform Commercial Code documentation with state Secretary of State, where necessary.Liaise with Creative, Legal & Business Affairs, and Copyright departments as necessary on incomplete information.Maintaining relevant USCO data and documentation across BMG systems.Monitor and advise of changes in USCO policies, procedures, costs, etc.Information ServiceReview, research and provide timely responses to rights queries and information requests. Including, but not limited to those from:BMG US business functions (e.g. Royalty Processing, Synch/Marketing, Business Affairs, Creative.)BMG writers and clients.BMG sub-publishers and affiliates.Business partners (e.g. Hal Leonard.)Performing/Mechanical rights societies.3rd party writers and publishers.Customer RelationsAssist in maintaining and developing the relationships with admin customers and partners. Including, but not limited to:Writers/clients.Performing rights organizations.Co-publishers.Industry organizations.MiscellaneousEngage in and handle other ongoing processes or ad-hoc projects as business needs dictate or as assigned by supervisor.Miscellaneous Information Service Projects.Supervision of temporary employees and interns as necessary.Your ProfileYou have a strong interest in music publishing and music publishing administration. You are comfortable working in a high volume, fast-paced environment while handling a high volume of varying and sometimes complex data entry.You have exceptional attention to detail and the ability to track and follow through on multiple concurrent projects to completion.Must-HavesFoundational knowledge of music publishing and the role that USCO Registration and Recordation play in protecting IP rights holdersProficiency: Microsoft ExcelStrong analytic and problem solving skillsWhat’s In It For YouRobust medical/dental/vision packagesTwo weeks’ vacation in year one; three weeks’ vacation in year two$500 fitness reimbursementDiverse possibilities of personal and professional development including trainings and Lynda.comDollar for dollar match of up to 6% into 401(k) after your first year with the companyA fun and respectful community of people with a passion for music

Your Profile: You have a strong interest in music publishing and music publishing administration. You are comfortable working in a high volume, fast-paced environment while handling a high volume of varying and sometimes complex data entry.

Title: Assistant Royalties Manager (UK)
Description: Joining the growing Royalties team in our brand-new London office, the Assistant Royalties Manager will manage a small team and assist in delivering global publishing royalties to our clients in an accurate and timely manner by running operational reporting and analysis for the wider Publishing Operations team to help streamline & co-ordinate the collection, processing, and accounting of royalties.Continue to article...You will need the ability to remain optimistic and persistent when working to analyse gaps and errors in payment to our clients, and have high levels of confidence in decision making when faced with difficult challenges.Working across several areas of the business liaising with key stakeholders, such as copyright and tech teams, requires excellent communication skills as well as a detail-driven mindset and that you are comfortable with repetitive and data-heavy tasks while remaining positive and determined to complete all tasks accurately and efficiently.Founded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model.Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.What would you do as an Assistant Royalties Manager?Work closely with Copyright, Accounts, Clients, Synch and Tech teams under the guidance of the Senior Manager to help ensure effective, accurate and timely statement delivery to publishing clientsSolve problems arising from distributions and support copyright teams with any direction they may seek regarding royalties or royalty processingMake sure that due statements, data files, and receipts are received accurately and on a timely basis, and handled accordingly in line with internal schedules, standards and deadlines.Run and keep on top of multiple regular reportsYou may be asked to take on extra responsibilities – please be aware that the above list is not exhaustiveYou will ideally have……numeracy, attention to detail, accuracy and analytical skills…excellent communication skills with experience communicating to a wide-ranging audience of varying seniority, skill, and knowledge…experience in supervising, managing and motivating team members…track-record of excellent organization & prioritisation skills working under high pressure and to tight deadlines…ability to think of improvements to existing tools and processes to continually enhance what we do to achieve our goals…comfortable using Excel to calculate %’s and manipulate large sets of data using formulas and pivot tables…background and knowledge of SQL or other domain-specific language used in programming is preferred…solid understanding of music publishing, copyright & royalty processes

Description: Reporting to the VP, Tracking & Analysis, the Royalties Tracking Assistant will be an essential member of a small, but tight-knit international income tracking and analysis team based in our brand new London office.Continue to article...The team is responsible for the assurance of revenue from affiliates around the world, as well as finding additional opportunities to grow revenue further in new and existing markets.You will have the opportunity to support the team with structuring existing royalty tracking and analysis processes to drive maximum value and provide our clients with the most transparent and accurate reporting in the music industry.Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized.Our global technology platform is able to track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more.Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK. We’re currently over 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re still growing.The role is ideal for someone with a maths or economics degree with a business background. You need to be passionate about music, and enjoy looking at numbers and graphs.Being numerical, and very organized is essential and ideally, you have experience working in a fast-moving and rapidly growing start-up.What would you do as a Tracking Analyst?Analyze inconsistencies in payment of royalties from overseas collection societies due to songwriters and publishersProvide reports on all analysis, tracking and distribution activitiesRecommend improvements to current processesStructure ad-hoc analytical tasksCommunicate/Present findings using clear and concise languageResearch and summarize relevant industry insightsHelp develop Kobalt’s analytical functionsYou will ideally have……numeracy skills…excellent data gathering/analysis as well as interpreting, reporting and problem-solving capabilities…first-class communication skills…intermediate Excel skills (pivot tables and formulas like ‘if’, index match, v-lookup etc.)…PowerPoint and data presentation skills…basic SQL skills, or an aptitude for programming i.e. can demonstrate logical thinking and be able to explain in words how they would go about retrieving information…high level of planning, prioritization and organizational skills…experience of working in a fast-paced, start-up environment

Title: Head of Catalog (US)

Continue to article...In this Product Partnerships role, you will help craft Google’s overall affiliate strategy and work with internal product teams, aligning our partnerships with business objectives. This includes analyzing partnership relationships, conducting competitive assessments and coordinating with multiple product teams including Chrome, Social, Knowledge, Android and YouTube.You will have the rare opportunity to build a long-lasting relationship with our top partners. You cultivate these existing partnerships to make sure they can take full advantage of Google’s ever-growing suite of offerings. You possess strong relationship-building skills and are apt to see mutually beneficial opportunities with partners in order to best represent our users, products and programs.Catalog music is a critical part of YouTube and YouTube Music. As Head of Catalog, you will be responsible for developing catalog focused strategies with key partners. You will be responsible for providing ongoing relationship management to YouTube’s key US catalog label partners and legacy artists and their teams, with a focus on supporting labels and artists with promotional opportunities to help build their success and helping them get full value out of YouTube’s tools.In this role you will interact regularly with labels, artists and their teams and also collaborate closely with internal teams. Title: Copyright and Contracts Executive (UK)
Description: ABRSM is the UK’s largest music education body, one of its largest music publishers and the world’s leading provider of music exams, offering assessments to more than 630,000 candidates in 93 countries every year. We support the teaching and learning of music, in partnership with the Royal Schools of Music. Our mission is to inspire achievement in music.Continue to article...ABRSM Publishing is a wholly-owned subsidiary of ABRSM. We are currently recruiting for a Copyright and Contracts Executive to join the Copyright team within our Products and Services department.One of the main responsibilities of this role is to advise the business on contractual and licensing matters, in order to minimise risks and to protect ABRSM and its assets. The job holder will deal with complex contractual and licensing matters and infringements arising within the company and, when required, liaise with ABRSM’s solicitors. There will also be the requirement to work as a flexible member of the team, carrying out all core Copyright work as required.We are looking for someone who has solid experience of working in music publishing in copyright and licensing role(s), with particular emphasis on drafting contracts and licences. You will also ideally have a relevant legal qualification e.g. a law degree and legal practice course or paralegal experience.ABRSM is a friendly company that offers its employees a range of benefits, including an interest-free season ticket loan (after passing a probationary period) and a contributory pension scheme. We are based in contemporary offices with good facilities for staff in a central location in the City of London, which has excellent transport links.For further details and to apply, please visit our careers site: http://www.abrsmcareers.org/Applications must be submitted online by 5pm on 30 July 2018.For enquiries please contact Nicky Prentice: nprentice@abrsm.ac.ukABRSM is committed to safeguarding and promoting the welfare of children and young people and we expect our workforce to share this commitment.

Title: Senior Creative Sync Manager (US)
Description: Rocking Horse Road Productions is a small, independently owned music licensing company with a music supervision department, a boutique management department and its sync department, which represents RHR Music Publishing and Coversion Music – the sync focused covers catalogue. Continue to article...RHR is currently looking for a full-time Sync Manager to take over the day-to-day running, and overall growth, of the sync department. This includes running the department fully on a day to day basis, so previous management experience is an asset. We are looking for someone who will treat the department as their own personal business and not just a pay check. The Sync Manager position will offer a lot of freedom to the ideal candidate, but it takes someone with an entrepreneurial spirit and a background in the industry to really lead a small worldwide team, and continue our growth trajectory. Even though there is a small team – this position is for someone who can be present and involved in all aspects of sync. Because this is a home (or other) based position, the perfect Sync Manager can be based anywhere – but preference is for LA. However, if you think this is TOTALLY YOUR JOB, please apply. Travel will be required. We need a strong and focused individual looking to make their mark on something and looking to make a real difference to a small business with a ton of potential. The key responsibilities will include:Learn and know the ins and outs of the current cataloguesPitching catalogue songs to briefBrief generation Relationship management with artists and their teamsLiaising with pitch partners and growing that network worldwideAttending events and conferences as neededDefining the overall goals and procedures for the department Drafting contracts and licenses and collect contracts for signatureA&R and submission review and responsesPromote and grow Conversion Music – at home and overseasOversee promotions and promotional and marketing tools updatesThe ideal candidate will…Have a high level understanding and a background in rights clearanceAlready possess a vast array of contacts in the film, television, video game, advertising and trailer worldsLove to network – including attending parties and events. Must love people!Be results based and goal orientedBe proactive and be able to identify new opportunitiesHave an extremely strong work ethic and driveKnow that ‘work hours’ in this type of business are not always straightforward(Obviously) have a real passion and knowledge for music, the music industry, and music in mediaPlease send salary expectations and resume or other type of creative application to: jb@coversionmusic.com If you’ve landed some great syncs in the past, let us know. If you want to name drop, please do. We’re weighing everything very carefully to find the perfect person to join our team and lead the division to greatness.

Description: What’s next in music happens on SoundCloud first. As the world’s largest open audio platform, SoundCloud is powered by a connected community of creators, listeners and curators who share, discover and influence what’s new, now and next in music and audio.Continue to article...We believe that our community comes first – they’re at the heart of everything we do – our goal is to provide people using SoundCloud with the help they need, when they need it. Young & Co are one of the leading music specialist firms of accountants and tax advisors, providing accountancy and taxation services to music industry clients. Continue to article...Over the past 18 years, C.C. Young & Co. has enjoyed continued growth and now has departments specialising in tax, touring, recording, publishing, and business management services for artists and managers.It is seeking a Senior Accountant to join the team dealing with clients who are copyright holders such as record companies, publishers, writers and composers.Client Work To ensure effective and proactive management of high profile clients’ day to day affairs, e.g. cash flow, debtor collection, tax and VAT provisions etc. To prepare year end accounts for Limited Companies, Limited Liability Partnerships and sole traders.To oversee the day to day running of affairs for high profile clients, to include liaising with label managers, collection societies, lawyers etc; To prepare Corporation Tax returns for Limited CompaniesTo prepare self-assessment returns for individuals To liaise with Copyright Controllers regarding reading and interpreting of recording and publishing agreements, inclusion of royalty statements into accounts and to examine completeness of Royalty Statements.Liaise with clients regarding business, tax and financial planning.To prepare VAT Returns for your allocated client portfolio; and to assist the Head of Department with ad hoc projects that may arise.Staff Development To monitor timekeeping within the team, both in terms of attendance and productivityTo support Trainee Accountants through their studiesTo mentor and encourage team continuing professional educationWorking ConditionsWork to be carried out onsite at the Chancery Lane offices of C.C. Young & Co. Attendance at in office and out of office meetings with Staff and Clients may be required – advance notice will be given if travel arrangements are required.Contribute to Leadership Development Where appropriate to contribute to practice development and assist in development of senior staff in the leadership team.Those interested in the role should email a CV to careers@ccyoung.co.uk

Client Work: To ensure effective and  proactive  management of high profile clients’ day to day affairs, e.g. cash flow, debtor collection, tax and VAT provisions etc.
To prepare year end accounts for Limited Companies, Limited Liability Partnerships and sole traders.
To oversee the day to day running of affairs for high profile clients, to include liaising with label managers, collection societies, lawyers etc;
To prepare Corporation Tax returns for Limited Companies
To prepare self-assessment returns for individuals

To liaise with Copyright Controllers regarding reading and interpreting of recording and publishing agreements, inclusion of royalty statements into accounts and to examine completeness of Royalty Statements.
Liaise with clients regarding business, tax and financial planning.
To prepare VAT Returns for your allocated client portfolio; and to assist the Head of Department with ad hoc projects that may arise.
Staff Development: To monitor timekeeping within the team, both in terms of attendance and productivity
To support Trainee Accountants through their studies
To mentor and encourage team continuing professional education
Working Conditions: Work to be carried out onsite at the Chancery Lane offices of C.C. Young & Co.
Attendance at in office and out of office meetings with Staff and Clients may be required – advance notice will be given if travel arrangements are required.
Contribute to Leadership Development: Where appropriate to contribute to practice development and assist in development of senior staff in the leadership team.

decisions through a combination of analysis, experience, and judgmentProven negotiating skills and influencing abilities with small and large label partners, artists, and artist managersExtensive industry network with broad music industry awarenessStrong organizational skills including prioritizing, scheduling, time management and meeting deadlinesStrong quantitative analysis, problem-solving and project management skillsStrong and successful communication skills which include the ability to write compelling, concise documentsProven English and communication skills – both verbal and writtenHighly polished Spanish communication skills – speaking, writing, and presentingPREFERRED QUALIFICATIONSNimbleness and comfort with ambiguity; demonstrated track record of creative problem solving; thinks big, starts small, grows fastAnalytical and quantitative skills, ability to use hard data and metrics to back up assumptions and develop business cases. Ability to clearly communicate these data insights to othersHigh attention to detail including proven ability to manage multiple, competing priorities simultaneouslyProactive attitude; willingness to roll up sleeves and dig deep to achieve goalsProven experience developing and implementing multi-channel marketing campaigns that support market segment share growthAmazon is an Equal Opportunity-Affirmative Action Employer – Minority/Female/Disability/Vet. Qualified applicants with criminal histories will be considered in a manner consistent with all applicable local, state, and federal laws.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: 7 years of experience in the music industry in a function involving sales, account management, business development, deal-making, and/or licensing
A proven track record of setting and exceeding goals; evidence of the ability to consistently make good decisions through a combination of analysis, experience, and judgment
Proven negotiating skills and influencing abilities with small and large label partners, artists, and artist managers

High attention to detail including proven ability to manage multiple, competing priorities simultaneously
Proactive attitude; willingness to roll up sleeves and dig deep to achieve goals
Proven experience developing and implementing multi-channel marketing campaigns that support market segment share growth

Title: Copyright Assistant, Spanish speaking (UK)
Description: Joining the Publishing Copyright department in our London office, the primary role of the Copyright Assistant will be to assist the team in managing our catalogue in Spain as well as other Spanish speaking territories in Latin America by ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at the collection societies.Continue to article...The successful candidate will need to have excellent communication skills when dealing with general enquiries from internal and external sources and can deal with pressure but remain optimistic and persistent to complete tasks accurately and efficiently.Kobalt is the leading technology-powered music services company, offering end-to-end solutions for over 50,000 artists and songwriters and 800 labels and publishing companies.Through Kobalt technology and services, creators benefit from more money, better reporting, and maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized.With the award-winning Kobalt desktop portal and app, Kobalt clients get real-time insight into their work and activity.Collection of royalties has traditionally been time-consuming and costly. Kobalt has developed centralised, automated and efficient administration services supported by a scalable, proprietary, dedicated system using the latest technology.Because of Kobalt’s innovative approach, Kobalt offers a reduction in royalty collection time of up to 50%, more effective collection as well as substantially improved transparency.We’re currently over 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re expecting to grow. That’s where you come in. If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!What would you do as a Copyright Assistant?Ensuring works and agreements are registered correctly at the Spanish speaking collection societies in Latin America and EuropeDealing with general enquiries regarding our claims to works and the terms of our publishing agreementsHandling song disputes and duplicate claimsProcessing new works and agreements as the company acquires rights to new cataloguesYou may be asked to take on extra responsibilities – please be aware that the above list is not exhaustiveYou will ideally have…high level of written and spoken Spanish as well English…excellent communication skills: the duties require a very high level of communication internally and externally; we are looking for someone who can adapt communication to the audience, be persuasive but remain humble and professional…track-record of excellent organization and prioritisation skills…problem-solver skills with a detail-driven mindset…basic Excel skills but a willingness to learn and improve…previous admin and copyright experience in a similar role, as well as a good understanding of music publishing, is highly desirableApplicants must be eligible to work in the United Kingdom. Verification checks will be conducted during the interview process and a full DBS check on acceptance of offer.Kobalt is an equal opportunity employer encouraging diversity in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or any other status or characteristic protected by law.

Title: Artist Marketing Manager (UK)
Description: Arts & Media is currently recruiting an Artist Marketing Manager for a leading London-based music company whose business is diversified within areas to include artist services, distribution, retail sales and digital marketing.Continue to article...They are a well established and recognised face of the industry and are presently going through a new phase in their expansion.This is an exciting time to get on board with a forward thinking, progressive company whose environment and ethos encourages change and development, keeping up and ahead of industry developments.This is a new position which has come about due to recent success and ongoing successes within the department.Working alongside the Director of Artist Marketing, the Senior Artist Marketing Manager will effectively and creatively manage all aspects of marketing campaigns for assigned artists as approved artist and label services campaigns.The candidate will manage all marketing activity in line with commercial objectives and approved budget, and build and maintain strong relationships with artist management, key internal departments and external agencies/suppliers.Candidates for this role will have a proven and successful career built within a label or artist management company dealing with frontline release campaigns, and will have solid knowledge and experience of artist and label services including campaign management, budget management and creative planning.You will have excellent knowledge of the digital space, including music retail, analytics and social media with an ability to adapt to a constantly changing landscape.So if you possess the above skill set and are a confident communicator who thinks on their feet and likes to go the extra mile get in touch with us right away… and of course you will be someone whose life is enriched by music of all genres.Please send all applications to jenny@themusicmarket.co.ukDue to the volume of applications, we cannot respond to every candidate that applies for this vacancy. Should you not hear from us within 10 working days, please assume you have been unsuccessful at this time. If you are already registered with us, please contact your consultant directly. By submitting your cv for consideration, you are consenting to its retention for the purpose of securing you work. Any information you provide in response to this advertisement will be subject to the protection of GDPR, our policy for which can be found here.

Title: Head of Music Programming (US)
Description: Continue to article...Amazon Music is seeking an experienced music executive to lead our Programming efforts in the world’s largest music market, the US.This role will report to Amazon’s Global Head of Programming and Content Strategy and will architect playlist, station and discovery experiences for US customers, and manage the team that delivers those week-to-week.The ideal candidate will be a seasoned strategist, with an understanding of customer acquisition and engagement that contemplates all the complexities of the current digital music business.The candidate will have the skills to build on this knowledge, and deliver the best experience possible, for all music fans, and will have a proven track record of delivering results.This leader should demonstrate strength in working cross-functionally and building consensus, with strong communication skills.The Programming team at Amazon works with counterparts across Product, Label Relations, Editorial, Marketing, Merchandising, Original Content, and Operations.This leader will enhance those relationships and play a key role in working globally with Amazon Music offices around the world, and shaping our global programming strategy.The ideal candidate will additionally have an entrepreneurial spirit and will be highly analytical.He/she will have a reputation as a respected voice within the industry and will bring tenure and a track record of building and developing people and organizations, making data-driven decisions, identifying and adding process improvements and advocating on behalf of customers.A track record of proven experience delivering results both within and outside his/her organization is a must.Key responsibilities:Manage a large programming team that has depth of knowledge and expertise in all key genres.Set both short-term and long-term strategic goals and objectives for the programming team.Build out and oversee all station and playlist programming for Amazon Music customers, and apply a deep understanding of customer expectation to both.Shape the customer experience for engagement with music across visual platforms and Alexa-enabled devices.Develop relationships with content creators and the industry to build unique programming for Amazon Music customers.Determine the metrics and analytics that best inform our programming.Become an evangelist for Amazon MusicShare expertise and best practices globally.Work with internal and external stakeholders to improve and expand Amazon’s content offerings to customers.Focus on the growth and scaling of our programming team, which includes recruiting and developing the best.Create an environment which motivates people to thrive in their expertise and deliver ambitious results.BASIC QUALIFICATIONSBachelor’s Degree10 years of experience in the music industry5 years of management experience. Clear evidence of managing and motivating a team, both direct and indirect reports.A proven track record building customer-facing programming for a digital or online music service – or – 5 years of building customer-facing programming across a diverse set of genres.A proven track record of setting and exceeding aggressive goals; evidence and history of good decisions driven by analysis, experience, and judgment.Proven influence and impact within the music industry.Strong organizational skills including prioritizing, scheduling, time management and meeting deadlines.Strong quantitative analysis, problem solving and project management skillsPREFERRED QUALIFICATIONSMaster’s DegreeA proven track record of invention or innovationDemonstrated experience of driving process and tool improvementsExtensive industry network with broad music industry awarenessStrong and successful communication skills, include the ability to write compelling, concise documents.Amazon.com is an Equal Opportunity-Affirmative Action Employer – Minority / Female / Disability / Veteran / Gender Identity / Sexual Orientation.

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor’s Degree
10 years of experience in the music industry
5 years of management experience. Clear evidence of managing and motivating a team, both direct and indirect reports.
A proven track record building customer-facing programming for a digital or online music service , or , 5 years of building customer-facing programming across a diverse set of genres.
A proven track record of setting and exceeding aggressive goals; evidence and history of good decisions driven by analysis, experience, and judgment.
Proven influence and impact within the music industry.
Strong organizational skills including prioritizing, scheduling, time management and meeting deadlines.
Strong quantitative analysis, problem solving and project management skills
PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: Master’s Degree
A proven track record of invention or innovation
Demonstrated experience of driving process and tool improvements
Extensive industry network with broad music industry awareness
Strong and successful communication skills, include the ability to write compelling, concise documents.

Title: Product Manager for Traktor (DE)
Description: As a Product Manager for our TRAKTOR Business Unit (f/m) you know what our customers want and can translate it into requirements a development team can understand. You have a strong desire to bring products to the market our customers will love.Continue to article...You also understand how to bridge the worlds of business and technology. Working directly with a cross-functional hardware development team, you will guide products from conception to launch. You will be working in a cross-functional program team that drives the definition and execution of a part of our product roadmap, including team members from Sales, Marketing, Customer Support and Finance. As a Product Manager, you will help shape the future of NI Traktor products.WHAT YOU’LL DOIdentify opportunities on the DJ gear market and define product vision and strategyResearch and understand customer needs and define product requirementsCreate a product roadmap and communicate it to stakeholdersGuide a cross-functional development team to develop new DJ products and enhance existing productsManage a product backlog, write user stories and quantify their business valuePrioritize what the development team should do next. Review and release the results after every sprintWork closely with the engineering team to determine the best technical solutions and reasonable schedulesWHAT YOU’LL BRING2 years’ professional experience as Product Manager or Product Owner, ideally working with hardware teams.Experience in the music industry, especially DJing or music production. Familiarity with Native Instruments’ products would be a bonusExperience driving complex strategic initiatives and leading a team to a common goal. You can easily transition between high-level strategy and the concrete requirements of day-to-day business.You have a precise and focused mindset, and you appreciate a constantly improving product development setup.You have strong analytical skills and can manage a product through its lifecycle by monitoring relevant metrics and KPIs.You have a good understanding of the technologies used in our products.Your approach to your work is goal-oriented, thorough and meticulous.You have excellent communication skills and your command of English is fluent.As a conversation starter, please send us 2-3 examples of product features you produced that you’re most proud of.Native Instruments embraces diversity.As an equal opportunities employer, we believe the foundation of our dynamic and pioneering spirit starts with a fair and inclusive culture. Our company culture focuses on bringing unique ideas together to fuel innovation.All applicants will receive equal consideration for employment at Native Instruments and we encourage everyone to apply – regardless of gender identity, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or any other characteristic protected by law. Help us reach our goal in making the future of music diverse, inclusive, and exciting.

WHAT YOU\u2019LL DO: Identify opportunities on the DJ gear market and define product vision and strategy
Research and understand customer needs and define product requirements
Create a product roadmap and communicate it to stakeholders
Guide a cross-functional development team to develop new DJ products and enhance existing products
Manage a product backlog, write user stories and quantify their business value
Prioritize what the development team should do next. Review and release the results after every sprint
Work closely with the engineering team to determine the best technical solutions and reasonable schedules
WHAT YOU\u2019LL BRING: 2 years’ professional experience as Product Manager or Product Owner, ideally working with hardware teams.
Experience in the music industry, especially DJing or music production. Familiarity with Native Instruments’ products would be a bonus
Experience driving complex strategic initiatives and leading a team to a common goal. You can easily transition between high-level strategy and the concrete requirements of day-to-day business.
You have a precise and focused mindset, and you appreciate a constantly improving product development setup.
You have strong analytical skills and can manage a product through its lifecycle by monitoring relevant metrics and KPIs.
You have a good understanding of the technologies used in our products.
Your approach to your work is goal-oriented, thorough and meticulous.
You have excellent communication skills and your command of English is fluent.

Title: Policy and Research Officer (UK)
Description: This is a great opportunity for a public affairs professional to join the dynamic and innovative ISM. Continue to article...We are well known for our high-profile campaigns and policy initiatives on everything from Brexit to diversity to musicians’ rights to music education and arts policy. We are now looking for a policy and research professional to drive our public affairs work forward.You will be joining a professional staff team based in Bayswater, London where the emphasis is on good communication, innovation and team work.RESPONSIBILITIESUndertake research into all areas of interest for the music sector and ISM members e.g. tax and status issues, worker rights, health and safety, diversity and inclusion, music education, music industry, copyright, Brexit.Produce authoritative briefings and reports to support the work of public affairs. Keep fully up to date with all issues which could affect the music industry, the music profession and music education. Maintain an up to date list of all relevant consultations and dates for responses.Respond in a timely fashion to relevant stakeholder including government consultations ensuring there is relevant collaboration with other staff members e.g. legal and communications where necessary.Maintain up to date data on all relevant Parliamentarians, Parliamentary Committees, Government departments, Ministers, civil servants, and special advisors. Compile and update lists of stakeholders and contact details, tracking our engagement with them. Draft position papers, rebuttals and other public affairs resources as required. Update the campaigns section of the ISM website and other websites as necessary.Identify opportunities to take forward the work of the ISM’s public affairs team. Attend stakeholder meetings on behalf of the Public Affairs function.Carry out polls and surveys as required. Produce briefings and speeches for the ISM staff team, Board members and other stakeholders as appropriate.Working with the Public Affairs and Campaigns Manager, develop and manage ISM networks and groups including, but not limited to: our SIGs: Performers; Composers and Educators, ISM Ambassadors, ISM Advocates and ISM Honorary MembersAssist at public affairs events. Ensure effective, efficient and timely communication with all colleagues and stakeholders.Other tasks as directed by the Chief Executive and Public Affairs and Campaigns Manager.Person Specification Essential:Degree or equivalent level of education.At least 2 years’ experience working in the field of public affairs / policy. Strong political awareness across a wide range of stakeholders.Demonstrable track record of success in previous roles.Experience of responding to consultations and developing policy briefings.Experience of conducting surveys and handling data.A positive ‘can do’ attitude with a clear focus on outcomes and impact.Strong networking and relationship building skills. Good team player who works well under pressure and to deadlines.Excellent written and oral communication skills and the ability to communicate persuasively to a wide range of audiences.Excellent attention to detail.High levels of emotional intelligence. Desirable: Experience of the music or the creative sector.Applications are welcome from candidates from all sectors who can demonstrate a track record of success. For an informal discussion about this post please contact Deborah Annetts on 020 7313 9320.To apply please send a full CV and covering letter saying why you are the person for the job to recruitment@ism.org.Closing date is 09.00 on Monday 9 July 2018. First round interviews will be in the following week.

RESPONSIBILITIES: Undertake research into all areas of interest for the music sector and ISM members e.g. tax and status issues, worker rights, health and safety, diversity and inclusion, music education, music industry, copyright, Brexit.

Title: Sync Licensing Manager (UK)
Description: Village Green, a division of Major Tom Media, is a burgeoning independent recording and publishing company reshaping the landscape of electronic, minimalist, ambient and classical music.Continue to article...Our roster is comprised of a diverse range of world class artists, producers and composers with an ever-growing catalogue of music rich in cinematic quality.We are looking to hire an experienced Sync Licensing Manager to oversee all aspects of sync licensing for the label’s ever expanding roster and catalogue.The chosen candidate will work closely alongside the Major Tom team and as part of an uncompromisingly creative collective of music supervisors, producers, composers and musicians.Your role will be to procure licensing and composition opportunities across a variety of mediums on a global level.A proven track record of successful licensing procurements and a strong network within the music, commercial, film & TV industries are essential along with a deep understanding of the sector and a genuine passion for alternative music.Please contact mark@villagegreen.tv to apply.

Description: The global explosion of digital content consumption has created some seriously exciting opportunities in the world of entertainment and media.Continue to article...To be successful in this transformational landscape, we look for talented, entrepreneurial people to drive continued growth and enhance our strategic offering to artists and clients.The Creative Services Manager role encompasses account management, team leadership, stakeholder engagement, administration and elements of marketing and sales.As the 2IC, this key role will contribute to a significant revenue area of our business.A typical day in the life of a Creative Services Manager includes…Leading as a 2IC and providing guidance and mentorship to the immediate team.Managing and facilitating requests from a portfolio of clients across media, entertainment and gaming.Identifying opportunities and presenting creative pitches to clients to drive revenue.Engaging internal departments and partners locally and internationally to maximise artist opportunities.Providing insights and market research to inspire the team, driving creative input and new ideas.Overseeing complex administrative projects relating to client requirements.What we're looking for in a Creative Services Manager…Experience in a client-facing role within the media, creative agency, entertainment or digital world.Excellent people manager who loves to lead and inspire.A people person, collaborator and influencer.Highly creative and entrepreneurial. Identifying opportunities and presenting creative pitches to clients to drive revenue.

Title: Project Manager (UK)

Title: Lead Counsel, Music Rights & Implementation (US)
Description: Facebook seeks a highly creative and motivated team player to join its Music Legal team. Continue to article...You will play a significant role as we integrate music into our family of products, enabling our users to build community around the music they love.
