Title: Paralegal, Music (US)
Description: Continue to article...Netflix is looking for a Paralegal to join its growing global music group as part of the Business & Legal Affairs Team based in Los Angeles.The ideal candidate shares our passion for music and the entertainment industry to help support our expanding slate of Originals content.Someone who seeks out and thrives in an environment where freedom and responsibility are a tenet, and passion, innovation and curiosity are mandates.A stunning and selfless colleague for whom no task is too small or insurmountable.The Music Group is based in Los Angeles, London and Berlin and is responsible for all of Netflix’s global music business and strategy, from the production and licensing of Netflix Originals through to the distribution of the Netflix service globally.This role is based in our Los Angeles office and will report to a Senior Counsel/Director of Music and will be responsible for drafting and negotiating various music agreements in support of the broader Music Group.This role will have exposure to and the opportunity to work on all types of production content across the company including scripted and unscripted series, film features, indie film, documentaries and kids and family programming.REQUIREMENTSThe successful candidate will have: 5 years of series/film production music experience specifically in structuring, negotiating and drafting music agreements including composer agreements, music supervisor agreements, songwriter agreements, music producer agreements, artist agreements, sideartist agreements and record company and music publishing waivers. In-depth knowledge of music copyright and the series/film production process – from script to budget to music clearance/licensing to composer engagement/recording through to delivery, cue sheets, music publishing administration and launch.Practical knowledge of the music and technology industries (including active relationships with music agents, managers, music publishers and record companies); and an understanding of the evolving theatrical, VOD, TV, syndication and home video landscapes. International market experience and international production experience is a plus. Experience providing in-house support in a television or film production company is highly preferred.The successful candidate is comfortable drafting, negotiating and working independently and has exceptional judgment in making decisions on issues that arise in music contracts. If you are that candidate, your organizational skills, in addition to your drafting skills, are unparalleled and you have an unwavering attention to contract detail at all times. You relish the opportunity to draft new concepts and innovative commercial terms from scratch. You are self-motivated, quick-thinking, solution-oriented, and able to calmly juggle diverse responsibilities with grace and enthusiasm. You are able to execute multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment with a high degree of precision and professionalism. You are self-reliant, yet you also effectively collaborate with others and excel in a team-oriented environment. This is an opportunity to be part of a public company with a great consumer brand and to help Netflix shape the future of entertainment.THE SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATE WILL ALSO POSSESS THE FOLLOWING:Clear, concise communication skillsAbility to distill salient points and frame and present issues succinctlyStrong analytical capabilities and judgment, even when the rules are unclear and the issues are of first impressionAbility to work proactively, independently and reliably on multiple projects under tight time frames in a fast-paced production environmentAbility to pivot quickly and be resourceful, think practically and be solution-orientedCuriosity and eagerness to tackle additional projects and matters outside area of expertiseExceptional judgment, particularly in handling confidential informationHighly collaborative with the ability to be fluid and respond positively to change, feedback and new ideas.Sense of humor

REQUIREMENTS: The successful candidate will have: 5 years of series/film production music experience specifically in structuring, negotiating and drafting music agreements including composer agreements, music supervisor agreements, songwriter agreements, music producer agreements, artist agreements, sideartist agreements and record company and music publishing waivers.
THE SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATE WILL ALSO POSSE: Ability to distill salient points and frame and present issues succinctly
Strong analytical capabilities and judgment, even when the rules are unclear and the issues are of first impression
Ability to work proactively, independently and reliably on multiple projects under tight time frames in a fast-paced production environment
Ability to pivot quickly and be resourceful, think practically and be solution-oriented
Curiosity and eagerness to tackle additional projects and matters outside area of expertise
Exceptional judgment, particularly in handling confidential information
Highly collaborative with the ability to be fluid and respond positively to change, feedback and new ideas.Sense of humor

Title: Label Music Business Development Manager (US)
Description: Facebook is seeking a Label Music Business Development Manager who is passionate about the changing music ecosystem, technology and supporting our goal of connecting people through social media, the mobile eco-system and business strategy.Continue to article...This role will support Facebook’s strategy and negotiations with music labels (both majors and independents), as well as collaborate with our product and media partnerships teams to ensure a coordinated and best-in-class licensing structure.The position is full-time and based in our office in Menlo Park.RESPONSIBILITIESSupport label licensing strategy and conduct negotiations with major music labels, including playing a lead role in negotiations when necessary.Represent Facebook in meetings with music industry executives from other companies.Be a source of insights about the music ecosystem and partners to our executives.Execute in an intense, fast-paced, and highly iterative environment.MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS5 years of digital music negotiation experience.BS or BA degree.PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONSProven track record of hands-on deal experience in music licensing.Extensive knowledge of the music industry, digital business models and technology.Ability to operate independently and pro-actively to drive business goals.Proven track record with high standards of professionalism.Exceptional interpersonal skills and ability to develop strong working relationships.Creative, resourceful, detail-oriented, highly organized.Strong communication skills.Ability to meet multiple objectives in an entrepreneurial environment with little supervision.

RESPONSIBILITIES: Support label licensing strategy and conduct negotiations with major music labels, including playing a lead role in negotiations when necessary.
Represent Facebook in meetings with music industry executives from other companies.
Be a source of insights about the music ecosystem and partners to our executives.
Execute in an intense, fast-paced, and highly iterative environment.
MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: 5 years of digital music negotiation experience.
BS or BA degree.
PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: Proven track record of hands-on deal experience in music licensing.
Extensive knowledge of the music industry, digital business models and technology.
Ability to operate independently and pro-actively to drive business goals.
Proven track record with high standards of professionalism.
Exceptional interpersonal skills and ability to develop strong working relationships.
Creative, resourceful, detail-oriented, highly organized.
Strong communication skills.
Ability to meet multiple objectives in an entrepreneurial environment with little supervision.

Description: High Time is looking for a Creative Sync Licensing Manager to join our new Los Angeles team.Continue to article...Role & ResponsibilitiesRespond creatively to client briefsProactively identify and create new business opportunitiesMaintain relationships with music supervisorsPromote new signings and releases in conjunction with artist campaignsProcure bespoke writing and composition opportunities for our roster of writersArrange events and showcasesUpdate and coordinate with label and publishing partners both in US and other territoriesGenerate and present regular activity reportsExperience & Attributes2-4 years’ experience in sync licensing at a record label or publisherPre-existing relationships within the LA music supervision communityIn depth understanding of copyright (master and publishing)Confident self-starterExemplary interpersonal and communication skillsExceptional attention to detailExperience with Adobe Creative Suite, DISCO, MailChimp, CMSTo apply, please send applications to jobs@hightime.io

Pre-existing relationships within the LA music supervision community

Title: Manager, Music Licensing (US)
Description: We’re a music company first and foremost. We love music, and it shows in the creativity, talent and breadth of our roster, and the craft that goes into making our music.Continue to article...Simply put, we create original, high-quality music for brands, broadcasters, filmmakers and creatives, all over the world and we work with a roster of more than 1000 artists, including renowned composers, respected singer songwriters and both known and emerging artists, streaming on all major platforms.Audio Network has more than 150,000 tracks in its catalogue, spanning every genre and mood imaginable.From Coca-Cola and Saatchi & Saatchi to HBO…We’ve helped them tell their iconic stories. Will you help shape ours?Who are we looking for?Manager, Music Licensing – NYYour RoleSupports Director of Branded Content with existing and new client relationshipsLead generation across all sectors with a focus on advertising and branded contentHandle incoming website requests, and phone calls related to pricing and licensesCreate a solid pipeline of new clients.Prospect new business in key marketsAttend and present as needed at external client meetings, events and functions to aid business developmentCollaborate with internal Music Researchers to provide custom Audio Network playlists for all client requests and searchesData entry and organization; logging and maintain contact history and information for clientsStrategize with sales lead to maintain existing clients and build sustainable relationships.Plan and contribute to marketing events and showcasesConference and event prep (building contact lists to invite clients to events or meetings at conferences)Your ExperienceAt least 3 years’ proven experience in branded content and/or music licensingExperience with client services and pitchingStructured and target-based performanceA true team player!Strong communication and presentation skillsMarketing knowledge and a creative flairPassion for and expert knowledge in music and/or branded contentProficient in Microsoft Office including; Word, Excel and PowerPointExperience with Salesforce preferredYour Special SkillsProactive and resourceful, with the ability to perform independently while working well in a team environmentDetail-orientedA good communicator, with excellent oral and written communication skillsAble to prioritize and manage time efficiently, with strong organizational skillsSelf-accountable for salesThis summarizes the main key responsibilities of your role but there may be other reasonable requests made and other responsibilities included from time to time.Excellent benefits including generous PTO, medical, dental, vision and 401K

Your Role: Supports Director of Branded Content with existing and new client relationships
Lead generation across all sectors with a focus on advertising and branded content
Handle incoming website requests, and phone calls related to pricing and licenses
Create a solid pipeline of new clients.
Prospect new business in key markets
Attend and present as needed at external client meetings, events and functions to aid business development
Collaborate with internal Music Researchers to provide custom Audio Network playlists for all client requests and searches
Data entry and organization; logging and maintain contact history and information for clients
Strategize with sales lead to maintain existing clients and build sustainable relationships.
Plan and contribute to marketing events and showcases
Conference and event prep (building contact lists to invite clients to events or meetings at conferences)
Your Experience: At least 3 years’ proven experience in branded content and/or music licensing
Experience with client services and pitching
Marketing knowledge and a creative flair
Passion for and expert knowledge in music and/or branded content
Proficient in Microsoft Office including; Word, Excel and PowerPoint
Your Special Skills: Proactive and resourceful, with the ability to perform independently while working well in a team environment
Detail-oriented
A good communicator, with excellent oral and written communication skills
Able to prioritize and manage time efficiently, with strong organizational skills
Self-accountable for sales

Title: Recruiter, Music & Shared Services (contract role, US)
Description: Reporting to the VP Global Recruitment you will Support hiring across a range of disciplines, building relationships with key managers in Los Angeles and across the US offices. You will advise and guide them through Kobalt’s recruitment processes and policies and consistently deliver a diverse and balanced shortlist for each open role.Continue to article...Key metrics will be ensuring new hires meet our cultural values, are hired in a timely and cost effective manner and that all parties have a first-class recruitment experience.To be successful in the role, you need to be confident, determined and ambitious, but always with a humble approach to build trustworthy relationships, and not to enforce them.The role involves a high level of diligence, organization and prioritization and is a great opportunity to shape the talent that comes into Kobalt as a key stage of our growth.BACKGROUNDFounded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform can track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.This is an established yet fast growing creator-centric company of artists and songwriters including The Chainsmokers, Kelly Clarkson, Miles Davis, Dave Grohl, Lauv, Zayn Malik, Max Martin, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, Paul McCartney, Stevie Nicks, Pitbull, Elvis Presley, Skrillex, Sam Smith, and many more. Kobalt represents on average over 40% of the top 100 songs and albums in both the US and UK.We’re currently 600 people based across 10 offices and we’re still growing!WHAT DOES A RECRUITER AT KOBALT DO?Provide an exceptional recruitment experience from the first point of contact with line manager or candidate through to offer and start date.Support hiring managers in sourcing and hiring top talent by: assisting with writing job descriptions, advising on sourcing approach, posting jobs, doing initial screenings and engaging talent, managing candidate communications, scheduling and participating in interviews, post-interview debriefs, reference checking, preparing management reports and facilitating effective use of Greenhouse Applicant Tracking System.Conduct a high volume of phone, video, and onsite interviewsSource candidates via various external sources including proactively data mining on LinkedIn and through networking groups, identifying alternative sources for cost effective recruiting.Increase Kobalt’s involvement and outreach in the music / tech communities by attending industry networking and recruiting events.Build qualified candidate pools to meet needs of future opportunitiesProvide interview/systems training to all US Kobalt employees involved in hiring.Manage multiple openings across a range of skill setsManage recruitment and advertising suppliers to facilitate hiringBe knowledgeable about Kobalt and effective in communicating the business and its culture and valuesPresent new and creative ways of engaging talent; keeping up with the latest trends on talent acquisitionSupport new recruitment initiatives such as diversity and inclusion and internshipsWHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKING FOR?Experience of high volume recruiting ideally in a creative or tech industryWeb savvy with experience with recruitment advertising, tools, platforms, CV search and recruiting websites including LinkedInExperienced in selecting and using an ATS (we use Greenhouse)In-depth screening and face to face interview experience – including use of competency-based interview techniquesAnalytical with the ability to manage data, set up and run reports and queries of data and present it to senior team with recommendationsA people person: you’re friendly, social, and genuinely enjoy working with othersApproachable and good listener/note taker and can build a trustworthy relationship with internal stakeholdersStrong organisational and prioritisation skills – carefully plans and structures their work load but can be flexible as priorities frequently changeEnjoys administrative work (intermediate Excel), pays attention to the small details and takes great care making sure all the boxes are ticked and that systems and databases are updated regularly – hands on approach to all aspects of the recruitment end to end processExemplary communication skills and ability to quickly secure credibility in relationshipsA passion for and good understanding of the music industryTenacious and able to concentrate on tasks for a long period of timeCan cope with a high-pressure and fast-paced environment but remains optimistic & persistent to make things happen

BACKGROUND: Founded in 2000 by Willard Ahdritz, Kobalt is creating the music company of the future. Kobalt is a global music and technology company built for artists, songwriters, publishers and labels as an alternative to the traditional music business model. Kobalt have built the technology infrastructure to enable better reporting and for artists to maintain ownership and control over how their work is distributed, tracked, collected, and monetized. This global technology platform can track and collect royalties for the billions of micro-payments per song in digital music today.
WHAT DOES A RECRUITER AT KOBALT DO?: Provide an exceptional recruitment experience from the first point of contact with line manager or candidate through to offer and start date.

Support hiring managers in sourcing and hiring top talent by: assisting with writing job descriptions, advising on sourcing approach, posting jobs, doing initial screenings and engaging talent, managing candidate communications, scheduling and participating in interviews, post-interview debriefs, reference checking, preparing management reports and facilitating effective use of Greenhouse Applicant Tracking System.
Conduct a high volume of phone, video, and onsite interviews
Source candidates via various external sources including proactively data mining on LinkedIn and through networking groups, identifying alternative sources for cost effective recruiting.
Increase Kobalt’s involvement and outreach in the music / tech communities by attending industry networking and recruiting events.
Build qualified candidate pools to meet needs of future opportunities
Provide interview/systems training to all US Kobalt employees involved in hiring.
Manage multiple openings across a range of skill sets
Manage recruitment and advertising suppliers to facilitate hiring
Be knowledgeable about Kobalt and effective in communicating the business and its culture and values
Present new and creative ways of engaging talent; keeping up with the latest trends on talent acquisition
Support new recruitment initiatives such as diversity and inclusion and internships
WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE ARE WE LOOKIN: Experience of high volume recruiting ideally in a creative or tech industry
Web savvy with experience with recruitment advertising, tools, platforms, CV search and recruiting websites including LinkedIn
Experienced in selecting and using an ATS (we use Greenhouse)
In-depth screening and face to face interview experience – including use of competency-based interview techniques
Analytical with the ability to manage data, set up and run reports and queries of data and present it to senior team with recommendations
A people person: you’re friendly, social, and genuinely enjoy working with others
Approachable and good listener/note taker and can build a trustworthy relationship with internal stakeholders
Strong organisational and prioritisation skills – carefully plans and structures their work load but can be flexible as priorities frequently change

Enjoys administrative work (intermediate Excel), pays attention to the small details and takes great care making sure all the boxes are ticked and that systems and databases are updated regularly – hands on approach to all aspects of the recruitment end to end process
Exemplary communication skills and ability to quickly secure credibility in relationships
A passion for and good understanding of the music industry
Tenacious and able to concentrate on tasks for a long period of time
Can cope with a high-pressure and fast-paced environment but remains optimistic & persistent to make things happen

Title: Head of Music and Events (UK)
Description: The Head of Music and Events is a fundamental role within War Child’s highly ambitious London-based Fundraising Leadership Team.Continue to article...Responsible for one of four income generating teams within Fundraising, the successful candidate will lead a team that leverages our profile and heritage within the music industry to raise vital funds and grow sustainable income for War Child UK.Check out the job description here.How to applyPlease download the application pack from https://www.warchild.org.uk/who-we-are/jobsAll applications must be sent to recruitment@warchild.org.uk by 11:59pm on Sunday 8th July 2018Due to limited resources, we will contact only the shortlisted candidatesInterviews will be held the week commencing 17th July 2018

Title: Digital Account Manager (UK)
Description: As the streaming landscape is continuing to grow and change, AWAL is looking for a personable as well as analytical Digital Account Manager to join our Global Digital Accounts team.Continue to article...This amazing opportunity will allow you to work with an incredibly diverse roster of up and coming as well as established acts, helping to drive their playlisting and profiles across digital services.Reporting to the Senior Director, Digital Accounts, you will help with the day to day management of new release information and pitching materials to ensure new and ongoing releases are presented to all UK & International digital accounts as well as building long term playlisting strategies and stories.To be successful in the role, we need someone with a natural incline towards sales, but with a humble approach who is focussed on getting results in innovative and forward-thinking ways.You will be the primary contact for selected platforms and responsible for monitoring and reporting on streams, sales and trends using AWAL’s proprietary analytics tools, so it’s pivotal that you have an analytical mindset, an eye for details and love digging the data!AWAL is the partner of choice for innovative artists at all stages of their careers as we transform the way music is released by creating a high-quality path for artists to remain outside of the traditional label system while maintaining ownership of their masters.Leading artists who have already chosen AWAL includes Lauv, Nick Cave & The Bad Seeds, The Kooks, Tom Misch, The Wombats, Bruno Major, You Me At Six and Laura Marling.If you feel like the fast-paced, rewarding environment of our rapidly growing company is for you – share your story! Apply today and tell us why you are the next best addition to our successful team!What would you do as a Digital Account Manager?Represent AWAL releases to selected DSPsAssist with the day to day relationship with key accounts in the UK and internationally.Build and maintain relationships with key contacts at the DSPsLiaise with project marketing leads to help define and deliver digital sales and streaming strategiesPrioritise release schedule and process pitching information across the AWAL rosterPrepare presentations for playlist and editorial pitching meetingsManage the information flow to our network of international Account ManagersRepresent catalogues to digital accounts as well as frontline releasesGenerate weekly reports on the status of artist campaigns including playlist and on-platform profile resultsAnalyse performance of releases using AWAL’s analytics toolsLiaise with the Digital Operations department to ensure content delivery feeds are timely and completeCommunicate new developments and initiatives at the digital services to the Marketing and Label Management teamsYou will ideally have2 years’ experience working in the music industry, background in Digital Account Management preferredDeep understanding of digital retail platformsComprehensive knowledge of current and future trends in music landscapeWorking operational knowledge of digital delivery processes and digital services requirementsGlobal outlook and understanding of international marketsStrong verbal, written, communication and analytical skillsExcellent time management skillsOrganized self-starter, able to multi-task, adapt to changing priorities, and work in a high-pressure environmentApplicants must be eligible to work in the United Kingdom. Verification checks will be conducted during the interview process and a full DBS check on acceptance of offer. Kobalt is an equal opportunity employer encouraging diversity in the workplace. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or any other status or characteristic protected by law.

Title: Director, Business & Legal Affairs (US)
Description: Octave Music Group, Inc. is transforming the way businesses use music, content and technology to engage consumers in shared spaces. Continue to article...Our offerings are comprised of TouchTunes, the largest in-venue interactive music platform featured in over 75,000 bars and restaurants across North America and Europe, and PlayNetwork, the leading global provider of music and branded media for customer experience. As of 2017, we reach more than 100 million consumers every day in 127 countries around the world through 450 brands, 185,000 locations and 2,500 local partners.  The Director, Business & Legal Affairs will draft and negotiate music license agreements on behalf of TouchTunes and PlayNetwork businesses.The role will also liaise with and provide guidance to other departments (including finance, marketing, business development and product development).Responsibilities:Draft and negotiate music license agreements with record labels, music publishers and societies, including within the United States and internationallyWork closely with the rest of the department to ensure all agreements are accurately summarized and communicated to relevant departmentsProvide advice and guidance in developing and implementing new business strategies with respect to new products and additional territoriesEnsure compliance with contractual and legal obligations, including the participation in the development and implementation of contract-related policies, procedures and reporting Required qualifications:J.D. and active license to practice law in New York StateAt least 3 years’ related experienceMust possess music industry knowledge, including basic knowledge with respect to international markets Required skills/competencies:Strong negotiating, analytical, and organizational skillsStrong interpersonal skills to interface with outside attorneys, senior management, and general staffAbility to work with minimal supervision, self-manage tasks, set priorities and multi-taskSound and practical business judgementStrong attention to detail and the ability to manage a high-volume workload efficiently and smoothlyExcellent written and verbal communication skillsImpeccable follow-up and follow-through capabilitiesComfort in a fast-paced, team-oriented environmentDiscretion is a must. This role requires a high level of confidentiality in all communications Behaviors:Leads by example and exemplifies the Company Values in actions and behaviorsInspires others through his/her professionalism and work integrityResponds creatively to Ccontinuous changeD and steady growth environmentSolutions-oriented team playerSound judgment and decision makingSelf-Starter: seeks out information, training, and other resources needed to facilitate continual professional development necessary to be successful in this positionParticipates in the building and maintaining of a positive and healthy organizational culture Results-oriented, goal driven attitude Work requirements:Deductive Reasoning 4 The ability to apply general rules to specific problems to produce answers that make senseOral Comprehension 4 The ability to listen to and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentencesOral Expression 4 The ability to communicate information and ideas in speaking so others will understand.Written Comprehension 4 The ability to read and understand information and ideas presented in writingWritten Expression 4 The ability to communicate information and ideas in writing so others will understandAbility to perform primarily sedentary work standing or sitting (for 6 hours per day), frequently using a computer (visual acuity, fine motor skills) and telephone (talking/hearing)Must be authorized to work in the United States The Octave Music Group, Inc. and its subsidiaries provide equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, creed, sex, gender identity or expression, age, national origin or ancestry, citizenship, disability, sexual orientation, marital status, pregnancy, veteran status, membership in the uniformed services, genetic information, or any other basis protected by applicable law. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

Title: Account Manager (US)
Description: David Weise and Associates (DWA) is a full service business management firm proud to service the best in the industry. We are highly regarded for being an energetic firm, with the perfect blend of youthful enthusiasm and veteran experience. of each product, drawing from needs expressed by musicians and their support teams (managers, accountants, labels, booking agents, etc)Identify product requirements by working closely with schedules to ensure products delivered as plannedPrepare models / business cases that justify the recommendations and decisions for the feature and product prioritization decisions being made and to customerAbility to travel 1 – 2 times/month primarily in the US, but also globally, to visit customers and partnersExceptional communication skills, both written and verbalPassion for music, concerts, and events4-year degree BS/BA in a technical, business, or other analytical field requiredContact: Dawn Griffith, dawn@lyricfinancial.comLyric Financial is an equal opportunity employer and does not Description: Cooking Vinyl is looking for a new Production Manager to manage its busy UK and international manufacturing and digital delivery requirements.Continue to article...A minimum of one years’ experience within a record label, distributor, manufacturer or DSP is required for this role, which is based in west London.The position involves but is not limited to the following:Overseeing the manufacture and digital distribution of all Cooking Vinyl repertoireWorking to strict production schedules for UK and international releasesBooking mastering sessions; ordering the appropriate production masters and getting them in to manufactureLiaising with product and marketing managers, UK distributer, international managers, brokers and artist managersApproving artwork pdfs, wet proofs, test pressings, test dvds, test blu rays in consultation with management/artistDesign and manufacture promo CDsObtaining quotes on packaging concepts; costingsBriefing designers; ensuring all design work is done to specifications; checking and approving artworkMaintaining and updating the Cooking Vinyl catalogue database / generating catalogue numbers, UPCs and ISRCsCompiling all the necessary credits and performer information to write label copyDeveloping a relationship with manufacturing brokers and working with them to get the best price and serviceArchiving all physical and digital assetsGathering orders from UK and international territories; ordering of all stock and parts; overseeing replication, fulfilment and delivery of productEnsuring international licensees are supplied with parts for local manufacture (audio and artwork masters, printed parts etc.) and approving artwork amendmentsMaintaining and sending out the weekly Cooking Vinyl Release ScheduleCooking Vinyl is a pioneering artist services company and record label which has been successful for over 30 years.Since being founded in 1986, it has partnered with many of the greats of British music and beyond, including The Prodigy, Billy Bragg, Marilyn Manson, Röyksopp, Madness, Alison Moyet, Turin Brakes, Gary Numan, Amanda Palmer, Goldie, Seth Lakeman, Suzanne Vega, The Cult, Lissie, Passenger and Richard Ashcroft.Cooking Vinyl’s recent additions include Nina Nesbitt, Lewis Watson, Fantastic Negrito, Maximo Park and Lucy Spraggan.If this opportunity sounds like it fits your skill set and you believe you have the drive and ambition we need, send a short covering letter along with a copy of your resume to: info@cookingvinyl.com by 25th July.Cooking Vinyl is an equal opportunities employer.
